35pub const CFE_STATUS_NO_COUNTER_INCREMENT: CFE_Status_t = 0x48000001;

37pub const CFE_STATUS_WRONG_MSG_LENGTH: CFE_Status_t = 0xc8000002;

39pub const CFE_STATUS_UNKNOWN_MSG_ID: CFE_Status_t = 0xc8000003;
40#[doc = "@brief Bad Command Code\nThis error code will be returned when a message identification process\ndetermined that the command code is does not correspond to any known value"]
41pub const CFE_STATUS_BAD_COMMAND_CODE: CFE_Status_t = 0xc8000004;
42#[doc = "@brief External failure\nThis error indicates that the operation failed for\nsome reason outside the scope of CFE.  The real failure may\nhave been in OSAL, PSP, or another dependent library.\nDetails of the original failure should be written to syslog\nand/or a system event before returning this error."]
43pub const CFE_STATUS_EXTERNAL_RESOURCE_FAIL: CFE_Status_t = 0xc8000005;
44#[doc = "@brief Request already pending\nCommands or requests are already pending or the pending request\nlimit has been reached.  No more requests can be made until\nthe current request(s) complete."]
45pub const CFE_STATUS_REQUEST_ALREADY_PENDING: int32 = 0xc8000006;
46#[doc = "@brief Request or input value failed basic structural validation\nA message or table input was not in the proper format to be understood\nand processed by an application, and was rejected."]
47pub const CFE_STATUS_VALIDATION_FAILURE: int32 = 0xc8000007;
48#[doc = "@brief Request or input value is out of range\nA message, table, or function call input contained a value that was outside\nthe acceptable range, and the request was rejected."]
49pub const CFE_STATUS_RANGE_ERROR: int32 = 0xc8000008;
50#[doc = "@brief Cannot process request at this time\nThe system is not currently in the correct state to accept the request at\nthis time."]
51pub const CFE_STATUS_INCORRECT_STATE: int32 = 0xc8000009;

55pub const CFE_EVS_UNKNOWN_FILTER: CFE_Status_t = 0xc2000001;

61pub const CFE_EVS_APP_FILTER_OVERLOAD: CFE_Status_t = 0xc2000004;

73pub const CFE_EVS_NOT_IMPLEMENTED: CFE_Status_t = 0xc200ffff;

87pub const CFE_ES_ERR_LOAD_LIB: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000009;

89pub const CFE_ES_BAD_ARGUMENT: CFE_Status_t = 0xc400000a;

91pub const CFE_ES_ERR_CHILD_TASK_REGISTER: CFE_Status_t = 0xc400000b;

93pub const CFE_ES_CDS_ALREADY_EXISTS: CFE_Status_t = 0x4400000d;

95pub const CFE_ES_CDS_INSUFFICIENT_MEMORY: CFE_Status_t = 0xc400000e;

99pub const CFE_ES_CDS_INVALID_SIZE: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000010;
100#[doc = "@brief CDS Invalid\nThe CDS contents are invalid."]
101pub const CFE_ES_CDS_INVALID: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000012;
102#[doc = "@brief CDS Access Error\nThe CDS was inaccessible"]
103pub const CFE_ES_CDS_ACCESS_ERROR: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000013;
104#[doc = "@brief File IO Error\nOccurs when a file operation fails"]
105pub const CFE_ES_FILE_IO_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000014;
106#[doc = "@brief Reset Area Access Error\nOccurs when the BSP is not successful in returning the reset area address."]
107pub const CFE_ES_RST_ACCESS_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000015;

111pub const CFE_ES_ERR_CHILD_TASK_DELETE: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000018;
112#[doc = "@brief Child Task Delete Passed Main Task\nThere was an attempt to delete a cFE App Main Task with\nthe #CFE_ES_DeleteChildTask API."]
113pub const CFE_ES_ERR_CHILD_TASK_DELETE_MAIN_TASK: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000019;
114#[doc = "@brief CDS Block CRC Error\nOccurs when trying to read a CDS Data block and the CRC of the current\ndata does not match the stored CRC for the data.  Either the contents of\nthe CDS Data Block are corrupted or the CDS Control Block is corrupted."]
115pub const CFE_ES_CDS_BLOCK_CRC_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xc400001A;
116#[doc = "@brief Mutex Semaphore Delete Error\nOccurs when trying to delete a Mutex that belongs to a task that ES\nis cleaning up."]
117pub const CFE_ES_MUT_SEM_DELETE_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xc400001B;
118#[doc = "@brief Binary Semaphore Delete Error\nOccurs when trying to delete a Binary Semaphore that belongs to a task that ES\nis cleaning up."]
119pub const CFE_ES_BIN_SEM_DELETE_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xc400001C;
120#[doc = "@brief Counting Semaphore Delete Error\nOccurs when trying to delete a Counting Semaphore that belongs to a task that ES\nis cleaning up."]
121pub const CFE_ES_COUNT_SEM_DELETE_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xc400001D;
122#[doc = "@brief Queue Delete Error\nOccurs when trying to delete a Queue that belongs to a task that ES\nis cleaning up."]
123pub const CFE_ES_QUEUE_DELETE_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xc400001E;
124#[doc = "@brief File Close Error\nOccurs when trying to close a file that belongs to a task that ES\nis cleaning up."]
125pub const CFE_ES_FILE_CLOSE_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xc400001F;
126#[doc = "@brief CDS Wrong Type Error\nOccurs when Table Services is trying to delete a Critical Data Store that\nis not a Critical Table Image or when Executive Services is trying to delete\na Critical Table Image."]
127pub const CFE_ES_CDS_WRONG_TYPE_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000020;
128#[doc = "@brief CDS Owner Active Error\nOccurs when an attempt was made to delete a CDS when an application\nwith the same name associated with the CDS is still present.  CDSs\ncan ONLY be deleted when Applications that created them are not present\nin the system."]
129pub const CFE_ES_CDS_OWNER_ACTIVE_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000022;
130#[doc = "@brief Application Cleanup Error\nOccurs when an attempt was made to Clean Up an application\nwhich involves calling Table, EVS, and SB cleanup functions, then\ndeleting all ES resources, child tasks, and unloading the\nobject module. The approach here is to keep going even though one\nof these steps had an error. There will be syslog messages detailing\neach problem."]
131pub const CFE_ES_APP_CLEANUP_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000023;
132#[doc = "@brief Timer Delete Error\nOccurs when trying to delete a Timer that belongs to a task that ES\nis cleaning up."]
133pub const CFE_ES_TIMER_DELETE_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000024;
134#[doc = "@brief Buffer Not In Pool\nThe specified address is not in the memory pool."]
135pub const CFE_ES_BUFFER_NOT_IN_POOL: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000025;
136#[doc = "@brief Task Delete Error\nOccurs when trying to delete a task that ES\nis cleaning up."]
137pub const CFE_ES_TASK_DELETE_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000026;
138#[doc = "@brief Operation Timed Out\nOccurs if the timeout for a given operation was exceeded"]
139pub const CFE_ES_OPERATION_TIMED_OUT: CFE_Status_t = 0xc4000027;
140#[doc = "@brief Library Already Loaded\nOccurs if CFE_ES_LoadLibrary detects that the requested\nlibrary name is already loaded."]
141pub const CFE_ES_LIB_ALREADY_LOADED: CFE_Status_t = 0x44000028;
142#[doc = "@brief System Log Message Truncated\nThis information code means the last syslog message was truncated\ndue to insufficient space in the log buffer."]
143pub const CFE_ES_ERR_SYS_LOG_TRUNCATED: CFE_Status_t = 0x44000029;
144#[doc = "@brief Resource ID is not available\nThis error indicates that the maximum resource identifiers\n(App ID, Lib ID, Counter ID, etc) has already been reached\nand a new ID cannot be allocated."]
145pub const CFE_ES_NO_RESOURCE_IDS_AVAILABLE: CFE_Status_t = 0xc400002B;
146#[doc = "@brief Invalid pool block\nSoftware attempted to \"put\" a block back into a pool which\ndoes not appear to belong to that pool.  This may mean the\npool has become unusable due to memory corruption."]
147pub const CFE_ES_POOL_BLOCK_INVALID: CFE_Status_t = 0xc400002C;

149pub const CFE_ES_ERR_DUPLICATE_NAME: CFE_Status_t = 0xc400002E;

151pub const CFE_ES_NOT_IMPLEMENTED: CFE_Status_t = 0xc400ffff;

153pub const CFE_FS_BAD_ARGUMENT: CFE_Status_t = 0xc6000001;
154#[doc = "@brief Invalid Path\nFS was unable to extract a filename from a path string"]
155pub const CFE_FS_INVALID_PATH: CFE_Status_t = 0xc6000002;
156#[doc = "@brief Filename Too Long\nFS filename string is too long"]
157pub const CFE_FS_FNAME_TOO_LONG: CFE_Status_t = 0xc6000003;
158#[doc = "@brief Not Implemented\nCurrent version of cFE does not have the function or the feature\nof the function implemented.  This could be due to either an early\nbuild of the cFE for this platform or the platform does not support\nthe specified feature."]
159pub const CFE_FS_NOT_IMPLEMENTED: CFE_Status_t = 0xc600ffff;
160#[doc = "@brief Time Out\nIn #CFE_SB_ReceiveBuffer, this return value indicates that a packet has not\nbeen received in the time given in the \"timeout\" parameter."]
161pub const CFE_SB_TIME_OUT: CFE_Status_t = 0xca000001;
162#[doc = "@brief No Message\nWhen \"Polling\" a pipe for a message in #CFE_SB_ReceiveBuffer, this return\nvalue indicates that there was not a message on the pipe."]
163pub const CFE_SB_NO_MESSAGE: CFE_Status_t = 0xca000002;
164#[doc = "@brief Bad Argument\nA parameter given by a caller to a Software Bus API did not pass\nvalidation checks."]
165pub const CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT: CFE_Status_t = 0xca000003;
166#[doc = "@brief Max Pipes Met\nThis error code will be returned from #CFE_SB_CreatePipe when the\nSB cannot accommodate the request to create a pipe because the maximum\nnumber of pipes (#CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MAX_PIPES) are in use. This configuration\nparameter is defined in the cfe_platform_cfg.h file."]
167pub const CFE_SB_MAX_PIPES_MET: CFE_Status_t = 0xca000004;
168#[doc = "@brief Pipe Create Error\nThe maximum number of queues(#OS_MAX_QUEUES) are in use. Or possibly a\nlower level problem with creating the underlying queue has occurred\nsuch as a lack of memory. If the latter is the problem, the status\ncode displayed in the event must be tracked."]
169pub const CFE_SB_PIPE_CR_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xca000005;
170#[doc = "@brief Pipe Read Error\nThis return value indicates an error at the Queue read level. This\nerror typically cannot be corrected by the caller. Some possible\ncauses are: queue was not properly initialized or created, the number\nof bytes read from the queue was not the number of bytes requested in\nthe read. The queue id is invalid. Similar errors regarding the pipe\nwill be caught by higher level code in the Software Bus."]
171pub const CFE_SB_PIPE_RD_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xca000006;
172#[doc = "@brief Message Too Big\nThe size field in the message header indicates the message exceeds the\nmax Software Bus message size. The max size is defined by\nconfiguration parameter #CFE_MISSION_SB_MAX_SB_MSG_SIZE in cfe_mission_cfg.h"]
173pub const CFE_SB_MSG_TOO_BIG: CFE_Status_t = 0xca000007;
174#[doc = "@brief Buffer Allocation Error\nReturned when the memory in the SB message buffer pool has been depleted.\nThe amount of memory in the pool is dictated by the configuration parameter\n#CFE_PLATFORM_SB_BUF_MEMORY_BYTES specified in the cfe_platform_cfg.h file. Also\nthe memory statistics, including current utilization figures and high\nwater marks for the SB Buffer memory pool can be monitored by sending\na Software Bus command to send the SB statistics packet."]
175pub const CFE_SB_BUF_ALOC_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xca000008;
176#[doc = "@brief Max Messages Met\nWill be returned when calling one of the SB subscription API\'s if the\nSB routing table cannot accommodate another unique message ID because\nthe platform configuration parameter #CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MAX_MSG_IDS has been met."]
177pub const CFE_SB_MAX_MSGS_MET: CFE_Status_t = 0xca000009;
178#[doc = "@brief Max Destinations Met\nWill be returned when calling one of the SB subscription API\'s if the\nSB routing table cannot accommodate another destination for a\nparticular the given message ID. This occurs when the number of\ndestinations in use meets the platform configuration parameter\n#CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MAX_DEST_PER_PKT."]
179pub const CFE_SB_MAX_DESTS_MET: CFE_Status_t = 0xca00000a;
180#[doc = "@brief Internal Error\nThis error code will be returned by the #CFE_SB_Subscribe API if the\ncode detects an internal index is out of range. The most likely\ncause would be a Single Event Upset."]
181pub const CFE_SB_INTERNAL_ERR: CFE_Status_t = 0xca00000c;
182#[doc = "@brief Wrong Message Type\nThis error code will be returned when a request such as #CFE_MSG_SetMsgTime\nis made on a packet that does not include a field for msg time."]
183pub const CFE_SB_WRONG_MSG_TYPE: CFE_Status_t = 0xca00000d;
184#[doc = "@brief Buffer Invalid\nThis error code will be returned when a request to release or send a\nzero copy buffer is invalid, such as if the handle or buffer is not\ncorrect or the buffer was previously released."]
185pub const CFE_SB_BUFFER_INVALID: CFE_Status_t = 0xca00000e;
186#[doc = "@brief Not Implemented\nCurrent version of cFE does not have the function or the feature\nof the function implemented.  This could be due to either an early\nbuild of the cFE for this platform or the platform does not support\nthe specified feature."]
187pub const CFE_SB_NOT_IMPLEMENTED: CFE_Status_t = 0xca00ffff;
188#[doc = "@brief Invalid Handle\nThe calling Application attempted to pass a\nTable handle that represented too large an index or\nidentified a Table Access Descriptor that was not used."]
189pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000001;
190#[doc = "@brief Invalid Name\nThe calling Application attempted to register a table whose\nname length exceeded the platform configuration value of\n#CFE_MISSION_TBL_MAX_NAME_LENGTH or was zero characters long."]
191pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_NAME: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000002;
192#[doc = "@brief Invalid Size\nThe calling Application attempted to register a table:\na) that was a double buffered table with size greater than #CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_DBL_TABLE_SIZE\nb) that was a single buffered table with size greater than #CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_SNGL_TABLE_SIZE\nc) that had a size of zero"]
193pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_SIZE: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000003;
194#[doc = "@brief Update Pending\nThe calling Application has identified a table that has a load pending."]
195pub const CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATE_PENDING: CFE_Status_t = 0x4c000004;
196#[doc = "@brief Never Loaded\nTable has not been loaded with data."]
197pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_NEVER_LOADED: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000005;
198#[doc = "@brief Registry Full\nAn application attempted to create a table and the Table\nregistry already contained #CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_NUM_TABLES in it."]
199pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_REGISTRY_FULL: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000006;
200#[doc = "@brief Duplicate Warning\nThis is an error that the registration is trying to replace\nan existing table with the same name.  The previous table\nstays in place and the new table is rejected."]
201pub const CFE_TBL_WARN_DUPLICATE: CFE_Status_t = 0x4c000007;
202#[doc = "@brief No Access\nThe calling application either failed when calling #CFE_TBL_Register,\nfailed when calling #CFE_TBL_Share or forgot to call either one."]
203pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000008;
204#[doc = "@brief Unregistered\nThe calling application is trying to access a table that has\nbeen unregistered."]
205pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_UNREGISTERED: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000009;
206#[doc = "@brief Handles Full\nAn application attempted to create a table and the Table\nHandle Array already used all CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_NUM_HANDLES in it."]
207pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_HANDLES_FULL: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc00000B;
208#[doc = "@brief Duplicate Table With Different Size\nAn application attempted to register a table with the same name\nas a table that is already in the registry.  The size of the new\ntable is different from the size already in the registry."]
209pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_DUPLICATE_DIFF_SIZE: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc00000C;
210#[doc = "@brief Duplicate Table And Not Owned\nAn application attempted to register a table with the same name\nas a table that is already in the registry.  The previously registered\ntable is owned by a different application."]
211pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_DUPLICATE_NOT_OWNED: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc00000D;
212#[doc = "@brief Updated\nThe calling Application has identified a table that has been updated.<BR>\n\\b NOTE: This is a nominal return code informing the calling application\nthat the table identified in the call has had its contents updated since\nthe last time the application obtained its address or status."]
213pub const CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATED: CFE_Status_t = 0x4c00000E;
214#[doc = "@brief No Buffer Available\nThe calling Application has tried to allocate a working buffer but\nnone were available."]
215pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_BUFFER_AVAIL: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc00000F;
216#[doc = "@brief Dump Only Error\nThe calling Application has attempted to perform a load on a\ntable that was created with \"Dump Only\" attributes."]
217pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_DUMP_ONLY: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000010;
218#[doc = "@brief Illegal Source Type\nThe calling Application called #CFE_TBL_Load with an illegal\nvalue for the second parameter."]
219pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_ILLEGAL_SRC_TYPE: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000011;
220#[doc = "@brief Load In Progress\nThe calling Application called #CFE_TBL_Load when another Application\nwas trying to load the table."]
221pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_LOAD_IN_PROGRESS: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000012;
222#[doc = "@brief File Too Large\nThe calling Application called #CFE_TBL_Load with a filename that specified a file\nthat contained more data than the size of the table OR which contained more data\nthan specified in the table header."]
223pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_FILE_TOO_LARGE: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000014;
224#[doc = "@brief Short File Warning\nThe calling Application called #CFE_TBL_Load with a filename that specified a file\nthat started with the first byte of the table but contained less data than the size of the table.\nIt should be noted that #CFE_TBL_WARN_PARTIAL_LOAD also indicates a partial load (one that starts\nat a non-zero offset)."]
225pub const CFE_TBL_WARN_SHORT_FILE: CFE_Status_t = 0x4c000015;
226#[doc = "@brief Bad Content ID\nThe calling Application called #CFE_TBL_Load with a filename that specified a file\nwhose content ID was not that of a table image."]
227pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_BAD_CONTENT_ID: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000016;
228#[doc = "@brief No Update Pending\nThe calling Application has attempted to update a table without a pending load."]
229pub const CFE_TBL_INFO_NO_UPDATE_PENDING: CFE_Status_t = 0x4c000017;
230#[doc = "@brief Table Locked\nThe calling Application tried to update a table that is locked by another user."]
231pub const CFE_TBL_INFO_TABLE_LOCKED: CFE_Status_t = 0x4c000018;
232#[doc = "Validation Pending\nThe calling Application should call #CFE_TBL_Validate for the specified table."]
233pub const CFE_TBL_INFO_VALIDATION_PENDING: CFE_Status_t = 0x4c000019;
234#[doc = "No Validation Pending\nThe calling Application tried to validate a table that did not have a validation request pending."]
235pub const CFE_TBL_INFO_NO_VALIDATION_PENDING: CFE_Status_t = 0x4c00001A;
236#[doc = "@brief Bad Subtype ID\nThe calling Application tried to access a table file whose Subtype identifier indicated it was not\na table image file."]
237pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_BAD_SUBTYPE_ID: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc00001B;
238#[doc = "@brief File Size Inconsistent\nThe calling Application tried to access a table file whose Subtype identifier indicated it was not\na table image file."]
239pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_FILE_SIZE_INCONSISTENT: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc00001C;
240#[doc = "@brief No Standard Header\nThe calling Application tried to access a table file whose standard cFE File Header was the wrong size, etc."]
241pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_STD_HEADER: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc00001D;
242#[doc = "@brief No Table Header\nThe calling Application tried to access a table file whose standard cFE\nTable File Header was the wrong size, etc."]
243pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_TBL_HEADER: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc00001E;
244#[doc = "@brief Filename Too Long\nThe calling Application tried to load a table using a filename\nthat was too long."]
245pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_FILENAME_TOO_LONG: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc00001F;
246#[doc = "@brief File For Wrong Table\nThe calling Application tried to load a table using a file whose\nheader indicated that it was for a different table."]
247pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_FILE_FOR_WRONG_TABLE: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000020;
248#[doc = "@brief Load Incomplete\nThe calling Application tried to load a table file whose header\nclaimed the load was larger than what was actually read from the file."]
249pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_LOAD_INCOMPLETE: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000021;
250#[doc = "@brief Partial Load Warning\nThe calling Application tried to load a table file whose header\nclaimed the load did not start with the first byte. It should be noted\nthat #CFE_TBL_WARN_SHORT_FILE also indicates a partial load."]
251pub const CFE_TBL_WARN_PARTIAL_LOAD: CFE_Status_t = 0x4c000022;
252#[doc = "@brief Partial Load Error\nThe calling Application tried to load a table file whose header\nclaimed the load did not start with the first byte and the table\nimage had NEVER been loaded before.  Partial loads are not allowed\non uninitialized tables.  It should be noted that\n#CFE_TBL_WARN_SHORT_FILE also indicates a partial load."]
253pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_PARTIAL_LOAD: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000023;
254#[doc = "@brief Dump Pending\nThe calling Application should call #CFE_TBL_Manage for the specified table.\nThe ground has requested a dump of the Dump-Only table and needs to synchronize\nwith the owning application."]
255pub const CFE_TBL_INFO_DUMP_PENDING: CFE_Status_t = 0x4c000024;
256#[doc = "@brief Invalid Options\nThe calling Application has used an illegal combination of table options.\nA summary of the illegal combinations are as follows:\n\\par   #CFE_TBL_OPT_USR_DEF_ADDR cannot be combined with any of the following:\n-# #CFE_TBL_OPT_DBL_BUFFER\n-# #CFE_TBL_OPT_LOAD_DUMP\n-# #CFE_TBL_OPT_CRITICAL\n\\par   #CFE_TBL_OPT_DBL_BUFFER cannot be combined with the following:\n-# #CFE_TBL_OPT_USR_DEF_ADDR\n-# #CFE_TBL_OPT_DUMP_ONLY"]
257pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_OPTIONS: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000025;
258#[doc = "@brief Not Critical Warning\nThe calling Application attempted to register a table as \"Critical\".\nTable Services failed to create an appropriate Critical Data Store\n(See System Log for reason) to save the table contents.  The table\nwill be treated as a normal table from now on."]
259pub const CFE_TBL_WARN_NOT_CRITICAL: CFE_Status_t = 0x4c000026;
260#[doc = "@brief Recovered Table\nThe calling Application registered a critical table whose previous\ncontents were discovered in the Critical Data Store.  The discovered\ncontents were copied back into the newly registered table as the\ntable\'s initial contents.<BR>\n\\b NOTE: In this situation, the contents of the table are \\b NOT\nvalidated using the table\'s validation function."]
261pub const CFE_TBL_INFO_RECOVERED_TBL: CFE_Status_t = 0x4c000027;
262#[doc = "@brief Bad Spacecraft ID\nThe selected table file failed validation for Spacecraft ID.\nThe platform configuration file has verification of table files\nenabled for Spacecraft ID and an attempt was made to load a table\nwith an invalid Spacecraft ID in the table file header."]
263pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_BAD_SPACECRAFT_ID: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000028;
264#[doc = "@brief Bad Processor ID\nThe selected table file failed validation for Processor ID.\nThe platform configuration file has verification of table files\nenabled for Processor ID and an attempt was made to load a table\nwith an invalid Processor ID in the table file header."]
265pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_BAD_PROCESSOR_ID: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc000029;
266#[doc = "@brief Message Error\nError code indicating that the TBL command was not processed\nsuccessfully and that the error counter should be incremented."]
267pub const CFE_TBL_MESSAGE_ERROR: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc00002a;
268#[doc = "*  Error code indicating that the TBL file is shorter than\n*  indicated in the file header."]
269pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_SHORT_FILE: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc00002b;
270#[doc = "*  Error code indicating that the TBL file could not be\n*  opened by the OS."]
271pub const CFE_TBL_ERR_ACCESS: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc00002c;
272#[doc = "@brief Bad Argument\nA parameter given by a caller to a Table API did not pass\nvalidation checks."]
273pub const CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc00002d;
274#[doc = "@brief Not Implemented\nCurrent version of cFE does not have the function or the feature\nof the function implemented.  This could be due to either an early\nbuild of the cFE for this platform or the platform does not support\nthe specified feature."]
275pub const CFE_TBL_NOT_IMPLEMENTED: CFE_Status_t = 0xcc00ffff;
276#[doc = "@brief Not Implemented\nCurrent version of cFE does not have the function or the feature\nof the function implemented.  This could be due to either an early\nbuild of the cFE for this platform or the platform does not support\nthe specified feature."]
277pub const CFE_TIME_NOT_IMPLEMENTED: CFE_Status_t = 0xce00ffff;
278#[doc = "@brief Internal Only\nOne of the TIME Services API functions to set the time with data\nfrom an external time source has been called, but TIME Services\nhas been commanded to not accept external time data.  However,\nthe command is still a signal for the Time Server to generate\na \"time at the tone\" command packet using internal data."]
279pub const CFE_TIME_INTERNAL_ONLY: CFE_Status_t = 0xce000001;
280#[doc = "@brief Out Of Range\nOne of the TIME Services API functions to set the time with data\nfrom an external time source has been called, but TIME Services\nhas determined that the new time data is invalid.  However,\nthe command is still a signal for the Time Server to generate\na \"time at the tone\" command packet using internal data.\nNote that the test for invalid time update data only occurs if TIME\nServices has previously been commanded to set the clock state\nto \"valid\"."]
281pub const CFE_TIME_OUT_OF_RANGE: CFE_Status_t = 0xce000002;
282#[doc = "@brief Too Many Sync Callbacks\nAn attempt to register too many cFE Time Services Synchronization\ncallbacks has been made.  Only one callback function is allowed per\napplication.  It is expected that the application itself will\ndistribute the single callback to child threads as needed."]
283pub const CFE_TIME_TOO_MANY_SYNCH_CALLBACKS: CFE_Status_t = 0xce000003;
284#[doc = "@brief Callback Not Registered\nAn attempt to unregister a cFE Time Services Synchronization\ncallback has failed because the specified callback function was not\nlocated in the Synchronization Callback Registry."]
285pub const CFE_TIME_CALLBACK_NOT_REGISTERED: CFE_Status_t = 0xce000004;
286#[doc = "@brief Bad Argument\nA parameter given by a caller to a TIME Services API did not pass\nvalidation checks."]
287pub const CFE_TIME_BAD_ARGUMENT: CFE_Status_t = 0xce000005;

313// #[doc = "@brief A resource ID value that represents an undefined/unused resource\nThis constant may be used to initialize local variables of the\nCFE_ResourceId_t type to a safe value that will not alias a valid ID.\nBy design, this value is also the result of zeroing a CFE_ResourceId_t\ntype via standard functions like memset(), such that objects initialized\nusing this method will also be set to safe values."]
314// pub const CFE_RESOURCEID_UNDEFINED: /* ? */ = /* ((CFE_ResourceId_t)CFE_RESOURCEID_WRAP(0)) */;
315// #[doc = "@brief A resource ID value that represents a reserved entry\nThis is not a valid value for any resource type, but is used to mark\ntable entries that are not available for use.  For instance, this may\nbe used while setting up an entry initially."]
316// pub const CFE_RESOURCEID_RESERVED: /* ? */ = /* ((CFE_ResourceId_t)CFE_RESOURCEID_WRAP(0xFFFFFFFF)) */;
317//

319// ----------------------------------------
320// #[doc = "\\brief Reserved value for CFE_SB_MsgId_t that will not match any valid MsgId\nThis rvalue macro can be used for static/compile-time data initialization to ensure that\nthe initialized value does not alias to a valid MsgId object."]
321// pub const CFE_SB_MSGID_RESERVED: /* ? */ = /* CFE_SB_MSGID_WRAP_VALUE(0) */;
322// #[doc = "\\brief A literal of the CFE_SB_MsgId_t type representing an invalid ID\nThis value should be used for runtime initialization of CFE_SB_MsgId_t values.\n\\note This may be a compound literal in a future revision.  Per C99, compound\nliterals are lvalues, not rvalues, so this value should not be used in\nstatic/compile-time data initialization.  For static data initialization\npurposes (rvalue), #CFE_SB_MSGID_RESERVED should be used instead.\nHowever, in the current implementation, they are equivalent."]
323// pub const CFE_SB_INVALID_MSG_ID: /* ? */ = /* CFE_SB_MSGID_C(0) */;
324// #[doc = "\\brief  A CFE_SB_PipeId_t value which is always invalid\nThis may be used as a safe initializer for CFE_SB_PipeId_t values"]
325// pub const CFE_SB_INVALID_PIPE: /* ? */ = /* CFE_SB_PIPEID_C(CFE_RESOURCEID_UNDEFINED) */;

346// #[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Mission Default CRC algorithm\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      Indicates the which CRC algorithm should be used as the default\n*      for verifying the contents of Critical Data Stores and when calculating\n*      Table Image data integrity values.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*      Currently only CFE_ES_CrcType_16_ARC is supported (see brief in CFE_ES_CrcType_Enum\n*      definition in cfe_es_api_typedefs.h)"]

353// ----------------------------------------
354// #[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define ES Task Stack Size\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the cFE_ES Task Stack Size\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 2048 on this configuration parameter.  There\n*       are no restrictions on the upper limit however, the maximum stack size\n*       is system dependent and should be verified.  Most operating systems provide\n*       tools for measuring the amount of stack used by a task during operation. It\n*       is always a good idea to verify that no more than 1/2 of the stack is used."]
355// pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_START_TASK_STACK_SIZE: /* ? */ = /* CFE_PLATFORM_ES_DEFAULT_STACK_SIZE */;
356//

359// #[doc = "*  \\cfeevscfg Default EVS Message Format Mode\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the default message format (long or short) for event messages being\n*       sent to the ground. Choose between #CFE_EVS_MsgFormat_LONG or\n*       #CFE_EVS_MsgFormat_SHORT.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The valid settings are #CFE_EVS_MsgFormat_LONG or #CFE_EVS_MsgFormat_SHORT"]
360// pub const CFE_PLATFORM_EVS_DEFAULT_MSG_FORMAT_MODE: /* ? */ = /* CFE_EVS_MsgFormat_LONG */;

The filtering will begin after the SB task initializes\n*       and stay in effect until a cmd to SB changes it.\n*       This allows the operator to set limits on the number of event messages that\n*       are sent during system initialization.\n*       NOTE: Set all unused event values and mask values to zero\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This filtering applies only to SB events.\n*       These parameters have a lower limit of 0 and an upper limit of 65535."]

459#[doc = "\\brief The maximum number of condition variables to support\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_MAX_CONDVARS configuration option"]
460pub const OS_MAX_CONDVARS: u32 = 4;
461#[doc = "\\brief The maximum number of modules to support\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_MAX_MODULES configuration option"]
462pub const OS_MAX_MODULES: u32 = 20;
463#[doc = "\\brief The maximum number of timebases to support\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_MAX_TIMEBASES configuration option"]
464pub const OS_MAX_TIMEBASES: u32 = 5;
465#[doc = "\\brief The maximum number of timer callbacks to support\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_MAX_TIMERS configuration option"]
466pub const OS_MAX_TIMERS: u32 = 10;
467#[doc = "\\brief The maximum number of concurrently open files to support\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_MAX_NUM_OPEN_FILES configuration option"]
468pub const OS_MAX_NUM_OPEN_FILES: u32 = 50;
469#[doc = "\\brief The maximum number of concurrently open directories to support\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_MAX_NUM_OPEN_DIRS configuration option"]
470pub const OS_MAX_NUM_OPEN_DIRS: u32 = 4;
471#[doc = "\\brief The maximum number of file systems to support\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_MAX_FILE_SYSTEMS configuration option"]
472pub const OS_MAX_FILE_SYSTEMS: u32 = 14;
473#[doc = "\\brief The maximum length of symbols\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_MAX_SYM_LEN configuration option\n\\note This value must include a terminating NUL character"]
474pub const OS_MAX_SYM_LEN: u32 = 64;
475#[doc = "\\brief The maximum length of OSAL file names\nThis limit applies specifically to the file name portion, not the\ndirectory portion, of a path name.\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_MAX_FILE_NAME configuration option\n\\note This value must include a terminating NUL character"]
476pub const OS_MAX_FILE_NAME: u32 = 20;
477#[doc = "\\brief The maximum length of OSAL path names\nThis limit applies to the overall length of a path name, including the\nfile name and directory portions.\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_MAX_PATH_LEN configuration option\n\\note This value must include a terminating NUL character"]
478pub const OS_MAX_PATH_LEN: u32 = 64;
479#[doc = "\\brief The maximum length of OSAL resource names\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_MAX_API_NAME configuration option\n\\note This value must include a terminating NUL character"]
480pub const OS_MAX_API_NAME: u32 = 20;
481#[doc = "\\brief The maximum size of the socket address structure\nThis is part of the Socket API, and should be set large enough to hold\nthe largest address type in use on the target system.\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_SOCKADDR_MAX_LEN configuration option"]
482pub const OS_SOCKADDR_MAX_LEN: u32 = 28;
483#[doc = "\\brief The maximum size of output produced by a single OS_printf()\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_PRINTF_BUFFER_SIZE configuration option"]
484pub const OS_BUFFER_SIZE: u32 = 172;
485#[doc = "\\brief The maximum number of OS_printf() output strings to buffer\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_PRINTF_BUFFER_DEPTH configuration option"]
486pub const OS_BUFFER_MSG_DEPTH: u32 = 100;
487#[doc = "\\brief Priority level of the background utility task\nThis task is responsible for writing buffered output of OS_printf to the actual\nconsole device, and any other future maintenance task.\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_UTILITYTASK_PRIORITY configuration option"]
488pub const OS_UTILITYTASK_PRIORITY: u32 = 10;
489#[doc = "\\brief The stack size of the background utility task\nThis task is responsible for writing buffered output of OS_printf to the actual\nconsole device, and any other future maintenance task.\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_UTILITYTASK_STACK_SIZE configuration option"]
490pub const OS_UTILITYTASK_STACK_SIZE: u32 = 2048;
491#[doc = "\\brief The maximum size of a shell command\nThis limit is only applicable if shell support is enabled.\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_MAX_CMD_LEN configuration option\n\\note This value must include a terminating NUL character"]
492pub const OS_MAX_CMD_LEN: u32 = 1000;
493#[doc = "\\brief The maximum depth of OSAL queues\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH configuration option"]
494pub const OS_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH: u32 = 50;
495#[doc = "\\brief The name of the temporary file used to store shell commands\nThis configuration is only applicable if shell support is enabled, and\nonly necessary/relevant on some OS implementations.\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_SHELL_CMD_INPUT_FILE_NAME configuration option"]
496pub const OS_SHELL_CMD_INPUT_FILE_NAME: &[u8; 1] = b"\0";
497#[doc = "\\brief The name of the primary console device\nThis is the device to which OS_printf() output is written.  The output\nmay be configured to tag each line with this prefix for identification.\nBased on the OSAL_CONFIG_PRINTF_CONSOLE_NAME configuration option"]
498pub const OS_PRINTF_CONSOLE_NAME: &[u8; 1] = b"\0";
499#[doc = "\\brief Flags added to all tasks on creation\nAdded to the task flags on creation\nSupports adding floating point support for all tasks when the OS requires it"]
500pub const OS_ADD_TASK_FLAGS: u32 = 0;
501#[doc = "\\brief The maximum number of console devices to support\nFixed value based on current OSAL implementation, not user configurable."]
502pub const OS_MAX_CONSOLES: u32 = 1;
503#[doc = "\\brief The system-specific file extension used on loadable module files\nFixed value based on system selection, not user configurable."]
504pub const OS_MODULE_FILE_EXTENSION: &[u8; 4] = b".so\0";
505#[doc = "Device name length"]
506pub const OS_FS_DEV_NAME_LEN: u32 = 32;
507#[doc = "Physical drive name length"]
508pub const OS_FS_PHYS_NAME_LEN: u32 = 64;
509#[doc = "Volume name length"]
510pub const OS_FS_VOL_NAME_LEN: u32 = 32;
511#[doc = "@brief Semaphore full state"]
512pub const OS_SEM_FULL: u32 = 1;
513#[doc = "@brief Semaphore empty state"]
514pub const OS_SEM_EMPTY: u32 = 0;
515pub const OS_PEND: i32 = -1;
516pub const OS_CHECK: u32 = 0;
517#[doc = "@brief Maximum length of a local/native path name string\nThis is a concatenation of the OSAL virtual path with the system\nmount point or device name"]
518pub const OS_MAX_LOCAL_PATH_LEN: u32 = 128;
519#[doc = "@brief Error string name length\nThe sizes of strings in OSAL functions are built with this limit in mind.\nAlways check the uses of os_err_name_t when changing this value."]
520pub const OS_ERROR_NAME_LENGTH: u32 = 35;
521#[doc = "@brief Status converted to string length limit\nUsed for sizing os_status_string_t intended for use in printing osal_status_t values\nSized to fit LONG_MIN including NULL termination"]
522pub const OS_STATUS_STRING_LENGTH: u32 = 12;
523#[doc = "@brief Successful execution"]
524pub const OS_SUCCESS: u32 = 0;
525#[doc = "@brief Failed execution"]
526pub const OS_ERROR: i32 = -1;
527#[doc = "@brief Invalid pointer"]
528pub const OS_INVALID_POINTER: i32 = -2;
529#[doc = "@brief Address misalignment"]
530pub const OS_ERROR_ADDRESS_MISALIGNED: i32 = -3;
531#[doc = "@brief Error timeout"]
532pub const OS_ERROR_TIMEOUT: i32 = -4;
533#[doc = "@brief Invalid Interrupt number"]
534pub const OS_INVALID_INT_NUM: i32 = -5;
535#[doc = "@brief Semaphore failure"]
536pub const OS_SEM_FAILURE: i32 = -6;
537#[doc = "@brief Semaphore timeout"]
538pub const OS_SEM_TIMEOUT: i32 = -7;
539#[doc = "@brief Queue empty"]
540pub const OS_QUEUE_EMPTY: i32 = -8;
541#[doc = "@brief Queue full"]
542pub const OS_QUEUE_FULL: i32 = -9;
543#[doc = "@brief Queue timeout"]
544pub const OS_QUEUE_TIMEOUT: i32 = -10;
545#[doc = "@brief Queue invalid size"]
546pub const OS_QUEUE_INVALID_SIZE: i32 = -11;
547#[doc = "@brief Queue ID error"]
548pub const OS_QUEUE_ID_ERROR: i32 = -12;
549#[doc = "@brief name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME"]
550pub const OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG: i32 = -13;
551#[doc = "@brief No free IDs"]
552pub const OS_ERR_NO_FREE_IDS: i32 = -14;
553#[doc = "@brief Name taken"]
554pub const OS_ERR_NAME_TAKEN: i32 = -15;
555#[doc = "@brief Invalid ID"]
556pub const OS_ERR_INVALID_ID: i32 = -16;
557#[doc = "@brief Name not found"]
558pub const OS_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND: i32 = -17;
559#[doc = "@brief Semaphore not full"]
560pub const OS_ERR_SEM_NOT_FULL: i32 = -18;
561#[doc = "@brief Invalid priority"]
562pub const OS_ERR_INVALID_PRIORITY: i32 = -19;
563#[doc = "@brief Invalid semaphore value"]
564pub const OS_INVALID_SEM_VALUE: i32 = -20;
565#[doc = "@brief File error"]
566pub const OS_ERR_FILE: i32 = -27;
567#[doc = "@brief Not implemented"]
568pub const OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED: i32 = -28;
569#[doc = "@brief Timer invalid arguments"]
570pub const OS_TIMER_ERR_INVALID_ARGS: i32 = -29;
571#[doc = "@brief Timer ID error"]
572pub const OS_TIMER_ERR_TIMER_ID: i32 = -30;
573#[doc = "@brief Timer unavailable"]
574pub const OS_TIMER_ERR_UNAVAILABLE: i32 = -31;
575#[doc = "@brief Timer internal error"]
576pub const OS_TIMER_ERR_INTERNAL: i32 = -32;
577#[doc = "@brief Object in use"]
578pub const OS_ERR_OBJECT_IN_USE: i32 = -33;
579#[doc = "@brief Bad address"]
580pub const OS_ERR_BAD_ADDRESS: i32 = -34;
581#[doc = "@brief Incorrect object state"]
582pub const OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE: i32 = -35;
583#[doc = "@brief Incorrect object type"]
584pub const OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_TYPE: i32 = -36;
585#[doc = "@brief Stream disconnected"]
586pub const OS_ERR_STREAM_DISCONNECTED: i32 = -37;
587#[doc = "@brief Requested operation not support on supplied object(s)"]
588pub const OS_ERR_OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED: i32 = -38;
589#[doc = "@brief Invalid Size"]
590pub const OS_ERR_INVALID_SIZE: i32 = -40;
591#[doc = "@brief Size of output exceeds limit"]
592pub const OS_ERR_OUTPUT_TOO_LARGE: i32 = -41;
593#[doc = "@brief Invalid argument value (other than ID or size)"]
594pub const OS_ERR_INVALID_ARGUMENT: i32 = -42;
595#[doc = "@brief FS path too long"]
596pub const OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG: i32 = -103;
597#[doc = "@brief FS name too long"]
598pub const OS_FS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG: i32 = -104;
599#[doc = "@brief FS drive not created"]
600pub const OS_FS_ERR_DRIVE_NOT_CREATED: i32 = -106;
601#[doc = "@brief FS device not free"]
602pub const OS_FS_ERR_DEVICE_NOT_FREE: i32 = -107;
603#[doc = "@brief FS path invalid"]
604pub const OS_FS_ERR_PATH_INVALID: i32 = -108;
605#[doc = "Read only file access"]
606pub const OS_READ_ONLY: u32 = 0;
607#[doc = "Write only file access"]
608pub const OS_WRITE_ONLY: u32 = 1;
609#[doc = "Read write file access"]
610pub const OS_READ_WRITE: u32 = 2;
611#[doc = "Seek offset set"]
612pub const OS_SEEK_SET: u32 = 0;
613#[doc = "Seek offset current"]
614pub const OS_SEEK_CUR: u32 = 1;
615#[doc = "Seek offset end"]
616pub const OS_SEEK_END: u32 = 2;
617#[doc = "Unused, API takes bool"]
618pub const OS_CHK_ONLY: u32 = 0;
619#[doc = "Unused, API takes bool"]
620pub const OS_REPAIR: u32 = 1;
621#[doc = "@brief Object index mask"]
622pub const OS_OBJECT_INDEX_MASK: u32 = 65535;
623#[doc = "@brief Object type shift"]
624pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_SHIFT: u32 = 16;
625#[doc = "@brief Object type undefined"]
626pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_UNDEFINED: u32 = 0;
627#[doc = "@brief Object task type"]
628pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_OS_TASK: u32 = 1;
629#[doc = "@brief Object queue type"]
630pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_OS_QUEUE: u32 = 2;
631#[doc = "@brief Object counting semaphore type"]
632pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_OS_COUNTSEM: u32 = 3;
633#[doc = "@brief Object binary semaphore type"]
634pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_OS_BINSEM: u32 = 4;
635#[doc = "@brief Object mutex type"]
636pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_OS_MUTEX: u32 = 5;
637#[doc = "@brief Object stream type"]
638pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_OS_STREAM: u32 = 6;
639#[doc = "@brief Object directory type"]
640pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_OS_DIR: u32 = 7;
641#[doc = "@brief Object timebase type"]
642pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_OS_TIMEBASE: u32 = 8;
643#[doc = "@brief Object timer callback type"]
644pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_OS_TIMECB: u32 = 9;
645#[doc = "@brief Object module type"]
646pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_OS_MODULE: u32 = 10;
647#[doc = "@brief Object file system type"]
648pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_OS_FILESYS: u32 = 11;
649#[doc = "@brief Object console type"]
650pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_OS_CONSOLE: u32 = 12;
651#[doc = "@brief Object condition variable type"]
652pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_OS_CONDVAR: u32 = 13;
653#[doc = "@brief Object user type"]
654pub const OS_OBJECT_TYPE_USER: u32 = 16;
655#[doc = "@brief Requests OS_ModuleLoad() to add the symbols to the global symbol table\nWhen supplied as the \"flags\" argument to OS_ModuleLoad(), this indicates\nthat the symbols in the loaded module should be added to the global symbol\ntable.  This will make symbols in this library available for use when\nresolving symbols in future module loads.\nThis is the default mode of operation for OS_ModuleLoad().\n@note On some operating systems, use of this option may make it difficult\nto unload the module in the future, if the symbols are in use by other entities."]
656pub const OS_MODULE_FLAG_GLOBAL_SYMBOLS: u32 = 0;
657#[doc = "@brief Requests OS_ModuleLoad() to keep the symbols local/private to this module\nWhen supplied as the \"flags\" argument to OS_ModuleLoad(), this indicates\nthat the symbols in the loaded module should NOT be added to the global\nsymbol table.  This means the symbols in the loaded library will not be\navailable for use by other modules.\nUse this option is recommended for cases where no other entities will need\nto reference symbols within this module.  This helps ensure that the module\ncan be more safely unloaded in the future, by preventing other modules from\nbinding to it.  It also helps reduce the likelihood of symbol name conflicts\namong modules.\n@note To look up symbols within a module loaded with this flag, use\nOS_SymbolLookupInModule() instead of OS_SymbolLookup().  Also note that\nreferences obtained using this method are not tracked by the OS; the\napplication must ensure that all references obtained in this manner have\nbeen cleaned up/released before unloading the module."]
658pub const OS_MODULE_FLAG_LOCAL_SYMBOLS: u32 = 1;
659#[doc = "@brief Upper limit for OSAL task priorities"]
660pub const OS_MAX_TASK_PRIORITY: u32 = 255;
661#[doc = "@brief Floating point enabled state for a task"]
662pub const OS_FP_ENABLED: u32 = 1;
663#[doc = "*  \\cfemissioncfg cFE Maximum length for pathnames within data exchange structures\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant dictates the size of pathnames within all structures\n*       used for external data exchange, such as Software bus messages and table definitions.\n*       This is typically the same as OS_MAX_PATH_LEN but that is OSAL dependent --\n*       and as such it definable on a per-processor/OS basis and hence may be different\n*       across multiple processors.  It is recommended to set this to the value of the\n*       largest OS_MAX_PATH_LEN in use on any CPU on the mission.\n*\n*       This affects only the layout of command/telemetry messages and table definitions;\n*       internal allocation may use the platform-specific OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value.\n*\n*       This length must include an extra character for NULL termination.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       All CPUs within the same SB domain (mission) and ground tools must share the\n*       same definition.\n*       Note this affects the size of messages, so it must not cause any message\n*       to exceed the max length.\n*\n*       This value should be kept as a multiple of 4, to maintain alignment of\n*       any possible neighboring fields without implicit padding."]
664pub const CFE_MISSION_MAX_PATH_LEN: u32 = 64;
665#[doc = "*  \\cfemissioncfg cFE Maximum length for filenames within data exchange structures\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant dictates the size of filenames within all structures\n*       used for external data exchange, such as Software bus messages and table definitions.\n*       This is typically the same as OS_MAX_FILE_LEN but that is OSAL dependent --\n*       and as such it definable on a per-processor/OS basis and hence may be different\n*       across multiple processors.  It is recommended to set this to the value of the\n*       largest OS_MAX_FILE_LEN in use on any CPU on the mission.\n*\n*       This affects only the layout of command/telemetry messages and table definitions;\n*       internal allocation may use the platform-specific OS_MAX_FILE_LEN value.\n*\n*       This length must include an extra character for NULL termination.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       All CPUs within the same SB domain (mission) and ground tools must share the\n*       same definition.\n*       Note this affects the size of messages, so it must not cause any message\n*       to exceed the max length.\n*\n*       This value should be kept as a multiple of 4, to maintain alignment of\n*       any possible neighboring fields without implicit padding."]
666pub const CFE_MISSION_MAX_FILE_LEN: u32 = 20;
667#[doc = "*  \\cfemissioncfg cFE Maximum length for API names within data exchange structures\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant dictates the size of filenames within all structures\n*       used for external data exchange, such as Software bus messages and table definitions.\n*       This is typically the same as OS_MAX_API_LEN but that is OSAL dependent --\n*       and as such it definable on a per-processor/OS basis and hence may be different\n*       across multiple processors.  It is recommended to set this to the value of the\n*       largest OS_MAX_API_LEN in use on any CPU on the mission.\n*\n*       This affects only the layout of command/telemetry messages and table definitions;\n*       internal allocation may use the platform-specific OS_MAX_API_LEN value.\n*\n*       This length must include an extra character for NULL termination.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       All CPUs within the same SB domain (mission) must share the same definition\n*       Note this affects the size of messages, so it must not cause any message\n*       to exceed the max length.\n*\n*       This value should be kept as a multiple of 4, to maintain alignment of\n*       any possible neighboring fields without implicit padding."]
668pub const CFE_MISSION_MAX_API_LEN: u32 = 20;
669#[doc = "*  \\cfemissioncfg cFE Maximum number of files in a message/data exchange\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant dictates the maximum number of files within all structures\n*       used for external data exchange, such as Software bus messages and table definitions.\n*       This is typically the same as OS_MAX_NUM_OPEN_FILES but that is OSAL dependent --\n*       and as such it definable on a per-processor/OS basis and hence may be different\n*       across multiple processors.  It is recommended to set this to the value of the\n*       largest OS_MAX_NUM_OPEN_FILES in use on any CPU on the mission.\n*\n*       This affects only the layout of command/telemetry messages and table definitions;\n*       internal allocation may use the platform-specific OS_MAX_NUM_OPEN_FILES value.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       All CPUs within the same SB domain (mission) must share the same definition\n*       Note this affects the size of messages, so it must not cause any message\n*       to exceed the max length.\n*"]
670pub const CFE_MISSION_MAX_NUM_FILES: u32 = 50;
671#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Mission Max Apps in a message\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      Indicates the maximum number of apps in a telemetry housekeeping message\n*\n*      This affects the layout of command/telemetry messages but does not affect run\n*      time behavior or internal allocation.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       All CPUs within the same SB domain (mission) must share the same definition\n*       Note this affects the size of messages, so it must not cause any message\n*       to exceed the max length."]
672pub const CFE_MISSION_ES_MAX_APPLICATIONS: u32 = 16;
673#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Max Number of Performance IDs for messages\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the maximum number of perf ids allowed.\n*\n*       Each performance id is used to identify something that needs to be\n*       measured. Performance ids are limited to the range of 0 to\n*       #CFE_MISSION_ES_PERF_MAX_IDS - 1. Any performance ids outside of this\n*       range will be ignored and will be flagged as an error.\n*\n*      This affects the layout of telemetry messages but does not affect run\n*      time behavior or internal allocation.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       All CPUs within the same SB domain (mission) must share the same definition\n*       Note this affects the size of messages, so it must not cause any message\n*       to exceed the max length.\n*"]
674pub const CFE_MISSION_ES_PERF_MAX_IDS: u32 = 128;
675#[doc = "\\cfeescfg Maximum number of block sizes in pool structures\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      The upper limit for the number of block sizes supported in the generic\n*      pool implementation, which in turn implements the memory pools and CDS.\n*      This definition is used as the array size with the pool stats structure,\n*      and therefore should be consistent across all CPUs in a mission, as well\n*      as with the ground station.\n*\n*      There is also a platform-specific limit which may be fewer than this\n*      value.\n*\n*  \\par Limits:\n*       Must be at least one.  No specific upper limit, but the number is\n*       anticipated to be reasonably small (i.e. tens, not hundreds).  Large\n*       values have not been tested.\n*\n*"]
676pub const CFE_MISSION_ES_POOL_MAX_BUCKETS: u32 = 17;
677#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Maximum Length of CDS Name\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      Indicates the maximum length (in characters) of the CDS name (\'CDSName\')\n*      portion of a Full CDS Name of the following form:\n*      \"ApplicationName.CDSName\"\n*\n*      This length does not need to include an extra character for NULL termination.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This value should be kept as a multiple of 4, to maintain alignment of\n*       any possible neighboring fields without implicit padding.\n*"]
678pub const CFE_MISSION_ES_CDS_MAX_NAME_LENGTH: u32 = 16;
679#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Maximum Length of Full CDS Name in messages\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      Indicates the maximum length (in characters) of the entire CDS name\n*      of the following form: \"ApplicationName.CDSName\"\n*\n*       This affects the layout of command/telemetry messages but does not affect run\n*       time behavior or internal allocation.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       All CPUs within the same SB domain (mission) must share the same definition\n*       Note this affects the size of messages, so it must not cause any message\n*       to exceed the max length.\n*\n*       This value should be kept as a multiple of 4, to maintain alignment of\n*       any possible neighboring fields without implicit padding."]
680pub const CFE_MISSION_ES_CDS_MAX_FULL_NAME_LEN: u32 = 40;
681#[doc = "*  \\cfeevscfg Maximum Event Message Length\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      Indicates the maximum length (in characters) of the formatted text\n*      string portion of an event message\n*\n*      This length does not need to include an extra character for NULL termination.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*      Not Applicable"]
682pub const CFE_MISSION_EVS_MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH: u32 = 122;
683#[doc = "*  \\cfesbcfg Maximum SB Message Size\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      The following definition dictates the maximum message size allowed on\n*      the software bus. SB checks the pkt length field in the header of all\n*      messages sent. If the pkt length field indicates the message is larger\n*      than this define, SB sends an event and rejects the send.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*      This parameter has a lower limit of 6 (CCSDS primary header size).  There\n*      are no restrictions on the upper limit however, the maximum message size is\n*      system dependent and should be verified.  Total message size values that are\n*      checked against this configuration are defined by a 16 bit data word."]
684pub const CFE_MISSION_SB_MAX_SB_MSG_SIZE: u32 = 32768;
685#[doc = "*  \\cfesbcfg Maximum Number of pipes that SB command/telemetry messages may hold\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Dictates the maximum number of unique Pipes the SB message definitions will hold.\n*\n*       This affects the layout of command/telemetry messages but does not affect run\n*       time behavior or internal allocation.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       All CPUs within the same SB domain (mission) must share the same definition\n*       Note this affects the size of messages, so it must not cause any message\n*       to exceed the max length.\n*"]
686pub const CFE_MISSION_SB_MAX_PIPES: u32 = 64;
687#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Maximum Table Name Length\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      Indicates the maximum length (in characters) of the table name\n*      (\'TblName\') portion of a Full Table Name of the following\n*      form: \"ApplicationName.TblName\"\n*\n*      This length does not need to include an extra character for NULL termination.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This value should be kept as a multiple of 4, to maintain alignment of\n*       any possible neighboring fields without implicit padding."]
688pub const CFE_MISSION_TBL_MAX_NAME_LENGTH: u32 = 16;
689#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Maximum Length of Full Table Name in messages\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      Indicates the maximum length (in characters) of the entire table name\n*      within software bus messages, in \"AppName.TableName\" notation.\n*\n*      This affects the layout of command/telemetry messages but does not affect run\n*      time behavior or internal allocation.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       All CPUs within the same SB domain (mission) must share the same definition\n*       Note this affects the size of messages, so it must not cause any message\n*       to exceed the max length.\n*\n*       This value should be kept as a multiple of 4, to maintain alignment of\n*       any possible neighboring fields without implicit padding."]
690pub const CFE_MISSION_TBL_MAX_FULL_NAME_LEN: u32 = 40;
691#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Default Time Format\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      The following definitions select either UTC or TAI as the default\n*      (mission specific) time format.  Although it is possible for an\n*      application to request time in a specific format, most callers\n*      should use CFE_TIME_GetTime(), which returns time in the default\n*      format.  This avoids having to modify each individual caller\n*      when the default choice is changed.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*      if CFE_MISSION_TIME_CFG_DEFAULT_TAI is defined as true then CFE_MISSION_TIME_CFG_DEFAULT_UTC must be\n*      defined as false.\n*      if CFE_MISSION_TIME_CFG_DEFAULT_TAI is defined as false then CFE_MISSION_TIME_CFG_DEFAULT_UTC must be\n*      defined as true."]
692pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_CFG_DEFAULT_TAI: u32 = 1;
693pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_CFG_DEFAULT_UTC: u32 = 0;
694#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Default Time Format\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      The following definition enables the use of a simulated time at\n*      the tone signal using a software bus message.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*      Not Applicable"]
695pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_CFG_FAKE_TONE: u32 = 1;
696#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Default Time and Tone Order\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      Time Services may be configured to expect the time at the tone\n*      data packet to either precede or follow the tone signal.  If the\n*      time at the tone data packet follows the tone signal, then the\n*      data within the packet describes what the time \"was\" at the tone.\n*      If the time at the tone data packet precedes the tone signal, then\n*      the data within the packet describes what the time \"will be\" at\n*      the tone.  One, and only one, of the following symbols must be set to true:\n*      - CFE_MISSION_TIME_AT_TONE_WAS\n*      - CFE_MISSION_TIME_AT_TONE_WILL_BE\n*      Note: If Time Services is defined as using a simulated tone signal\n*            (see #CFE_MISSION_TIME_CFG_FAKE_TONE above), then the tone data packet\n*            must follow the tone signal.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*      Either CFE_MISSION_TIME_AT_TONE_WAS or CFE_MISSION_TIME_AT_TONE_WILL_BE must be set to true.\n*      They may not both be true and they may not both be false."]
697pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_AT_TONE_WAS: u32 = 1;
698pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_AT_TONE_WILL_BE: u32 = 0;
699#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Min and Max Time Elapsed\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      Based on the definition of Time and Tone Order\n*      (CFE_MISSION_TIME_AT_TONE_WAS/WILL_BE) either the \"time at the tone\" signal or\n*      data packet will follow the other. This definition sets the valid window\n*      of time for the second of the pair to lag behind the first. Time\n*      Services will invalidate both the tone and packet if the second does not\n*      arrive within this window following the first.\n*\n*      For example, if the data packet follows the tone, it might be valid for\n*      the data packet to arrive between zero and 100,000 micro-seconds after\n*      the tone.  But, if the tone follows the packet, it might be valid\n*      only if the packet arrived between 200,000 and 700,000 micro-seconds\n*      before the tone.\n*\n*      Note: units are in micro-seconds\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       0 to 999,999 decimal"]
700pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_MIN_ELAPSED: u32 = 0;
701pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_MAX_ELAPSED: u32 = 200000;
702#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Default Time Values\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      Default time values are provided to avoid problems due to time\n*      calculations performed after startup but before commands can be\n*      processed.  For example, if the default time format is UTC then\n*      it is important that the sum of MET and STCF always exceed the\n*      value of Leap Seconds to prevent the UTC time calculation\n*     <tt>(time = MET + STCF - Leap Seconds) </tt> from resulting in a negative\n*     (very large) number.<BR><BR>\n*     Some past missions have also created known (albeit wrong) default\n*     timestamps.  For example, assume the epoch is defined as Jan 1, 1970\n*     and further assume the default time values are set to create a timestamp\n*     of Jan 1, 2000.  Even though the year 2000 timestamps are wrong, it\n*     may be of value to keep the time within some sort of bounds acceptable\n*     to the software.<BR><BR>\n*     Note: Sub-second units are in micro-seconds (0 to 999,999) and\n*           all values must be defined\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Not Applicable"]
703pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_DEF_MET_SECS: u32 = 1000;
704pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_DEF_MET_SUBS: u32 = 0;
705pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_DEF_STCF_SECS: u32 = 1000000;
706pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_DEF_STCF_SUBS: u32 = 0;
707pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_DEF_LEAPS: u32 = 37;
708pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_DEF_DELAY_SECS: u32 = 0;
709pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_DEF_DELAY_SUBS: u32 = 1000;
710#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Default EPOCH Values\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      Default ground time epoch values\n*      Note: these values are used only by the CFE_TIME_Print() API function\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*      Year - must be within 136 years\n*      Day - Jan 1 = 1, Feb 1 = 32, etc.\n*      Hour - 0 to 23\n*      Minute - 0 to 59\n*      Second - 0 to 59\n*      Micros - 0 to 999999"]
711pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_EPOCH_YEAR: u32 = 1980;
712pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_EPOCH_DAY: u32 = 1;
713pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_EPOCH_HOUR: u32 = 0;
714pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_EPOCH_MINUTE: u32 = 0;
715pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_EPOCH_SECOND: u32 = 0;
716pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_EPOCH_MICROS: u32 = 0;
717#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Time File System Factor\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      Define the s/c vs file system time conversion constant...\n*\n*      Note: this value is intended for use only by CFE TIME API functions to\n*      convert time values based on the ground system epoch (s/c time) to\n*      and from time values based on the file system epoch (fs time).\n*\n*      FS time  = S/C time + factor\n*      S/C time = FS time - factor\n*\n*      Worksheet:\n*\n*      S/C epoch = Jan 1, 2005  (LRO ground system epoch)\n*      FS epoch  = Jan 1, 1980  (vxWorks DOS file system epoch)\n*\n*      Delta = 25 years, 0 days, 0 hours, 0 minutes, 0 seconds\n*\n*      Leap years = 1980, 1984, 1988, 1992, 1996, 2000, 2004\n*      (divisible by 4 -- except if by 100 -- unless also by 400)\n*\n*      1 year   =  31,536,000 seconds\n*      1 day    =      86,400 seconds\n*      1 hour   =       3,600 seconds\n*      1 minute =          60 seconds\n*\n*      25 years = 788,400,000 seconds\n*      7 extra leap days = 604,800 seconds\n*\n*      total delta = 789,004,800 seconds\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*      Not Applicable"]
718pub const CFE_MISSION_TIME_FS_FACTOR: u32 = 789004800;
719#[doc = "\\brief Max length of description field in a standard cFE File Header"]
720pub const CFE_FS_HDR_DESC_MAX_LEN: u32 = 32;
721#[doc = "\\brief Magic Number for cFE compliant files (= \'cFE1\')"]
722pub const CFE_FS_FILE_CONTENT_ID: u32 = 1665549617;
723#[doc = "\\brief cFE Status converted to string length limit\nUsed for sizing CFE_StatusString_t intended for use in printing CFE_Status_t values\nSized for 0x%08x and NULL"]
724pub const CFE_STATUS_STRING_LENGTH: u32 = 11;
725#[doc = "@brief A fixed bit that will be set in all CFE resource ID values\nIn simple mode this is zero/disabled so that OSAL IDs and CFE IDs will\nhave the same underlying values.  This replicates historical behavior where\nCFE Task IDs and OSAL task IDs are same thing and are interchangeable."]
726#[doc = "@brief A fixed bit that should be set in all CFE resource ID values\nIn strict mode this is nonzero so that OSAL IDs and CFE IDs will have\ndifferent values.  This means that CFE Task IDs will not be interchangeable\nwith OSAL task IDs, either in value or type."]
727pub const CFE_RESOURCEID_MARK: u32 = 0;
728#[doc = "\\brief Indicates that the memory pool selection will not use a semaphore"]
729pub const CFE_ES_NO_MUTEX: u32 = 0;
730#[doc = "\\brief Indicates that the memory pool selection will use a semaphore"]
731pub const CFE_ES_USE_MUTEX: u32 = 1;
732#[doc = "\\brief Stops any filtering.  All messages are sent."]
733pub const CFE_EVS_NO_FILTER: u32 = 0;
734#[doc = "\\brief Sends the first event.  All remaining messages are filtered."]
735pub const CFE_EVS_FIRST_ONE_STOP: u32 = 65535;
736#[doc = "\\brief Sends the first 2 events.  All remaining messages are filtered."]
737pub const CFE_EVS_FIRST_TWO_STOP: u32 = 65534;
738#[doc = "\\brief Sends the first 4 events.  All remaining messages are filtered."]
739pub const CFE_EVS_FIRST_4_STOP: u32 = 65532;
740#[doc = "\\brief Sends the first 8 events.  All remaining messages are filtered."]
741pub const CFE_EVS_FIRST_8_STOP: u32 = 65528;
742#[doc = "\\brief Sends the first 16 events.  All remaining messages are filtered."]
743pub const CFE_EVS_FIRST_16_STOP: u32 = 65520;
744#[doc = "\\brief Sends the first 32 events.  All remaining messages are filtered."]
745pub const CFE_EVS_FIRST_32_STOP: u32 = 65504;
746#[doc = "\\brief Sends the first 64 events.  All remaining messages are filtered."]
747pub const CFE_EVS_FIRST_64_STOP: u32 = 65472;
748#[doc = "\\brief Sends every other event."]
749pub const CFE_EVS_EVERY_OTHER_ONE: u32 = 1;
750#[doc = "\\brief Sends two, filters one, sends two, filters one, etc"]
751pub const CFE_EVS_EVERY_OTHER_TWO: u32 = 2;
752#[doc = "\\brief Sends every fourth event message.  All others are filtered."]
753pub const CFE_EVS_EVERY_FOURTH_ONE: u32 = 3;
754pub const CFE_TIME_PRINTED_STRING_SIZE: u32 = 24;
755#[doc = "\\cfeescfg CFE core application startup timeout\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      The upper limit for the amount of time that the cFE core applications\n*      (ES, SB, EVS, TIME, TBL) are each allotted to reach their respective\n*      \"ready\" states.\n*\n*      The CFE \"main\" thread starts individual tasks for each of the core applications\n*      (except FS).  Each of these must perform some initialization work before the\n*      next core application can be started, so the main thread waits to ensure that the\n*      application has reached the \"ready\" state before starting the next application.\n*\n*      If any core application fails to start, then it indicates a major problem with\n*      the system and startup is aborted.\n*\n*      Units are in milliseconds\n*\n*  \\par Limits:\n*       Must be defined as an integer value that is greater than\n*       or equal to zero.\n*"]
756pub const CFE_PLATFORM_CORE_MAX_STARTUP_MSEC: u32 = 30000;
757#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define ES Task Priority\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the cFE_ES Task priority.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Not Applicable"]
758pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_START_TASK_PRIORITY: u32 = 68;
759#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Default virtual path for persistent storage\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       This configures the default location in the virtual file system\n*       for persistent/non-volatile storage.  Files such as the startup\n*       script, app/library dynamic modules, and configuration tables are\n*       expected to be stored in this directory.\n*"]
760pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_NONVOL_DISK_MOUNT_STRING: &[u8; 4] = b"/cf\0";
761#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Default virtual path for volatile storage\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      The #CFE_PLATFORM_ES_RAM_DISK_MOUNT_STRING parameter is used to set the cFE mount path\n*      for the CFE RAM disk. This is a parameter for missions that do not want to\n*      use the default value of \"/ram\", or for missions that need to have a different\n*      value for different CPUs or Spacecraft.\n*      Note that the vxWorks OSAL cannot currently handle names that have more than one\n*      path separator in it. The names \"/ram\", \"/ramdisk\", \"/disk123\" will all work, but\n*      \"/disks/ram\" will not.\n*      Multiple separators can be used with the posix or RTEMS ports.\n*"]
762pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_RAM_DISK_MOUNT_STRING: &[u8; 5] = b"/ram\0";
763#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Max Number of Applications\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the maximum number of applications that can be loaded into the\n*       system. This number does not include child tasks.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 6.  The lower limit corresponds to the cFE internal\n*       applications.  There are no restrictions on the upper limit however, the\n*       maximum number of applications is system dependent and should be verified.\n*       AppIDs that are checked against this configuration are defined by a 32 bit\n*       data word."]
764pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MAX_APPLICATIONS: u32 = 32;
765#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Max Number of Shared libraries\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the maximum number of cFE Shared libraries that can be loaded into\n*       the system.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 1.  There are no restrictions on the upper limit\n*       however, the maximum number of libraries is system dependent and should be\n*       verified."]
766pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MAX_LIBRARIES: u32 = 10;
767#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Max Number of ER (Exception and Reset) log entries\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the maximum number of ER (Exception and Reset) log entries\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 1.  There are no restrictions on the upper limit\n*       however, the maximum number of log entries is system dependent and should be\n*       verified."]
768pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_ER_LOG_ENTRIES: u32 = 20;
769#[doc = "\\cfeescfg Maximum size of CPU Context in ES Error Log\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       This should be large enough to accommodate the CPU context\n*       information supplied by the PSP on the given platform.\n*\n*  \\par Limits:\n*       Must be greater than zero and a multiple of sizeof(uint32).\n*       Limited only by the available memory and the number of entries\n*       in the error log. Any context information beyond this size will\n*       be truncated."]
770pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_ER_LOG_MAX_CONTEXT_SIZE: u32 = 256;
771#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Size of the cFE System Log.\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the size in bytes of the cFE system log. The system log holds\n*       variable length strings that are terminated by a linefeed and null\n*       character.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 512.  There are no restrictions on the upper limit\n*       however, the maximum system log size is system dependent and should be\n*       verified."]
772pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_SYSTEM_LOG_SIZE: u32 = 3072;
773#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Number of entries in the ES Object table\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the number of entries in the ES Object table. This table controls\n*       the core cFE startup.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 15.  There are no restrictions on the upper limit\n*       however, the maximum object table size is system dependent and should be\n*       verified."]
774pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_OBJECT_TABLE_SIZE: u32 = 30;
775#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Max Number of Generic Counters\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the maximum number of Generic Counters that can be registered.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This parameter has a lower limit of 1 and an upper limit of 65535."]
776pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MAX_GEN_COUNTERS: u32 = 8;
777#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define ES Application Control Scan Rate\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       ES Application Control Scan Rate. This parameter controls the speed that ES\n*       scans the Application Table looking for App Delete/Restart/Reload requests.\n*       All Applications are deleted, restarted, or reloaded by the ES Application.\n*       ES will periodically scan for control requests to process. The scan rate is\n*       controlled by this parameter, which is given in milliseconds. A value of\n*       1000 means that ES will scan the Application Table once per second. Be\n*       careful not to set the value of this too low, because ES will use more CPU\n*       cycles scanning the table.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 100 and an upper limit of 20000 on this\n*       configuration parameter. millisecond units."]
778pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_APP_SCAN_RATE: u32 = 1000;
779#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define ES Application Kill Timeout\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      ES Application Kill Timeout. This parameter controls the number of\n*      \"scan periods\" that ES will wait for an application to Exit after getting\n*      the signal Delete, Reload or Restart. The sequence works as follows:\n*      -# ES will set the control request for an App to Delete/Restart/Reload and\n*         set this kill timer to the value in this parameter.\n*      -# If the App is responding and Calls it\'s RunLoop function, it will drop out\n*         of it\'s main loop and call CFE_ES_ExitApp. Once it calls Exit App, then\n*         ES can delete, restart, or reload the app the next time it scans the app\n*         table.\n*      -# If the App is not responding, the ES App will decrement this Kill Timeout\n*         value each time it runs. If the timeout value reaches zero, ES will kill\n*         the app.\n*\n*      The Kill timeout value depends on the #CFE_PLATFORM_ES_APP_SCAN_RATE. If the Scan Rate\n*      is 1000, or 1 second, and this #CFE_PLATFORM_ES_APP_KILL_TIMEOUT is set to 5, then it\n*      will take 5 seconds to kill a non-responding App.\n*      If the Scan Rate is 250, or 1/4 second, and the #CFE_PLATFORM_ES_APP_KILL_TIMEOUT is\n*      set to 2, then it will take 1/2 second to time out.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 1 and an upper limit of 100 on this configuration\n*       parameter. Units are number of #CFE_PLATFORM_ES_APP_SCAN_RATE cycles."]
780pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_APP_KILL_TIMEOUT: u32 = 5;
781#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg ES Ram Disk Sector Size\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the ram disk sector size. The ram disk is 1 of 4 memory areas that\n*       are preserved on a processor reset.\n*       NOTE: Changing this value changes memory allocation, and may\n*       require changes to platform specific values (in CFE_PSP) such as\n*       USER_RESERVED_MEM in VxWorks depending on the memory areas\n*       being used for preserved data and on OS specific behavior.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 128.  There are no restrictions on the upper limit\n*       however, the maximum RAM disk sector size is system dependent and should be\n*       verified."]
782pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_RAM_DISK_SECTOR_SIZE: u32 = 512;
783#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg ES Ram Disk Number of Sectors\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the ram disk number of sectors. The ram disk is one of four memory\n*       areas that are preserved on a processor reset.\n*       NOTE: Changing this value changes memory allocation, and may\n*       require changes to platform specific values (in CFE_PSP) such as\n*       USER_RESERVED_MEM in VxWorks depending on the memory areas\n*       being used for preserved data and on OS specific behavior.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 128.  There are no restrictions on the upper limit\n*       however, the maximum number of RAM sectors is system dependent and should be\n*       verified."]
784pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_RAM_DISK_NUM_SECTORS: u32 = 4096;
785#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Percentage of Ram Disk Reserved for Decompressing Apps\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      The #CFE_PLATFORM_ES_RAM_DISK_PERCENT_RESERVED parameter is used to make sure that the\n*      Volatile ( RAM ) Disk has a defined amount of free space during a processor\n*      reset. The cFE uses the Volatile disk to decompress cFE applications during\n*      system startup. If this Volatile disk happens to get filled with logs and\n*      misc files, then a processor reset may not work, because there will be no\n*      room to decompress cFE apps. To solve that problem, this parameter sets the\n*      \"Low Water Mark\" for disk space on a Processor reset. It should be set to\n*      allow the largest cFE Application to be decompressed.\n*      During a Processor reset, if there is not sufficient space left on the disk,\n*      it will be re-formatted in order to clear up some space.\n*\n*      This feature can be turned OFF by setting the parameter to 0.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 0 and an upper limit of 75 on this configuration\n*       parameter.Units are percentage. A setting of zero will turn this feature\n*       off."]
786pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_RAM_DISK_PERCENT_RESERVED: u32 = 30;
787#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Critical Data Store Size\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the Critical Data Store (CDS) area size in bytes size. The CDS is\n*       one of four memory areas that are preserved during a processor reset.\n*       NOTE: Changing this value changes memory allocation, and may\n*       require changes to platform specific values (in CFE_PSP) such as\n*       USER_RESERVED_MEM in VxWorks depending on the memory areas\n*       being used for preserved data and on OS specific behavior.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 8192 and an upper limit of UINT_MAX (4 Gigabytes)\n*       on this configuration parameter."]
788pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_SIZE: u32 = 131072;
789#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define User Reserved Memory Size\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       User Reserved Memory Size. This is the size in bytes of the cFE User\n*       reserved Memory area. This is a block of memory that is available for cFE\n*       application use. The address is obtained by calling\n*       #CFE_PSP_GetUserReservedArea. The User Reserved Memory is one of four memory\n*       areas that are preserved during a processor reset.\n*       NOTE: Changing this value changes memory allocation, and may\n*       require changes to platform specific values (in CFE_PSP) such as\n*       USER_RESERVED_MEM in VxWorks depending on the memory areas\n*       being used for preserved data and on OS specific behavior.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 1024 and an upper limit of UINT_MAX (4 Gigabytes)\n*       on this configuration parameter."]
790pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_USER_RESERVED_SIZE: u32 = 1048576;
791#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Memory Pool Alignment Size\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Ensures that buffers obtained from a memory pool are aligned\n*       to a certain minimum block size.  Note the allocator will always\n*       align to the minimum required by the CPU architecture.  This may\n*       be set greater than the CPU requirement as desired for optimal\n*       performance.\n*\n*       For some architectures/applications it may be beneficial to set this\n*       to the cache line size of the target CPU, or to use special SIMD\n*       instructions that require a more stringent memory alignment.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This must always be a power of 2, as it is used as a binary address mask."]
