792pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEMPOOL_ALIGN_SIZE_MIN: u32 = 4;
793#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg ES Nonvolatile Startup Filename\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant defines the path and name of the file that\n*       contains a list of modules that will be loaded and started by the cFE after\n*       the cFE finishes its startup sequence.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The length of each string, including the NULL terminator cannot exceed the\n*       #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value."]
794pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_NONVOL_STARTUP_FILE: &[u8; 23] = b"/cf/cfe_es_startup.scr\0";
795#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg ES Volatile Startup Filename\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant defines the path and name of the file that\n*       contains a list of modules that will be loaded and started by the cFE after\n*       the cFE finishes its startup sequence.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The length of each string, including the NULL terminator cannot exceed the\n*       #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value."]
796pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_VOLATILE_STARTUP_FILE: &[u8; 24] = b"/ram/cfe_es_startup.scr\0";
797#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Default Application Information Filename\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant defines the filename used to store information\n*       pertaining to all of the Applications that are registered with Executive\n*       Services. This filename is used only when no filename is specified in the\n*       the command to query all system apps.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The length of each string, including the NULL terminator cannot exceed the\n*       #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value."]
798pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_DEFAULT_APP_LOG_FILE: &[u8; 25] = b"/ram/cfe_es_app_info.log\0";
799#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Default Application Information Filename\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant defines the filename used to store information\n*       pertaining to all of the Applications that are registered with Executive\n*       Services. This filename is used only when no filename is specified in the\n*       the command to query all system tasks.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The length of each string, including the NULL terminator cannot exceed the\n*       #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value."]
800pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_DEFAULT_TASK_LOG_FILE: &[u8; 25] = b"/ram/cfe_es_taskinfo.log\0";
801#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Default System Log Filename\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant defines the filename used to store important\n*       information (as ASCII text strings) that might not be able to be sent in an\n*       Event Message. This filename is used only when no filename is specified in\n*       the command to dump the system log. No file specified in the cmd means the\n*       first character in the cmd filename is a NULL terminator (zero).\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The length of each string, including the NULL terminator cannot exceed the\n*       #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value."]
802pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_DEFAULT_SYSLOG_FILE: &[u8; 23] = b"/ram/cfe_es_syslog.log\0";
803#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Default Exception and Reset (ER) Log Filename\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant defines the filename used to store the\n*       Exception and Reset (ER) Log. This filename is used only when no filename is\n*       specified in the command to dump the ER log. No file specified in the cmd\n*       means the first character in the cmd filename is a NULL terminator (zero).\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The length of each string, including the NULL terminator cannot exceed the\n*       #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value."]
804pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_DEFAULT_ER_LOG_FILE: &[u8; 19] = b"/ram/cfe_erlog.log\0";
805#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Default Performance Data Filename\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant defines the filename used to store the\n*       Performance Data. This filename is used only when no filename is specified\n*       in the command to stop performance data collecting.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The length of each string, including the NULL terminator cannot exceed the\n*       #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value."]
806pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_DEFAULT_PERF_DUMP_FILENAME: &[u8; 21] = b"/ram/cfe_es_perf.dat\0";
807#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Default Critical Data Store Registry Filename\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant defines the filename used to store the\n*       Critical Data Store Registry. This filename is used only when no filename is\n*       specified in the command to stop performance data collecting.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The length of each string, including the NULL terminator cannot exceed the\n*       #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value."]
808pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_DEFAULT_CDS_REG_DUMP_FILE: &[u8; 21] = b"/ram/cfe_cds_reg.log\0";
809#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Default System Log Mode following Power On Reset\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the default mode for the operation of the ES System log following a power\n*       on reset. The log may operate in either Overwrite mode = 0, where once the\n*       log becomes full the oldest message in the log will be overwritten, or\n*       Discard mode = 1, where once the log becomes full the contents of the log are\n*       preserved and the new event is discarded.  This constant may hold a value of\n*       either 0 or 1 depending on the desired default.\n*       Overwrite Mode = 0, Discard Mode = 1.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 0 and an upper limit of 1 on this configuration\n*       parameter."]
810pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_DEFAULT_POR_SYSLOG_MODE: u32 = 0;
811#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Default System Log Mode following Processor Reset\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the default mode for the operation of the ES System log following a\n*       processor reset. The log may operate in either Overwrite mode = 0, where once\n*       the log becomes full the oldest message in the log will be overwritten, or\n*       Discard mode = 1, where once the log becomes full the contents of the log are\n*       preserved and the new event is discarded.  This constant may hold a value of\n*       either 0 or 1 depending on the desired default.\n*       Overwrite Mode = 0, Discard Mode = 1.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 0 and an upper limit of 1 on this configuration\n*       parameter."]
812pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_DEFAULT_PR_SYSLOG_MODE: u32 = 1;
813#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Max Size of Performance Data Buffer\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the maximum size of the performance data buffer. Units are number of\n*       performance data entries. An entry is defined by a 32 bit data word followed\n*       by a 64 bit time stamp.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 1025.  There are no restrictions on the upper limit\n*       however, the maximum buffer size is system dependent and should be verified.\n*       The units are number of entries. An entry is defined by a 32 bit data word followed\n*       by a 64 bit time stamp."]
814pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_PERF_DATA_BUFFER_SIZE: u32 = 10000;
815#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Filter Mask Setting for Disabling All Performance Entries\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the filter mask for disabling all performance entries. The value is a\n*       bit mask.  For each bit, 0 means the corresponding entry is disabled and\n*       1 means it is enabled."]
816pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_PERF_FILTMASK_NONE: u32 = 0;
817#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Filter Mask Setting for Enabling All Performance Entries\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the filter mask for enabling all performance entries. The value is a\n*       bit mask.  For each bit, 0 means the corresponding entry is disabled and\n*       1 means it is enabled."]
818pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_PERF_FILTMASK_ALL: i32 = -1;
819#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Default Filter Mask Setting for Performance Data Buffer\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the default filter mask for the performance data buffer. The value is a\n*       bit mask.  For each bit, 0 means the corresponding entry is disabled and 1\n*       means it is enabled.\n*"]
820pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_PERF_FILTMASK_INIT: i32 = -1;
821#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Default Filter Trigger Setting for Disabling All Performance Entries\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the default trigger mask for disabling all performance data entries. The value\n*       is a bit mask.  For each bit, 0 means the trigger for the corresponding entry is\n*       disabled and 1 means it is enabled.\n*"]
822pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_PERF_TRIGMASK_NONE: u32 = 0;
823#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Filter Trigger Setting for Enabling All Performance Entries\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the trigger mask for enabling all performance data entries. The value is\n*       a bit mask.  For each bit, 0 means the trigger for the corresponding entry is\n*       disabled and 1 means it is enabled.\n*"]
824pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_PERF_TRIGMASK_ALL: i32 = -1;
825#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Default Filter Trigger Setting for Performance Data Buffer\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the default trigger mask for the performance data buffer. The value is a\n*       32-bit mask.  For each bit, 0 means the trigger for the corresponding entry is\n*       disabled and 1 means it is enabled.\n*"]
826pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_PERF_TRIGMASK_INIT: u32 = 0;
827#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Performance Analyzer Child Task Priority\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       This parameter defines the priority of the child task spawned by the\n*       Executive Services to write performance data to a file.  Lower numbers\n*       are higher priority, with 1 being the highest priority in the case of a\n*       child task.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Valid range for a child task is 1 to 255 however, the priority cannot\n*       be higher (lower number) than the ES parent application priority."]
828pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_PERF_CHILD_PRIORITY: u32 = 200;
829#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Performance Analyzer Child Task Stack Size\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       This parameter defines the stack size of the child task spawned by the\n*       Executive Services to write performance data to a file.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       It is recommended this parameter be greater than or equal to 4KB. This parameter\n*       is limited by the maximum value allowed by the data type. In this case, the data\n*       type is an unsigned 32-bit integer, so the valid range is 0 to 0xFFFFFFFF."]
830pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_PERF_CHILD_STACK_SIZE: u32 = 4096;
831#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Performance Analyzer Child Task Delay\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       This parameter defines the delay time (in milliseconds) between performance\n*       data file writes performed by the Executive Services Performance Analyzer\n*       Child Task.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       It is recommended this parameter be greater than or equal to 20ms. This parameter\n*       is limited by the maximum value allowed by the data type. In this case, the data\n*       type is an unsigned 32-bit integer, so the valid range is 0 to 0xFFFFFFFF."]
832pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_PERF_CHILD_MS_DELAY: u32 = 20;
833#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Performance Analyzer Child Task Number of Entries Between Delay\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       This parameter defines the number of performance analyzer entries the Performance\n*       Analyzer Child Task will write to the file between delays.\n*"]
834pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_PERF_ENTRIES_BTWN_DLYS: u32 = 50;
835#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Default Stack Size for an Application\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       This parameter defines a default stack size. This parameter is used by the\n*       cFE Core Applications.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 2048.  There are no restrictions on the upper limit\n*       however, the maximum stack size is system dependent and should be verified.\n*       Most operating systems provide tools for measuring the amount of stack used by a\n*       task during operation. It is always a good idea to verify that no more than 1/2\n*       of the stack is used."]
836pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_DEFAULT_STACK_SIZE: u32 = 8192;
837#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Maximum Number of Registered CDS Blocks\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Maximum number of registered CDS Blocks\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 8.  There are no restrictions on the upper limit\n*       however, the maximum number of CDS entries is system dependent and\n*       should be verified."]
838pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MAX_NUM_ENTRIES: u32 = 512;
839#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Number of Processor Resets Before a Power On Reset\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Number of Processor Resets before a Power On Reset is called. If set to 2,\n*       then 2 processor resets will occur, and the 3rd processor reset will be a\n*       power on reset instead.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 0.  There are no restrictions on the upper limit\n*       however, the maximum number of processor resets may be system dependent and\n*       should be verified."]
840pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MAX_PROCESSOR_RESETS: u32 = 2;
841#[doc = "\\cfeescfg Maximum number of block sizes in pool structures\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      The upper limit for the number of block sizes supported in the generic\n*      pool implementation, which in turn implements the memory pools and CDS.\n*\n*  \\par Limits:\n*       Must be at least one.  No specific upper limit, but the number is\n*       anticipated to be reasonably small (i.e. tens, not hundreds).  Large\n*       values have not been tested.\n*\n*       The ES and CDS block size lists must correlate with this value"]
842pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_POOL_MAX_BUCKETS: u32 = 17;
843#[doc = "\\cfeescfg Maximum number of memory pools\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      The upper limit for the number of memory pools that can concurrently\n*      exist within the system.\n*\n*      The CFE_SB and CFE_TBL core subsystems each define a memory pool.\n*\n*      Individual applications may also create memory pools, so this value\n*      should be set sufficiently high enough to support the applications\n*      being used on this platform.\n*\n*  \\par Limits:\n*       Must be at least 2 to support CFE core - SB and TBL pools.  No\n*       specific upper limit."]
844pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MAX_MEMORY_POOLS: u32 = 10;
845#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define Default ES Memory Pool Block Sizes\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Default Intermediate ES Memory Pool Block Sizes.  If an application\n*       is using the CFE_ES Memory Pool APIs (#CFE_ES_PoolCreate, #CFE_ES_PoolCreateNoSem,\n*       #CFE_ES_GetPoolBuf and #CFE_ES_PutPoolBuf) but finds these sizes\n*       inappropriate for their use, they may wish to use the #CFE_ES_PoolCreateEx\n*       API to specify their own intermediate block sizes\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       These sizes MUST be increasing and MUST be an integral multiple of 4.  Also,\n*       CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MAX_BLOCK_SIZE must be larger than CFE_MISSION_SB_MAX_SB_MSG_SIZE and both\n*       CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_SNGL_TABLE_SIZE and CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_DBL_TABLE_SIZE.  Note that if Table\n*       Services have been removed from the CFE, the table size limits are still\n*       enforced although the table size definitions may be reduced."]
846pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_01: u32 = 8;
847pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_02: u32 = 16;
848pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_03: u32 = 32;
849pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_04: u32 = 48;
850pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_05: u32 = 64;
851pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_06: u32 = 96;
852pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_07: u32 = 128;
853pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_08: u32 = 160;
854pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_09: u32 = 256;
855pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_10: u32 = 512;
856pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_11: u32 = 1024;
857pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_12: u32 = 2048;
858pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_13: u32 = 4096;
859pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_14: u32 = 8192;
860pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_15: u32 = 16384;
861pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_16: u32 = 32768;
862pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_MAX_BLOCK_SIZE: u32 = 80000;
863#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define ES Critical Data Store Memory Pool Block Sizes\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Intermediate ES Critical Data Store Memory Pool Block Sizes\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       These sizes MUST be increasing and MUST be an integral multiple of 4."]
864pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_01: u32 = 8;
865pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_02: u32 = 16;
866pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_03: u32 = 32;
867pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_04: u32 = 48;
868pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_05: u32 = 64;
869pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_06: u32 = 96;
870pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_07: u32 = 128;
871pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_08: u32 = 160;
872pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_09: u32 = 256;
873pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_10: u32 = 512;
874pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_11: u32 = 1024;
875pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_12: u32 = 2048;
876pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_13: u32 = 4096;
877pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_14: u32 = 8192;
878pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_15: u32 = 16384;
879pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_16: u32 = 32768;
880pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MAX_BLOCK_SIZE: u32 = 80000;
881#[doc = "\\cfeescfg Poll timer for startup sync delay\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      During startup, some tasks may need to synchronize their own initialization\n*      with the initialization of other applications in the system.\n*\n*      CFE ES implements an API to accomplish this, that performs a task delay (sleep)\n*      while polling the overall system state until other tasks are ready.\n*\n*      This value controls the amount of time that the CFE_ES_ApplicationSyncDelay\n*      will sleep between each check of the system state.  This should be large enough\n*      to allow other tasks to run, but not so large as to noticeably delay the startup\n*      completion.\n*\n*      Units are in milliseconds\n*\n*  \\par Limits:\n*       Must be defined as an integer value that is greater than\n*       or equal to zero."]
882pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_STARTUP_SYNC_POLL_MSEC: u32 = 50;
883#[doc = "\\cfeescfg Startup script timeout\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*      The upper limit for the total amount of time that all apps listed in the CFE ES startup\n*      script may take to all become ready.\n*\n*      Unlike the \"core\" app timeout, this is a soft limit; if the allotted time is exceeded,\n*      it probably indicates an issue with one of the apps, but does not cause CFE ES to take\n*      any additional action other than logging the event to the syslog.\n*\n*      Units are in milliseconds\n*\n*  \\par Limits:\n*       Must be defined as an integer value that is greater than\n*       or equal to zero."]
884pub const CFE_PLATFORM_ES_STARTUP_SCRIPT_TIMEOUT_MSEC: u32 = 1000;
885#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define EVS Task Priority\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the cFE_EVS Task priority.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Not Applicable"]
886pub const CFE_PLATFORM_EVS_START_TASK_PRIORITY: u32 = 61;
887#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define EVS Task Stack Size\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the cFE_EVS Task Stack Size\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 2048 on this configuration parameter.  There\n*       are no restrictions on the upper limit however, the maximum stack size\n*       is system dependent and should be verified.  Most operating systems provide\n*       tools for measuring the amount of stack used by a task during operation. It\n*       is always a good idea to verify that no more than 1/2 of the stack is used."]
888pub const CFE_PLATFORM_EVS_START_TASK_STACK_SIZE: u32 = 8192;
889#[doc = "*  \\cfeevscfg Define Maximum Number of Event Filters per Application\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Maximum number of events that may be filtered per application.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There are no restrictions on the lower and upper limits however,\n*       the maximum number of event filters is system dependent and should be\n*       verified."]
890pub const CFE_PLATFORM_EVS_MAX_EVENT_FILTERS: u32 = 8;
891#[doc = "*  \\cfeevscfg Maximum number of event before squelching\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Maximum number of events that may be emitted per app per second.\n*       Setting this to 0 will cause events to be unrestricted.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This number must be less than or equal to INT_MAX/1000"]
892pub const CFE_PLATFORM_EVS_MAX_APP_EVENT_BURST: u32 = 32;
893#[doc = "*  \\cfeevscfg Sustained number of event messages per second per app before squelching\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Sustained number of events that may be emitted per app per second.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This number must be less than or equal to #CFE_PLATFORM_EVS_MAX_APP_EVENT_BURST.\n*       Values lower than 8 may cause functional and unit test failures."]
894pub const CFE_PLATFORM_EVS_APP_EVENTS_PER_SEC: u32 = 15;
895#[doc = "*  \\cfeevscfg Default Event Log Filename\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant defines the filename used to store the Event\n*       Services local event log. This filename is used only when no filename is\n*       specified in the command to dump the event log.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The length of each string, including the NULL terminator cannot exceed the\n*       #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value."]
896pub const CFE_PLATFORM_EVS_DEFAULT_LOG_FILE: &[u8; 17] = b"/ram/cfe_evs.log\0";
897#[doc = "*  \\cfeevscfg Maximum Number of Events in EVS Local Event Log\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Dictates the EVS local event log capacity. Units are the number of events.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There are no restrictions on the lower and upper limits however,\n*       the maximum log size is system dependent and should be verified."]
898pub const CFE_PLATFORM_EVS_LOG_MAX: u32 = 20;
899#[doc = "*  \\cfeevscfg Default EVS Application Data Filename\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant defines the filename used to store the EVS\n*       Application Data(event counts/filtering information). This filename is\n*       used only when no filename is specified in the command to dump the event\n*       log.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The length of each string, including the NULL terminator cannot exceed the\n*       #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value."]
900pub const CFE_PLATFORM_EVS_DEFAULT_APP_DATA_FILE: &[u8; 21] = b"/ram/cfe_evs_app.dat\0";
901#[doc = "*  \\cfeevscfg Default EVS Output Port State\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the default port state (enabled or disabled) for the four output\n*       ports defined within the Event Service. Port 1 is usually the uart output\n*       terminal. To enable a port, set the proper bit to a 1. Bit 0 is port 1,\n*       bit 1 is port2 etc.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The valid settings are 0x0 to 0xF."]
902pub const CFE_PLATFORM_EVS_PORT_DEFAULT: u32 = 1;
903#[doc = "*  \\cfeevscfg Default EVS Event Type Filter Mask\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines a state of on or off for all four event types. The term event\n*       \'type\' refers to the criticality level and may be Debug, Informational,\n*       Error or Critical. Each event type has a bit position. (bit 0 = Debug,\n*       bit 1 = Info, bit 2 = Error, bit 3 = Critical). This is a global setting,\n*       meaning it applies to all applications. To filter an event type, set its\n*       bit to zero. For example,\n*       0xE means Debug = OFF, Info = ON, Error = ON, Critical = ON\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The valid settings are 0x0 to 0xF."]
904pub const CFE_PLATFORM_EVS_DEFAULT_TYPE_FLAG: u32 = 14;
905#[doc = "*  \\cfeevscfg Default EVS Local Event Log Mode\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines a state of overwrite(0) or discard(1) for the operation of the\n*       EVS local event log. The log may operate in either Overwrite mode = 0,\n*       where once the log becomes full the oldest event in the log will be\n*       overwritten, or Discard mode = 1, where once the log becomes full the\n*       contents of the log are preserved and the new event is discarded.\n*       Overwrite Mode = 0, Discard Mode = 1.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The valid settings are 0 or 1"]
906pub const CFE_PLATFORM_EVS_DEFAULT_LOG_MODE: u32 = 1;
907#[doc = "*  \\cfesbcfg Maximum Number of Unique Message IDs SB Routing Table can hold\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Dictates the maximum number of unique MsgIds the SB routing table will hold.\n*       This constant has a direct effect on the size of SB\'s tables and arrays.\n*       Keeping this count as low as possible will save memory.\n*       To see the run-time, high-water mark and the current utilization figures\n*       regarding this parameter, send an SB command to \'Send Statistics Pkt\'.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This must be a power of two if software bus message routing hash implementation\n*       is being used.  Lower than 64 will cause unit test failures, and\n*       telemetry reporting is impacted below 32.  There is no hard\n*       upper limit, but impacts memory footprint.  For software bus message routing\n*       search implementation the number of msg ids subscribed to impacts performance.\n*"]
908pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MAX_MSG_IDS: u32 = 256;
909#[doc = "*  \\cfesbcfg Maximum Number of Unique Pipes SB Routing Table can hold\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Dictates the maximum number of unique Pipes the SB routing table will hold.\n*       This constant has a direct effect on the size of SB\'s tables and arrays.\n*       Keeping this count as low as possible will save memory.\n*       To see the run-time, high-water mark and the current utilization figures\n*       regarding this parameter, send an SB command to \'Send Statistics Pkt\'.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This parameter has a lower limit of 1.  This parameter must also be less than\n*       or equal to OS_MAX_QUEUES.\n*"]
910pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MAX_PIPES: u32 = 64;
911#[doc = "*  \\cfesbcfg Maximum Number of unique local destinations a single MsgId can have\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Dictates the maximum number of unique local destinations a single MsgId can\n*       have.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This parameter has a lower limit of 1.  There are no restrictions on the upper\n*       limit however, the maximum number of destinations per packet is system dependent\n*       and should be verified.  Destination number values that are checked against this\n*       configuration are defined by a 16 bit data word.\n*"]
912pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MAX_DEST_PER_PKT: u32 = 16;
913#[doc = "*  \\cfesbcfg Default Subscription Message Limit\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Dictates the default Message Limit when using the #CFE_SB_Subscribe API. This will\n*       limit the number of messages with a specific message ID that can be received through\n*       a subscription. This only changes the default; other message limits can be set on a per\n*       subscription basis using #CFE_SB_SubscribeEx .\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This parameter has a lower limit of 4 and an upper limit of 65535.\n*"]
914pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_DEFAULT_MSG_LIMIT: u32 = 4;
915#[doc = "*  \\cfesbcfg Size of the SB buffer memory pool\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Dictates the size of the SB memory pool. For each message the SB\n*       sends, the SB dynamically allocates from this memory pool, the memory needed\n*       to process the message. The memory needed to process each message is msg\n*       size + msg descriptor(CFE_SB_BufferD_t). This memory pool is also used\n*       to allocate destination descriptors (CFE_SB_DestinationD_t) during the\n*       subscription process.\n*       To see the run-time, high-water mark and the current utilization figures\n*       regarding this parameter, send an SB command to \'Send Statistics Pkt\'.\n*       Some memory statistics have been added to the SB housekeeping packet.\n*       NOTE: It is important to monitor these statistics to ensure the desired\n*       memory margin is met.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This parameter has a lower limit of 512 and an upper limit of UINT_MAX (4 Gigabytes).\n*"]
916pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_BUF_MEMORY_BYTES: u32 = 524288;
917#[doc = "*  \\cfesbcfg Highest Valid Message Id\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant dictates the range of valid message ID\'s, from 0\n*       to CFE_PLATFORM_SB_HIGHEST_VALID_MSGID (inclusive).\n*\n*       Although this can be defined differently across platforms, each platform can\n*       only publish/subscribe to message ids within their allowable range. Typically\n*       this value is set the same across all mission platforms to avoid this complexity.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This parameter has a lower limit is 1, and an upper limit of 0xFFFFFFFE.\n*\n*       When using the direct message map implementation for software bus routing, this\n*       value is used to size the map where a value of 0x1FFF results in a 16 KBytes map\n*       and 0xFFFF is 128 KBytes.\n*\n*       When using the hash implementation for software bus routing, a multiple of the\n*       CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MAX_MSG_IDS is used to size the message map.  In that case\n*       the range selected here does not impact message map memory use, so it\'s\n*       reasonable to use up to the full range supported by the message ID implementation."]
918pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_HIGHEST_VALID_MSGID: u32 = 8191;
919#[doc = "*  \\cfesbcfg Default Routing Information Filename\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant defines the filename used to store the software\n*       bus routing information.  This filename is used only when no filename is\n*       specified in the command.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The length of each string, including the NULL terminator cannot exceed the\n*       #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value."]
920pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_DEFAULT_ROUTING_FILENAME: &[u8; 22] = b"/ram/cfe_sb_route.dat\0";
921#[doc = "*  \\cfesbcfg Default Pipe Information Filename\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant defines the filename used to store the software\n*       bus pipe information. This filename is used only when no filename is\n*       specified in the command.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The length of each string, including the NULL terminator cannot exceed the\n*       #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value."]
922pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_DEFAULT_PIPE_FILENAME: &[u8; 21] = b"/ram/cfe_sb_pipe.dat\0";
923#[doc = "*  \\cfesbcfg Default Message Map Filename\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       The value of this constant defines the filename used to store the software\n*       bus message map information. This filename is used only when no filename is\n*       specified in the command. The message map is a lookup table (array of 16bit\n*       words) that has an element for each possible MsgId value and holds the\n*       routing table index for that MsgId. The Msg Map provides fast access to the\n*       destinations of a message.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The length of each string, including the NULL terminator cannot exceed the\n*       #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value."]
924pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_DEFAULT_MAP_FILENAME: &[u8; 23] = b"/ram/cfe_sb_msgmap.dat\0";
925pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_FILTER_MASK1: u32 = 65532;
926pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_FILTER_MASK2: u32 = 65532;
927pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_FILTER_MASK3: u32 = 65520;
928pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_FILTER_MASK4: u32 = 65520;
929pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_FILTERED_EVENT5: u32 = 0;
930pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_FILTER_MASK5: u32 = 0;
931pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_FILTERED_EVENT6: u32 = 0;
932pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_FILTER_MASK6: u32 = 0;
933pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_FILTERED_EVENT7: u32 = 0;
934pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_FILTER_MASK7: u32 = 0;
935pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_FILTERED_EVENT8: u32 = 0;
936pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_FILTER_MASK8: u32 = 0;
937#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define SB Memory Pool Block Sizes\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Software Bus Memory Pool Block Sizes\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       These sizes MUST be increasing and MUST be an integral multiple of 4.\n*       The number of block sizes defined cannot exceed\n*       #CFE_PLATFORM_ES_POOL_MAX_BUCKETS"]
938pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_01: u32 = 8;
939pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_02: u32 = 16;
940pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_03: u32 = 20;
941pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_04: u32 = 36;
942pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_05: u32 = 64;
943pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_06: u32 = 96;
944pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_07: u32 = 128;
945pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_08: u32 = 160;
946pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_09: u32 = 256;
947pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_10: u32 = 512;
948pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_11: u32 = 1024;
949pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_12: u32 = 2048;
950pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_13: u32 = 4096;
951pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_14: u32 = 8192;
952pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_15: u32 = 16384;
953pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MEM_BLOCK_SIZE_16: u32 = 32768;
954pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_MAX_BLOCK_SIZE: u32 = 32896;
955#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define SB Task Priority\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the cFE_SB Task priority.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Not Applicable"]
956pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_START_TASK_PRIORITY: u32 = 64;
957#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define SB Task Stack Size\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the cFE_SB Task Stack Size\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 2048 on this configuration parameter.  There\n*       are no restrictions on the upper limit however, the maximum stack size\n*       is system dependent and should be verified.  Most operating systems provide\n*       tools for measuring the amount of stack used by a task during operation. It\n*       is always a good idea to verify that no more than 1/2 of the stack is used."]
958pub const CFE_PLATFORM_SB_START_TASK_STACK_SIZE: u32 = 8192;
959#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define TBL Task Priority\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the cFE_TBL Task priority.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Not Applicable"]
960pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_START_TASK_PRIORITY: u32 = 70;
961#[doc = "*  \\cfeescfg Define TBL Task Stack Size\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the cFE_TBL Task Stack Size\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 2048 on this configuration parameter.  There\n*       are no restrictions on the upper limit however, the maximum stack size\n*       is system dependent and should be verified.  Most operating systems provide\n*       tools for measuring the amount of stack used by a task during operation. It\n*       is always a good idea to verify that no more than 1/2 of the stack is used."]
962pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_START_TASK_STACK_SIZE: u32 = 8192;
963#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Size of Table Services Table Memory Pool\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the TOTAL size of the memory pool that cFE Table Services allocates\n*       from the system.  The size must be large enough to provide memory for each\n*       registered table, the inactive buffers for double buffered tables and for\n*       the shared inactive buffers for single buffered tables.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The cFE does not place a limit on the size of this parameter."]
964pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_BUF_MEMORY_BYTES: u32 = 524288;
965#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Maximum Size Allowed for a Double Buffered Table\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the maximum allowed size (in bytes) of a double buffered table.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The cFE does not place a limit on the size of this parameter but it must be\n*       less than half of #CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_BUF_MEMORY_BYTES."]
966pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_DBL_TABLE_SIZE: u32 = 16384;
967#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Maximum Size Allowed for a Single Buffered Table\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the maximum allowed size (in bytes) of a single buffered table.\n*       \\b NOTE: This size determines the size of all shared table buffers.\n*       Therefore, this size will be multiplied by #CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_SIMULTANEOUS_LOADS\n*       below when allocating memory for shared tables.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The cFE does not place a limit on the size of this parameter but it must be\n*       small enough to allow for #CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_SIMULTANEOUS_LOADS number of tables\n*       to fit into #CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_BUF_MEMORY_BYTES."]
968pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_SNGL_TABLE_SIZE: u32 = 16384;
969#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Maximum Number of Tables Allowed to be Registered\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the maximum number of tables supported by this processor\'s Table Services.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This number must be less than 32767.  It should be recognized that this parameter\n*       determines the size of the Table Registry.  An excessively high number will waste\n*       memory."]
970pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_NUM_TABLES: u32 = 128;
971#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Maximum Number of Critical Tables that can be Registered\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the maximum number of critical tables supported by this processor\'s Table Services.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This number must be less than 32767.  It should be recognized that this parameter\n*       determines the size of the Critical Table Registry which is maintained in the Critical\n*       Data Store.  An excessively high number will waste Critical Data Store memory.  Therefore,\n*       this number must not exceed the value defined in CFE_PLATFORM_ES_CDS_MAX_NUM_ENTRIES."]
972pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_CRITICAL_TABLES: u32 = 32;
973#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Maximum Number of Table Handles\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the maximum number of Table Handles.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This number must be less than 32767.  This number must be at least as big as\n*       the number of tables (#CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_NUM_TABLES) and should be set higher if tables\n*       are shared between applications."]
974pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_NUM_HANDLES: u32 = 256;
975#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Maximum Number of Simultaneous Loads to Support\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the maximum number of single buffered tables that can be\n*       loaded simultaneously.  This number is used to determine the number\n*       of shared buffers to allocate.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This number must be less than 32767.  An excessively high number will\n*       degrade system performance and waste memory.  A number less than 5 is\n*       suggested but not required."]
976pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_SIMULTANEOUS_LOADS: u32 = 4;
977#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Maximum Number of Simultaneous Table Validations\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the maximum number of pending validations that\n*       the Table Services can handle at any one time.  When a\n*       table has a validation function, a validation request is\n*       made of the application to perform that validation.  This\n*       number determines how many of those requests can be\n*       outstanding at any one time.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This number must be less than 32767.  An excessively high number will\n*       degrade system performance and waste memory.  A number less than 20 is\n*       suggested but not required."]
978pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_MAX_NUM_VALIDATIONS: u32 = 10;
979#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Default Filename for a Table Registry Dump\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the file name used to store the table registry when\n*       no filename is specified in the dump registry command.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       The length of each string, including the NULL terminator cannot exceed the\n*       #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN value."]
980pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_DEFAULT_REG_DUMP_FILE: &[u8; 21] = b"/ram/cfe_tbl_reg.log\0";
981#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Number of Spacecraft ID\'s specified for validation\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the number of specified spacecraft ID values that\n*       are verified during table loads.  If the number is zero\n*       then no validation of the spacecraft ID field in the table\n*       file header is performed when tables are loaded.  Non-zero\n*       values indicate how many values from the list of spacecraft\n*       ID\'s defined below are compared to the spacecraft ID field\n*       in the table file header.  The ELF2CFETBL tool may be used\n*       to create table files with specified spacecraft ID values.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This number must be greater than or equal to zero and\n*       less than or equal to 2."]
982pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_VALID_SCID_COUNT: u32 = 0;
983#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Spacecraft ID values used for table load validation\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the spacecraft ID values used for validating the\n*       spacecraft ID field in the table file header.  To be valid,\n*       the spacecraft ID specified in the table file header must\n*       match one of the values defined here.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This value can be any 32 bit unsigned integer."]
984pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_VALID_SCID_1: u32 = 66;
985#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Number of Processor ID\'s specified for validation\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the number of specified processor ID values that\n*       are verified during table loads.  If the number is zero\n*       then no validation of the processor ID field in the table\n*       file header is performed when tables are loaded.  Non-zero\n*       values indicate how many values from the list of processor\n*       ID\'s defined below are compared to the processor ID field\n*       in the table file header.  The ELF2CFETBL tool may be used\n*       to create table files with specified processor ID values.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This number must be greater than or equal to zero and\n*       less than or equal to 4."]
986pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_VALID_PRID_COUNT: u32 = 0;
987#[doc = "*  \\cfetblcfg Processor ID values used for table load validation\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the processor ID values used for validating the\n*       processor ID field in the table file header.  To be valid,\n*       the spacecraft ID specified in the table file header must\n*       match one of the values defined here.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       This value can be any 32 bit unsigned integer."]
988pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_VALID_PRID_1: u32 = 1;
989pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_VALID_PRID_3: u32 = 0;
990pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TBL_VALID_PRID_4: u32 = 0;
991#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Time Server or Time Client Selection\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       This configuration parameter selects whether the Time task functions as a\n*       time \"server\" or \"client\".  A time server generates the \"time at the tone\"\n*       packet which is received by time clients.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Enable one, and only one by defining either CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SERVER or\n*       CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_CLIENT AS true.  The other must be defined as false."]
992pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SERVER: u32 = 1;
993pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_CLIENT: u32 = 0;
994#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Local MET or Virtual MET Selection for Time Servers\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Depending on the specific hardware system configuration, it may be possible\n*       for Time Servers to read the \"local\" MET from a h/w register rather than\n*       having to track the MET as the count of tone signal interrupts (virtual MET)\n*\n*       Time Clients must be defined as using a virtual MET.  Also, a Time Server\n*       cannot be defined as having both a h/w MET and an external time source (they\n*       both cannot synchronize to the same tone).\n*\n*       Note: \"disable\" this define (set to false) only for Time Servers with local hardware\n*       that supports a h/w MET that is synchronized to the tone signal !!!\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Only applies if #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SERVER is set to true."]
995pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_VIRTUAL: u32 = 1;
996#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Include or Exclude the Primary/Redundant Tone Selection Cmd\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Depending on the specific hardware system configuration, it may be possible\n*       to switch between a primary and redundant tone signal.  If supported by\n*       hardware, this definition will enable command interfaces to select the\n*       active tone signal. Both Time Clients and Time Servers support this feature.\n*       Note: Set the CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SIGNAL define to true to enable tone signal commands.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Not Applicable"]
997pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SIGNAL: u32 = 0;
998#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Include or Exclude the Internal/External Time Source Selection Cmd\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       By default, Time Servers maintain time using an internal MET which may be a\n*       h/w register or software counter, depending on available hardware. The\n*       following definition enables command interfaces to switch between an\n*       internal MET, or external time data received from one of several supported\n*       external time sources. Only a Time Server may be configured to use external\n*       time data.\n*       Note: Set the CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SOURCE define to true to include the Time Source\n*             Selection Command (command allows selection between the internal\n*             or external time source). Then choose the external source with the\n*             CFE_TIME_CFG_SRC_??? define.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Only applies if #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SERVER is set to true."]
999pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SOURCE: u32 = 0;
1000#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Choose the External Time Source for Server only\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       If #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SOURCE is set to true, then one of the following external time\n*       source types must also be set to true.  Do not set any of the external time\n*       source types to true unless #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SOURCE is set to true.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       -# If #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SOURCE is set to true then one and only one of the following\n*       three external time sources can and must be set true:\n*       #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SRC_MET, #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SRC_GPS, #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SRC_TIME\n*       -# Only applies if #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SERVER is set to true."]
1001pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SRC_MET: u32 = 0;
1002pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SRC_GPS: u32 = 0;
1003pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SRC_TIME: u32 = 0;
1004#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Define the Max Delta Limits for Time Servers using an Ext Time Source\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       If #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SOURCE is set to true and one of the external time sources is\n*       also set to true, then the delta time limits for range checking is used.\n*\n*       When a new time value is received from an external source, the value is\n*       compared against the \"expected\" time value. If the delta exceeds the\n*       following defined amount, then the new time data will be ignored. This range\n*       checking is only performed after the clock state has been commanded to\n*       \"valid\". Until then, external time data is accepted unconditionally.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Applies only if both #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SERVER and #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SOURCE are set\n*       to true."]
1005pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_MAX_DELTA_SECS: u32 = 0;
1006pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_MAX_DELTA_SUBS: u32 = 500000;
1007#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Define the Local Clock Rollover Value in seconds and subseconds\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Specifies the capability of the local clock.  Indicates the time at which\n*       the local clock rolls over.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Not Applicable"]
1008pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_MAX_LOCAL_SECS: u32 = 27;
1009pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_MAX_LOCAL_SUBS: u32 = 0;
1010#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Define Timing Limits From One Tone To The Next\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines limits to the timing of the 1Hz tone signal. A tone signal is valid\n*       only if it arrives within one second (plus or minus the tone limit) from\n*       the previous tone signal.Units are microseconds as measured with the local\n*       clock.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Not Applicable"]
1011pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_TONE_LIMIT: u32 = 20000;
1012#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Define Time to Start Flywheel Since Last Tone\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Define time to enter flywheel mode (in seconds since last tone data update)\n*       Units are microseconds as measured with the local clock.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Not Applicable"]
1013pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_START_FLY: u32 = 2;
1014#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Define Periodic Time to Update Local Clock Tone Latch\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Define Periodic Time to Update Local Clock Tone Latch. Applies only when\n*       in flywheel mode. This define dictates the period at which the simulated\n*       \'last tone\' time is updated. Units are seconds.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       Not Applicable"]
1015pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_LATCH_FLY: u32 = 8;
1016#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Define TIME Task Priorities\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the cFE_TIME Task priority.\n*       Defines the cFE_TIME Tone Task priority.\n*       Defines the cFE_TIME 1HZ Task priority.\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of zero and an upper limit of 255 on these\n*       configuration parameters.  Remember that the meaning of each task\n*       priority is inverted -- a \"lower\" number has a \"higher\" priority."]
1017pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_START_TASK_PRIORITY: u32 = 60;
1018pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_TONE_TASK_PRIORITY: u32 = 25;
1019pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_ONEHZ_TASK_PRIORITY: u32 = 25;
1020#[doc = "*  \\cfetimecfg Define TIME Task Stack Sizes\n*\n*  \\par Description:\n*       Defines the cFE_TIME Main Task Stack Size\n*       Defines the cFE_TIME Tone Task Stack Size\n*       Defines the cFE_TIME 1HZ Task Stack Size\n*\n*  \\par Limits\n*       There is a lower limit of 2048 on these configuration parameters.  There\n*       are no restrictions on the upper limit however, the maximum stack size\n*       is system dependent and should be verified.  Most operating systems provide\n*       tools for measuring the amount of stack used by a task during operation. It\n*       is always a good idea to verify that no more than 1/2 of the stack is used."]
1021pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_START_TASK_STACK_SIZE: u32 = 8192;
1022pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_TONE_TASK_STACK_SIZE: u32 = 4096;
1023pub const CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_ONEHZ_TASK_STACK_SIZE: u32 = 8192;
1024#[doc = "\\brief Configuration parameter used by SBN App"]
1025pub const CFE_SB_SUB_ENTRIES_PER_PKT: u32 = 20;
1026#[doc = "\\brief Option used with #CFE_SB_ReceiveBuffer to request immediate pipe status"]
1027pub const CFE_SB_POLL: u32 = 0;
1028#[doc = "\\brief Option used with #CFE_SB_ReceiveBuffer to force a wait for next message"]
1029pub const CFE_SB_PEND_FOREVER: i32 = -1;
1030#[doc = "\\brief Subtype specifier used in #CFE_SB_SingleSubscriptionTlm_t by SBN App"]
1031pub const CFE_SB_SUBSCRIPTION: u32 = 0;
1032#[doc = "\\brief Subtype specified used in #CFE_SB_SingleSubscriptionTlm_t by SBN App"]
1033pub const CFE_SB_UNSUBSCRIPTION: u32 = 1;
1034#[doc = "@defgroup CFESBPipeOptions cFE SB Pipe options\n@{"]
1035pub const CFE_SB_PIPEOPTS_IGNOREMINE: u32 = 1;
1036#[doc = "\\brief Table buffer mask"]
1037pub const CFE_TBL_OPT_BUFFER_MSK: u32 = 1;
1038#[doc = "\\brief Single buffer table"]
1039pub const CFE_TBL_OPT_SNGL_BUFFER: u32 = 0;
1040#[doc = "\\brief Double buffer table"]
1041pub const CFE_TBL_OPT_DBL_BUFFER: u32 = 1;
1042#[doc = "\\brief Table load/dump mask"]
1043pub const CFE_TBL_OPT_LD_DMP_MSK: u32 = 2;
1044#[doc = "\\brief Load/Dump table"]
1045pub const CFE_TBL_OPT_LOAD_DUMP: u32 = 0;
1046#[doc = "\\brief Dump only table"]
1047pub const CFE_TBL_OPT_DUMP_ONLY: u32 = 2;
1048#[doc = "\\brief Table user defined mask"]
1049pub const CFE_TBL_OPT_USR_DEF_MSK: u32 = 4;
1050#[doc = "\\brief Not user defined table"]
1051pub const CFE_TBL_OPT_NOT_USR_DEF: u32 = 0;
1052pub const CFE_TBL_OPT_USR_DEF_ADDR: u32 = 6;
1053#[doc = "\\brief Table critical mask"]
1054pub const CFE_TBL_OPT_CRITICAL_MSK: u32 = 8;
1055#[doc = "\\brief Not critical table"]
1056pub const CFE_TBL_OPT_NOT_CRITICAL: u32 = 0;
1057#[doc = "\\brief Critical table"]
1058pub const CFE_TBL_OPT_CRITICAL: u32 = 8;
1059#[doc = "@brief Default table options"]
1060pub const CFE_TBL_OPT_DEFAULT: u32 = 0;
1061#[doc = "\\brief Table maximum full name length\nThe full length of table names is defined at the mission scope.\nThis is defined here to support applications that depend on cfe_tbl.h\nproviding this value."]
1062pub const CFE_TBL_MAX_FULL_NAME_LEN: u32 = 40;
1063#[doc = "\\brief PSP Status converted to string length limit\nUsed for sizing CFE_PSP_StatusString_t intended for use in printing CFE_PSP_Status_t values\nSized for %ld (LONG_MIN) including NULL"]
1064pub const CFE_PSP_STATUS_STRING_LENGTH: u32 = 12;
1065pub const CFE_PSP_MEM_RAM: u32 = 1;
1066pub const CFE_PSP_MEM_EEPROM: u32 = 2;
1067pub const CFE_PSP_MEM_ANY: u32 = 3;
1068pub const CFE_PSP_MEM_INVALID: u32 = 4;
1069pub const CFE_PSP_MEM_ATTR_WRITE: u32 = 1;
1070pub const CFE_PSP_MEM_ATTR_READ: u32 = 2;
1071pub const CFE_PSP_MEM_ATTR_READWRITE: u32 = 3;
1072pub const CFE_PSP_MEM_SIZE_BYTE: u32 = 1;
1073pub const CFE_PSP_MEM_SIZE_WORD: u32 = 2;
1074pub const CFE_PSP_MEM_SIZE_DWORD: u32 = 4;
1075#[doc = "@brief The name of the software/RTOS timebase for general system timers.\nThis name may be referred to by CFE TIME and/or SCH when setting up its own timers."]
1076pub const CFE_PSP_SOFT_TIMEBASE_NAME: &[u8; 11] = b"cFS-Master\0";
1077pub const CFE_PSP_PANIC_STARTUP: u32 = 1;
1078pub const CFE_PSP_PANIC_VOLATILE_DISK: u32 = 2;
1079pub const CFE_PSP_PANIC_MEMORY_ALLOC: u32 = 3;
1080pub const CFE_PSP_PANIC_NONVOL_DISK: u32 = 4;
1081pub const CFE_PSP_PANIC_STARTUP_SEM: u32 = 5;
1082pub const CFE_PSP_PANIC_CORE_APP: u32 = 6;
1083pub const CFE_PSP_PANIC_GENERAL_FAILURE: u32 = 7;
1084#[doc = "Volatile disk, CDS and User Reserved memory may be valid"]
1085pub const CFE_PSP_RST_TYPE_PROCESSOR: u32 = 1;
1086#[doc = "All memory has been cleared"]
1087pub const CFE_PSP_RST_TYPE_POWERON: u32 = 2;
1088#[doc = "Placeholder to indicate 1+ the maximum value that the PSP will ever use."]
1089pub const CFE_PSP_RST_TYPE_MAX: u32 = 3;
1090#[doc = "@brief Reset caused by power having been removed and restored"]
1091pub const CFE_PSP_RST_SUBTYPE_POWER_CYCLE: u32 = 1;
1092#[doc = "@brief Reset caused by reset button on the board"]
1093pub const CFE_PSP_RST_SUBTYPE_PUSH_BUTTON: u32 = 2;
1094#[doc = "@brief Reset was caused by a reset line having been stimulated by a hardware special command"]
1095pub const CFE_PSP_RST_SUBTYPE_HW_SPECIAL_COMMAND: u32 = 3;
1096#[doc = "@brief Reset was caused by a watchdog timer expiring"]
1097pub const CFE_PSP_RST_SUBTYPE_HW_WATCHDOG: u32 = 4;
1098#[doc = "@brief Reset was caused by cFE ES processing a @link #CFE_ES_RESTART_CC Reset Command @endlink"]
1099pub const CFE_PSP_RST_SUBTYPE_RESET_COMMAND: u32 = 5;
1100#[doc = "@brief Reset was caused by a Processor Exception"]
1101pub const CFE_PSP_RST_SUBTYPE_EXCEPTION: u32 = 6;
1102#[doc = "@brief Reset was caused in an unknown manner"]
1103pub const CFE_PSP_RST_SUBTYPE_UNDEFINED_RESET: u32 = 7;
1104#[doc = "@brief Reset was caused by a JTAG or BDM connection"]
1105pub const CFE_PSP_RST_SUBTYPE_HWDEBUG_RESET: u32 = 8;
1106#[doc = "@brief Reset reverted to a cFE POWERON due to a boot bank switch"]
1107pub const CFE_PSP_RST_SUBTYPE_BANKSWITCH_RESET: u32 = 9;
1108#[doc = "@brief Placeholder to indicate 1+ the maximum value that the PSP will ever use."]
1109pub const CFE_PSP_RST_SUBTYPE_MAX: u32 = 10;
1110pub type int16 = i16;
1111pub type int32 = i32;
1112pub type int64 = i64;
1113pub type uint8 = u8;
1114pub type uint16 = u16;
1115pub type uint32 = u32;
1116pub type cpuaddr = usize;
1117#[doc = " A type to be used for OSAL resource identifiers.\n This typedef is backward compatible with the IDs from older versions of OSAL"]
1118pub type osal_id_t = uint32;
1119#[doc = " A type used to represent a number of blocks or buffers\n\n This is used with file system and queue implementations."]
1120pub type osal_blockcount_t = usize;
1121#[doc = " A type used to represent an index into a table structure\n\n This is used when referring directly to a table index as\n opposed to an object ID.  It is primarily intended for\n internal use, but is also output from public APIs such as\n OS_ObjectIdToArrayIndex()."]
1122pub type osal_index_t = uint32;
1123#[doc = " A type used to represent the runtime type or category of an OSAL object"]
1124pub type osal_objtype_t = uint32;
1125#[doc = " The preferred type to represent OSAL status codes defined in osapi-error.h"]
1126pub type osal_status_t = int32;
1127#[doc = " @brief General purpose OSAL callback function\n\n This may be used by multiple APIS"]
1128pub type OS_ArgCallback_t =
1129    ::core::option::Option<unsafe extern "C" fn(object_id: osal_id_t, arg: *mut libc::c_void)>;
1130unsafe extern "C" {
1131    #[doc = " Gets the OSAL version/baseline ID as a string\n\n This returns the content of the #OS_VERSION macro defined above, and is specifically\n just the baseline and development build ID (if applicable), without any extra info.\n\n \\returns Basic version identifier.  This is a fixed value string and is never NULL."]
1132    pub fn OS_GetVersionString() -> *const libc::c_char;
1133}
1134unsafe extern "C" {
1135    #[doc = " Gets the OSAL version code name\n\n All NASA CFE/CFS components (including CFE framework, OSAL and PSP) that work\n together will share the same code name.\n\n \\returns OSAL code name.  This is a fixed value string and is never NULL."]
1136    pub fn OS_GetVersionCodeName() -> *const libc::c_char;
1137}
1138unsafe extern "C" {
1139    #[doc = " \\brief Obtain the OSAL numeric version number\n\n This retrieves the numeric OSAL version identifier as an array of 4 uint8 values.\n\n The array of numeric values is in order of precedence:\n  [0] = Major Number\n  [1] = Minor Number\n  [2] = Revision Number\n  [3] = Mission Revision\n\n The \"Mission Revision\" (last output) also indicates whether this is an\n official release, a patched release, or a development version.\n  0 indicates an official release\n  1-254 local patch level (reserved for mission use)\n  255 indicates a development build\n\n \\param[out] VersionNumbers  A fixed-size array to be filled with the version numbers"]
1140    pub fn OS_GetVersionNumber(VersionNumbers: *mut uint8);
1141}
1142unsafe extern "C" {
1143    #[doc = " \\brief Obtain the OSAL library numeric build number\n\n The build number is a monotonically increasing number that (coarsely)\n reflects the number of commits/changes that have been merged since the\n epoch release.  During development cycles this number should increase\n after each subsequent merge/modification.\n\n Like other version information, this is a fixed number assigned at compile time.\n\n \\returns The OSAL library build number"]
1144    pub fn OS_GetBuildNumber() -> uint32;
1145}
1146#[doc = " @brief OSAL binary semaphore properties"]
1147#[repr(C)]
1148#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
1149pub struct OS_bin_sem_prop_t {
1150    pub name: [libc::c_char; 20usize],
1151    pub creator: osal_id_t,
1152    pub value: int32,
1153}
1154unsafe extern "C" {
1155    #[doc = " @brief Creates a binary semaphore\n\n Creates a binary semaphore with initial value specified by\n sem_initial_value and name specified by sem_name. sem_id will be\n returned to the caller\n\n @param[out]  sem_id will be set to the non-zero ID of the newly-created resource @nonnull\n @param[in]   sem_name the name of the new resource to create @nonnull\n @param[in]   sem_initial_value the initial value of the binary semaphore\n @param[in]   options Reserved for future use, should be passed as 0.\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if sen name or sem_id are NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NO_FREE_IDS if all of the semaphore ids are taken\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TAKEN if this is already the name of a binary semaphore\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if the OS call failed @covtest"]
1156    pub fn OS_BinSemCreate(
1157        sem_id: *mut osal_id_t,
1158        sem_name: *const libc::c_char,
1159        sem_initial_value: uint32,
1160        options: uint32,
1161    ) -> int32;
1162}
1163unsafe extern "C" {
1164    #[doc = " @brief Unblock all tasks pending on the specified semaphore\n\n The function unblocks all tasks pending on the specified semaphore. However,\n this function does not change the state of the semaphore.\n\n @param[in] sem_id The object ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a binary semaphore\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if an unspecified failure occurs @covtest"]
1165    pub fn OS_BinSemFlush(sem_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1166}
1167unsafe extern "C" {
1168    #[doc = " @brief Increment the semaphore value\n\n The function  unlocks the semaphore referenced by sem_id by performing\n a semaphore unlock operation on that semaphore.  If the semaphore value\n resulting from this operation is positive, then no threads were blocked\n waiting for the semaphore to become unlocked; the semaphore value is\n simply incremented for this semaphore.\n\n @param[in] sem_id The object ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a binary semaphore\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if an unspecified failure occurs @covtest"]
1169    pub fn OS_BinSemGive(sem_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1170}
1171unsafe extern "C" {
1172    #[doc = " @brief Decrement the semaphore value\n\n The locks the semaphore referenced by sem_id by performing a\n semaphore lock operation on that semaphore.  If the semaphore value\n is currently zero, then the calling thread shall not return from\n the call until it either locks the semaphore or the call is\n interrupted.\n\n @param[in] sem_id The object ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID the Id passed in is not a valid binary semaphore\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if an unspecified failure occurs @covtest"]
1173    pub fn OS_BinSemTake(sem_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1174}
1175unsafe extern "C" {
1176    #[doc = " @brief Decrement the semaphore value with a timeout\n\n The function locks the semaphore referenced by sem_id. However,\n if the semaphore cannot be locked without waiting for another process\n or thread to unlock the semaphore, this wait shall be terminated when\n the specified timeout, msecs, expires.\n\n @param[in] sem_id The object ID to operate on\n @param[in] msecs  The maximum amount of time to block, in milliseconds\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_SEM_TIMEOUT if semaphore was not relinquished in time\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the ID passed in is not a valid semaphore ID\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if an unspecified failure occurs @covtest"]
1177    pub fn OS_BinSemTimedWait(sem_id: osal_id_t, msecs: uint32) -> int32;
1178}
1179unsafe extern "C" {
1180    #[doc = " @brief Deletes the specified Binary Semaphore\n\n This is the function used to delete a binary semaphore in the operating system.\n This also frees the respective sem_id to be used again when another semaphore is created.\n\n @param[in] sem_id The object ID to delete\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid binary semaphore\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if an unspecified failure occurs @covtest"]
1181    pub fn OS_BinSemDelete(sem_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1182}
1183unsafe extern "C" {
1184    #[doc = " @brief Find an existing semaphore ID by name\n\n This function tries to find a binary sem Id given the name of a bin_sem\n The id is returned through sem_id\n\n @param[out] sem_id will be set to the ID of the existing resource\n @param[in]  sem_name the name of the existing resource to find @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER is semid or sem_name are NULL pointers\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND if the name was not found in the table"]
1185    pub fn OS_BinSemGetIdByName(sem_id: *mut osal_id_t, sem_name: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1186}
1187unsafe extern "C" {
1188    #[doc = " @brief Fill a property object buffer with details regarding the resource\n\n This function will pass back a pointer to structure that contains\n all of the relevant info( name and creator) about the specified binary\n semaphore.\n\n @param[in] sem_id The object ID to operate on\n @param[out]  bin_prop The property object buffer to fill @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid semaphore\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the bin_prop pointer is null\n @retval #OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED @copybrief OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED"]
1189    pub fn OS_BinSemGetInfo(sem_id: osal_id_t, bin_prop: *mut OS_bin_sem_prop_t) -> int32;
1190}
1191#[doc = " @brief OSAL time interval structure\n\n This is used to represent a basic time interval.\n\n When used with OS_GetLocalTime/OS_SetLocalTime, this represents the\n interval from the OS's epoch point, typically 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 UTC\n on systems that have a persistent real time clock (RTC), or the system\n boot time if there is no RTC available.\n\n Applications should not directly access fields within this structure,\n as the definition may change in future versions of OSAL.  Instead,\n applications should use the accessor/conversion methods defined below."]
1192#[repr(C)]
1193#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
1194pub struct OS_time_t {
1195    #[doc = "< Ticks elapsed since reference point"]
1196    pub ticks: int64,
1197}
1198pub const OS_TIME_TICK_RESOLUTION_NS: _bindgen_ty_1 = 100;
1199pub const OS_TIME_TICKS_PER_SECOND: _bindgen_ty_1 = 10000000;
1200pub const OS_TIME_TICKS_PER_MSEC: _bindgen_ty_1 = 10000;
1201pub const OS_TIME_TICKS_PER_USEC: _bindgen_ty_1 = 10;
1202#[doc = " @brief Multipliers/divisors to convert ticks into standardized units\n\n Various fixed conversion factor constants used by the conversion routines\n\n A 100ns tick time allows max intervals of about +/- 14000 years in\n a 64-bit signed integer value.\n\n @note Applications should not directly use these values, but rather use\n conversion routines below to obtain standardized units (seconds/microseconds/etc)."]
1203pub type _bindgen_ty_1 = libc::c_uint;
1204unsafe extern "C" {
1205    #[doc = " @brief Get the local time\n\n This function gets the local time from the underlying OS.\n\n @note Mission time management typically uses the cFE Time Service\n\n @param[out]  time_struct An OS_time_t that will be set to the current time @nonnull\n\n @return Get local time status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if time_struct is null"]
1206    pub fn OS_GetLocalTime(time_struct: *mut OS_time_t) -> int32;
1207}
1208unsafe extern "C" {
1209    #[doc = " @brief Set the local time\n\n This function sets the local time on the underlying OS.\n\n @note Mission time management typically uses the cFE Time Services\n\n @param[in]  time_struct An OS_time_t containing the current time @nonnull\n\n @return Set local time status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if time_struct is null"]
1210    pub fn OS_SetLocalTime(time_struct: *const OS_time_t) -> int32;
1211}
1212unsafe extern "C" {
1213    #[doc = " @brief Gets an absolute time value relative to the current time\n\n This function adds the given interval, expressed in milliseconds, to the\n current clock and returns the result.\n\n @note This is intended to ease transitioning from a relative timeout value to\n and absolute timeout value.  The result can be passed to any function\n that accepts an absolute timeout, to mimic the behavior of a relative timeout.\n\n @param[in]  relative_msec A relative time interval, in milliseconds\n\n @returns Absolute time value after adding interval"]
1214    pub fn OS_TimeFromRelativeMilliseconds(relative_msec: int32) -> OS_time_t;
1215}
1216unsafe extern "C" {
1217    #[doc = " @brief Gets a relative time value from an absolute time\n\n This function computes the number of milliseconds until the given\n absolute time value is reached in the system clock.\n\n @note This is intended to ease transitioning from a relative timeout value to\n and absolute timeout value.  The result can be passed to any function\n that accepts a relative timeout, to mimic the behavior of an absolute timeout.\n\n The return value of this function is intended to be compatible with the relative\n timeout parameter of various OSAL APIs e.g. OS_TimedRead() / OS_TimedWrite()\n\n @param[in]  time An absolute time value\n\n @returns Milliseconds until time value will be reached\n @retval OS_CHECK (0) if time is the current time or is in the past\n @retval OS_PEND (-1) if time is far in the future (not expressable as an int32)"]
1218    pub fn OS_TimeToRelativeMilliseconds(time: OS_time_t) -> int32;
1219}
1220#[doc = "< no-op/reserved event id value"]
1221pub const OS_Event_t_OS_EVENT_RESERVED: OS_Event_t = 0;
1222#[doc = " resource/id has been newly allocated but not yet created.\n\n This event is invoked from WITHIN the locked region, in\n the context of the task which is allocating the resource.\n\n If the handler returns non-success, the error will be returned\n to the caller and the creation process is aborted."]
1223pub const OS_Event_t_OS_EVENT_RESOURCE_ALLOCATED: OS_Event_t = 1;
1224#[doc = " resource/id has been fully created/finalized.\n\n Invoked outside locked region, in the context\n of the task which created the resource.\n\n Data object is not used, passed as NULL.\n\n Return value is ignored - this is for information purposes only."]
1225pub const OS_Event_t_OS_EVENT_RESOURCE_CREATED: OS_Event_t = 2;
1226#[doc = " resource/id has been deleted.\n\n Invoked outside locked region, in the context\n of the task which deleted the resource.\n\n Data object is not used, passed as NULL.\n\n Return value is ignored - this is for information purposes only."]
1227pub const OS_Event_t_OS_EVENT_RESOURCE_DELETED: OS_Event_t = 3;
1228#[doc = " New task is starting.\n\n Invoked outside locked region, in the context\n of the task which is currently starting, before\n the entry point is called.\n\n Data object is not used, passed as NULL.\n\n If the handler returns non-success, task startup is aborted\n and the entry point is not called."]
1229pub const OS_Event_t_OS_EVENT_TASK_STARTUP: OS_Event_t = 4;
1230#[doc = "< placeholder for end of enum, not used"]
1231pub const OS_Event_t_OS_EVENT_MAX: OS_Event_t = 5;
1232#[doc = " @brief A set of events that can be used with BSP event callback routines"]
1233pub type OS_Event_t = libc::c_uint;
1234#[doc = " @brief A callback routine for event handling.\n\n @param[in]    event      The event that occurred\n @param[in]    object_id  The associated object_id, or 0 if not associated with an object\n @param[inout] data       An abstract data/context object associated with the event, or NULL.\n @return status Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes."]
1235pub type OS_EventHandler_t = ::core::option::Option<
1236    unsafe extern "C" fn(event: OS_Event_t, object_id: osal_id_t, data: *mut libc::c_void) -> int32,
1237>;
1238unsafe extern "C" {
1239    #[doc = " @brief Application startup\n\n Startup abstraction such that the same BSP can be used for operations and testing."]
1240    pub fn OS_Application_Startup();
1241}
1242unsafe extern "C" {
1243    #[doc = " @brief Application run\n\n Run abstraction such that the same BSP can be used for operations and testing."]
1244    pub fn OS_Application_Run();
1245}
1246unsafe extern "C" {
1247    #[doc = " @brief Initialization of API\n\n This function returns initializes the internal data structures of the OS\n Abstraction Layer. It must be called in the application startup code before\n calling any other OS routines.\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes. Any error code (negative)\n         means the OSAL can not be initialized.  Typical platform specific response\n         is to abort since additional OSAL calls will have undefined behavior.\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERROR   @copybrief OS_ERROR @covtest"]
1248    pub fn OS_API_Init() -> int32;
1249}
1250unsafe extern "C" {
1251    #[doc = " @brief Teardown/de-initialization of OSAL API\n\n This is the inverse of OS_API_Init().  It will release all OS resources and\n return the system to a state similar to what it was prior to invoking\n OS_API_Init() initially.\n\n Normally for embedded applications, the OSAL is initialized after boot and will remain\n initialized in memory until the processor is rebooted.  However for testing and\n development purposes, it is potentially useful to reset back to initial conditions.\n\n For testing purposes, this API is designed/intended to be compatible with the\n UtTest_AddTeardown() routine provided by the UT-Assert subsystem.\n\n @note This is a \"best-effort\" routine and it may not always be possible/guaranteed\n to recover all resources, particularly in the case of off-nominal conditions, or if\n a resource is used outside of OSAL.\n\n For example, while this will attempt to unload all dynamically-loaded modules, doing\n so may not be possible and/or may induce undefined behavior if resources are in use by\n tasks/functions outside of OSAL."]
1252    pub fn OS_API_Teardown();
1253}
1254unsafe extern "C" {
1255    #[doc = " @brief Background thread implementation - waits forever for events to occur.\n\n This should be called from the BSP main routine or initial thread after all other\n board and application initialization has taken place and all other tasks are running.\n\n Typically just waits forever until \"OS_shutdown\" flag becomes true."]
1256    pub fn OS_IdleLoop();
1257}
1258unsafe extern "C" {
1259    #[doc = " @brief delete all resources created in OSAL.\n\n provides a means to clean up all resources allocated by this\n instance of OSAL.  It would typically be used during an orderly\n shutdown but may also be helpful for testing purposes."]
1260    pub fn OS_DeleteAllObjects();
1261}
1262unsafe extern "C" {
1263    #[doc = " @brief Initiate orderly shutdown\n\n Indicates that the OSAL application should perform an orderly shutdown\n of ALL tasks, clean up all resources, and exit the application.\n\n This allows the task currently blocked in OS_IdleLoop() to wake up, and\n for that function to return to its caller.\n\n This is preferred over e.g. OS_ApplicationExit() which exits immediately and\n does not provide for any means to clean up first.\n\n @param[in] flag set to true to initiate shutdown, false to cancel"]
1264    pub fn OS_ApplicationShutdown(flag: uint8);
1265}
1266unsafe extern "C" {
1267    #[doc = " @brief Exit/Abort the application\n\n Indicates that the OSAL application should exit and return control to the OS\n This is intended for e.g. scripted unit testing where the test needs to end\n without user intervention.\n\n This function does not return.  Production code typically should not ever call this.\n\n @note This exits the entire process including tasks that have been created."]
1268    pub fn OS_ApplicationExit(Status: int32);
1269}
1270unsafe extern "C" {
1271    #[doc = " @brief Callback routine registration\n\n This hook enables the application code to perform extra platform-specific\n operations on various system events such as resource creation/deletion.\n\n @note Some events are invoked while the resource is \"locked\" and therefore\n application-defined handlers for these events should not block or attempt\n to access other OSAL resources.\n\n @param[in] handler The application-provided event handler @nonnull\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes.\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if handler is NULL"]
1272    pub fn OS_RegisterEventHandler(handler: OS_EventHandler_t) -> int32;
1273}
1274unsafe extern "C" {
1275    #[doc = " @brief get string length\n\n Provides an OSAL routine to get the functionality\n of the (non-C99) \"strnlen()\" function, via the\n C89/C99 standard \"memchr()\" function instead.\n\n @param[in]    s       The input string\n @param[in]    maxlen  Maximum length to check\n @retval Length of the string or maxlen, whichever is smaller.\n"]
1276    pub fn OS_strnlen(s: *const libc::c_char, maxlen: usize) -> usize;
1277}
1278#[doc = " @brief OSAL condition variable properties"]
1279#[repr(C)]
1280#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
1281pub struct OS_condvar_prop_t {
1282    pub name: [libc::c_char; 20usize],
1283    pub creator: osal_id_t,
1284}
1285unsafe extern "C" {
1286    #[doc = " @brief Creates a condition variable resource\n\n A condition variable adds a more sophisticated synchronization option for mutexes,\n such that it can operate on arbitrary user-defined conditions rather than simply a\n counter or boolean (as in the case of simple semaphores).\n\n Creating a condition variable resource in OSAL will in turn create both a basic mutex\n as well as a synchronization overlay.
