The underlying mutex is similar to the mutex\n functionality provided by the OSAL mutex subsystem, and can be locked and unlocked\n normally.\n\n This mutex is intended to protect access to any arbitrary user-defined data object that\n serves as the condition being tested.\n\n A task that needs a particular state of the object should follow this general flow:\n  - Lock the underlying mutex\n  - Test for the condition being waited for (a user-defined check on user-defined data)\n  - If condition IS NOT met, then call OS_CondVarWait() to wait, then repeat test\n  - If condition IS met, then unlock the underlying mutex and continue\n\n A task that changes the state of the object should follow this general flow:\n  - Lock the underlying mutex\n  - Change the state as necessary\n  - Call either OS_CondVarSignal() or OS_CondVarBroadcast()\n  - Unlock the underlying mutex\n\n @param[out]  var_id will be set to the non-zero ID of the newly-created resource @nonnull\n @param[in]   var_name the name of the new resource to create @nonnull\n @param[in]   options reserved for future use.  Should be passed as 0.\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if var_id or var_name are NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NO_FREE_IDS if there are no more free condition variable Ids\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TAKEN if there is already a condition variable with the same name"]
1287    pub fn OS_CondVarCreate(
1288        var_id: *mut osal_id_t,
1289        var_name: *const libc::c_char,
1290        options: uint32,
1291    ) -> int32;
1292}
1293unsafe extern "C" {
1294    #[doc = " @brief Locks/Acquires the underlying mutex associated with a condition variable\n\n The mutex should always be locked by a task before reading or modifying the\n data object associated with a condition variable.\n\n @note This lock must be acquired by a task before invoking OS_CondVarWait() or\n OS_CondVarTimedWait() on the same condition variable.\n\n @param[in] var_id The object ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid condition variable"]
1295    pub fn OS_CondVarLock(var_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1296}
1297unsafe extern "C" {
1298    #[doc = " @brief Unlocks/Releases the underlying mutex associated with a condition variable\n\n The mutex should be unlocked by a task once reading or modifying the\n data object associated with a condition variable is complete.\n\n @param[in] var_id The object ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid condition variable"]
1299    pub fn OS_CondVarUnlock(var_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1300}
1301unsafe extern "C" {
1302    #[doc = " @brief Signals the condition variable resource referenced by var_id.\n\n This function may be used to indicate when the state of a data object has been\n changed.\n\n If there are threads blocked on the condition variable object referenced by\n var_id when this function is called, one of those threads will be unblocked,\n as determined by the scheduling policy.\n\n @param[in] var_id The object ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid condition variable"]
1303    pub fn OS_CondVarSignal(var_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1304}
1305unsafe extern "C" {
1306    #[doc = " @brief Broadcasts the condition variable resource referenced by var_id.\n\n This function may be used to indicate when the state of a data object has been\n changed.\n\n If there are threads blocked on the condition variable object referenced by\n var_id when this function is called, all threads will be unblocked.\n\n Note that although all threads are unblocked, because the mutex is re-acquired\n before the wait function returns, only a single task will be testing the condition\n at a given time.  The order with which each blocked task runs is determined by the\n scheduling policy.\n\n @param[in] var_id The object ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid condition variable"]
1307    pub fn OS_CondVarBroadcast(var_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1308}
1309unsafe extern "C" {
1310    #[doc = " @brief Waits on the condition variable object referenced by var_id.\n\n The calling task will be blocked until another task calls the function\n OS_CondVarSignal() or OS_CondVarBroadcast() on the same condition variable.\n\n The underlying mutex associated with the condition variable must be locked and\n owned by the calling task at the time this function is invoked.  As part of this\n call, the mutex will be unlocked as the task blocks.  This is done in such a way\n that there is no possibility that another task could aquire the mutex before the\n calling task has actually blocked.\n\n This atomicity with respect to blocking the task and unlocking the mutex is a\n critical difference between condition variables and other synchronization\n primitives.  It avoids a window of opportunity where inherent in the simpler\n synchronization resource types where the state of the data could change between\n the time that the calling task tested the state and the time that the task\n actually blocks on the sync resource.\n\n @param[in] var_id The object ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID the id passed in is not a valid condvar"]
1311    pub fn OS_CondVarWait(var_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1312}
1313unsafe extern "C" {
1314    #[doc = " @brief Time-limited wait on the condition variable object referenced by var_id.\n\n Identical in operation to OS_CondVarWait(), except that the maximum amount of\n time that the task will be blocked is limited.\n\n The abs_wakeup_time refers to the absolute time of the system clock at which the\n task should be unblocked to run, regardless of the state of the condition variable.\n This refers to the same system clock that is the subject of the OS_GetLocalTime() API.\n\n @param[in] var_id The object ID to operate on\n @param[in] abs_wakeup_time The system time at which the task should be unblocked @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID the id passed in is not a valid condvar"]
1315    pub fn OS_CondVarTimedWait(var_id: osal_id_t, abs_wakeup_time: *const OS_time_t) -> int32;
1316}
1317unsafe extern "C" {
1318    #[doc = " @brief Deletes the specified condition variable.\n\n Delete the condition variable and releases any related system resources.\n\n @param[in] var_id The object ID to delete\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid condvar"]
1319    pub fn OS_CondVarDelete(var_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1320}
1321unsafe extern "C" {
1322    #[doc = " @brief Find an existing condition variable ID by name\n\n This function tries to find an existing condition variable ID given the name.\n The id is returned through var_id.\n\n @param[out] var_id will be set to the ID of the existing resource\n @param[in]  var_name the name of the existing resource to find @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER is var_id or var_name are NULL pointers\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND if the name was not found in the table"]
1323    pub fn OS_CondVarGetIdByName(var_id: *mut osal_id_t, var_name: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1324}
1325unsafe extern "C" {
1326    #[doc = " @brief Fill a property object buffer with details regarding the resource\n\n This function will fill a structure to contain the information\n (name and creator) about the specified condition variable.\n\n @param[in]  var_id The object ID to operate on\n @param[out] condvar_prop The property object buffer to fill @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid semaphore\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the mut_prop pointer is null"]
1327    pub fn OS_CondVarGetInfo(var_id: osal_id_t, condvar_prop: *mut OS_condvar_prop_t) -> int32;
1328}
1329#[doc = " @brief OSAL counting semaphore properties"]
1330#[repr(C)]
1331#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
1332pub struct OS_count_sem_prop_t {
1333    pub name: [libc::c_char; 20usize],
1334    pub creator: osal_id_t,
1335    pub value: int32,
1336}
1337unsafe extern "C" {
1338    #[doc = " @brief Creates a counting semaphore\n\n Creates a counting semaphore with initial value specified by\n sem_initial_value and name specified by sem_name. sem_id will be\n returned to the caller.\n\n @note Underlying RTOS implementations may or may not impose a specific upper limit\n to the value of a counting semaphore.  If the OS has a specific limit and the\n sem_initial_value exceeds this limit, then #OS_INVALID_SEM_VALUE is returned.  On\n other implementations, any 32-bit integer value may be acceptable.  For maximum\n portability, it is recommended to keep counting semaphore values within the\n range of a \"short int\" (i.e. between 0 and 32767).  Many platforms do accept\n larger values, but may not be guaranteed.\n\n @param[out]  sem_id will be set to the non-zero ID of the newly-created resource @nonnull\n @param[in]   sem_name the name of the new resource to create @nonnull\n @param[in]   sem_initial_value the initial value of the counting semaphore\n @param[in]   options Reserved for future use, should be passed as 0.\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if sen name or sem_id are NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NO_FREE_IDS if all of the semaphore ids are taken\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TAKEN if this is already the name of a counting semaphore\n @retval #OS_INVALID_SEM_VALUE if the semaphore value is too high @covtest\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if an unspecified implementation error occurs @covtest"]
1339    pub fn OS_CountSemCreate(
1340        sem_id: *mut osal_id_t,
1341        sem_name: *const libc::c_char,
1342        sem_initial_value: uint32,
1343        options: uint32,
1344    ) -> int32;
1345}
1346unsafe extern "C" {
1347    #[doc = " @brief Increment the semaphore value\n\n The function  unlocks the semaphore referenced by sem_id by performing\n a semaphore unlock operation on that semaphore.  If the semaphore value\n resulting from this operation is positive, then no threads were blocked\n waiting for the semaphore to become unlocked; the semaphore value is\n simply incremented for this semaphore.\n\n @param[in] sem_id The object ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a counting semaphore\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if an unspecified implementation error occurs @covtest"]
1348    pub fn OS_CountSemGive(sem_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1349}
1350unsafe extern "C" {
1351    #[doc = " @brief Decrement the semaphore value\n\n The locks the semaphore referenced by sem_id by performing a\n semaphore lock operation on that semaphore.  If the semaphore value\n is currently zero, then the calling thread shall not return from\n the call until it either locks the semaphore or the call is\n interrupted.\n\n @param[in] sem_id The object ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID the Id passed in is not a valid counting semaphore\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if an unspecified implementation error occurs @covtest"]
1352    pub fn OS_CountSemTake(sem_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1353}
1354unsafe extern "C" {
1355    #[doc = " @brief Decrement the semaphore value with timeout\n\n The function locks the semaphore referenced by sem_id. However,\n if the semaphore cannot be locked without waiting for another process\n or thread to unlock the semaphore, this wait shall be terminated when\n the specified timeout, msecs, expires.\n\n @param[in] sem_id The object ID to operate on\n @param[in] msecs  The maximum amount of time to block, in milliseconds\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_SEM_TIMEOUT if semaphore was not relinquished in time\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the ID passed in is not a valid semaphore ID\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if an unspecified implementation error occurs @covtest"]
1356    pub fn OS_CountSemTimedWait(sem_id: osal_id_t, msecs: uint32) -> int32;
1357}
1358unsafe extern "C" {
1359    #[doc = " @brief Deletes the specified counting Semaphore.\n\n @param[in] sem_id The object ID to delete\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid counting semaphore\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if an unspecified implementation error occurs @covtest"]
1360    pub fn OS_CountSemDelete(sem_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1361}
1362unsafe extern "C" {
1363    #[doc = " @brief Find an existing semaphore ID by name\n\n This function tries to find a counting sem Id given the name of a count_sem\n The id is returned through sem_id\n\n @param[out] sem_id will be set to the ID of the existing resource\n @param[in]  sem_name the name of the existing resource to find @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER is semid or sem_name are NULL pointers\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND if the name was not found in the table"]
1364    pub fn OS_CountSemGetIdByName(sem_id: *mut osal_id_t, sem_name: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1365}
1366unsafe extern "C" {
1367    #[doc = " @brief Fill a property object buffer with details regarding the resource\n\n This function will pass back a pointer to structure that contains\n all of the relevant info( name and creator) about the specified counting\n semaphore.\n\n @param[in]  sem_id The object ID to operate on\n @param[out] count_prop The property object buffer to fill @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid semaphore\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the count_prop pointer is null\n @retval #OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED @copybrief OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED"]
1368    pub fn OS_CountSemGetInfo(sem_id: osal_id_t, count_prop: *mut OS_count_sem_prop_t) -> int32;
1369}
1370#[doc = " @brief Directory entry"]
1371#[repr(C)]
1372#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
1373pub struct os_dirent_t {
1374    pub FileName: [libc::c_char; 20usize],
1375}
1376unsafe extern "C" {
1377    #[doc = " @brief Opens a directory\n\n Prepares for reading the files within a directory\n\n @param[out] dir_id    Location to store handle ID of the directory @nonnull\n @param[in]  path      The directory to open @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if dir_id or path is NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the path argument exceeds the maximum length\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_INVALID if the path argument is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the directory could not be opened"]
1378    pub fn OS_DirectoryOpen(dir_id: *mut osal_id_t, path: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1379}
1380unsafe extern "C" {
1381    #[doc = " @brief Closes an open directory\n\n The directory referred to by dir_id will be closed\n\n @param[in] dir_id    The handle ID of the directory\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the directory handle is invalid"]
1382    pub fn OS_DirectoryClose(dir_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1383}
1384unsafe extern "C" {
1385    #[doc = " @brief Rewinds an open directory\n\n Resets a directory read handle back to the first file.\n\n @param[in] dir_id    The handle ID of the directory\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the directory handle is invalid"]
1386    pub fn OS_DirectoryRewind(dir_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1387}
1388unsafe extern "C" {
1389    #[doc = " @brief Reads the next name in the directory\n\n Obtains directory entry data for the next file from an open directory\n\n @param[in]  dir_id    The handle ID of the directory\n @param[out] dirent    Buffer to store directory entry information @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if dirent argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the directory handle is invalid\n @retval #OS_ERROR at the end of the directory or if the OS call otherwise fails"]
1390    pub fn OS_DirectoryRead(dir_id: osal_id_t, dirent: *mut os_dirent_t) -> int32;
1391}
1392unsafe extern "C" {
1393    #[doc = " @brief Makes a new directory\n\n Makes a directory specified by path.\n\n @param[in]  path      The new directory name @nonnull\n @param[in]  access    The permissions for the directory (reserved for future use)\n\n @note Current implementations do not utilize the \"access\" parameter.  Applications\n should still pass the intended value (#OS_READ_WRITE or #OS_READ_ONLY) to be compatible\n with future implementations.\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if path is NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the path is too long to be stored locally\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_INVALID if path cannot be parsed\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the OS call fails @covtest"]
1394    pub fn OS_mkdir(path: *const libc::c_char, access: uint32) -> int32;
1395}
1396unsafe extern "C" {
1397    #[doc = " @brief Removes a directory from the file system.\n\n Removes a directory from the structure.\n The directory must be empty prior to this operation.\n\n @param[in]  path      The directory to remove\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if path is NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_INVALID if path cannot be parsed\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the directory remove operation failed @covtest"]
1398    pub fn OS_rmdir(path: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1399}
1400#[doc = " @brief For the @ref OS_GetErrorName() function, to ensure\n everyone is making an array of the same length.\n\n Implementation note for developers:\n\n The sizes of strings in OSAL functions are built with this\n #OS_ERROR_NAME_LENGTH limit in mind.  Always check the uses of os_err_name_t\n when changing this value."]
1401pub type os_err_name_t = [libc::c_char; 35usize];
1402#[doc = " @brief For the @ref OS_StatusToString() function, to ensure\n everyone is making an array of the same length."]
1403pub type os_status_string_t = [libc::c_char; 12usize];
1404unsafe extern "C" {
1405    #[doc = " @brief Convert an error number to a string\n\n @param[in] error_num Error number to convert\n @param[out] err_name Buffer to store error string\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS if successfully converted to a string\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if err_name is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERROR if error could not be converted"]
1406    pub fn OS_GetErrorName(error_num: int32, err_name: *mut os_err_name_t) -> int32;
1407}
1408unsafe extern "C" {
1409    #[doc = " @brief Convert status to a string\n\n @param[in] status Status value to convert\n @param[out] status_string Buffer to store status converted to string\n\n @return Passed in string pointer"]
1410    pub fn OS_StatusToString(
1411        status: osal_status_t,
1412        status_string: *mut os_status_string_t,
1413    ) -> *mut libc::c_char;
1414}
1415#[doc = " @brief OSAL file properties"]
1416#[repr(C)]
1417#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone)]
1418pub struct OS_file_prop_t {
1419    pub Path: [libc::c_char; 64usize],
1420    pub User: osal_id_t,
1421    pub IsValid: uint8,
1422}
1423impl Default for OS_file_prop_t {
1424    fn default() -> Self {
1425        let mut s = ::core::mem::MaybeUninit::<Self>::uninit();
1426        unsafe {
1427            ::core::ptr::write_bytes(s.as_mut_ptr(), 0, 1);
1428            s.assume_init()
1429        }
1430    }
1431}
1432#[doc = " @brief File system status\n\n @note This used to be directly typedef'ed to the \"struct stat\" from the C library\n\n Some C libraries (glibc in particular) actually define member names to reference into\n sub-structures, so attempting to reuse a name like \"st_mtime\" might not work."]
1433#[repr(C)]
1434#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
1435pub struct os_fstat_t {
1436    pub FileModeBits: uint32,
1437    pub FileTime: OS_time_t,
1438    pub FileSize: usize,
1439}
1440pub const OS_FILESTAT_MODE_EXEC: _bindgen_ty_2 = 1;
1441pub const OS_FILESTAT_MODE_WRITE: _bindgen_ty_2 = 2;
1442pub const OS_FILESTAT_MODE_READ: _bindgen_ty_2 = 4;
1443pub const OS_FILESTAT_MODE_DIR: _bindgen_ty_2 = 65536;
1444#[doc = " @brief File stat mode bits\n\n We must also define replacements for the stat structure's mode bits.\n This is currently just a small subset since the OSAL just presents a very\n simplified view of the filesystem to the upper layers.  And since not all\n OS'es are POSIX, the more POSIX-specific bits are not relevant anyway."]
1445pub type _bindgen_ty_2 = libc::c_uint;
1446pub const OS_file_flag_t_OS_FILE_FLAG_NONE: OS_file_flag_t = 0;
1447pub const OS_file_flag_t_OS_FILE_FLAG_CREATE: OS_file_flag_t = 1;
1448pub const OS_file_flag_t_OS_FILE_FLAG_TRUNCATE: OS_file_flag_t = 2;
1449#[doc = " @brief Flags that can be used with opening of a file (bitmask)"]
1450pub type OS_file_flag_t = libc::c_uint;
1451unsafe extern "C" {
1452    #[doc = " @brief Open or create a file\n\n Implements the same as OS_open/OS_creat but follows the OSAL paradigm\n of outputting the ID/descriptor separately from the return value, rather\n than relying on the user to convert it back.\n\n @param[out] filedes     The handle ID (OS_OBJECT_ID_UNDEFINED on failure) @nonnull\n @param[in]  path        File name to create or open @nonnull\n @param[in]  flags       The file permissions - see @ref OS_file_flag_t\n @param[in]  access_mode Intended access mode - see @ref OSFileAccess\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the command was not executed properly\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if pointer argument was NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_NO_FREE_IDS if all available file handles are in use\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG if the filename portion of the path exceeds OS_MAX_FILE_NAME\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_INVALID if the path argument is not valid\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the path argument exceeds OS_MAX_PATH_LEN\n"]
1453    pub fn OS_OpenCreate(
1454        filedes: *mut osal_id_t,
1455        path: *const libc::c_char,
1456        flags: int32,
1457        access_mode: int32,
1458    ) -> int32;
1459}
1460unsafe extern "C" {
1461    #[doc = " @brief Closes an open file handle\n\n This closes regular file handles and any other file-like resource, such as\n network streams or pipes.\n\n @param[in] filedes   The handle ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the file descriptor passed in is invalid\n @retval #OS_ERROR if an unexpected/unhandled error occurs @covtest"]
1462    pub fn OS_close(filedes: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1463}
1464unsafe extern "C" {
1465    #[doc = " @brief Read from a file handle\n\n Reads up to nbytes from a file, and puts them into buffer.\n\n If the file position is at the end of file (or beyond, if the OS allows) then this\n function will return 0.\n\n @param[in]  filedes  The handle ID to operate on\n @param[out] buffer   Storage location for file data @nonnull\n @param[in]  nbytes   Maximum number of bytes to read @nonzero\n\n @note All OSAL error codes are negative int32 values.  Failure of this\n call can be checked by testing if the result is less than 0.\n\n @return A non-negative byte count or appropriate error code, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if buffer is a null pointer\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_SIZE if the passed-in size is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERROR if OS call failed @covtest\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the file descriptor passed in is invalid\n @retval 0 if at end of file/stream data"]
1466    pub fn OS_read(filedes: osal_id_t, buffer: *mut libc::c_void, nbytes: usize) -> int32;
1467}
1468unsafe extern "C" {
1469    #[doc = " @brief Write to a file handle\n\n Writes to a file. copies up to a maximum of nbytes of buffer to the file\n described in filedes\n\n @param[in] filedes   The handle ID to operate on\n @param[in] buffer    Source location for file data @nonnull\n @param[in] nbytes    Maximum number of bytes to read @nonzero\n\n @note All OSAL error codes are negative int32 values.  Failure of this\n call can be checked by testing if the result is less than 0.\n\n @return A non-negative byte count or appropriate error code, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if buffer is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_SIZE if the passed-in size is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERROR if OS call failed @covtest\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the file descriptor passed in is invalid\n @retval 0 if file/stream cannot accept any more data"]
1470    pub fn OS_write(filedes: osal_id_t, buffer: *const libc::c_void, nbytes: usize) -> int32;
1471}
1472unsafe extern "C" {
1473    #[doc = " @brief File/Stream input read with a timeout\n\n This implements a time-limited read and is primarily intended for use with\n sockets but may also work with any other stream-like resource that the underlying\n OS supports, such as pipes or special devices.\n\n If data is immediately available on the file/socket, this will return that data\n along with the actual number of bytes that were immediately available.  It will\n not block.\n\n If the file position is at the end of file or end of stream data (e.g. if the remote\n end has closed the connection), then this function will immediately return 0 without\n blocking for the timeout period.\n\n If no data is immediately available, but the underlying resource/stream is still\n connected to a peer, this will wait up to the given timeout for additional\n data to appear.  If no data appears within the timeout period, then this returns\n the #OS_ERROR_TIMEOUT status code.  This allows the caller to differentiate\n an open (but idle) socket connection from a connection which has been closed\n by the remote peer.\n\n In all cases this will return successfully as soon as at least 1 byte of actual\n data is available.  It will not attempt to read the entire input buffer.\n\n If an EOF condition occurs prior to timeout, this function returns zero.\n\n @param[in]  filedes   The handle ID to operate on\n @param[out] buffer    Storage location for file data @nonnull\n @param[in]  nbytes    Maximum number of bytes to read @nonzero\n @param[in]  abstime   Absolute time at which this function should return, if no data is readable\n\n @returns Byte count on success or appropriate error code, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_ERROR_TIMEOUT if no data became available during timeout period\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the file descriptor passed in is invalid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_SIZE if the passed-in size is not valid\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the passed-in buffer is not valid\n @retval 0 if at end of file/stream data"]
1474    pub fn OS_TimedReadAbs(
1475        filedes: osal_id_t,
1476        buffer: *mut libc::c_void,
1477        nbytes: usize,
1478        abstime: OS_time_t,
1479    ) -> int32;
1480}
1481unsafe extern "C" {
1482    #[doc = " @brief File/Stream input read with a timeout\n\n This implements a time-limited read and is primarily intended for use with\n sockets but may also work with any other stream-like resource that the underlying\n OS supports, such as pipes or special devices.\n\n If data is immediately available on the file/socket, this will return that data\n along with the actual number of bytes that were immediately available.  It will\n not block.\n\n If the file position is at the end of file or end of stream data (e.g. if the remote\n end has closed the connection), then this function will immediately return 0 without\n blocking for the timeout period.\n\n If no data is immediately available, but the underlying resource/stream is still\n connected to a peer, this will wait up to the given timeout for additional\n data to appear.  If no data appears within the timeout period, then this returns\n the #OS_ERROR_TIMEOUT status code.  This allows the caller to differentiate\n an open (but idle) socket connection from a connection which has been closed\n by the remote peer.\n\n In all cases this will return successfully as soon as at least 1 byte of actual\n data is available.  It will not attempt to read the entire input buffer.\n\n If an EOF condition occurs prior to timeout, this function returns zero.\n\n @param[in]  filedes   The handle ID to operate on\n @param[out] buffer    Storage location for file data @nonnull\n @param[in]  nbytes    Maximum number of bytes to read @nonzero\n @param[in]  timeout   Maximum time to wait, in milliseconds, relative to current time (OS_PEND = forever)\n\n @returns Byte count on success or appropriate error code, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_ERROR_TIMEOUT if no data became available during timeout period\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the file descriptor passed in is invalid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_SIZE if the passed-in size is not valid\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the passed-in buffer is not valid\n @retval 0 if at end of file/stream data"]
1483    pub fn OS_TimedRead(
1484        filedes: osal_id_t,
1485        buffer: *mut libc::c_void,
1486        nbytes: usize,
1487        timeout: int32,
1488    ) -> int32;
1489}
1490unsafe extern "C" {
1491    #[doc = " @brief File/Stream output write with a timeout\n\n This implements a time-limited write and is primarily intended for use with\n sockets but may also work with any other stream-like resource that the underlying\n OS supports.\n\n If output buffer space is immediately available on the file/socket, this will\n place data into the buffer and return the actual number of bytes that were\n queued for output.  It will not block.\n\n If no output buffer space is immediately available, this will wait up to the\n given timeout for space to become available.  If no space becomes available within\n the timeout period, then this returns an error code (not zero).\n\n In all cases this will return successfully as soon as at least 1 byte of actual\n data is output.  It will _not_ attempt to write the entire output buffer.\n\n If an EOF condition occurs prior to timeout, this function returns zero.\n\n @param[in] filedes   The handle ID to operate on\n @param[in] buffer    Source location for file data @nonnull\n @param[in] nbytes    Maximum number of bytes to read @nonzero\n @param[in] abstime   Absolute time at which this function should return, if no data is readable\n\n @return A non-negative byte count or appropriate error code, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_ERROR_TIMEOUT if no data became available during timeout period\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the file descriptor passed in is invalid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_SIZE if the passed-in size is not valid\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the passed-in buffer is not valid\n @retval 0 if file/stream cannot accept any more data"]
1492    pub fn OS_TimedWriteAbs(
1493        filedes: osal_id_t,
1494        buffer: *const libc::c_void,
1495        nbytes: usize,
1496        abstime: OS_time_t,
1497    ) -> int32;
1498}
1499unsafe extern "C" {
1500    #[doc = " @brief File/Stream output write with a timeout\n\n This implements a time-limited write and is primarily intended for use with\n sockets but may also work with any other stream-like resource that the underlying\n OS supports.\n\n If output buffer space is immediately available on the file/socket, this will\n place data into the buffer and return the actual number of bytes that were\n queued for output.  It will not block.\n\n If no output buffer space is immediately available, this will wait up to the\n given timeout for space to become available.  If no space becomes available within\n the timeout period, then this returns an error code (not zero).\n\n In all cases this will return successfully as soon as at least 1 byte of actual\n data is output.  It will _not_ attempt to write the entire output buffer.\n\n If an EOF condition occurs prior to timeout, this function returns zero.\n\n @param[in] filedes   The handle ID to operate on\n @param[in] buffer    Source location for file data @nonnull\n @param[in] nbytes    Maximum number of bytes to read @nonzero\n @param[in] timeout   Maximum time to wait, in milliseconds, relative to current time (OS_PEND = forever)\n\n @return A non-negative byte count or appropriate error code, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_ERROR_TIMEOUT if no data became available during timeout period\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the file descriptor passed in is invalid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_SIZE if the passed-in size is not valid\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the passed-in buffer is not valid\n @retval 0 if file/stream cannot accept any more data"]
1501    pub fn OS_TimedWrite(
1502        filedes: osal_id_t,
1503        buffer: *const libc::c_void,
1504        nbytes: usize,
1505        timeout: int32,
1506    ) -> int32;
1507}
1508unsafe extern "C" {
1509    #[doc = " @brief Changes the permissions of a file\n\n @param[in] path        File to change @nonnull\n @param[in] access_mode Desired access mode - see @ref OSFileAccess\n\n @note Some file systems do not implement permissions.  If the underlying OS\n       does not support this operation, then #OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED is returned.\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS @covtest\n @retval #OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if the filesystem does not support this call\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the path argument is NULL"]
1510    pub fn OS_chmod(path: *const libc::c_char, access_mode: uint32) -> int32;
1511}
1512unsafe extern "C" {
1513    #[doc = " @brief Obtain information about a file or directory\n\n Returns information about a file or directory in an os_fstat_t structure\n\n @param[in]  path      The file to operate on @nonnull\n @param[out] filestats Buffer to store file information @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if path or filestats is NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the path is too long to be stored locally\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG if the name of the file is too long to be stored\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_INVALID if path cannot be parsed\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the OS call failed"]
1514    pub fn OS_stat(path: *const libc::c_char, filestats: *mut os_fstat_t) -> int32;
1515}
1516unsafe extern "C" {
1517    #[doc = " @brief Seeks to the specified position of an open file\n\n Sets the read/write pointer to a specific offset in a specific file.\n\n @param[in] filedes   The handle ID to operate on\n @param[in] offset    The file offset to seek to\n @param[in] whence    The reference point for offset, see @ref OSFileOffset\n\n @return Byte offset from the beginning of the file or appropriate error code,\nsee @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the file descriptor passed in is invalid\n @retval #OS_ERROR if OS call failed @covtest"]
1518    pub fn OS_lseek(filedes: osal_id_t, offset: int32, whence: uint32) -> int32;
1519}
1520unsafe extern "C" {
1521    #[doc = " @brief Removes a file from the file system\n\n Removes a given filename from the drive\n\n @note The behavior of this API on an open file is not defined at the OSAL level\n due to dependencies on the underlying OS which may or may not allow the related\n operation based on a variety of potential configurations.  For portability,\n it is recommended that applications ensure the file is closed prior to removal.\n\n @param[in]  path      The file to operate on @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERROR if there is no device or the driver returns error\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if path is NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if path is too long to be stored locally\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_INVALID if path cannot be parsed\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG if the name of the file to remove is too long"]
1522    pub fn OS_remove(path: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1523}
1524unsafe extern "C" {
1525    #[doc = " @brief Renames a file\n\n Changes the name of a file, where the source and destination\n reside on the same file system.\n\n @note The behavior of this API on an open file is not defined at the OSAL level\n due to dependencies on the underlying OS which may or may not allow the related\n operation based on a variety of potential configurations.  For portability,\n it is recommended that applications ensure the file is closed prior to removal.\n\n @param[in]  old_filename      The original filename @nonnull\n @param[in]  new_filename      The desired filename @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the file could not be opened or renamed.\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if old_filename or new_filename are NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_INVALID if path cannot be parsed\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the paths given are too long to be stored locally\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG if the new name is too long to be stored locally"]
1526    pub fn OS_rename(old_filename: *const libc::c_char, new_filename: *const libc::c_char)
1527    -> int32;
1528}
1529unsafe extern "C" {
1530    #[doc = " @brief Copies a single file from src to dest\n\n @note The behavior of this API on an open file is not defined at the OSAL level\n due to dependencies on the underlying OS which may or may not allow the related\n operation based on a variety of potential configurations.  For portability,\n it is recommended that applications ensure the file is closed prior to removal.\n\n @param[in]  src       The source file to operate on @nonnull\n @param[in]  dest      The destination file @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the file could not be accessed\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if src or dest are NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_INVALID if path cannot be parsed\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the paths given are too long to be stored locally\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG if the dest name is too long to be stored locally"]
1531    pub fn OS_cp(src: *const libc::c_char, dest: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1532}
1533unsafe extern "C" {
1534    #[doc = " @brief Move a single file from src to dest\n\n This first attempts to rename the file, which is faster if\n the source and destination reside on the same file system.\n\n If this fails, it falls back to copying the file and removing\n the original.\n\n @note The behavior of this API on an open file is not defined at the OSAL level\n due to dependencies on the underlying OS which may or may not allow the related\n operation based on a variety of potential configurations.  For portability,\n it is recommended that applications ensure the file is closed prior to removal.\n\n @param[in]  src       The source file to operate on @nonnull\n @param[in]  dest      The destination file @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the file could not be renamed.\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if src or dest are NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_INVALID if path cannot be parsed\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the paths given are too long to be stored locally\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG if the dest name is too long to be stored locally"]
1535    pub fn OS_mv(src: *const libc::c_char, dest: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1536}
1537unsafe extern "C" {
1538    #[doc = " @brief Obtain information about an open file\n\n Copies the information of the given file descriptor into a structure passed in\n\n @param[in]  filedes  The handle ID to operate on\n @param[out] fd_prop  Storage buffer for file information @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the file descriptor passed in is invalid\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the fd_prop argument is NULL"]
1539    pub fn OS_FDGetInfo(filedes: osal_id_t, fd_prop: *mut OS_file_prop_t) -> int32;
1540}
1541unsafe extern "C" {
1542    #[doc = " @brief Checks to see if a file is open\n\n This function takes a filename and determines if the file is open. The function\n will return success if the file is open.\n\n @param[in]  Filename      The file to operate on @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS if the file is open\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the file is not open\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the filename argument is NULL"]
1543    pub fn OS_FileOpenCheck(Filename: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1544}
1545unsafe extern "C" {
1546    #[doc = " @brief Close all open files\n\n Closes All open files that were opened through the OSAL\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERROR   if one or more file close returned an error @covtest"]
1547    pub fn OS_CloseAllFiles() -> int32;
1548}
1549unsafe extern "C" {
1550    #[doc = " @brief Close a file by filename\n\n Allows a file to be closed by name.\n This will only work if the name passed in is the same name used to open\n the file.\n\n @param[in]  Filename      The file to close @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_INVALID if the file is not found\n @retval #OS_ERROR   if the file close returned an error @covtest\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the filename argument is NULL"]
1551    pub fn OS_CloseFileByName(Filename: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1552}
1553#[doc = " @brief OSAL file system info"]
1554#[repr(C)]
1555#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
1556pub struct os_fsinfo_t {
1557    #[doc = "< @brief Total number of file descriptors"]
1558    pub MaxFds: uint32,
1559    #[doc = "< @brief Total number that are free"]
1560    pub FreeFds: uint32,
1561    #[doc = "< @brief Maximum number of volumes"]
1562    pub MaxVolumes: uint32,
1563    #[doc = "< @brief Total number of volumes free"]
1564    pub FreeVolumes: uint32,
1565}
1566#[repr(C)]
1567#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
1568pub struct OS_statvfs_t {
1569    #[doc = "< Block size of underlying FS"]
1570    pub block_size: usize,
1571    #[doc = "< Total blocks in underlying FS"]
1572    pub total_blocks: osal_blockcount_t,
1573    #[doc = "< Available blocks in underlying FS"]
1574    pub blocks_free: osal_blockcount_t,
1575}
1576unsafe extern "C" {
1577    #[doc = " @brief Create a fixed mapping between an existing directory and a virtual OSAL mount point.\n\n This mimics the behavior of a \"FS_BASED\" entry in the VolumeTable but is registered\n at runtime.  It is intended to be called by the PSP/BSP prior to starting the application.\n\n @note OSAL virtual mount points are required to be a single, non-empty top-level directory\n name.  Virtual path names always follow the form /\\<virt_mount_point\\>/\\<relative_path\\>/\\<file\\>.\n Only the relative path may be omitted/empty (i.e. /\\<virt_mount_point\\>/\\<file\\>) but the\n virtual mount point must be present and not an empty string.  In particular this means\n it is not possible to directly refer to files in the \"root\" of the native file system\n from OSAL.  However it is possible to create a virtual map to the root, such as by calling:\n\n      OS_FileSysAddFixedMap(&fs_id, \"/\", \"/root\");\n\n @param[out]  filesys_id  A buffer to store the ID of the file system mapping @nonnull\n @param[in]   phys_path   The native system directory (an existing mount point) @nonnull\n @param[in]   virt_path   The virtual mount point of this filesystem @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the overall phys_path is too long\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG if the phys_path basename (filesystem name) is too long\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if any argument is NULL"]
1578    pub fn OS_FileSysAddFixedMap(
1579        filesys_id: *mut osal_id_t,
1580        phys_path: *const libc::c_char,
1581        virt_path: *const libc::c_char,
1582    ) -> int32;
1583}
1584unsafe extern "C" {
1585    #[doc = " @brief Makes a file system on the target\n\n Makes a file system on the target.  Highly dependent on underlying OS and\n dependent on OS volume table definition.\n\n @note The \"volname\" parameter of RAM disks should always begin with the string \"RAM\",\n   e.g. \"RAMDISK\" or \"RAM0\",\"RAM1\", etc if multiple devices are created.  The underlying\n   implementation uses this to select the correct filesystem type/format, and this may\n   also be used to differentiate between RAM disks and real physical disks.\n\n @param[in]   address   The address at which to start the new disk.  If address == 0\n                        space will be allocated by the OS.\n @param[in]   devname   The underlying kernel device to use, if applicable. @nonnull\n @param[in]   volname   The name of the volume (see note) @nonnull\n @param[in]   blocksize The size of a single block on the drive\n @param[in]   numblocks The number of blocks to allocate for the drive\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if devname or volname is NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the overall devname or volname is too long\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_DEVICE_NOT_FREE if the volume table is full\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_DRIVE_NOT_CREATED if an unexpected/unhandled OS error occurs @covtest"]
1586    pub fn OS_mkfs(
1587        address: *mut libc::c_char,
1588        devname: *const libc::c_char,
1589        volname: *const libc::c_char,
1590        blocksize: usize,
1591        numblocks: osal_blockcount_t,
1592    ) -> int32;
1593}
1594unsafe extern "C" {
1595    #[doc = " @brief Mounts a file system\n\n Mounts a file system / block device at the given mount point.\n\n @param[in]   devname    The name of the drive to mount. devname is the same from #OS_mkfs @nonnull\n @param[in]   mountpoint The name to call this disk from now on @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND if the device name does not exist in OSAL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the mount point string is too long\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if any argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERROR if an unexpected/unhandled OS error occurs @covtest"]
1596    pub fn OS_mount(devname: *const libc::c_char, mountpoint: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1597}
1598unsafe extern "C" {
1599    #[doc = " @brief Initializes an existing file system\n\n Initializes a file system on the target.\n\n @note The \"volname\" parameter of RAM disks should always begin with the string \"RAM\",\n   e.g. \"RAMDISK\" or \"RAM0\",\"RAM1\", etc if multiple devices are created.  The underlying\n   implementation uses this to select the correct filesystem type/format, and this may\n   also be used to differentiate between RAM disks and real physical disks.\n\n @param[in]   address   The address at which to start the new disk.  If address == 0,\n                        then space will be allocated by the OS\n @param[in]   devname   The underlying kernel device to use, if applicable. @nonnull\n @param[in]   volname   The name of the volume (see note) @nonnull\n @param[in]   blocksize The size of a single block on the drive\n @param[in]   numblocks The number of blocks to allocate for the drive\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if devname or volname are  NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the name is too long\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_DEVICE_NOT_FREE if the volume table is full\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_DRIVE_NOT_CREATED if an unexpected/unhandled OS error occurs @covtest"]
1600    pub fn OS_initfs(
1601        address: *mut libc::c_char,
1602        devname: *const libc::c_char,
1603        volname: *const libc::c_char,
1604        blocksize: usize,
1605        numblocks: osal_blockcount_t,
1606    ) -> int32;
1607}
1608unsafe extern "C" {
1609    #[doc = " @brief Removes a file system\n\n This function will remove or un-map the target file system. Note that this is not\n the same as un-mounting the file system.\n\n @param[in]   devname   The name of the \"generic\" drive @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if devname is NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the devname is too long\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND if the devname does not exist in OSAL\n @retval #OS_ERROR if an unexpected/unhandled OS error occurs @covtest"]
1610    pub fn OS_rmfs(devname: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1611}
1612unsafe extern "C" {
1613    #[doc = " @brief Unmounts a mounted file system\n\n This function will unmount a drive from the file system and make all open file\n descriptors useless.\n\n @note Any open file descriptors referencing this file system should\n be closed prior to unmounting a drive\n\n @param[in]   mountpoint The mount point to remove from #OS_mount @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if name is NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the absolute path given is too long\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND if the mountpoint is not mounted in OSAL\n @retval #OS_ERROR if an unexpected/unhandled OS error occurs @covtest"]
1614    pub fn OS_unmount(mountpoint: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1615}
1616unsafe extern "C" {
1617    #[doc = " @brief Obtains information about size and free space in a volume\n\n Populates the supplied OS_statvfs_t structure, which includes\n the block size and total/free blocks in a file system volume.\n\n This replaces two older OSAL calls:\n\n OS_fsBlocksFree() is determined by reading the blocks_free\n      output struct member\n OS_fsBytesFree() is determined by multiplying blocks_free\n      by the block_size member\n\n @param[in]  name       The device/path to operate on @nonnull\n @param[out] statbuf    Output structure to populate @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if name or statbuf is NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the name is too long\n @retval #OS_ERROR if an unexpected/unhandled OS error occurs @covtest"]
1618    pub fn OS_FileSysStatVolume(name: *const libc::c_char, statbuf: *mut OS_statvfs_t) -> int32;
1619}
1620unsafe extern "C" {
1621    #[doc = " @brief Checks the health of a file system and repairs it if necessary\n\n Checks the drives for inconsistencies and optionally also repairs it\n\n @note not all operating systems implement this function.  If the underlying\n       OS does not provide a facility to check the volume, then OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED\n       will be returned.\n\n @param[in]  name     The device/path to operate on @nonnull\n @param[in]  repair   Whether to also repair inconsistencies\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS             @copybrief OS_SUCCESS @covtest\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER     Name is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED @copybrief OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the name is too long\n @retval #OS_ERROR               @copybrief OS_ERROR @covtest"]
1622    pub fn OS_chkfs(name: *const libc::c_char, repair: bool) -> int32;
1623}
1624unsafe extern "C" {
1625    #[doc = " @brief Obtains the physical drive name associated with a mount point\n\n Returns the name of the physical volume associated with the drive,\n when given the OSAL mount point of the drive\n\n @param[out] PhysDriveName    Buffer to store physical drive name @nonnull\n @param[in]  MountPoint       OSAL mount point @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if either parameter is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND if the MountPoint is not mounted in OSAL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if the MountPoint is too long"]
1626    pub fn OS_FS_GetPhysDriveName(
1627        PhysDriveName: *mut libc::c_char,
1628        MountPoint: *const libc::c_char,
1629    ) -> int32;
1630}
1631unsafe extern "C" {
1632    #[doc = " @brief Translates an OSAL Virtual file system path to a host Local path\n\n Translates a virtual path to an actual system path name\n\n @note The buffer provided in the LocalPath argument is required to be at\n       least OS_MAX_PATH_LEN characters in length.\n\n @param[in]  VirtualPath      OSAL virtual path name @nonnull\n @param[out] LocalPath        Buffer to store native/translated path name @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if either parameter is NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG if the filename component is too long\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_INVALID if either parameter cannot be interpreted as a path\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_TOO_LONG if either input or output pathnames are too long"]
1633    pub fn OS_TranslatePath(
1634        VirtualPath: *const libc::c_char,
1635        LocalPath: *mut libc::c_char,
1636    ) -> int32;
1637}
1638unsafe extern "C" {
1639    #[doc = " @brief Returns information about the file system\n\n Returns information about the file system in an os_fsinfo_t.\n This includes the number of open files and file systems\n\n @param[out] filesys_info     Buffer to store filesystem information @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if filesys_info is NULL"]
1640    pub fn OS_GetFsInfo(filesys_info: *mut os_fsinfo_t) -> int32;
1641}
1642#[doc = " @brief OSAL heap properties\n\n @sa OS_HeapGetInfo()"]
1643#[repr(C)]
1644#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
1645pub struct OS_heap_prop_t {
1646    pub free_bytes: usize,
1647    pub free_blocks: osal_blockcount_t,
1648    pub largest_free_block: usize,
1649}
1650unsafe extern "C" {
1651    #[doc = " @brief Return current info on the heap\n\n @param[out] heap_prop Storage buffer for heap info\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the heap_prop argument is NULL"]
1652    pub fn OS_HeapGetInfo(heap_prop: *mut OS_heap_prop_t) -> int32;
1653}
1654unsafe extern "C" {
1655    #[doc = " @brief Abstraction for the system printf() call\n\n This function abstracts out the printf type statements. This is\n useful for using OS- specific thats that will allow non-polled\n print statements for the real time systems.\n\n Operates in a manner similar to the printf() call defined by the standard C\n library and takes all the parameters and formatting options of printf.\n This abstraction may implement additional buffering, if necessary,\n to improve the real-time performance of the call.\n\n Strings (including terminator) longer than #OS_BUFFER_SIZE will be truncated.\n\n The output of this routine also may be dynamically enabled or disabled by\n the OS_printf_enable() and OS_printf_disable() calls, respectively.\n\n @param[in] string Format string, followed by additional arguments"]
1656    pub fn OS_printf(string: *const libc::c_char, ...);
1657}
1658unsafe extern "C" {
1659    #[doc = " @brief This function disables the output from OS_printf."]
1660    pub fn OS_printf_disable();
1661}
1662unsafe extern "C" {
1663    #[doc = " @brief This function enables the output from OS_printf.\n"]
1664    pub fn OS_printf_enable();
1665}
1666unsafe extern "C" {
1667    #[doc = " @brief Obtain the name of an object given an arbitrary object ID\n\n All OSAL resources generally have a name associated with them.  This\n allows application code to retrieve the name of any valid OSAL object ID.\n\n @param[in]  object_id The object ID to operate on\n @param[out] buffer Buffer in which to store the name @nonnull\n @param[in]  buffer_size Size of the output storage buffer @nonzero\n\n @returns Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS  @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the passed-in ID is not a valid OSAL ID\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the passed-in buffer is invalid\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG if the name will not fit in the buffer provided"]
1668    pub fn OS_GetResourceName(
1669        object_id: osal_id_t,
1670        buffer: *mut libc::c_char,
1671        buffer_size: usize,
1672    ) -> int32;
1673}
1674unsafe extern "C" {
1675    #[doc = " @brief Obtain the type of an object given an arbitrary object ID\n\n Given an arbitrary object ID, get the type of the object\n\n @param[in] object_id The object ID to operate on\n\n @return The object type portion of the object_id, see @ref OSObjectTypes for\n         expected values"]
1676    pub fn OS_IdentifyObject(object_id: osal_id_t) -> osal_objtype_t;
1677}
1678unsafe extern "C" {
1679    #[doc = " @brief Converts an abstract ID into a number suitable for use as an array index.\n\n This will return a unique zero-based integer number in the range of [0,MAX) for\n any valid object ID.  This may be used by application code as an array index\n for indexing into local tables.\n\n @note This does NOT verify the validity of the ID, that is left to the caller.\n This is only the conversion logic.\n\n This routine accepts any object type, and returns a value based on the\n maximum number of objects for that type.  This is equivalent to invoking\n OS_ObjectIdToArrayIndex() with the idtype set to OS_OBJECT_TYPE_UNDEFINED.\n\n @sa OS_ObjectIdToArrayIndex\n\n @param[in]  object_id    The object ID to operate on\n @param[out] *ArrayIndex  The Index to return @nonnull\n\n @returns Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the object_id argument is not valid\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the ArrayIndex is NULL"]
1680    pub fn OS_ConvertToArrayIndex(object_id: osal_id_t, ArrayIndex: *mut osal_index_t) -> int32;
1681}
1682unsafe extern "C" {
1683    #[doc = " @brief Converts an abstract ID into a number suitable for use as an array index.\n\n This will return a unique zero-based integer number in the range of [0,MAX) for\n any valid object ID.  This may be used by application code as an array index\n for indexing into local tables.\n\n This routine operates on a specific object type, and returns a value based on the\n maximum number of objects for that type.\n\n If the idtype is passed as #OS_OBJECT_TYPE_UNDEFINED, then object type verification\n is skipped and any object ID will be accepted and converted to an index.  In this\n mode, the range of the output depends on the actual passed-in object type.\n\n If the idtype is passed as any other value, the passed-in ID value is first\n confirmed to be the correct type.  This check will guarantee that the output\n is within an expected range; for instance, if the type is passed as\n #OS_OBJECT_TYPE_OS_TASK, then the output index is guaranteed to be between 0 and\n #OS_MAX_TASKS-1 after successful conversion.\n\n @param[in]  idtype       The object type to convert\n @param[in]  object_id    The object ID to operate on\n @param[out] *ArrayIndex  The Index to return @nonnull\n\n @returns Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the object_id argument is not valid\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the ArrayIndex is NULL"]
1684    pub fn OS_ObjectIdToArrayIndex(
1685        idtype: osal_objtype_t,
1686        object_id: osal_id_t,
1687        ArrayIndex: *mut osal_index_t,
1688    ) -> int32;
1689}
1690unsafe extern "C" {
1691    #[doc = " @brief call the supplied callback function for all valid object IDs\n\n Loops through all defined OSAL objects of all types and calls callback_ptr on each one\n If creator_id is nonzero then only objects with matching creator id are processed.\n\n @param[in]  creator_id   Filter objects to those created by a specific task\n                          This may be passed as OS_OBJECT_CREATOR_ANY to return all objects\n @param[in]  callback_ptr Function to invoke for each matching object ID\n @param[in]  callback_arg Opaque Argument to pass to callback function (may be NULL)"]
1692    pub fn OS_ForEachObject(
1693        creator_id: osal_id_t,
1694        callback_ptr: OS_ArgCallback_t,
1695        callback_arg: *mut libc::c_void,
1696    );
1697}
1698unsafe extern "C" {
1699    #[doc = " @brief call the supplied callback function for valid object IDs of a specific type\n\n Loops through all defined OSAL objects of a specific type and calls callback_ptr on each one\n If creator_id is nonzero then only objects with matching creator id are processed.\n\n @param[in]  objtype      The type of objects to iterate\n @param[in]  creator_id   Filter objects to those created by a specific task\n                          This may be passed as OS_OBJECT_CREATOR_ANY to return all objects\n @param[in]  callback_ptr Function to invoke for each matching object ID\n @param[in]  callback_arg Opaque Argument to pass to callback function (may be NULL)"]
1700    pub fn OS_ForEachObjectOfType(
1701        objtype: osal_objtype_t,
1702        creator_id: osal_id_t,
1703        callback_ptr: OS_ArgCallback_t,
1704        callback_arg: *mut libc::c_void,
1705    );
1706}
1707#[doc = " @brief OSAL module address properties"]
1708#[repr(C)]
1709#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
1710pub struct OS_module_address_t {
1711    pub valid: uint32,
1712    pub flags: uint32,
1713    pub code_address: cpuaddr,
1714    pub code_size: cpuaddr,
1715    pub data_address: cpuaddr,
1716    pub data_size: cpuaddr,
1717    pub bss_address: cpuaddr,
1718    pub bss_size: cpuaddr,
1719}
1720#[doc = " @brief OSAL module properties"]
1721#[repr(C)]
1722#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone)]
1723pub struct OS_module_prop_t {
1724    pub entry_point: cpuaddr,
1725    pub host_module_id: cpuaddr,
1726    pub filename: [libc::c_char; 64usize],
1727    pub name: [libc::c_char; 20usize],
1728    pub addr: OS_module_address_t,
1729}
1730impl Default for OS_module_prop_t {
1731    fn default() -> Self {
1732        let mut s = ::core::mem::MaybeUninit::<Self>::uninit();
1733        unsafe {
1734            ::core::ptr::write_bytes(s.as_mut_ptr(), 0, 1);
1735            s.assume_init()
1736        }
1737    }
1738}
1739#[doc = " @brief Associates a single symbol name with a memory address.\n\n If the OS_STATIC_SYMBOL_TABLE feature is enabled, then\n an array of these structures should be provided by the\n application.  When the application needs to find a symbol\n address, the static table will be checked in addition\n to (or instead of) the OS/library-provided lookup function.\n\n This static symbol allows systems that do not implement\n dynamic module loading to maintain the same semantics\n as dynamically loaded modules."]
1740#[repr(C)]
1741#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone)]
1742pub struct _bindgen_ty_3 {
1743    pub Name: *const libc::c_char,
1744    pub Address: ::core::option::Option<unsafe extern "C" fn()>,
1745    pub Module: *const libc::c_char,
1746}
1747impl Default for _bindgen_ty_3 {
1748    fn default() -> Self {
1749        let mut s = ::core::mem::MaybeUninit::<Self>::uninit();
1750        unsafe {
1751            ::core::ptr::write_bytes(s.as_mut_ptr(), 0, 1);
1752            s.assume_init()
1753        }
1754    }
1755}
1756#[doc = " @brief Associates a single symbol name with a memory address.\n\n If the OS_STATIC_SYMBOL_TABLE feature is enabled, then\n an array of these structures should be provided by the\n application.  When the application needs to find a symbol\n address, the static table will be checked in addition\n to (or instead of) the OS/library-provided lookup function.\n\n This static symbol allows systems that do not implement\n dynamic module loading to maintain the same semantics\n as dynamically loaded modules."]
1757pub type OS_static_symbol_record_t = _bindgen_ty_3;
1758unsafe extern "C" {
1759    #[doc = " @brief Find the Address of a Symbol\n\n This calls to the OS dynamic symbol lookup implementation,\n and/or checks a static symbol table for a matching symbol name.\n\n The static table is intended to support embedded targets that do\n not have module loading capability or have it disabled.\n\n @param[out] symbol_address Set to the address of the symbol @nonnull\n @param[in]  symbol_name    Name of the symbol to look up @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the symbol could not be found\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if one of the pointers passed in are NULL"]
1760    pub fn OS_SymbolLookup(symbol_address: *mut cpuaddr, symbol_name: *const libc::c_char)
1761    -> int32;
1762}
1763unsafe extern "C" {
1764    #[doc = " @brief Find the Address of a Symbol within a module\n\n This is similar to OS_SymbolLookup() but for a specific module ID.\n This should be used to look up a symbol in a module that has been\n loaded with the #OS_MODULE_FLAG_LOCAL_SYMBOLS flag.\n\n @param[in]  module_id      Module ID that should contain the symbol\n @param[out] symbol_address Set to the address of the symbol @nonnull\n @param[in]  symbol_name    Name of the symbol to look up @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the symbol could not be found\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if one of the pointers passed in are NULL"]
1765    pub fn OS_ModuleSymbolLookup(
1766        module_id: osal_id_t,
1767        symbol_address: *mut cpuaddr,
1768        symbol_name: *const libc::c_char,
1769    ) -> int32;
1770}
1771unsafe extern "C" {
1772    #[doc = " @brief Dumps the system symbol table to a file\n\n Dumps the system symbol table to the specified filename\n\n @note Not all RTOS implementations support this API.  If the underlying\n module subsystem does not provide a facility to iterate through the\n symbol table, then the #OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED status code is returned.\n\n @param[in] filename   File to write to @nonnull\n @param[in] size_limit Maximum number of bytes to write\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS             @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED @copybrief OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the filename argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_INVALID if the filename argument is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG if any of the symbol names are too long @covtest\n @retval #OS_ERR_OUTPUT_TOO_LARGE if the size_limit was reached before completing all symbols @covtest\n @retval #OS_ERROR if an other/unspecified error occurs @covtest"]
1773    pub fn OS_SymbolTableDump(filename: *const libc::c_char, size_limit: usize) -> int32;
1774}
1775unsafe extern "C" {
1776    #[doc = " @brief Loads an object file\n\n Loads an object file into the running operating system\n\n The \"flags\" parameter may influence how the loaded module symbols are made\n available for use in the application.  See #OS_MODULE_FLAG_LOCAL_SYMBOLS\n and #OS_MODULE_FLAG_GLOBAL_SYMBOLS for descriptions.\n\n @param[out] module_id    Non-zero OSAL ID corresponding to the loaded module\n @param[in]  module_name  Name of module @nonnull\n @param[in]  filename     File containing the object code to load @nonnull\n @param[in]  flags        Options for the loaded module\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if one of the parameters is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_NO_FREE_IDS if the module table is full\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TAKEN if the name is in use\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG if the module_name is too long\n @retval #OS_FS_ERR_PATH_INVALID if the filename argument is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERROR if an other/unspecified error occurs @covtest"]
1777    pub fn OS_ModuleLoad(
1778        module_id: *mut osal_id_t,
1779        module_name: *const libc::c_char,
1780        filename: *const libc::c_char,
1781        flags: uint32,
1782    ) -> int32;
1783}
1784unsafe extern "C" {
1785    #[doc = " @brief Unloads the module file\n\n Unloads the module file from the running operating system\n\n @param[in] module_id    OSAL ID of the previously the loaded module\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the module id invalid\n @retval #OS_ERROR if an other/unspecified error occurs @covtest"]
1786    pub fn OS_ModuleUnload(module_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1787}
1788unsafe extern "C" {
1789    #[doc = " @brief Obtain information about a module\n\n Returns information about the loadable module\n\n @param[in]  module_id    OSAL ID of the previously the loaded module\n @param[out] module_info  Buffer to store module information @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the module id invalid\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the pointer to the ModuleInfo structure is invalid\n @retval #OS_ERROR if an other/unspecified error occurs @covtest"]
1790    pub fn OS_ModuleInfo(module_id: osal_id_t, module_info: *mut OS_module_prop_t) -> int32;
1791}
1792#[doc = " @brief OSAL mutex properties"]
1793#[repr(C)]
1794#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
1795pub struct OS_mut_sem_prop_t {
1796    pub name: [libc::c_char; 20usize],
1797    pub creator: osal_id_t,
1798}
1799unsafe extern "C" {
1800    #[doc = " @brief Creates a mutex semaphore\n\n Mutex semaphores are always created in the unlocked (full) state.\n\n @param[out]  sem_id will be set to the non-zero ID of the newly-created resource @nonnull\n @param[in]   sem_name the name of the new resource to create @nonnull\n @param[in]   options reserved for future use.  Should be passed as 0.\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if sem_id or sem_name are NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NO_FREE_IDS if there are no more free mutex Ids\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TAKEN if there is already a mutex with the same name\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if the OS call failed @covtest"]
1801    pub fn OS_MutSemCreate(
1802        sem_id: *mut osal_id_t,
1803        sem_name: *const libc::c_char,
1804        options: uint32,
1805    ) -> int32;
1806}
1807unsafe extern "C" {
1808    #[doc = " @brief Releases the mutex object referenced by sem_id.\n\n If there are threads blocked on the mutex object referenced by\n mutex when this function is called, resulting in the mutex becoming\n available, the scheduling policy shall determine which thread shall\n acquire the mutex.\n\n @param[in] sem_id The object ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid mutex\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if an unspecified error occurs @covtest"]
1809    pub fn OS_MutSemGive(sem_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1810}
1811unsafe extern "C" {
1812    #[doc = " @brief Acquire the mutex object referenced by sem_id.\n\n If the mutex is already locked, the calling thread shall\n block until the mutex becomes available. This operation shall\n return with the mutex object referenced by mutex in the locked state\n with the calling thread as its owner.\n\n @param[in] sem_id The object ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID the id passed in is not a valid mutex\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if an unspecified error occurs @covtest"]
1813    pub fn OS_MutSemTake(sem_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1814}
1815unsafe extern "C" {
1816    #[doc = " @brief Deletes the specified Mutex Semaphore.\n\n Delete the semaphore.  This also frees the respective sem_id such that it can be\n used again when another is created.\n\n @param[in] sem_id The object ID to delete\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid mutex\n @retval #OS_SEM_FAILURE if an unspecified error occurs @covtest"]
1817    pub fn OS_MutSemDelete(sem_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1818}
1819unsafe extern "C" {
1820    #[doc = " @brief Find an existing mutex ID by name\n\n This function tries to find a mutex sem Id given the name of a mut_sem.\n The id is returned through sem_id\n\n @param[out] sem_id will be set to the ID of the existing resource\n @param[in]  sem_name the name of the existing resource to find @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER is semid or sem_name are NULL pointers\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND if the name was not found in the table"]
1821    pub fn OS_MutSemGetIdByName(sem_id: *mut osal_id_t, sem_name: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1822}
1823unsafe extern "C" {
1824    #[doc = " @brief Fill a property object buffer with details regarding the resource\n\n This function will pass back a pointer to structure that contains\n all of the relevant info( name and creator) about the specified mutex\n semaphore.\n\n @param[in]  sem_id The object ID to operate on\n @param[out] mut_prop The property object buffer to fill @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid semaphore\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the mut_prop pointer is null"]
1825    pub fn OS_MutSemGetInfo(sem_id: osal_id_t, mut_prop: *mut OS_mut_sem_prop_t) -> int32;
1826}
1827unsafe extern "C" {
1828    #[doc = " @brief  Gets the network ID of the local machine\n\n The ID is an implementation-defined value and may not be consistent\n in meaning across different platform types.\n\n @note This API may be removed in a future version of OSAL due to\n       inconsistencies between platforms.\n\n @return The ID or fixed value of -1 if the host id could not be found.\n         Note it is not possible to differentiate between error codes and valid\n         network IDs here. It is assumed, however, that -1 is never a valid ID."]
1829    pub fn OS_NetworkGetID() -> int32;
1830}
1831unsafe extern "C" {
1832    #[doc = " @brief Gets the local machine network host name\n\n If configured in the underlying network stack,\n this function retrieves the local hostname of the system.\n\n @param[out]  host_name    Buffer to hold name information @nonnull\n @param[in]   name_len     Maximum length of host name buffer @nonzero\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_SIZE if the name_len is zero\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the host_name is NULL"]
1833    pub fn OS_NetworkGetHostName(host_name: *mut libc::c_char, name_len: usize) -> int32;
1834}
1835#[doc = " @brief OSAL queue properties"]
1836#[repr(C)]
1837#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
1838pub struct OS_queue_prop_t {
1839    pub name: [libc::c_char; 20usize],
1840    pub creator: osal_id_t,
1841}
1842unsafe extern "C" {
1843    #[doc = " @brief Create a message queue\n\n This is the function used to create a queue in the operating system.\n Depending on the underlying operating system, the memory for the queue\n will be allocated automatically or allocated by the code that sets up\n the queue. Queue names must be unique; if the name already exists this\n function fails. Names cannot be NULL.\n\n @param[out]  queue_id will be set to the non-zero ID of the newly-created resource @nonnull\n @param[in]   queue_name the name of the new resource to create @nonnull\n @param[in]   queue_depth the maximum depth of the queue\n @param[in]   data_size the size of each entry in the queue @nonzero\n @param[in]   flags options for the queue (reserved for future use, pass as 0)\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if a pointer passed in is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NO_FREE_IDS if there are already the max queues created\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TAKEN if the name is already being used on another queue\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_SIZE if data_size is 0\n @retval #OS_QUEUE_INVALID_SIZE if the queue depth exceeds the limit\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the OS create call fails"]
1844    pub fn OS_QueueCreate(
1845        queue_id: *mut osal_id_t,
1846        queue_name: *const libc::c_char,
1847        queue_depth: osal_blockcount_t,
1848        data_size: usize,
1849        flags: uint32,
1850    ) -> int32;
1851}
1852unsafe extern "C" {
1853    #[doc = " @brief Deletes the specified message queue.\n\n This is the function used to delete a queue in the operating system.\n This also frees the respective queue_id to be used again when another queue is created.\n\n @note If There are messages on the queue, they will be lost and any subsequent\n calls to QueueGet or QueuePut to this queue will result in errors\n\n @param[in] queue_id The object ID to delete\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in does not exist\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the OS call returns an unexpected error @covtest"]
