1854    pub fn OS_QueueDelete(queue_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1855}
1856unsafe extern "C" {
1857    #[doc = " @brief Receive a message on a message queue\n\n If a message is pending, it is returned immediately.  Otherwise the calling task\n will block until a message arrives or the timeout expires.\n\n @param[in]   queue_id The object ID to operate on\n @param[out]  data The buffer to store the received message @nonnull\n @param[in]   size The size of the data buffer @nonzero\n @param[out]  size_copied Set to the actual size of the message @nonnull\n @param[in]   timeout The maximum amount of time to block, or OS_PEND to wait forever\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the given ID does not exist\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if a pointer passed in is NULL\n @retval #OS_QUEUE_EMPTY if the Queue has no messages on it to be received\n @retval #OS_QUEUE_TIMEOUT if the timeout was OS_PEND and the time expired\n @retval #OS_QUEUE_INVALID_SIZE if the size copied from the queue was not correct\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the OS call returns an unexpected error @covtest"]
1858    pub fn OS_QueueGet(
1859        queue_id: osal_id_t,
1860        data: *mut libc::c_void,
1861        size: usize,
1862        size_copied: *mut usize,
1863        timeout: int32,
1864    ) -> int32;
1865}
1866unsafe extern "C" {
1867    #[doc = " @brief Put a message on a message queue.\n\n @param[in]  queue_id The object ID to operate on\n @param[in]  data The buffer containing the message to put @nonnull\n @param[in]  size The size of the data buffer @nonzero\n @param[in]  flags Currently reserved/unused, should be passed as 0\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the queue id passed in is not a valid queue\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the data pointer is NULL\n @retval #OS_QUEUE_INVALID_SIZE if the data message is too large for the queue\n @retval #OS_QUEUE_FULL if the queue cannot accept another message\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the OS call returns an unexpected error @covtest"]
1868    pub fn OS_QueuePut(
1869        queue_id: osal_id_t,
1870        data: *const libc::c_void,
1871        size: usize,
1872        flags: uint32,
1873    ) -> int32;
1874}
1875unsafe extern "C" {
1876    #[doc = " @brief Find an existing queue ID by name\n\n This function tries to find a queue Id given the name of the queue. The\n id of the queue is passed back in queue_id.\n\n @param[out]  queue_id will be set to the ID of the existing resource\n @param[in]   queue_name the name of the existing resource to find @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the name or id pointers are NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND the name was not found in the table"]
1877    pub fn OS_QueueGetIdByName(queue_id: *mut osal_id_t, queue_name: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
1878}
1879unsafe extern "C" {
1880    #[doc = " @brief Fill a property object buffer with details regarding the resource\n\n This function will pass back a pointer to structure that contains\n all of the relevant info (name and creator) about the specified queue.\n\n @param[in]   queue_id The object ID to operate on\n @param[out]  queue_prop The property object buffer to fill @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if queue_prop is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the ID given is not  a valid queue"]
1881    pub fn OS_QueueGetInfo(queue_id: osal_id_t, queue_prop: *mut OS_queue_prop_t) -> int32;
1882}
1883#[doc = " @brief An abstract structure capable of holding several OSAL IDs\n\n This is part of the select API and is manipulated using the\n related API calls.  It should not be modified directly by applications.\n\n Note: Math is to determine uint8 array size needed to represent\n       single bit OS_MAX_NUM_OPEN_FILES objects, + 7 rounds up\n       and 8 is the size of uint8.\n\n @sa OS_SelectFdZero(), OS_SelectFdAdd(), OS_SelectFdClear(), OS_SelectFdIsSet()"]
1884#[repr(C)]
1885#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
1886pub struct OS_FdSet {
1887    pub object_ids: [uint8; 7usize],
1888}
1889#[doc = "< @brief whether the stream is bound"]
1890pub const OS_StreamState_t_OS_STREAM_STATE_BOUND: OS_StreamState_t = 1;
1891#[doc = "< @brief whether the stream is connected"]
1892pub const OS_StreamState_t_OS_STREAM_STATE_CONNECTED: OS_StreamState_t = 2;
1893#[doc = "< @brief whether the stream is readable"]
1894pub const OS_StreamState_t_OS_STREAM_STATE_READABLE: OS_StreamState_t = 4;
1895#[doc = "< @brief whether the stream is writable"]
1896pub const OS_StreamState_t_OS_STREAM_STATE_WRITABLE: OS_StreamState_t = 8;
1897#[doc = "< @brief whether the stream is listening"]
1898pub const OS_StreamState_t_OS_STREAM_STATE_LISTENING: OS_StreamState_t = 16;
1899#[doc = " @brief For the OS_SelectSingle() function's in/out StateFlags parameter,\n the state(s) of the stream and the result of the select is a combination\n of one or more of these states.\n\n @sa OS_SelectSingle()"]
1900pub type OS_StreamState_t = libc::c_uint;
1901unsafe extern "C" {
1902    #[doc = " @brief Wait for events across multiple file handles\n\n Wait for any of the given sets of IDs to become readable or writable\n\n This function will block until any of the following occurs:\n  - At least one OSAL ID in the ReadSet is readable\n  - At least one OSAL ID in the WriteSet is writable\n  - The timeout has elapsed\n\n The sets are input/output parameters.  On entry, these indicate the\n file handle(s) to wait for.  On exit, these are set to the actual\n file handle(s) that have activity.\n\n If the timeout occurs this returns an error code and all output sets\n should be empty.\n\n This API is identical to OS_SelectMultiple() except for the timeout parameter.  In\n this call, timeout is expressed as an absolute value of the OS clock, in the same\n time domain as obtained via OS_GetLocalTime().  This allows for a more precise\n timeout than what is possible via the normal OS_SelectMultiple().\n\n @note This does not lock or otherwise protect the file handles in the\n given sets.  If a filehandle supplied via one of the FdSet arguments\n is closed or modified by another while this function is in progress,\n the results are undefined.  Because of this limitation, it is recommended\n to use OS_SelectSingle() whenever possible.\n\n @param[in,out] ReadSet     Set of handles to check/wait to become readable\n @param[in,out] WriteSet    Set of handles to check/wait to become writable\n @param[in]     abs_timeout The absolute time that the call may block until\n\n @sa OS_SelectMultiple()\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS If any handle in the ReadSet or WriteSet is readable or writable, respectively\n @retval #OS_ERROR_TIMEOUT If no handles in the ReadSet or WriteSet became readable or writable within the timeout\n @retval #OS_ERR_OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED if a specified handle does not support select\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if no valid handles were contained in the ReadSet/WriteSet"]
1903    pub fn OS_SelectMultipleAbs(
1904        ReadSet: *mut OS_FdSet,
1905        WriteSet: *mut OS_FdSet,
1906        abs_timeout: OS_time_t,
1907    ) -> int32;
1908}
1909unsafe extern "C" {
1910    #[doc = " @brief Wait for events across multiple file handles\n\n Wait for any of the given sets of IDs to become readable or writable\n\n This function will block until any of the following occurs:\n  - At least one OSAL ID in the ReadSet is readable\n  - At least one OSAL ID in the WriteSet is writable\n  - The timeout has elapsed\n\n The sets are input/output parameters.  On entry, these indicate the\n file handle(s) to wait for.  On exit, these are set to the actual\n file handle(s) that have activity.\n\n If the timeout occurs this returns an error code and all output sets\n should be empty.\n\n The timeout is expressed in milliseconds, relative to the time that the API was\n invoked.  Use OS_SelectMultipleAbs() for higher timing precision.\n\n @note This does not lock or otherwise protect the file handles in the\n given sets.  If a filehandle supplied via one of the FdSet arguments\n is closed or modified by another while this function is in progress,\n the results are undefined.  Because of this limitation, it is recommended\n to use OS_SelectSingle() whenever possible.\n\n @param[in,out] ReadSet  Set of handles to check/wait to become readable\n @param[in,out] WriteSet Set of handles to check/wait to become writable\n @param[in] msecs Indicates the timeout. Positive values will wait up to that many milliseconds. Zero will not wait\n (poll). Negative values will wait forever (pend)\n\n @sa OS_SelectMultipleAbs()\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS If any handle in the ReadSet or WriteSet is readable or writable, respectively\n @retval #OS_ERROR_TIMEOUT If no handles in the ReadSet or WriteSet became readable or writable within the timeout\n @retval #OS_ERR_OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED if a specified handle does not support select\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if no valid handles were contained in the ReadSet/WriteSet"]
1911    pub fn OS_SelectMultiple(
1912        ReadSet: *mut OS_FdSet,
1913        WriteSet: *mut OS_FdSet,
1914        msecs: int32,
1915    ) -> int32;
1916}
1917unsafe extern "C" {
1918    #[doc = " @brief Wait for events on a single file handle\n\n Wait for a single OSAL filehandle to change state\n\n This function can be used to wait for a single OSAL stream ID\n to become readable or writable.   On entry, the \"StateFlags\"\n parameter should be set to the desired state (OS_STREAM_STATE_READABLE\n and/or OS_STREAM_STATE_WRITABLE) and upon return the flags\n will be set to the state actually detected.\n\n As this operates on a single ID, the filehandle is protected\n during this call, such that another thread accessing the same\n handle will return an error.  However, it is important to note that\n once the call returns then other threads may then also read/write\n and affect the state before the current thread can service it.\n\n To mitigate this risk the application may prefer to use\n the OS_TimedRead/OS_TimedWrite calls.\n\n This API is identical to OS_SelectSingle() except for the timeout parameter.  In\n this call, timeout is expressed as an absolute value of the OS clock, in the same\n time domain as obtained via OS_GetLocalTime().  This allows for a more precise\n timeout than what is possible via the normal OS_SelectSingle().\n\n @param[in] objid The handle ID to select on\n @param[in,out] StateFlags State flag(s) (readable or writable) @nonnull\n @param[in] abs_timeout The absolute time that the call may block until\n\n @sa OS_SelectSingle()\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS If the handle is readable and/or writable, as requested\n @retval #OS_ERROR_TIMEOUT If the handle did not become readable or writable within the timeout\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the objid is not a valid handle"]
1919    pub fn OS_SelectSingleAbs(
1920        objid: osal_id_t,
1921        StateFlags: *mut uint32,
1922        abs_timeout: OS_time_t,
1923    ) -> int32;
1924}
1925unsafe extern "C" {
1926    #[doc = " @brief Wait for events on a single file handle\n\n Wait for a single OSAL filehandle to change state\n\n This function can be used to wait for a single OSAL stream ID\n to become readable or writable.   On entry, the \"StateFlags\"\n parameter should be set to the desired state (OS_STREAM_STATE_READABLE\n and/or OS_STREAM_STATE_WRITABLE) and upon return the flags\n will be set to the state actually detected.\n\n As this operates on a single ID, the filehandle is protected\n during this call, such that another thread accessing the same\n handle will return an error.  However, it is important to note that\n once the call returns then other threads may then also read/write\n and affect the state before the current thread can service it.\n\n To mitigate this risk the application may prefer to use\n the OS_TimedRead/OS_TimedWrite calls.\n\n The timeout is expressed in milliseconds, relative to the time that the API was\n invoked.  Use OS_SelectSingleAbs() for higher timing precision.\n\n @param[in] objid The handle ID to select on\n @param[in,out] StateFlags State flag(s) (readable or writable) @nonnull\n @param[in] msecs Indicates the timeout. Positive values will wait up to that many milliseconds. Zero will not wait\n (poll). Negative values will wait forever (pend)\n\n @sa OS_SelectSingleAbs()\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS If the handle is readable and/or writable, as requested\n @retval #OS_ERROR_TIMEOUT If the handle did not become readable or writable within the timeout\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the objid is not a valid handle"]
1927    pub fn OS_SelectSingle(objid: osal_id_t, StateFlags: *mut uint32, msecs: int32) -> int32;
1928}
1929unsafe extern "C" {
1930    #[doc = " @brief Clear a FdSet structure\n\n After this call the set will contain no OSAL IDs\n\n @param[out] Set Pointer to OS_FdSet object to clear @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief #OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL"]
1931    pub fn OS_SelectFdZero(Set: *mut OS_FdSet) -> int32;
1932}
1933unsafe extern "C" {
1934    #[doc = " @brief Add an ID to an FdSet structure\n\n After this call the set will contain the given OSAL ID\n\n @param[in,out] Set Pointer to OS_FdSet object to operate on @nonnull\n @param[in] objid The handle ID to add to the set\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief #OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the objid is not a valid handle"]
1935    pub fn OS_SelectFdAdd(Set: *mut OS_FdSet, objid: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1936}
1937unsafe extern "C" {
1938    #[doc = " @brief Clear an ID from an FdSet structure\n\n After this call the set will no longer contain the given OSAL ID\n\n @param[in,out] Set Pointer to OS_FdSet object to operate on @nonnull\n @param[in] objid The handle ID to remove from the set\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief #OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the objid is not a valid handle"]
1939    pub fn OS_SelectFdClear(Set: *mut OS_FdSet, objid: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1940}
1941unsafe extern "C" {
1942    #[doc = " @brief Check if an FdSet structure contains a given ID\n\n @param[in] Set Pointer to OS_FdSet object to operate on @nonnull\n @param[in] objid The handle ID to check for in the set\n\n @return Boolean set status\n @retval true  FdSet structure contains ID\n @retval false FDSet structure does not contain ID"]
1943    pub fn OS_SelectFdIsSet(Set: *const OS_FdSet, objid: osal_id_t) -> bool;
1944}
1945unsafe extern "C" {
1946    #[doc = " @brief Executes the command and sends output to a file\n\n Takes a shell command in and writes the output of that command to the specified file\n The output file must be opened previously with write access (OS_WRITE_ONLY or OS_READ_WRITE).\n\n @param[in] Cmd     Command to pass to shell @nonnull\n @param[in] filedes File to send output to.\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the command was not executed properly\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if Cmd argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the file descriptor passed in is invalid"]
1947    pub fn OS_ShellOutputToFile(Cmd: *const libc::c_char, filedes: osal_id_t) -> int32;
1948}
1949#[doc = "< @brief Invalid"]
1950pub const OS_SocketDomain_t_OS_SocketDomain_INVALID: OS_SocketDomain_t = 0;
1951#[doc = "< @brief IPv4 address family, most commonly used)"]
1952pub const OS_SocketDomain_t_OS_SocketDomain_INET: OS_SocketDomain_t = 1;
1953#[doc = "< @brief IPv6 address family, depends on OS/network stack support"]
1954pub const OS_SocketDomain_t_OS_SocketDomain_INET6: OS_SocketDomain_t = 2;
1955#[doc = "< @brief Maximum"]
1956pub const OS_SocketDomain_t_OS_SocketDomain_MAX: OS_SocketDomain_t = 3;
1957#[doc = " @brief Socket domain"]
1958pub type OS_SocketDomain_t = libc::c_uint;
1959#[doc = "< @brief Invalid"]
1960pub const OS_SocketType_t_OS_SocketType_INVALID: OS_SocketType_t = 0;
1961#[doc = "< @brief A connectionless, message-oriented socket"]
1962pub const OS_SocketType_t_OS_SocketType_DATAGRAM: OS_SocketType_t = 1;
1963#[doc = "< @brief A stream-oriented socket with the concept of a connection"]
1964pub const OS_SocketType_t_OS_SocketType_STREAM: OS_SocketType_t = 2;
1965#[doc = "< @brief Maximum"]
1966pub const OS_SocketType_t_OS_SocketType_MAX: OS_SocketType_t = 3;
1967#[doc = " @brief Socket type"]
1968pub type OS_SocketType_t = libc::c_uint;
1969#[doc = "< @brief Reserved value, no effect"]
1970pub const OS_SocketShutdownMode_t_OS_SocketShutdownMode_NONE: OS_SocketShutdownMode_t = 0;
1971#[doc = "< @brief Disable future reading"]
1972pub const OS_SocketShutdownMode_t_OS_SocketShutdownMode_SHUT_READ: OS_SocketShutdownMode_t = 1;
1973#[doc = "< @brief Disable future writing"]
1974pub const OS_SocketShutdownMode_t_OS_SocketShutdownMode_SHUT_WRITE: OS_SocketShutdownMode_t = 2;
1975#[doc = "< @brief Disable future reading or writing"]
1976pub const OS_SocketShutdownMode_t_OS_SocketShutdownMode_SHUT_READWRITE: OS_SocketShutdownMode_t = 3;
1977#[doc = " @brief Shutdown Mode"]
1978pub type OS_SocketShutdownMode_t = libc::c_uint;
1979#[doc = " @brief Storage buffer for generic network address\n\n This is a union type that helps to ensure a minimum\n alignment value for the data storage, such that it can\n be cast to the system-specific type without\n increasing alignment requirements."]
1980#[repr(C)]
1981#[derive(Copy, Clone)]
1982pub union OS_SockAddrData_t {
1983    #[doc = "< @brief Ensures length of at least OS_SOCKADDR_MAX_LEN"]
1984    pub Buffer: [uint8; 28usize],
1985    #[doc = "< @brief Ensures uint32 alignment"]
1986    pub AlignU32: uint32,
1987    #[doc = "< @brief Ensures pointer alignment"]
1988    pub AlignPtr: *mut libc::c_void,
1989}
1990impl Default for OS_SockAddrData_t {
1991    fn default() -> Self {
1992        let mut s = ::core::mem::MaybeUninit::<Self>::uninit();
1993        unsafe {
1994            ::core::ptr::write_bytes(s.as_mut_ptr(), 0, 1);
1995            s.assume_init()
1996        }
1997    }
1998}
1999#[doc = " @brief Encapsulates a generic network address\n\n This is just an abstract buffer type that holds a network address.\n It is allocated for the worst-case size defined by OS_SOCKADDR_MAX_LEN,\n and the real size is stored within."]
2000#[repr(C)]
2001#[derive(Copy, Clone)]
2002pub struct OS_SockAddr_t {
2003    #[doc = "< @brief Length of the actual address data"]
2004    pub ActualLength: usize,
2005    #[doc = "< @brief Abstract Address data"]
2006    pub AddrData: OS_SockAddrData_t,
2007}
2008impl Default for OS_SockAddr_t {
2009    fn default() -> Self {
2010        let mut s = ::core::mem::MaybeUninit::<Self>::uninit();
2011        unsafe {
2012            ::core::ptr::write_bytes(s.as_mut_ptr(), 0, 1);
2013            s.assume_init()
2014        }
2015    }
2016}
2017#[doc = " @brief Encapsulates socket properties\n\n This is for consistency with other OSAL resource types.\n Currently no extra properties are exposed here but this\n could change in a future revision of OSAL as needed."]
2018#[repr(C)]
2019#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
2020pub struct OS_socket_prop_t {
2021    #[doc = "< @brief Name of the socket"]
2022    pub name: [libc::c_char; 20usize],
2023    #[doc = "< @brief OSAL TaskID which opened the socket"]
2024    pub creator: osal_id_t,
2025}
2026unsafe extern "C" {
2027    #[doc = " @brief Initialize a socket address structure to hold an address of the given family\n\n The address is set to a suitable default value for the family.\n\n @param[out]  Addr         The address buffer to initialize @nonnull\n @param[in]   Domain       The address family\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if Addr argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if the system does not implement the requested domain"]
2028    pub fn OS_SocketAddrInit(Addr: *mut OS_SockAddr_t, Domain: OS_SocketDomain_t) -> int32;
2029}
2030unsafe extern "C" {
2031    #[doc = " @brief Get a string representation of a network host address\n\n The specific format of the output string depends on the address family.\n\n This string should be suitable to pass back into OS_SocketAddrFromString()\n which should recreate the same network address, and it should also\n be meaningful to a user of printed or logged as a C string.\n\n @note For IPv4, this would typically be the dotted-decimal format (X.X.X.X).\n\n @param[out]  buffer       Buffer to hold the output string @nonnull\n @param[in]   buflen       Maximum length of the output string @nonzero\n @param[in]   Addr         The network address buffer to convert @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_SIZE if passed-in buflen is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the address cannot be converted to string, or string buffer too small"]
2032    pub fn OS_SocketAddrToString(
2033        buffer: *mut libc::c_char,
2034        buflen: usize,
2035        Addr: *const OS_SockAddr_t,
2036    ) -> int32;
2037}
2038unsafe extern "C" {
2039    #[doc = " @brief Set a network host address from a string representation\n\n The specific format of the output string depends on the address family.\n\n The address structure should have been previously initialized using\n OS_SocketAddrInit() to set the address family type.\n\n @note For IPv4, this would typically be the dotted-decimal format (X.X.X.X).\n It is up to the discretion of the underlying implementation whether\n to accept hostnames, as this depends on the availability of DNS services.\n Since many embedded deployments do not have name services, this should\n not be relied upon.\n\n @param[out]  Addr         The address buffer to initialize @nonnull\n @param[in]   string       The string to initialize the address from @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the string cannot be converted to an address"]
2040    pub fn OS_SocketAddrFromString(Addr: *mut OS_SockAddr_t, string: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
2041}
2042unsafe extern "C" {
2043    #[doc = " @brief Get the port number of a network address\n\n For network protocols that have the concept of a port number (such\n as TCP/IP and UDP/IP) this function gets the port number from the\n address structure.\n\n @param[out]  PortNum      Buffer to store the port number @nonnull\n @param[in]   Addr         The network address buffer @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_BAD_ADDRESS if the address domain is not compatible"]
2044    pub fn OS_SocketAddrGetPort(PortNum: *mut uint16, Addr: *const OS_SockAddr_t) -> int32;
2045}
2046unsafe extern "C" {
2047    #[doc = " @brief Set the port number of a network address\n\n For network protocols that have the concept of a port number (such\n as TCP/IP and UDP/IP) this function sets the port number from the\n address structure.\n\n @param[out]  Addr         The network address buffer @nonnull\n @param[in]   PortNum      The port number to set\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_BAD_ADDRESS if the address domain is not compatible"]
2048    pub fn OS_SocketAddrSetPort(Addr: *mut OS_SockAddr_t, PortNum: uint16) -> int32;
2049}
2050unsafe extern "C" {
2051    #[doc = " @brief Opens a socket.\n\n A new, unconnected and unbound socket is allocated of the given domain and type.\n\n @param[out]  sock_id  Buffer to hold the non-zero OSAL ID @nonnull\n @param[in]   Domain   The domain / address family of the socket (INET or INET6, etc)\n @param[in]   Type     The type of the socket (STREAM or DATAGRAM)\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if the system does not implement the requested socket/address domain"]
2052    pub fn OS_SocketOpen(
2053        sock_id: *mut osal_id_t,
2054        Domain: OS_SocketDomain_t,
2055        Type: OS_SocketType_t,
2056    ) -> int32;
2057}
2058unsafe extern "C" {
2059    #[doc = " @brief Binds a socket to a given local address and enter listening (server) mode.\n\n This is a convenience/compatibility routine to perform both OS_SocketBindAddress() and\n OS_SocketListen() operations in a single call, intended to simplify the setup for a server\n role.\n\n If the socket is connectionless, then it only binds to the local address.\n\n @param[in]   sock_id  The socket ID\n @param[in]   Addr     The local address to bind to @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the sock_id parameter is not valid\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if the socket is already bound\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_TYPE if the handle is not a socket"]
2060    pub fn OS_SocketBind(sock_id: osal_id_t, Addr: *const OS_SockAddr_t) -> int32;
2061}
2062unsafe extern "C" {
2063    #[doc = " @brief Places the specified socket into a listening state.\n\n This function only applies to connection-oriented (stream) sockets that are intended\n to be used in a server-side role. This places the socket into a state where it can\n accept incoming connections from clients.\n\n @param[in]   sock_id  The socket ID\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the sock_id parameter is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if the socket is already listening\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_TYPE if the handle is not a stream socket"]
2064    pub fn OS_SocketListen(sock_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
2065}
2066unsafe extern "C" {
2067    #[doc = " @brief Binds a socket to a given local address.\n\n The specified socket will be bound to the local address and port, if available.\n This controls the source address reflected in network traffic transmitted via this\n socket.\n\n After binding to the address, a stream socket may be followed by a call to either\n OS_SocketListen() for a server role or to OS_SocketConnect() for a client role.\n\n @param[in]   sock_id  The socket ID\n @param[in]   Addr     The local address to bind to @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the sock_id parameter is not valid\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if the socket is already bound\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_TYPE if the handle is not a socket"]
2068    pub fn OS_SocketBindAddress(sock_id: osal_id_t, Addr: *const OS_SockAddr_t) -> int32;
2069}
2070unsafe extern "C" {
2071    #[doc = " @brief Connects a socket to a given remote address.\n\n The socket will be connected to the remote address and port, if available.\n This only applies to stream-oriented sockets.  Calling this on a datagram\n socket will return an error (these sockets should use SendTo/RecvFrom).\n\n This API is identical to OS_SocketConnect() except for the timeout parameter.  In\n this call, timeout is expressed as an absolute value of the OS clock, in the same\n time domain as obtained via OS_GetLocalTime().  This allows for a more precise\n timeout than what is possible via the normal OS_SocketConnect().\n\n @param[in]   sock_id     The socket ID\n @param[in]   Addr        The remote address to connect to @nonnull\n @param[in]   abs_timeout The absolute time that the call may block until\n\n @sa OS_SocketConnect()\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if the socket is already connected\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the sock_id parameter is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_TYPE if the handle is not a socket\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if Addr argument is NULL"]
2072    pub fn OS_SocketConnectAbs(
2073        sock_id: osal_id_t,
2074        Addr: *const OS_SockAddr_t,
2075        abs_timeout: OS_time_t,
2076    ) -> int32;
2077}
2078unsafe extern "C" {
2079    #[doc = " @brief Connects a socket to a given remote address.\n\n The socket will be connected to the remote address and port, if available.\n This only applies to stream-oriented sockets.  Calling this on a datagram\n socket will return an error (these sockets should use SendTo/RecvFrom).\n\n The timeout is expressed in milliseconds, relative to the time that the API was\n invoked.  Use OS_SocketConnectAbs() for higher timing precision.\n\n @param[in]   sock_id  The socket ID\n @param[in]   Addr     The remote address to connect to @nonnull\n @param[in]   timeout  The maximum amount of time to wait, or OS_PEND to wait forever\n\n @sa OS_SocketConnectAbs()\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if the socket is already connected\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the sock_id parameter is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_TYPE if the handle is not a socket\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if Addr argument is NULL"]
2080    pub fn OS_SocketConnect(
2081        sock_id: osal_id_t,
2082        Addr: *const OS_SockAddr_t,
2083        timeout: int32,
2084    ) -> int32;
2085}
2086unsafe extern "C" {
2087    #[doc = " @brief Implement graceful shutdown of a stream socket\n\n This can be utilized to indicate the end of data stream without immediately closing\n the socket, giving the remote side an indication that the data transfer is complete.\n\n @param[in]   sock_id  The socket ID\n @param[in]   Mode     Whether to shutdown reading, writing, or both.\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the sock_id parameter is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ARGUMENT if the Mode argument is not one of the valid options\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_TYPE if the handle is not a socket\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if the socket is not connected"]
2088    pub fn OS_SocketShutdown(sock_id: osal_id_t, Mode: OS_SocketShutdownMode_t) -> int32;
2089}
2090unsafe extern "C" {
2091    #[doc = " @brief Waits for and accept the next incoming connection on the given socket\n\n This is used for sockets operating in a \"server\" role.  The socket must be\n a stream type (connection-oriented) and previously bound to a local address\n using OS_SocketBind().  This will block the caller up to the given timeout\n or until an incoming connection request occurs, whichever happens first.\n\n The new stream connection is then returned to the caller and the original\n server socket ID can be reused for the next connection.\n\n This API is identical to OS_SocketAccept() except for the timeout parameter.  In\n this call, timeout is expressed as an absolute value of the OS clock, in the same\n time domain as obtained via OS_GetLocalTime().  This allows for a more precise\n timeout than what is possible via the normal OS_SocketAccept().\n\n @param[in]   sock_id      The server socket ID, previously bound using OS_SocketBind()\n @param[out]  connsock_id  The connection socket, a new ID that can be read/written @nonnull\n @param[in]   Addr         The remote address of the incoming connection @nonnull\n @param[in]   abs_timeout  The absolute time that the call may block until\n\n @sa OS_SocketAccept()\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the sock_id parameter is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_TYPE if the handle is not a socket\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if the socket is not bound or already connected"]
2092    pub fn OS_SocketAcceptAbs(
2093        sock_id: osal_id_t,
2094        connsock_id: *mut osal_id_t,
2095        Addr: *mut OS_SockAddr_t,
2096        abs_timeout: OS_time_t,
2097    ) -> int32;
2098}
2099unsafe extern "C" {
2100    #[doc = " @brief Waits for and accept the next incoming connection on the given socket\n\n This is used for sockets operating in a \"server\" role.  The socket must be\n a stream type (connection-oriented) and previously bound to a local address\n using OS_SocketBind().  This will block the caller up to the given timeout\n or until an incoming connection request occurs, whichever happens first.\n\n The new stream connection is then returned to the caller and the original\n server socket ID can be reused for the next connection.\n\n The timeout is expressed in milliseconds, relative to the time that the API was\n invoked.  Use OS_SocketAcceptAbs() for higher timing precision.\n\n @param[in]   sock_id      The server socket ID, previously bound using OS_SocketBind()\n @param[out]  connsock_id  The connection socket, a new ID that can be read/written @nonnull\n @param[in]   Addr         The remote address of the incoming connection @nonnull\n @param[in]   timeout      The maximum amount of time to wait, or OS_PEND to wait forever\n\n @sa OS_SocketAcceptAbs()\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the sock_id parameter is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_TYPE if the handle is not a socket\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if the socket is not bound or already connected"]
2101    pub fn OS_SocketAccept(
2102        sock_id: osal_id_t,
2103        connsock_id: *mut osal_id_t,
2104        Addr: *mut OS_SockAddr_t,
2105        timeout: int32,
2106    ) -> int32;
2107}
2108unsafe extern "C" {
2109    #[doc = " @brief Reads data from a message-oriented (datagram) socket\n\n If a message is already available on the socket, this should immediately return\n that data without blocking.  Otherwise, it may block up to the given timeout.\n\n This API is identical to OS_SocketRecvFrom() except for the timeout parameter.  In\n this call, timeout is expressed as an absolute value of the OS clock, in the same\n time domain as obtained via OS_GetLocalTime().  This allows for a more precise\n timeout than what is possible via the normal OS_SocketRecvFrom().\n\n @param[in]   sock_id      The socket ID, previously bound using OS_SocketBind()\n @param[out]  buffer       Pointer to message data receive buffer @nonnull\n @param[in]   buflen       The maximum length of the message data to receive @nonzero\n @param[out]  RemoteAddr   Buffer to store the remote network address (may be NULL)\n @param[in]   abs_timeout  The absolute time at which the call should return if nothing received\n\n @return Count of actual bytes received or error status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_SIZE if passed-in buflen is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the sock_id parameter is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_TYPE if the handle is not a socket"]
2110    pub fn OS_SocketRecvFromAbs(
2111        sock_id: osal_id_t,
2112        buffer: *mut libc::c_void,
2113        buflen: usize,
2114        RemoteAddr: *mut OS_SockAddr_t,
2115        abs_timeout: OS_time_t,
2116    ) -> int32;
2117}
2118unsafe extern "C" {
2119    #[doc = " @brief Reads data from a message-oriented (datagram) socket\n\n If a message is already available on the socket, this should immediately return\n that data without blocking.  Otherwise, it may block up to the given timeout.\n\n The timeout is expressed in milliseconds, relative to the time that the API was\n invoked.  Use OS_SocketRecvFromAbs() for higher timing precision.\n\n @param[in]   sock_id      The socket ID, previously bound using OS_SocketBind()\n @param[out]  buffer       Pointer to message data receive buffer @nonnull\n @param[in]   buflen       The maximum length of the message data to receive @nonzero\n @param[out]  RemoteAddr   Buffer to store the remote network address (may be NULL)\n @param[in]   timeout      The maximum amount of time to wait or OS_PEND to wait forever\n\n @sa OS_SocketRecvFromAbs()\n\n @return Count of actual bytes received or error status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_SIZE if passed-in buflen is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the sock_id parameter is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_TYPE if the handle is not a socket"]
2120    pub fn OS_SocketRecvFrom(
2121        sock_id: osal_id_t,
2122        buffer: *mut libc::c_void,
2123        buflen: usize,
2124        RemoteAddr: *mut OS_SockAddr_t,
2125        timeout: int32,
2126    ) -> int32;
2127}
2128unsafe extern "C" {
2129    #[doc = " @brief Sends data to a message-oriented (datagram) socket\n\n This sends data in a non-blocking mode.  If the socket is not currently able to\n queue the message, such as if its outbound buffer is full, then this returns\n an error code.\n\n @param[in]   sock_id      The socket ID, which must be of the datagram type\n @param[in]   buffer       Pointer to message data to send @nonnull\n @param[in]   buflen       The length of the message data to send @nonzero\n @param[in]   RemoteAddr   Buffer containing the remote network address to send to\n\n @return Count of actual bytes sent or error status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if argument is NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_SIZE if passed-in buflen is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the sock_id parameter is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_TYPE if the handle is not a socket"]
2130    pub fn OS_SocketSendTo(
2131        sock_id: osal_id_t,
2132        buffer: *const libc::c_void,
2133        buflen: usize,
2134        RemoteAddr: *const OS_SockAddr_t,
2135    ) -> int32;
2136}
2137unsafe extern "C" {
2138    #[doc = " @brief Gets an OSAL ID from a given name\n\n @note OSAL Sockets use generated names according to the address and type.\n\n @sa OS_SocketGetInfo()\n\n @param[out]  sock_id      Buffer to hold result @nonnull\n @param[in]   sock_name    Name of socket to find @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER is id or name are NULL pointers\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND if the name was not found in the table"]
2139    pub fn OS_SocketGetIdByName(sock_id: *mut osal_id_t, sock_name: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
2140}
2141unsafe extern "C" {
2142    #[doc = " @brief Gets information about an OSAL Socket ID\n\n OSAL Sockets use generated names according to the address and type.\n This allows applications to find the name of a given socket.\n\n @param[in]   sock_id      The socket ID\n @param[out]  sock_prop    Buffer to hold socket information @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid semaphore\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the count_prop pointer is null"]
2143    pub fn OS_SocketGetInfo(sock_id: osal_id_t, sock_prop: *mut OS_socket_prop_t) -> int32;
2144}
2145#[doc = " @brief Type to be used for OSAL task priorities.\n\n OSAL priorities are in reverse order, and range\n from 0 (highest; will preempt all other tasks) to\n 255 (lowest; will not preempt any other task)."]
2146pub type osal_priority_t = u8;
2147#[doc = " @brief Type to be used for OSAL stack pointer."]
2148pub type osal_stackptr_t = *mut libc::c_void;
2149#[doc = " @brief OSAL task properties"]
2150#[repr(C)]
2151#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
2152pub struct OS_task_prop_t {
2153    pub name: [libc::c_char; 20usize],
2154    pub creator: osal_id_t,
2155    pub stack_size: usize,
2156    pub priority: osal_priority_t,
2157}
2158pub type osal_task = libc::c_void;
2159pub type osal_task_entry = ::core::option::Option<unsafe extern "C" fn()>;
2160unsafe extern "C" {
2161    #[doc = " @brief Creates a task and starts running it.\n\n Creates a task and passes back the id of the task created. Task names must be unique;\n if the name already exists this function fails. Names cannot be NULL.\n\n Portable applications should always specify the actual stack size in the\n stack_size parameter, not 0.  This size value is not enforced/checked by OSAL,\n but is simply passed through to the RTOS for stack creation. Some RTOS\n implementations may assume 0 means a default stack size while others\n may actually create a task with no stack.\n\n Unlike stack_size, the stack_pointer is optional and can be specified as NULL.\n In that case, a stack of the requested size will be dynamically allocated from\n the system heap.\n\n @param[out]  task_id will be set to the non-zero ID of the newly-created resource @nonnull\n @param[in]   task_name the name of the new resource to create @nonnull\n @param[in]   function_pointer the entry point of the new task @nonnull\n @param[in]   stack_pointer pointer to the stack for the task, or NULL\n              to allocate a stack from the system memory heap\n @param[in]   stack_size the size of the stack @nonzero\n @param[in]   priority initial priority of the new task\n @param[in]   flags initial options for the new task\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if any of the necessary pointers are NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_SIZE if the stack_size argument is zero\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_PRIORITY if the priority is bad @covtest\n @retval #OS_ERR_NO_FREE_IDS if there can be no more tasks created\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TAKEN if the name specified is already used by a task\n @retval #OS_ERROR if an unspecified/other error occurs @covtest"]
2162    pub fn OS_TaskCreate(
2163        task_id: *mut osal_id_t,
2164        task_name: *const libc::c_char,
2165        function_pointer: osal_task_entry,
2166        stack_pointer: osal_stackptr_t,
2167        stack_size: usize,
2168        priority: osal_priority_t,
2169        flags: uint32,
2170    ) -> int32;
2171}
2172unsafe extern "C" {
2173    #[doc = " @brief Deletes the specified Task\n\n The task will be removed from the local tables. and the OS will\n be configured to stop executing the task at the next opportunity.\n\n @param[in] task_id The object ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the ID given to it is invalid\n @retval #OS_ERROR if the OS delete call fails @covtest"]
2174    pub fn OS_TaskDelete(task_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
2175}
2176unsafe extern "C" {
2177    #[doc = " @brief Exits the calling task\n\n The calling thread is terminated.  This function does not return."]
2178    pub fn OS_TaskExit();
2179}
2180unsafe extern "C" {
2181    #[doc = " @brief Installs a handler for when the task is deleted.\n\n This function is used to install a callback that is called when the task is deleted.\n The callback is called when OS_TaskDelete is called with the task ID. A task delete\n handler is useful for cleaning up resources that a task creates, before the task is\n removed from the system.\n\n @param[in]   function_pointer function to be called when task exits\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the calling context is not an OSAL task"]
2182    pub fn OS_TaskInstallDeleteHandler(function_pointer: osal_task_entry) -> int32;
2183}
2184unsafe extern "C" {
2185    #[doc = " @brief Delay a task for specified amount of milliseconds\n\n Causes the current thread to be suspended from execution for the period of millisecond.\n This is a scheduled wait (clock_nanosleep/rtems_task_wake_after/taskDelay), not a \"busy\" wait.\n\n @param[in]   millisecond    Amount of time to delay\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERROR if an unspecified/other error occurs @covtest"]
2186    pub fn OS_TaskDelay(millisecond: uint32) -> int32;
2187}
2188unsafe extern "C" {
2189    #[doc = " @brief Sets the given task to a new priority\n\n @param[in] task_id        The object ID to operate on\n @param[in] new_priority   Set the new priority\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the ID passed to it is invalid\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_PRIORITY if the priority is greater than the max allowed @covtest\n @retval #OS_ERROR if an unspecified/other error occurs @covtest"]
2190    pub fn OS_TaskSetPriority(task_id: osal_id_t, new_priority: osal_priority_t) -> int32;
2191}
2192unsafe extern "C" {
2193    #[doc = " @brief Obtain the task id of the calling task\n\n This function returns the task id of the calling task\n\n @return Task ID, or zero if the operation failed (zero is never a valid task ID)"]
2194    pub fn OS_TaskGetId() -> osal_id_t;
2195}
2196unsafe extern "C" {
2197    #[doc = " @brief Find an existing task ID by name\n\n This function tries to find a task Id given the name of a task\n\n @param[out]  task_id will be set to the ID of the existing resource\n @param[in]   task_name the name of the existing resource to find @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the pointers passed in are NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND if the name wasn't found in the table"]
2198    pub fn OS_TaskGetIdByName(task_id: *mut osal_id_t, task_name: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
2199}
2200unsafe extern "C" {
2201    #[doc = " @brief Fill a property object buffer with details regarding the resource\n\n This function will pass back a pointer to structure that contains\n all of the relevant info (creator, stack size, priority, name) about the\n specified task.\n\n @param[in]   task_id The object ID to operate on\n @param[out]  task_prop The property object buffer to fill @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the ID passed to it is invalid\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the task_prop pointer is NULL"]
2202    pub fn OS_TaskGetInfo(task_id: osal_id_t, task_prop: *mut OS_task_prop_t) -> int32;
2203}
2204unsafe extern "C" {
2205    #[doc = " @brief Reverse-lookup the OSAL task ID from an operating system ID\n\n This provides a method by which an external entity may find the OSAL task\n ID corresponding to a system-defined identifier (e.g. TASK_ID, pthread_t, rtems_id, etc).\n\n Normally OSAL does not expose the underlying OS-specific values to the application,\n but in some circumstances, such as exception handling, the OS may provide this information\n directly to a BSP handler outside of the normal OSAL API.\n\n @param[out]  task_id         The buffer where the task id output is stored @nonnull\n @param[in]   sysdata         Pointer to the system-provided identification data\n @param[in]   sysdata_size    Size of the system-provided identification data\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS @covtest\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if a pointer argument is NULL"]
2206    pub fn OS_TaskFindIdBySystemData(
2207        task_id: *mut osal_id_t,
2208        sysdata: *const libc::c_void,
2209        sysdata_size: usize,
2210    ) -> int32;
2211}
2212pub type OS_TimerSync_t =
2213    ::core::option::Option<unsafe extern "C" fn(timer_id: osal_id_t) -> uint32>;
2214#[doc = " @brief Time base properties"]
2215#[repr(C)]
2216#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
2217pub struct OS_timebase_prop_t {
2218    pub name: [libc::c_char; 20usize],
2219    pub creator: osal_id_t,
2220    pub nominal_interval_time: uint32,
2221    pub freerun_time: uint32,
2222    pub accuracy: uint32,
2223}
2224unsafe extern "C" {
2225    #[doc = " @brief Create an abstract Time Base resource\n\n An OSAL time base is an abstraction of a \"timer tick\" that can, in turn, be\n used for measurement of elapsed time between events.\n\n Time bases can be simulated by the operating system using the OS kernel-provided\n timing facilities, or based on a hardware timing source if provided by the BSP.\n\n A time base object has a servicing task associated with it, that runs at elevated\n priority and will thereby interrupt user-level tasks when timing ticks occur.\n\n If the external_sync function is passed as NULL, the operating system kernel\n timing resources will be utilized for a simulated timer tick.\n\n If the external_sync function is not NULL, this should point to a BSP-provided\n function that will block the calling task until the next tick occurs.  This\n can be used for synchronizing with hardware events.\n\n @note When provisioning a tunable RTOS kernel, such as RTEMS, the kernel should\n be configured to support at least (OS_MAX_TASKS + OS_MAX_TIMEBASES) threads,\n to account for the helper threads associated with time base objects.\n\n @note This configuration API must not be used from the context of a timer callback.\n Timers should only be configured from the context of normal OSAL tasks.\n\n @param[out]  timebase_id     will be set to the non-zero ID of the newly-created resource @nonnull\n @param[in]   timebase_name   The name of the time base @nonnull\n @param[in]   external_sync   A synchronization function for BSP hardware-based timer ticks\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TAKEN if the name specified is already used\n @retval #OS_ERR_NO_FREE_IDS if there can be no more timebase resources created\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if called from timer/timebase context\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG if the timebase_name is too long\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if a pointer argument is NULL"]
2226    pub fn OS_TimeBaseCreate(
2227        timebase_id: *mut osal_id_t,
2228        timebase_name: *const libc::c_char,
2229        external_sync: OS_TimerSync_t,
2230    ) -> int32;
2231}
2232unsafe extern "C" {
2233    #[doc = " @brief Sets the tick period for simulated time base objects\n\n This sets the actual tick period for timing ticks that are\n simulated by the RTOS kernel (i.e. the \"external_sync\" parameter\n on the call to OS_TimeBaseCreate() is NULL).\n\n The RTOS will be configured to wake up the helper thread at the\n requested interval.\n\n This function has no effect for time bases that are using\n a BSP-provided external_sync function.\n\n @note This configuration API must not be used from the context of a timer callback.\n Timers should only be configured from the context of normal OSAL tasks.\n\n @param[in]   timebase_id     The timebase resource to configure\n @param[in]   start_time      The amount of delay for the first tick, in microseconds.\n @param[in]   interval_time   The amount of delay between ticks, in microseconds.\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid timebase\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if called from timer/timebase context\n @retval #OS_TIMER_ERR_INVALID_ARGS if start_time or interval_time are out of range"]
2234    pub fn OS_TimeBaseSet(
2235        timebase_id: osal_id_t,
2236        start_time: uint32,
2237        interval_time: uint32,
2238    ) -> int32;
2239}
2240unsafe extern "C" {
2241    #[doc = " @brief Deletes a time base object\n\n The helper task and any other resources associated with the time base\n abstraction will be freed.\n\n @note This configuration API must not be used from the context of a timer callback.\n Timers should only be configured from the context of normal OSAL tasks.\n\n @param[in]   timebase_id     The timebase resource to delete\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid timebase\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if called from timer/timebase context"]
2242    pub fn OS_TimeBaseDelete(timebase_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
2243}
2244unsafe extern "C" {
2245    #[doc = " @brief Find the ID of an existing time base resource\n\n Given a time base name, find and output the ID associated with it.\n\n @note This configuration API must not be used from the context of a timer callback.\n Timers should only be configured from the context of normal OSAL tasks.\n\n @param[out]  timebase_id     will be set to the non-zero ID of the matching resource @nonnull\n @param[in]   timebase_name   The name of the timebase resource to find @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if timebase_id or timebase_name are NULL pointers\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND if the name was not found in the table\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if called from timer/timebase context"]
2246    pub fn OS_TimeBaseGetIdByName(
2247        timebase_id: *mut osal_id_t,
2248        timebase_name: *const libc::c_char,
2249    ) -> int32;
2250}
2251unsafe extern "C" {
2252    #[doc = " @brief Obtain information about a timebase resource\n\n Fills the buffer referred to by the timebase_prop parameter with\n relevant information about the time base resource.\n\n This function will pass back a pointer to structure that contains\n all of the relevant info( name and creator) about the specified timebase.\n\n @note This configuration API must not be used from the context of a timer callback.\n Timers should only be configured from the context of normal OSAL tasks.\n\n @param[in]   timebase_id     The timebase resource ID\n @param[out]  timebase_prop   Buffer to store timebase properties @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid timebase\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the timebase_prop pointer is null\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if called from timer/timebase context"]
2253    pub fn OS_TimeBaseGetInfo(
2254        timebase_id: osal_id_t,
2255        timebase_prop: *mut OS_timebase_prop_t,
2256    ) -> int32;
2257}
2258unsafe extern "C" {
2259    #[doc = " @brief  Read the value of the timebase free run counter\n\n Poll the timer free-running time counter in a lightweight fashion.\n\n The free run count is a monotonically increasing value reflecting the\n total time elapsed since the timebase inception.  Units are the\n same as the timebase itself, usually microseconds.\n\n Applications may quickly and efficiently calculate relative time\n differences by polling this value and subtracting the previous\n counter value.\n\n The absolute value of this counter is not relevant, because it\n will \"roll over\" after 2^32 units of time.  For a timebase with\n microsecond units, this occurs approximately every 4294 seconds,\n or about 1.2 hours.\n\n @note To ensure consistency of results, the application should\n sample the value at a minimum of two times the roll over frequency,\n and calculate the difference between the consecutive samples.\n\n @param[in]   timebase_id The timebase to operate on\n @param[out]  freerun_val Buffer to store the free run counter @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid timebase\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if pointer argument is NULL"]
2260    pub fn OS_TimeBaseGetFreeRun(timebase_id: osal_id_t, freerun_val: *mut uint32) -> int32;
2261}
2262pub type OS_TimerCallback_t = ::core::option::Option<unsafe extern "C" fn(timer_id: osal_id_t)>;
2263#[doc = " @brief Timer properties"]
2264#[repr(C)]
2265#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
2266pub struct OS_timer_prop_t {
2267    pub name: [libc::c_char; 20usize],
2268    pub creator: osal_id_t,
2269    pub start_time: uint32,
2270    pub interval_time: uint32,
2271    pub accuracy: uint32,
2272}
2273unsafe extern "C" {
2274    #[doc = " @brief Create a timer object\n\n A timer object is a resource that invokes the specified application-provided function\n upon timer expiration.  Timers may be one-shot or periodic in nature.\n\n This function creates a dedicated (hidden) time base object to service this timer,\n which is created and deleted with the timer object itself.  The internal time base\n is configured for an OS simulated timer tick at the same interval as the timer.\n\n The callback function should be declared according to the OS_TimerCallback_t\n function pointer type.  The timer_id value is passed to the callback function.\n\n @note clock_accuracy comes from the underlying OS tick value. The nearest integer\n       microsecond value is returned, so may not be exact.\n\n @note This configuration API must not be used from the context of a timer callback.\n Timers should only be configured from the context of normal OSAL tasks.\n\n @sa OS_TimerCallback_t\n\n @param[out]  timer_id        Will be set to the non-zero resource ID of the timer object @nonnull\n @param[in]   timer_name      Name of the timer object @nonnull\n @param[out]  clock_accuracy  Expected precision of the timer, in microseconds. This\n                              is the underlying tick value rounded to the nearest\n                              microsecond integer. @nonnull\n @param[in]   callback_ptr    The function pointer of the timer callback @nonnull.\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if any parameters are NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TAKEN if the name is already in use by another timer.\n @retval #OS_ERR_NO_FREE_IDS if all of the timers are already allocated.\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if invoked from a timer context\n @retval #OS_TIMER_ERR_INTERNAL if there was an error programming the OS timer @covtest"]
2275    pub fn OS_TimerCreate(
2276        timer_id: *mut osal_id_t,
2277        timer_name: *const libc::c_char,
2278        clock_accuracy: *mut uint32,
2279        callback_ptr: OS_TimerCallback_t,
2280    ) -> int32;
2281}
2282unsafe extern "C" {
2283    #[doc = " @brief Add a timer object based on an existing TimeBase resource\n\n A timer object is a resource that invokes the specified application-provided function\n upon timer expiration.  Timers may be one-shot or periodic in nature.\n\n This function uses an existing time base object to service this timer, which must\n exist prior to adding the timer.  The precision of the timer is the same\n as that of the underlying time base object. Multiple timer objects can be\n created referring to a single time base object.\n\n This routine also uses a different callback function prototype from OS_TimerCreate(),\n allowing a single opaque argument to be passed to the callback routine.\n The OSAL implementation does not use this parameter, and may be set NULL.\n\n The callback function for this method should be declared according to the\n OS_ArgCallback_t function pointer type.  The timer_id is passed in to the\n function by the OSAL, and the arg parameter is passed through from the\n callback_arg argument on this call.\n\n @note This configuration API must not be used from the context of a timer callback.\n Timers should only be configured from the context of normal OSAL tasks.\n\n @sa OS_ArgCallback_t\n\n @param[out]  timer_id        Will be set to the non-zero resource ID of the timer object @nonnull\n @param[in]   timer_name      Name of the timer object @nonnull\n @param[in]   timebase_id     The time base resource to use as a reference\n @param[in]   callback_ptr    Application-provided function to invoke @nonnull\n @param[in]   callback_arg    Opaque argument to pass to callback function, may be NULL\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if any parameters are NULL\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the timebase_id parameter is not valid\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TAKEN if the name is already in use by another timer.\n @retval #OS_ERR_NO_FREE_IDS if all of the timers are already allocated.\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if invoked from a timer context\n @retval #OS_TIMER_ERR_INTERNAL if there was an error programming the OS timer @covtest"]
2284    pub fn OS_TimerAdd(
2285        timer_id: *mut osal_id_t,
2286        timer_name: *const libc::c_char,
2287        timebase_id: osal_id_t,
2288        callback_ptr: OS_ArgCallback_t,
2289        callback_arg: *mut libc::c_void,
2290    ) -> int32;
2291}
2292unsafe extern "C" {
2293    #[doc = " @brief Configures a periodic or one shot timer\n\n This function programs the timer with a start time and an optional interval time.\n The start time is the time in microseconds when the user callback function will be\n called. If the interval time is non-zero, the timer will be reprogrammed with that\n interval in microseconds to call the user callback function periodically. If the start\n time and interval time are zero, the function will return an error.\n\n For a \"one-shot\" timer, the start_time configures the\n expiration time, and the interval_time should be passed as\n zero to indicate the timer is not to be automatically reset.\n\n @note The resolution of the times specified is limited to the clock accuracy\n       returned in the OS_TimerCreate call. If the times specified in the start_msec\n       or interval_msec parameters are less than the accuracy, they will be rounded\n       up to the accuracy of the timer.\n\n @note This configuration API must not be used from the context of a timer callback.\n Timers should only be configured from the context of normal OSAL tasks.\n\n @param[in] timer_id      The timer ID to operate on\n @param[in] start_time    Time in microseconds to the first expiration\n @param[in] interval_time Time in microseconds between subsequent intervals, value\n                          of zero will only call the user callback function once\n                          after the start_msec time.\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the timer_id is not valid.\n @retval #OS_TIMER_ERR_INTERNAL if there was an error programming the OS timer @covtest\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if called from timer/timebase context\n @retval #OS_TIMER_ERR_INVALID_ARGS if the start_time or interval_time is out of range, or both 0"]
2294    pub fn OS_TimerSet(timer_id: osal_id_t, start_time: uint32, interval_time: uint32) -> int32;
2295}
2296unsafe extern "C" {
2297    #[doc = " @brief Deletes a timer resource\n\n The application callback associated with the timer will be stopped,\n and the resources freed for future use.\n\n @note This configuration API must not be used from the context of a timer callback.\n Timers should only be configured from the context of normal OSAL tasks.\n\n @param[in] timer_id      The timer ID to operate on\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the timer_id is invalid.\n @retval #OS_TIMER_ERR_INTERNAL if there was a problem deleting the timer in the host OS @covtest\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if called from timer/timebase context"]
2298    pub fn OS_TimerDelete(timer_id: osal_id_t) -> int32;
2299}
2300unsafe extern "C" {
2301    #[doc = " @brief Locate an existing timer resource by name\n\n Outputs the ID associated with the given timer, if it exists.\n\n @note This configuration API must not be used from the context of a timer callback.\n Timers should only be configured from the context of normal OSAL tasks.\n\n @param[out] timer_id      Will be set to the timer ID corresponding to the name @nonnull\n @param[in]  timer_name    The timer name to find @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if timer_id or timer_name are NULL pointers\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_TOO_LONG name length including null terminator greater than #OS_MAX_API_NAME\n @retval #OS_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND if the name was not found in the table\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if called from timer/timebase context"]
2302    pub fn OS_TimerGetIdByName(timer_id: *mut osal_id_t, timer_name: *const libc::c_char) -> int32;
2303}
2304unsafe extern "C" {
2305    #[doc = " @brief Gets information about an existing timer\n\n This function takes timer_id, and looks it up in the OS table. It puts all of the\n information known about that timer into a structure pointer to by timer_prop.\n\n @note This configuration API must not be used from the context of a timer callback.\n Timers should only be configured from the context of normal OSAL tasks.\n\n @param[in]  timer_id      The timer ID to operate on\n @param[out] timer_prop    Buffer containing timer properties @nonnull\n                           - creator: the OS task ID of the task that created this timer\n                           - name: the string name of the timer\n                           - start_time: the start time in microseconds, if any\n                           - interval_time: the interval time in microseconds, if any\n                           - accuracy: the accuracy of the timer in microseconds\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref OSReturnCodes\n @retval #OS_SUCCESS @copybrief OS_SUCCESS\n @retval #OS_ERR_INVALID_ID if the id passed in is not a valid timer\n @retval #OS_INVALID_POINTER if the timer_prop pointer is null\n @retval #OS_ERR_INCORRECT_OBJ_STATE if called from timer/timebase context"]
2306    pub fn OS_TimerGetInfo(timer_id: osal_id_t, timer_prop: *mut OS_timer_prop_t) -> int32;
2307}
2308unsafe extern "C" {
2309    #[doc = " @defgroup OSAPIBsp OSAL BSP low level access APIs\n\n These are for OSAL internal BSP information access to pass any BSP-specific\n boot/command line/startup arguments through to the application, and return a\n status code back to the OS after exit.\n\n Not intended for user application use\n @{"]
2310    pub fn OS_BSP_SetResourceTypeConfig(ResourceType: uint32, ConfigOptionValue: uint32);
2311}
2312unsafe extern "C" {
2313    pub fn OS_BSP_GetResourceTypeConfig(ResourceType: uint32) -> uint32;
2314}
2315unsafe extern "C" {
2316    pub fn OS_BSP_GetArgC() -> uint32;
2317}
2318unsafe extern "C" {
2319    pub fn OS_BSP_GetArgV() -> *const *mut libc::c_char;
2320}
2321unsafe extern "C" {
2322    pub fn OS_BSP_SetExitCode(code: int32);
2323}
2324#[doc = " \\brief cFE Status type for readability and eventually type safety"]
2325pub type CFE_Status_t = int32;
2326#[doc = " @brief For the @ref CFE_ES_StatusToString() function, to ensure\n everyone is making an array of the same length."]
2327pub type CFE_StatusString_t = [libc::c_char; 11usize];
2328unsafe extern "C" {
2329    #[doc = " @brief Convert status to a string\n\n @param[in] status Status value to convert\n @param[out] status_string Buffer to store status converted to string\n\n @return Passed in string pointer"]
2330    pub fn CFE_ES_StatusToString(
2331        status: CFE_Status_t,
2332        status_string: *mut CFE_StatusString_t,
2333    ) -> *mut libc::c_char;
2334}
2335#[doc = " @brief A type that provides a common, abstract identifier for\n all ES managed resources (e.g. apps, tasks, counters, etc).\n\n Fundamentally an unsigned integer but users should treat it as\n opaque, and only go through the ES API for introspection."]
2336pub type CFE_ResourceId_t = uint32;
2337#[doc = " @brief Overwrite Log Mode"]
2338pub const CFE_ES_LogMode_CFE_ES_LogMode_OVERWRITE: CFE_ES_LogMode = 0;
2339#[doc = " @brief Discard Log Mode"]
2340pub const CFE_ES_LogMode_CFE_ES_LogMode_DISCARD: CFE_ES_LogMode = 1;
2341#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_ES_LogMode_Enum_t"]
2342pub type CFE_ES_LogMode = libc::c_uint;
2343#[doc = " @brief Identifies handling of log messages after storage is filled\n\n @sa enum CFE_ES_LogMode"]
2344pub type CFE_ES_LogMode_Enum_t = uint8;
2345#[doc = " @brief Restart application if exception occurs"]
2346pub const CFE_ES_ExceptionAction_CFE_ES_ExceptionAction_RESTART_APP: CFE_ES_ExceptionAction = 0;
2347#[doc = " @brief Restart processor if exception occurs"]
2348pub const CFE_ES_ExceptionAction_CFE_ES_ExceptionAction_PROC_RESTART: CFE_ES_ExceptionAction = 1;
2349#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_ES_ExceptionAction_Enum_t"]
2350pub type CFE_ES_ExceptionAction = libc::c_uint;
2351#[doc = " @brief Identifies action to take if exception occurs\n\n @sa enum CFE_ES_ExceptionAction"]
2352pub type CFE_ES_ExceptionAction_Enum_t = uint8;
2353#[doc = " @brief CFE core application"]
2354pub const CFE_ES_AppType_CFE_ES_AppType_CORE: CFE_ES_AppType = 1;
2355#[doc = " @brief CFE external application"]
2356pub const CFE_ES_AppType_CFE_ES_AppType_EXTERNAL: CFE_ES_AppType = 2;
2357#[doc = " @brief CFE library"]
2358pub const CFE_ES_AppType_CFE_ES_AppType_LIBRARY: CFE_ES_AppType = 3;
2359#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_ES_AppType_Enum_t"]
2360pub type CFE_ES_AppType = libc::c_uint;
2361#[doc = " @brief Identifies type of CFE application\n\n @sa enum CFE_ES_AppType"]
2362pub type CFE_ES_AppType_Enum_t = uint8;
2363#[doc = " @brief Reserved value, should not be used"]
2364pub const CFE_ES_RunStatus_CFE_ES_RunStatus_UNDEFINED: CFE_ES_RunStatus = 0;
2365#[doc = " @brief Indicates that the Application should continue to run"]
2366pub const CFE_ES_RunStatus_CFE_ES_RunStatus_APP_RUN: CFE_ES_RunStatus = 1;
2367#[doc = " @brief Indicates that the Application wants to exit normally"]
2368pub const CFE_ES_RunStatus_CFE_ES_RunStatus_APP_EXIT: CFE_ES_RunStatus = 2;
2369#[doc = " @brief Indicates that the Application is quitting with an error"]
2370pub const CFE_ES_RunStatus_CFE_ES_RunStatus_APP_ERROR: CFE_ES_RunStatus = 3;
2371#[doc = " @brief The cFE App caused an exception"]
2372pub const CFE_ES_RunStatus_CFE_ES_RunStatus_SYS_EXCEPTION: CFE_ES_RunStatus = 4;
2373#[doc = " @brief The system is requesting a restart of the cFE App"]
2374pub const CFE_ES_RunStatus_CFE_ES_RunStatus_SYS_RESTART: CFE_ES_RunStatus = 5;
2375#[doc = " @brief The system is requesting a reload of the cFE App"]
2376pub const CFE_ES_RunStatus_CFE_ES_RunStatus_SYS_RELOAD: CFE_ES_RunStatus = 6;
2377#[doc = " @brief The system is requesting that the cFE App is stopped"]
2378pub const CFE_ES_RunStatus_CFE_ES_RunStatus_SYS_DELETE: CFE_ES_RunStatus = 7;
2379#[doc = " @brief Indicates that the Core Application could not Init"]
2380pub const CFE_ES_RunStatus_CFE_ES_RunStatus_CORE_APP_INIT_ERROR: CFE_ES_RunStatus = 8;
2381#[doc = " @brief Indicates that the Core Application had a runtime failure"]
2382pub const CFE_ES_RunStatus_CFE_ES_RunStatus_CORE_APP_RUNTIME_ERROR: CFE_ES_RunStatus = 9;
2383#[doc = " @brief Reserved value, marker for the maximum state"]
2384pub const CFE_ES_RunStatus_CFE_ES_RunStatus_MAX: CFE_ES_RunStatus = 10;
2385#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_ES_RunStatus_Enum_t"]
2386pub type CFE_ES_RunStatus = libc::c_uint;
2387#[doc = " @brief Run Status and Exit Status identifiers\n\n @sa enum CFE_ES_RunStatus"]
2388pub type CFE_ES_RunStatus_Enum_t = uint32;
2389#[doc = " @brief reserved"]
2390pub const CFE_ES_SystemState_CFE_ES_SystemState_UNDEFINED: CFE_ES_SystemState = 0;
2391#[doc = " @brief single threaded mode while setting up CFE itself"]
2392pub const CFE_ES_SystemState_CFE_ES_SystemState_EARLY_INIT: CFE_ES_SystemState = 1;
2393#[doc = " @brief core apps (CFE_ES_ObjectTable) are starting (multi-threaded)"]
2394pub const CFE_ES_SystemState_CFE_ES_SystemState_CORE_STARTUP: CFE_ES_SystemState = 2;
2395#[doc = " @brief core is ready, starting other external apps/libraries (if any)"]
2396pub const CFE_ES_SystemState_CFE_ES_SystemState_CORE_READY: CFE_ES_SystemState = 3;
2397#[doc = " @brief startup apps have all completed their early init, but not necessarily operational yet"]
2398pub const CFE_ES_SystemState_CFE_ES_SystemState_APPS_INIT: CFE_ES_SystemState = 4;
2399#[doc = " @brief normal operation mode; all apps are RUNNING"]
2400pub const CFE_ES_SystemState_CFE_ES_SystemState_OPERATIONAL: CFE_ES_SystemState = 5;
2401#[doc = " @brief reserved for future use, all apps would be STOPPED"]
2402pub const CFE_ES_SystemState_CFE_ES_SystemState_SHUTDOWN: CFE_ES_SystemState = 6;
2403#[doc = " @brief Reserved value, marker for the maximum state"]
2404pub const CFE_ES_SystemState_CFE_ES_SystemState_MAX: CFE_ES_SystemState = 7;
2405#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_ES_SystemState_Enum_t"]
2406pub type CFE_ES_SystemState = libc::c_uint;
2407#[doc = " @brief The overall cFE System State\n\n These values are used with the #CFE_ES_WaitForSystemState API call to synchronize application startup.\n\n @note These are defined in order so that relational comparisons e.g. if (STATEA < STATEB) are possible\n\n @sa enum CFE_ES_SystemState"]
2408pub type CFE_ES_SystemState_Enum_t = uint32;
2409#[doc = " @brief Log entry from a core subsystem"]
2410pub const CFE_ES_LogEntryType_CFE_ES_LogEntryType_CORE: CFE_ES_LogEntryType = 1;
2411#[doc = " @brief Log entry from an application"]
2412pub const CFE_ES_LogEntryType_CFE_ES_LogEntryType_APPLICATION: CFE_ES_LogEntryType = 2;
2413#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_ES_LogEntryType_Enum_t"]
2414pub type CFE_ES_LogEntryType = libc::c_uint;
2415#[doc = " @brief Type of entry in the Error and Reset (ER) Log\n\n @sa enum CFE_ES_LogEntryType"]
2416pub type CFE_ES_LogEntryType_Enum_t = uint8;
2417#[doc = " @brief Initial state before app thread is started"]
2418pub const CFE_ES_AppState_CFE_ES_AppState_UNDEFINED: CFE_ES_AppState = 0;
2419#[doc = " @brief App thread has started, app performing early initialization of its own data"]
2420pub const CFE_ES_AppState_CFE_ES_AppState_EARLY_INIT: CFE_ES_AppState = 1;
2421#[doc = " @brief Early/Local initialization is complete.  First sync point."]
2422pub const CFE_ES_AppState_CFE_ES_AppState_LATE_INIT: CFE_ES_AppState = 2;
2423#[doc = " @brief All initialization is complete.  Second sync point."]
2424pub const CFE_ES_AppState_CFE_ES_AppState_RUNNING: CFE_ES_AppState = 3;
2425#[doc = " @brief Application is waiting on a Restart/Reload/Delete request"]
2426pub const CFE_ES_AppState_CFE_ES_AppState_WAITING: CFE_ES_AppState = 4;
2427#[doc = " @brief Application is stopped"]
2428pub const CFE_ES_AppState_CFE_ES_AppState_STOPPED: CFE_ES_AppState = 5;
2429#[doc = " @brief Reserved entry, marker for the maximum state"]
2430pub const CFE_ES_AppState_CFE_ES_AppState_MAX: CFE_ES_AppState = 6;
2431#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_ES_AppState_Enum_t"]
2432pub type CFE_ES_AppState = libc::c_uint;
2433#[doc = " @brief Application Run State\n\n The normal progression of APP states:\n UNDEFINED -> EARLY_INIT -> LATE_INIT -> RUNNING -> WAITING -> STOPPED\n\n @note These are defined in order so that relational comparisons e.g. if (STATEA < STATEB) are possible\n\n @sa enum CFE_ES_AppState"]
2434pub type CFE_ES_AppState_Enum_t = uint32;
2435#[doc = " @brief A type for Application IDs\n\n This is the type that is used for any API accepting or returning an App ID"]
2436pub type CFE_ES_AppId_t = CFE_ResourceId_t;
2437#[doc = " @brief A type for Task IDs\n\n This is the type that is used for any API accepting or returning a Task ID"]
2438pub type CFE_ES_TaskId_t = CFE_ResourceId_t;
2439#[doc = " @brief A type for Library IDs\n\n This is the type that is used for any API accepting or returning a Lib ID"]
2440pub type CFE_ES_LibId_t = CFE_ResourceId_t;
2441#[doc = " @brief A type for Counter IDs\n\n This is the type that is used for any API accepting or returning a Counter ID"]
2442pub type CFE_ES_CounterId_t = CFE_ResourceId_t;
2443#[doc = " @brief Memory Handle type\n\n Data type used to hold Handles of Memory Pools\n created via CFE_ES_PoolCreate and CFE_ES_PoolCreateNoSem"]
2444pub type CFE_ES_MemHandle_t = CFE_ResourceId_t;
2445#[doc = " @brief CDS Handle type\n\n Data type used to hold Handles of Critical Data Stores. See #CFE_ES_RegisterCDS"]
2446pub type CFE_ES_CDSHandle_t = CFE_ResourceId_t;
2447#[doc = " @brief Type used for task priority in CFE ES as\n including the commands/telemetry messages.\n\n @note the valid range is only 0-255 (same as OSAL) but\n a wider type is used for backward compatibility\n in binary formats of messages."]
2448pub type CFE_ES_TaskPriority_Atom_t = uint16;
2449#[doc = " @brief Type used for memory sizes and offsets in commands and telemetry\n\n For backward compatibility with existing CFE code this should be uint32,\n but all telemetry information will be limited to 4GB in size as a result.\n\n On 64-bit platforms this can be a 64-bit value which will allow larger\n memory objects, but this will break compatibility with existing control\n systems, and may also change the alignment/padding of messages.\n\n In either case this must be an unsigned type."]
2450pub type CFE_ES_MemOffset_t = uint32;
2451#[doc = " @brief Type used for memory addresses in command and telemetry messages\n\n For backward compatibility with existing CFE code this should be uint32,\n but if running on a 64-bit platform, addresses in telemetry will be\n truncated to 32 bits and therefore will not be valid.\n\n On 64-bit platforms this can be a 64-bit address which will allow the\n full memory address in commands and telemetry, but this will break\n compatibility with existing control systems, and may also change\n the alignment/padding of messages.\n\n In either case this must be an unsigned type.\n\n FSW code should access this value via the macros provided, which\n converts to the native \"cpuaddr\" type provided by OSAL.  This macro\n provides independence between the message representation and local\n representation of a memory address."]
