2939    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Set the specified generic counter\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine sets the specified generic counter to the specified value.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        None.\n\n \\param[in]   CounterId    The Counter to be set.\n\n \\param[in]   Count        The new value of the Counter.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS          \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_ES_BAD_ARGUMENT  \\copybrief CFE_ES_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_ES_RegisterGenCounter, #CFE_ES_DeleteGenCounter, #CFE_ES_IncrementGenCounter, #CFE_ES_GetGenCount,\n#CFE_ES_GetGenCounterIDByName"]
2940    pub fn CFE_ES_SetGenCount(CounterId: CFE_ES_CounterId_t, Count: uint32) -> CFE_Status_t;
2941}
2942unsafe extern "C" {
2943    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get the specified generic counter count\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine gets the value of a generic counter.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        None.\n\n \\param[in]   CounterId   The Counter to get the value from.\n\n \\param[out]  Count       Buffer to store value of the Counter @nonnull.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS          \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_ES_BAD_ARGUMENT  \\copybrief CFE_ES_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_ES_RegisterGenCounter, #CFE_ES_DeleteGenCounter, #CFE_ES_SetGenCount, #CFE_ES_IncrementGenCounter,\n#CFE_ES_GetGenCounterIDByName"]
2944    pub fn CFE_ES_GetGenCount(CounterId: CFE_ES_CounterId_t, Count: *mut uint32) -> CFE_Status_t;
2945}
2946unsafe extern "C" {
2947    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get the Id associated with a generic counter name\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine gets the Counter Id for a generic counter specified by name.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        None.\n\n \\param[out]  CounterIdPtr       Pointer to variable that is to receive the Counter's ID @nonnull.\n \\param[in]   CounterName        Pointer to null terminated character string containing a Counter name @nonnull.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                 \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND   \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_NAME_NOT_FOUND\n \\retval #CFE_ES_BAD_ARGUMENT         \\copybrief CFE_ES_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_ES_GetGenCounterName"]
2948    pub fn CFE_ES_GetGenCounterIDByName(
2949        CounterIdPtr: *mut CFE_ES_CounterId_t,
2950        CounterName: *const libc::c_char,
2951    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
2952}
2953unsafe extern "C" {
2954    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get a Counter name for a specified Counter ID\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine retrieves the cFE Counter name associated with a\n        specified Counter ID.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        In the case of a failure (#CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID), an empty string is returned.\n\n \\param[out]  CounterName   Pointer to a character array @nonnull of at least \\c BufferLength in size that will\n                            be filled with the Counter name.\n\n \\param[in]   CounterId     ID of Counter whose name is being requested.\n\n \\param[in]   BufferLength  The maximum number of characters, including the null terminator @nonzero, that can be put\n                            into the \\c CounterName buffer.  This routine will truncate the name to this length,\n                            if necessary.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                      \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID  \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_ES_BAD_ARGUMENT              \\copybrief CFE_ES_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_ES_GetGenCounterIDByName"]
2955    pub fn CFE_ES_GetGenCounterName(
2956        CounterName: *mut libc::c_char,
2957        CounterId: CFE_ES_CounterId_t,
2958        BufferLength: usize,
2959    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
2960}

2979#[doc = " @brief Events that identify a state change or action that is not an error"]
2980pub const CFE_EVS_EventType_CFE_EVS_EventType_INFORMATION: CFE_EVS_EventType = 2;
2981#[doc = " @brief Events that identify an error but are not catastrophic (e.g. - bad command"]
2982pub const CFE_EVS_EventType_CFE_EVS_EventType_ERROR: CFE_EVS_EventType = 3;
2983#[doc = " @brief Events that identify errors that are unrecoverable autonomously"]
2984pub const CFE_EVS_EventType_CFE_EVS_EventType_CRITICAL: CFE_EVS_EventType = 4;
2985#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_EVS_EventType_Enum_t"]
2986pub type CFE_EVS_EventType = libc::c_uint;
2987#[doc = " @brief Identifies type of event message\n\n @sa enum CFE_EVS_EventType"]
2988pub type CFE_EVS_EventType_Enum_t = uint16;

2990pub const CFE_EVS_EventFilter_CFE_EVS_EventFilter_BINARY: CFE_EVS_EventFilter = 0;
2991#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_EVS_EventFilter_Enum_t"]
2992pub type CFE_EVS_EventFilter = libc::c_uint;
2993#[doc = " @brief Identifies event filter schemes\n\n @sa enum CFE_EVS_EventFilter"]

3008#[repr(C)]
3009#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
3010pub struct CFE_EVS_BinFilter {
3011    #[doc = "< \\brief Numerical event identifier"]
3012    pub EventID: uint16,
3013    #[doc = "< \\brief Binary filter mask value"]
3014    pub Mask: uint16,
3015}
3016#[doc = " \\brief Event message filter definition structure"]
3017pub type CFE_EVS_BinFilter_t = CFE_EVS_BinFilter;
3018#[doc = "  \\brief Data structure used to hold system time values\n\n  \\par Description\n       The #CFE_TIME_SysTime_t data structure is used to hold time\n       values.  Time is referred to as the elapsed time (in seconds\n       and subseconds) since a specified epoch time.  The subseconds\n       field contains the number of 2^(-32) second intervals that have\n       elapsed since the epoch."]
3019#[repr(C)]
3020#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
3021pub struct CFE_TIME_SysTime {
3022    #[doc = "< \\brief Number of seconds since epoch"]
3023    pub Seconds: uint32,
3024    #[doc = "< \\brief Number of subseconds since epoch (LSB = 2^(-32) seconds)"]
3025    pub Subseconds: uint32,
3026}
3027#[doc = "  \\brief Data structure used to hold system time values\n\n  \\par Description\n       The #CFE_TIME_SysTime_t data structure is used to hold time\n       values.  Time is referred to as the elapsed time (in seconds\n       and subseconds) since a specified epoch time.  The subseconds\n       field contains the number of 2^(-32) second intervals that have\n       elapsed since the epoch."]
3028pub type CFE_TIME_SysTime_t = CFE_TIME_SysTime;
3029#[doc = " @brief The spacecraft time has been set"]
3030pub const CFE_TIME_FlagBit_CFE_TIME_FlagBit_CLKSET: CFE_TIME_FlagBit = 0;
3031#[doc = " @brief This instance of Time Services is flywheeling"]
3032pub const CFE_TIME_FlagBit_CFE_TIME_FlagBit_FLYING: CFE_TIME_FlagBit = 1;
3033#[doc = " @brief The clock source is set to internal"]
3034pub const CFE_TIME_FlagBit_CFE_TIME_FlagBit_SRCINT: CFE_TIME_FlagBit = 2;
3035#[doc = " @brief The clock signal is set to primary"]
3036pub const CFE_TIME_FlagBit_CFE_TIME_FlagBit_SIGPRI: CFE_TIME_FlagBit = 3;
3037#[doc = " @brief The Time Server is in flywheel mode"]
3038pub const CFE_TIME_FlagBit_CFE_TIME_FlagBit_SRVFLY: CFE_TIME_FlagBit = 4;
3039#[doc = " @brief This instance of Time Services was commanded into flywheel mode"]
3040pub const CFE_TIME_FlagBit_CFE_TIME_FlagBit_CMDFLY: CFE_TIME_FlagBit = 5;
3041#[doc = " @brief One time STCF Adjustment is to be done in positive direction"]
3042pub const CFE_TIME_FlagBit_CFE_TIME_FlagBit_ADDADJ: CFE_TIME_FlagBit = 6;
3043#[doc = " @brief 1 Hz STCF Adjustment is to be done in a positive direction"]
3044pub const CFE_TIME_FlagBit_CFE_TIME_FlagBit_ADD1HZ: CFE_TIME_FlagBit = 7;
3045#[doc = " @brief Time Client Latency is applied in a positive direction"]
3046pub const CFE_TIME_FlagBit_CFE_TIME_FlagBit_ADDTCL: CFE_TIME_FlagBit = 8;
3047#[doc = " @brief This instance of Time Services is a Time Server"]
3048pub const CFE_TIME_FlagBit_CFE_TIME_FlagBit_SERVER: CFE_TIME_FlagBit = 9;
3049#[doc = " @brief The tone received is good compared to the last tone received"]
3050pub const CFE_TIME_FlagBit_CFE_TIME_FlagBit_GDTONE: CFE_TIME_FlagBit = 10;
3051#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_TIME_FlagBit_Enum_t"]
3052pub type CFE_TIME_FlagBit = libc::c_uint;
3053#[doc = " @brief Bit positions of the various clock state flags\n\n @sa enum CFE_TIME_FlagBit"]
3054pub type CFE_TIME_FlagBit_Enum_t = uint8;
3055#[doc = " The spacecraft time has not been set since the last clock\n reset.  Times returned by clock routines have no relationship\n to any ground-based time reference.\n"]
3056pub const CFE_TIME_ClockState_CFE_TIME_ClockState_INVALID: CFE_TIME_ClockState = -1;
3057#[doc = " The spacecraft time has been set at least once since the last\n clock reset, and it is synchronized with the primary on-board\n time base.  Times returned by clock routines can be trusted.\n"]
3058pub const CFE_TIME_ClockState_CFE_TIME_ClockState_VALID: CFE_TIME_ClockState = 0;
3059#[doc = " The spacecraft time has been set at least once since the last\n clock reset, but it is not currently synchronized with the\n primary on-board time base.  Times returned by clock routines\n are a \"best guess\" based on a non-optimal oscillator.\n"]
3060pub const CFE_TIME_ClockState_CFE_TIME_ClockState_FLYWHEEL: CFE_TIME_ClockState = 1;
3061#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_TIME_ClockState_Enum_t"]
3062pub type CFE_TIME_ClockState = libc::c_int;
3063#[doc = " @brief Enumerated types identifying the quality of the current time\n\n \\par Description\n The #CFE_TIME_ClockState_Enum_t enumerations identify the three recognized states of the current time.\n If the clock has never been successfully synchronized with the primary onboard clock source, the\n time is considered to be #CFE_TIME_ClockState_INVALID.  If the time is currently synchronized (i.e. - the\n primary synchronization mechanism has not been dropped for any significant amount of time), then\n the current time is considered to be #CFE_TIME_ClockState_VALID.  If the time had, at some point in the past,\n been synchronized, but the synchronization with the primary onboard clock has since been lost, then\n the time is considered to be #CFE_TIME_ClockState_FLYWHEEL.  Since different clocks drift at different rates\n from one another, the accuracy of the time while in #CFE_TIME_ClockState_FLYWHEEL is dependent upon the time\n spent in that state.\n\n @sa enum CFE_TIME_ClockState"]
3064pub type CFE_TIME_ClockState_Enum_t = int16;
3065#[doc = " @brief Use Internal Source"]
3066pub const CFE_TIME_SourceSelect_CFE_TIME_SourceSelect_INTERNAL: CFE_TIME_SourceSelect = 1;
3067#[doc = " @brief Use External Source"]
3068pub const CFE_TIME_SourceSelect_CFE_TIME_SourceSelect_EXTERNAL: CFE_TIME_SourceSelect = 2;
3069#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_TIME_SourceSelect_Enum_t"]
3070pub type CFE_TIME_SourceSelect = libc::c_uint;
3071#[doc = " @brief Clock Source Selection Parameters\n\n @sa enum CFE_TIME_SourceSelect"]
3072pub type CFE_TIME_SourceSelect_Enum_t = uint8;

3074pub const CFE_TIME_ToneSignalSelect_CFE_TIME_ToneSignalSelect_PRIMARY: CFE_TIME_ToneSignalSelect =
3075    1;
3076#[doc = " @brief Redundant Source"]
3077pub const CFE_TIME_ToneSignalSelect_CFE_TIME_ToneSignalSelect_REDUNDANT: CFE_TIME_ToneSignalSelect =
3078    2;
3079#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_TIME_ToneSignalSelect_Enum_t"]
3080pub type CFE_TIME_ToneSignalSelect = libc::c_uint;
3081#[doc = " @brief Tone Signal Selection Parameters\n\n @sa enum CFE_TIME_ToneSignalSelect"]
3082pub type CFE_TIME_ToneSignalSelect_Enum_t = uint8;
3083#[doc = " @brief Add time adjustment"]
3084pub const CFE_TIME_AdjustDirection_CFE_TIME_AdjustDirection_ADD: CFE_TIME_AdjustDirection = 1;
3085#[doc = " @brief Subtract time adjustment"]
3086pub const CFE_TIME_AdjustDirection_CFE_TIME_AdjustDirection_SUBTRACT: CFE_TIME_AdjustDirection = 2;
3087#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_TIME_AdjustDirection_Enum_t"]
3088pub type CFE_TIME_AdjustDirection = libc::c_uint;
3089#[doc = " @brief STCF adjustment direction (for both one-time and 1Hz adjustments)\n\n @sa enum CFE_TIME_AdjustDirection"]
3090pub type CFE_TIME_AdjustDirection_Enum_t = uint8;
3091#[doc = " @brief Not in flywheel state"]
3092pub const CFE_TIME_FlywheelState_CFE_TIME_FlywheelState_NO_FLY: CFE_TIME_FlywheelState = 0;
3093#[doc = " @brief In flywheel state"]
3094pub const CFE_TIME_FlywheelState_CFE_TIME_FlywheelState_IS_FLY: CFE_TIME_FlywheelState = 1;
3095#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_TIME_FlywheelState_Enum_t"]
3096pub type CFE_TIME_FlywheelState = libc::c_uint;
3097#[doc = " @brief Fly-wheel status values\n\n @sa enum CFE_TIME_FlywheelState"]
3098pub type CFE_TIME_FlywheelState_Enum_t = uint8;
3099#[doc = " @brief Spacecraft time has not been set"]
3100pub const CFE_TIME_SetState_CFE_TIME_SetState_NOT_SET: CFE_TIME_SetState = 0;
3101#[doc = " @brief Spacecraft time has been set"]
3102pub const CFE_TIME_SetState_CFE_TIME_SetState_WAS_SET: CFE_TIME_SetState = 1;
3103#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_TIME_SetState_Enum_t"]
3104pub type CFE_TIME_SetState = libc::c_uint;
3105#[doc = " @brief Clock status values (has the clock been set to correct time)\n\n @sa enum CFE_TIME_SetState"]
3106pub type CFE_TIME_SetState_Enum_t = uint8;
3107#[doc = "< \\brief The first specified time is considered to be before the second specified time"]
3108pub const CFE_TIME_Compare_CFE_TIME_A_LT_B: CFE_TIME_Compare = -1;
3109#[doc = "< \\brief The two specified times are considered to be equal"]
3110pub const CFE_TIME_Compare_CFE_TIME_EQUAL: CFE_TIME_Compare = 0;
3111#[doc = "< \\brief The first specified time is considered to be after the second specified time"]
3112pub const CFE_TIME_Compare_CFE_TIME_A_GT_B: CFE_TIME_Compare = 1;
3113#[doc = "  \\brief Enumerated types identifying the relative relationships of two times\n\n  \\par Description\n       Since time fields contain numbers that are relative to an epoch time, then it is possible for a time value\n       to be \"negative\".  This can lead to some confusion about what relationship exists between two time values.\n       To resolve this confusion, the cFE provides the API #CFE_TIME_Compare which returns these enumerated values."]
3114pub type CFE_TIME_Compare = libc::c_int;
3115#[doc = "  \\brief Enumerated types identifying the relative relationships of two times\n\n  \\par Description\n       Since time fields contain numbers that are relative to an epoch time, then it is possible for a time value\n       to be \"negative\".  This can lead to some confusion about what relationship exists between two time values.\n       To resolve this confusion, the cFE provides the API #CFE_TIME_Compare which returns these enumerated values."]
3116pub use self::CFE_TIME_Compare as CFE_TIME_Compare_t;
3117#[doc = "   \\brief Time Synchronization Callback Function Ptr Type\n\n   \\par Description\n        Applications that wish to get direct notification of the receipt of the cFE Time Synchronization signal\n        (typically a 1 Hz signal), must register a callback function with the following prototype via the\n        #CFE_TIME_RegisterSynchCallback API."]
3118pub type CFE_TIME_SynchCallbackPtr_t = ::core::option::Option<unsafe extern "C" fn() -> int32>;
3119unsafe extern "C" {
3120    #[doc = " \\brief Register an application for receiving event services\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine registers an application with event services and allocates/initializes\n          the internal data structures used to support this application's events.  An application\n          may not send events unless it has called this routine.  The routine also accepts a filter\n          array structure for applications requiring event filtering.  In the current implementation\n          of the EVS, only the binary filtering scheme is supported.  See section TBD of the cFE\n          Application Programmer's Guide for a description of the behavior of binary filters.\n          Applications may call #CFE_EVS_Register more than once, but each call will wipe out all\n          filters registered by previous calls (filter registration is NOT cumulative).\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          Note: Event filters can be added, deleted or modified by ground commands.  All filtering\n                schemes include a default setting that results in no filtering (such as #CFE_EVS_NO_FILTER\n                for binary filters).\n\n          <b> Filter Scheme: </b> Binary <BR>\n          <b> Code: </b> CFE_EVS_EventFilter_BINARY <BR>\n          <b> Filter Structure: </b>\n          \\code\n                     typedef struct CFE_EVS_BinFilter {\n                           uint16  EventID,\n                           uint16  Mask ;\n                     } CFE_EVS_BinFilter_t;\n          \\endcode\n\n \\param[in] Filters            Pointer to an array of event message filters, or NULL if no filtering is desired.\n                               The structure of an event message filter depends on the FilterScheme selected.\n                               (see Filter Schemes mentioned above)\n\n \\param[in] NumEventFilters    The number of event message filters included in this call.  This must be less than\n                               or equal to the maximum number of events allowed per application\n                               (#CFE_PLATFORM_EVS_MAX_EVENT_FILTERS).\n\n \\param[in] FilterScheme       The event filtering scheme that this application will use.  For the first\n                               implementation of the event services, only filter type #CFE_EVS_EventFilter_BINARY\n                               will be supported.\n\n \\return Execution status below or from #CFE_ES_GetAppID, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                 \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_APP_FILTER_OVERLOAD \\copybrief CFE_EVS_APP_FILTER_OVERLOAD\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_UNKNOWN_FILTER      \\copybrief CFE_EVS_UNKNOWN_FILTER\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_APP_ILLEGAL_APP_ID  \\copybrief CFE_EVS_APP_ILLEGAL_APP_ID\n \\retval #CFE_ES_BAD_ARGUMENT         \\copybrief CFE_ES_BAD_ARGUMENT"]
3121    pub fn CFE_EVS_Register(
3122        Filters: *const libc::c_void,
3123        NumEventFilters: uint16,
3124        FilterScheme: uint16,
3125    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3126}
3127unsafe extern "C" {
3128    #[doc = " \\brief Generate a software event.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine generates a software event message.  If the EventID is not filtered,\n          the event will be sent as a software bus message, optionally logged in the local\n          event log, and optionally sent as an ASCII text string out the enabled output port(s).\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          This API only works within the context of a registered application or core service.\n          For messages outside the context of a registered application (for example early\n          in app initialization or if registration fails) #CFE_ES_WriteToSysLog can be used\n          for reporting.\n\n \\param[in] EventID            A numeric literal used to uniquely identify an application event.\n                               The \\c EventID is defined and supplied by the application sending the event.\n\n \\param[in] EventType          A numeric literal used to classify an event, one of:\n                                   \\arg #CFE_EVS_EventType_DEBUG\n                                   \\arg #CFE_EVS_EventType_INFORMATION\n                                   \\arg #CFE_EVS_EventType_ERROR\n                                   \\arg #CFE_EVS_EventType_CRITICAL\n\n \\param[in] Spec               A pointer to a null terminated text string @nonnull describing the output format\n                               for the event.  This is the same type of format string used for the ANSI\n                               \\c printf function.  Nominally the post-conversion string is limited to 80\n                               characters, but this limit is configurable through the parameter\n                               #CFE_MISSION_EVS_MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH.  Characters beyond the limit will be truncated.\n                               Do not use floating point conversions (%f, %e, %E, %g, and %G) in the format\n                               string unless your application will be running in a system that supports\n                               floating point arithmetic.  Do not use non-printable characters (\\\\t, \\\\n, etc.)\n                               in the format string; they will mess up the formatting when the events are\n                               displayed on the ground system.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_APP_NOT_REGISTERED \\copybrief CFE_EVS_APP_NOT_REGISTERED\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_APP_ILLEGAL_APP_ID \\copybrief CFE_EVS_APP_ILLEGAL_APP_ID\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_INVALID_PARAMETER  \\copybrief CFE_EVS_INVALID_PARAMETER\n\n \\sa #CFE_EVS_SendEventWithAppID, #CFE_EVS_SendTimedEvent"]
3129    pub fn CFE_EVS_SendEvent(
3130        EventID: uint16,
3131        EventType: CFE_EVS_EventType_Enum_t,
3132        Spec: *const libc::c_char,
3133        ...
3134    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3135}
3136unsafe extern "C" {
3137    #[doc = " \\brief Generate a software event given the specified Application ID.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine generates a software event message.  If the EventID is not filtered,\n          the event will be sent as a software bus message, optionally logged in the local\n          event log, and optionally sent as an ASCII text string out the enabled output port(s).\n          Note that this function should really only be used from within an API in order to\n          preserve the context of an Application's event.  In general, #CFE_EVS_SendEvent should be used.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          The Application ID must correspond to a registered application or core service.\n          For messages outside the context of a registered application (for example early\n          in app initialization or if registration fails) #CFE_ES_WriteToSysLog can be used\n          for reporting.\n\n \\param[in] EventID            A numeric literal used to uniquely identify an application event.\n                               The \\c EventID is defined and supplied by the application sending the event.\n\n \\param[in] EventType          A numeric literal used to classify an event, one of:\n                                   \\arg #CFE_EVS_EventType_DEBUG\n                                   \\arg #CFE_EVS_EventType_INFORMATION\n                                   \\arg #CFE_EVS_EventType_ERROR\n                                   \\arg #CFE_EVS_EventType_CRITICAL\n\n \\param[in] AppID              The Application ID from which the event message should appear.\n\n \\param[in] Spec               A pointer to a null terminated text string @nonnull describing the output format\n                               for the event.  This is the same type of format string used for the ANSI\n                               \\c printf function.  Nominally the post-conversion string is limited to 80\n                               characters, but this limit is configurable through the parameter\n                               #CFE_MISSION_EVS_MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH.  Characters beyond the limit will be truncated.\n                               Do not use floating point conversions (%f, %e, %E, %g, and %G) in the format\n                               string unless your application will be running in a system that supports\n                               floating point arithmetic.  Do not use non-printable characters (\\\\t, \\\\n, etc.)\n                               in the format string; they will mess up the formatting when the events are\n                               displayed on the ground system.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_APP_NOT_REGISTERED \\copybrief CFE_EVS_APP_NOT_REGISTERED\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_APP_ILLEGAL_APP_ID \\copybrief CFE_EVS_APP_ILLEGAL_APP_ID\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_INVALID_PARAMETER  \\copybrief CFE_EVS_INVALID_PARAMETER\n\n \\sa #CFE_EVS_SendEvent, #CFE_EVS_SendTimedEvent"]
3138    pub fn CFE_EVS_SendEventWithAppID(
3139        EventID: uint16,
3140        EventType: CFE_EVS_EventType_Enum_t,
3141        AppID: CFE_ES_AppId_t,
3142        Spec: *const libc::c_char,
3143        ...
3144    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3145}
3146unsafe extern "C" {
3147    #[doc = " \\brief Generate a software event with a specific time tag.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine is the same as #CFE_EVS_SendEvent except that the caller specifies the event time\n          instead of having the EVS use the current spacecraft time.  This routine should be used in\n          situations where an error condition is detected at one time, but the event message is reported\n          at a later time.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          This API only works within the context of a registered application or core service.\n          For messages outside the context of a registered application (for example early\n          in app initialization or if registration fails) #CFE_ES_WriteToSysLog can be used\n          for reporting.\n\n \\param[in] Time               The time to include in the event.  This will usually be a time returned\n                               by the function #CFE_TIME_GetTime.\n\n \\param[in] EventID            A numeric literal used to uniquely identify an application event.\n                               The \\c EventID is defined and supplied by the application sending the event.\n\n \\param[in] EventType          A numeric literal used to classify an event, one of:\n                                   \\arg #CFE_EVS_EventType_DEBUG\n                                   \\arg #CFE_EVS_EventType_INFORMATION\n                                   \\arg #CFE_EVS_EventType_ERROR\n                                   \\arg #CFE_EVS_EventType_CRITICAL\n\n \\param[in] Spec               A pointer to a null terminated text string @nonnull describing the output format\n                               for the event.  This is the same type of format string used for the ANSI\n                               \\c printf function.  Nominally the post-conversion string is limited to 80\n                               characters, but this limit is configurable through the parameter\n                               #CFE_MISSION_EVS_MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH.  Characters beyond the limit will be truncated.\n                               Do not use floating point conversions (%f, %e, %E, %g, and %G) in the format\n                               string unless your application will be running in a system that supports\n                               floating point arithmetic.  Do not use non-printable characters (\\\\t, \\\\n, etc.)\n                               in the format string; they will mess up the formatting when the events are\n                               displayed on the ground system.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_APP_NOT_REGISTERED \\copybrief CFE_EVS_APP_NOT_REGISTERED\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_APP_ILLEGAL_APP_ID \\copybrief CFE_EVS_APP_ILLEGAL_APP_ID\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_INVALID_PARAMETER  \\copybrief CFE_EVS_INVALID_PARAMETER\n\n \\sa #CFE_EVS_SendEvent, #CFE_EVS_SendEventWithAppID"]
3148    pub fn CFE_EVS_SendTimedEvent(
3149        Time: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t,
3150        EventID: uint16,
3151        EventType: CFE_EVS_EventType_Enum_t,
3152        Spec: *const libc::c_char,
3153        ...
3154    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3155}
3156unsafe extern "C" {
3157    #[doc = " \\brief Resets the calling application's event filter for a single event ID.\n\n \\par Description\n          Resets the filter such that the next event is treated like the first.\n          For example, if the filter was set to only send the first event, the\n          next event following the reset would be sent.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in] EventID            A numeric literal used to uniquely identify an application event.\n                               The \\c EventID is defined and supplied by the application sending the event.\n\n \\return Execution status below or from #CFE_ES_GetAppID, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_APP_NOT_REGISTERED \\copybrief CFE_EVS_APP_NOT_REGISTERED\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_APP_ILLEGAL_APP_ID \\copybrief CFE_EVS_APP_ILLEGAL_APP_ID\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_EVT_NOT_REGISTERED \\copybrief CFE_EVS_EVT_NOT_REGISTERED\n\n \\sa #CFE_EVS_ResetAllFilters"]
3158    pub fn CFE_EVS_ResetFilter(EventID: uint16) -> CFE_Status_t;
3159}
3160unsafe extern "C" {
3161    #[doc = " \\brief Resets all of the calling application's event filters.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine resets all the calling application's event filter counters to zero, providing a\n          quick and convenient method for resetting event filters.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\return Execution status below or from #CFE_ES_GetAppID, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_APP_NOT_REGISTERED \\copybrief CFE_EVS_APP_NOT_REGISTERED\n \\retval #CFE_EVS_APP_ILLEGAL_APP_ID \\copybrief CFE_EVS_APP_ILLEGAL_APP_ID\n\n \\sa #CFE_EVS_ResetFilter"]
3162    pub fn CFE_EVS_ResetAllFilters() -> CFE_Status_t;
3163}
3164#[doc = " @brief Executive Services Exception/Reset Log Type\n\n Executive Services Exception/Reset Log File which is generated in response to a\n \\link #CFE_ES_WRITE_ER_LOG_CC \\ES_WRITEERLOG2FILE \\endlink\n command.\n"]
3165pub const CFE_FS_SubType_CFE_FS_SubType_ES_ERLOG: CFE_FS_SubType = 1;
3166#[doc = " @brief Executive Services System Log Type\n\n Executive Services System Log File which is generated in response to a\n \\link #CFE_ES_WRITE_SYS_LOG_CC \\ES_WRITESYSLOG2FILE \\endlink\n command.\n"]
3167pub const CFE_FS_SubType_CFE_FS_SubType_ES_SYSLOG: CFE_FS_SubType = 2;
3168#[doc = " @brief Executive Services Information on All Applications File\n\n Executive Services Information on All Applications File which is generated in response to a\n \\link #CFE_ES_QUERY_ALL_CC \\ES_WRITEAPPINFO2FILE \\endlink\n command.\n"]
3169pub const CFE_FS_SubType_CFE_FS_SubType_ES_QUERYALL: CFE_FS_SubType = 3;
3170#[doc = " @brief Executive Services Performance Data File\n\n Executive Services Performance Analyzer Data File which is generated in response to a\n \\link #CFE_ES_STOP_PERF_DATA_CC \\ES_STOPLADATA \\endlink\n command.\n"]
3171pub const CFE_FS_SubType_CFE_FS_SubType_ES_PERFDATA: CFE_FS_SubType = 4;
3172#[doc = " @brief Executive Services Critical Data Store Registry Dump File\n\n Executive Services Critical Data Store Registry Dump File which is generated in response to a\n \\link #CFE_ES_DUMP_CDS_REGISTRY_CC \\ES_DUMPCDSREG \\endlink\n command.\n"]
3173pub const CFE_FS_SubType_CFE_FS_SubType_ES_CDS_REG: CFE_FS_SubType = 6;
3174#[doc = " @brief Table Services Registry Dump File\n\n Table Services Registry Dump File which is generated in response to a\n \\link #CFE_TBL_DUMP_REGISTRY_CC \\TBL_WRITEREG2FILE \\endlink\n command.\n"]
3175pub const CFE_FS_SubType_CFE_FS_SubType_TBL_REG: CFE_FS_SubType = 9;
3176#[doc = " @brief Table Services Table Image File\n\n Table Services Table Image File which is generated either on the ground or in response to a\n \\link #CFE_TBL_DUMP_CC \\TBL_DUMP \\endlink command.\n"]
3177pub const CFE_FS_SubType_CFE_FS_SubType_TBL_IMG: CFE_FS_SubType = 8;
3178#[doc = " @brief Event Services Application Data Dump File\n\n Event Services Application Data Dump File which is generated in response to a\n \\link #CFE_EVS_WRITE_APP_DATA_FILE_CC \\EVS_WRITEAPPDATA2FILE \\endlink\n command.\n"]
3179pub const CFE_FS_SubType_CFE_FS_SubType_EVS_APPDATA: CFE_FS_SubType = 15;
3180#[doc = " @brief Event Services Local Event Log Dump File\n\n Event Services Local Event Log Dump File which is generated in response to a\n \\link  #CFE_EVS_WRITE_LOG_DATA_FILE_CC \\EVS_WRITELOG2FILE \\endlink\n command.\n"]
3181pub const CFE_FS_SubType_CFE_FS_SubType_EVS_EVENTLOG: CFE_FS_SubType = 16;
3182#[doc = " @brief Software Bus Pipe Data Dump File\n\n Software Bus Pipe Data Dump File which is generated in response to a\n \\link #CFE_SB_WRITE_PIPE_INFO_CC \\SB_WRITEPIPE2FILE \\endlink\n command.\n"]
3183pub const CFE_FS_SubType_CFE_FS_SubType_SB_PIPEDATA: CFE_FS_SubType = 20;
3184#[doc = " @brief Software Bus Message Routing Data Dump File\n\n Software Bus Message Routing Data Dump File which is generated in response to a\n \\link #CFE_SB_WRITE_ROUTING_INFO_CC \\SB_WRITEROUTING2FILE \\endlink\n command.\n"]
3185pub const CFE_FS_SubType_CFE_FS_SubType_SB_ROUTEDATA: CFE_FS_SubType = 21;
3186#[doc = " @brief Software Bus Message Mapping Data Dump File\n\n Software Bus Message Mapping Data Dump File which is generated in response to a\n \\link #CFE_SB_WRITE_MAP_INFO_CC \\SB_WRITEMAP2FILE \\endlink\n command.\n"]
3187pub const CFE_FS_SubType_CFE_FS_SubType_SB_MAPDATA: CFE_FS_SubType = 22;
3188#[doc = " @brief Executive Services Query All Tasks Data File\n\n Executive Services Query All Tasks Data File which is generated in response to a\n \\link #CFE_ES_QUERY_ALL_TASKS_CC \\ES_WRITETASKINFO2FILE \\endlink\n command.\n"]
3189pub const CFE_FS_SubType_CFE_FS_SubType_ES_QUERYALLTASKS: CFE_FS_SubType = 23;
3190#[doc = " @brief File subtypes used within cFE\n\n This defines all the file subtypes used by cFE.\n Note apps can extend as needed but need to\n avoid conflicts (app context not currently included\n in the file header)."]
3191pub type CFE_FS_SubType = libc::c_uint;
3192#[doc = " @brief Content descriptor for File Headers\n\n @sa enum CFE_FS_SubType"]
3193pub type CFE_FS_SubType_Enum_t = uint32;
3194#[doc = " \\brief Standard cFE File header structure definition"]
3195#[repr(C)]
3196#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
3197pub struct CFE_FS_Header {
3198    #[doc = "< \\brief Identifies the content type (='cFE1'=0x63464531)"]
3199    pub ContentType: uint32,
3200    #[doc = "< \\brief Type of \\c ContentType, if necessary */\n/**< Standard SubType definitions can be found\n\\link #CFE_FS_SubType_ES_ERLOG here \\endlink"]
3201    pub SubType: uint32,
3202    #[doc = "< \\brief Length of this header to support external processing"]
3203    pub Length: uint32,
3204    #[doc = "< \\brief Spacecraft that generated the file"]
3205    pub SpacecraftID: uint32,
3206    #[doc = "< \\brief Processor that generated the file"]
3207    pub ProcessorID: uint32,
3208    #[doc = "< \\brief Application that generated the file"]
3209    pub ApplicationID: uint32,
3210    #[doc = "< \\brief File creation timestamp (seconds)"]
3211    pub TimeSeconds: uint32,
3212    #[doc = "< \\brief File creation timestamp (sub-seconds)"]
3213    pub TimeSubSeconds: uint32,
3214    #[doc = "< \\brief File description"]
3215    pub Description: [libc::c_char; 32usize],
3216}
3217#[doc = " \\brief Standard cFE File header structure definition"]
3218pub type CFE_FS_Header_t = CFE_FS_Header;
3219#[doc = "< Placeholder, unknown file category"]
3220pub const CFE_FS_FileCategory_t_CFE_FS_FileCategory_UNKNOWN: CFE_FS_FileCategory_t = 0;
3221#[doc = "< Dynamically loadable apps/libraries (e.g. .so, .o, .dll, etc)"]
3222pub const CFE_FS_FileCategory_t_CFE_FS_FileCategory_DYNAMIC_MODULE: CFE_FS_FileCategory_t = 1;
3223#[doc = "< Binary log file generated by various data dump commands"]
3224pub const CFE_FS_FileCategory_t_CFE_FS_FileCategory_BINARY_DATA_DUMP: CFE_FS_FileCategory_t = 2;
3225#[doc = "< Text-based log file generated by various commands"]
3226pub const CFE_FS_FileCategory_t_CFE_FS_FileCategory_TEXT_LOG: CFE_FS_FileCategory_t = 3;
3227#[doc = "< Text-based Script files (e.g. ES startup script)"]
3228pub const CFE_FS_FileCategory_t_CFE_FS_FileCategory_SCRIPT: CFE_FS_FileCategory_t = 4;
3229#[doc = "< Temporary/Ephemeral files"]
3230pub const CFE_FS_FileCategory_t_CFE_FS_FileCategory_TEMP: CFE_FS_FileCategory_t = 5;
3231#[doc = "< Placeholder, keep last"]
3232pub const CFE_FS_FileCategory_t_CFE_FS_FileCategory_MAX: CFE_FS_FileCategory_t = 6;
3233#[doc = " \\brief Generalized file types/categories known to FS\n\n This defines different categories of files, where they\n may reside in different default locations of the virtualized file system.\n\n This is different from, and should not be confused with, the \"SubType\"\n field in the FS header.  This value is only used at runtime for FS APIs\n and should not actually appear in any output file or message."]
3234pub type CFE_FS_FileCategory_t = libc::c_uint;
3235pub const CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_t_CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_UNDEFINED: CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_t = 0;
3236#[doc = "< File is completed successfully"]
3237pub const CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_t_CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_COMPLETE: CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_t = 1;
3238#[doc = "< Unable to create/open file"]
3239pub const CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_t_CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_CREATE_ERROR: CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_t = 2;
3240#[doc = "< Unable to write FS header"]
3241pub const CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_t_CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_HEADER_WRITE_ERROR:
3242    CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_t = 3;
3243#[doc = "< Unable to write data record"]
3244pub const CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_t_CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_RECORD_WRITE_ERROR:
3245    CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_t = 4;
3246pub const CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_t_CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_MAX: CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_t = 5;
3247pub type CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_t = libc::c_uint;
3248#[doc = " Data Getter routine provided by requester\n\n Outputs a data block.  Should return true if the file is complete (last record/EOF), otherwise return false.\n\n \\param[inout] Meta       Pointer to the metadata object\n \\param[in]    RecordNum  Incrementing record number counter\n \\param[out]   Buffer     Pointer to buffer data block, should be set by implementation\n \\param[out]   BufSize    Pointer to buffer data size, should be set by implementation\n\n \\returns End of file status\n \\retval  true if at last data record, and output file should be closed\n \\retval  false if not at last record, more data records to write\n\n \\note The implementation of this function must always set the \"Buffer\" and \"BufSize\" outputs.  If\n       no data is available, they may be set to NULL and 0, respectively."]
3249pub type CFE_FS_FileWriteGetData_t = ::core::option::Option<
3250    unsafe extern "C" fn(
3251        Meta: *mut libc::c_void,
3252        RecordNum: uint32,
3253        Buffer: *mut *mut libc::c_void,
3254        BufSize: *mut usize,
3255    ) -> bool,
3256>;
3257#[doc = " Event generator routine provided by requester\n\n Invoked from certain points in the file write process.  Implementation may invoke CFE_EVS_SendEvent() appropriately\n to inform of progress.\n\n \\param[inout] Meta       Pointer to the metadata object\n \\param[in]    Event      Generalized type of event to report (not actual event ID)\n \\param[in]    Status     Generalized status code (may be from OSAL or CFE)\n \\param[in]    RecordNum  Record number counter at which event occurred\n \\param[in]    BlockSize  Size of record being processed when event occurred (if applicable)\n \\param[in]    Position   File position/size when event occurred\n"]
3258pub type CFE_FS_FileWriteOnEvent_t = ::core::option::Option<
3259    unsafe extern "C" fn(
3260        Meta: *mut libc::c_void,
3261        Event: CFE_FS_FileWriteEvent_t,
3262        Status: int32,
3263        RecordNum: uint32,
3264        BlockSize: usize,
3265        Position: usize,
3266    ),
3267>;
3268#[doc = " \\brief External Metadata/State object associated with background file writes\n\n Applications intending to schedule background file write jobs should instantiate\n this object in static/global data memory.  This keeps track of the state of the\n file write request(s)."]
3269#[repr(C)]
3270#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone)]
3271pub struct CFE_FS_FileWriteMetaData {
3272    #[doc = "< Whether request is pending (volatile as it may be checked outside lock)"]
3273    pub IsPending: bool,
3274    #[doc = "< Name of file to write"]
3275    pub FileName: [libc::c_char; 64usize],
3276    #[doc = "< Type of file to write (for FS header)"]
3277    pub FileSubType: uint32,
3278    #[doc = "< Description of file (for FS header)"]
3279    pub Description: [libc::c_char; 32usize],
3280    #[doc = "< Application callback to get a data record"]
3281    pub GetData: CFE_FS_FileWriteGetData_t,
3282    #[doc = "< Application callback for abstract event processing"]
3283    pub OnEvent: CFE_FS_FileWriteOnEvent_t,
3284}
3285impl Default for CFE_FS_FileWriteMetaData {
3286    fn default() -> Self {
3287        let mut s = ::core::mem::MaybeUninit::<Self>::uninit();
3288        unsafe {
3289            ::core::ptr::write_bytes(s.as_mut_ptr(), 0, 1);
3290            s.assume_init()
3291        }
3292    }
3293}
3294#[doc = " \\brief External Metadata/State object associated with background file writes\n\n Applications intending to schedule background file write jobs should instantiate\n this object in static/global data memory.  This keeps track of the state of the\n file write request(s)."]
3295pub type CFE_FS_FileWriteMetaData_t = CFE_FS_FileWriteMetaData;
3296unsafe extern "C" {
3297    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Read the contents of the Standard cFE File Header\n\n \\par Description\n        This API will fill the specified #CFE_FS_Header_t variable with the\n        contents of the Standard cFE File Header of the file identified by\n        the given File Descriptor.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        -# The File has already been successfully opened using #OS_OpenCreate and\n           the caller has a legitimate File Descriptor.\n        -# File offset behavior: Agnostic on entry since it will move the offset to the start of the file,\n           on success the offset will be at the end of the header, undefined offset behavior for error cases.\n\n \\param[out] Hdr         Pointer to a variable of type #CFE_FS_Header_t @nonnull that will be\n                         filled with the contents of the Standard cFE File Header. *Hdr is the contents of the\n                         Standard cFE File Header for the specified file.\n \\param[in] FileDes      File Descriptor obtained from a previous call to #OS_OpenCreate\n                         that is associated with the file whose header is to be read.\n\n \\return Bytes read or error status from OSAL\n \\retval #CFE_FS_BAD_ARGUMENT  \\copybrief CFE_FS_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\note This function invokes OSAL API routines and the current implementation may return\n       OSAL error codes to the caller if failure occurs.  In a future version of CFE, the\n       status codes will be converted to a value in \\ref CFEReturnCodes.\n\n \\sa #CFE_FS_WriteHeader"]
3298    pub fn CFE_FS_ReadHeader(Hdr: *mut CFE_FS_Header_t, FileDes: osal_id_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3299}
3300unsafe extern "C" {
3301    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Initializes the contents of the Standard cFE File Header\n\n \\par Description\n        This API will clear the specified #CFE_FS_Header_t variable and\n        initialize the description field with the specified value\n\n \\param[in] Hdr          Pointer to a variable of type #CFE_FS_Header_t that will be\n                         cleared and initialized\n\n \\param[in]  Description Initializes Header's Description @nonnull\n\n \\param[in]  SubType     Initializes Header's SubType\n\n \\sa #CFE_FS_WriteHeader"]
3302    pub fn CFE_FS_InitHeader(
3303        Hdr: *mut CFE_FS_Header_t,
3304        Description: *const libc::c_char,
3305        SubType: uint32,
3306    );
3307}
3308unsafe extern "C" {
3309    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Write the specified Standard cFE File Header to the specified file\n\n \\par Description\n        This API will output the specified #CFE_FS_Header_t variable, with some\n        fields automatically updated, to the specified file as the Standard cFE\n        File Header. This API will automatically populate the following fields\n        in the specified #CFE_FS_Header_t:\n\n      -# \\link #CFE_FS_Header_t::ContentType \\c ContentType \\endlink - Filled with 0x63464531 ('cFE1')\n      -# \\link #CFE_FS_Header_t::Length \\c Length \\endlink - Filled with the sizeof(CFE_FS_Header_t)\n      -# \\link #CFE_FS_Header_t::SpacecraftID \\c SpacecraftID \\endlink - Filled with the Spacecraft ID\n      -# \\link #CFE_FS_Header_t::ProcessorID \\c ProcessorID \\endlink - Filled with the Processor ID\n      -# \\link #CFE_FS_Header_t::ApplicationID \\c ApplicationID \\endlink -  Filled with the Application ID\n      -# \\link #CFE_FS_Header_t::TimeSeconds \\c TimeSeconds \\endlink - Filled with the Time, in seconds, as obtained\n                                                                       by #CFE_TIME_GetTime\n      -# \\link #CFE_FS_Header_t::TimeSubSeconds \\c TimeSubSeconds \\endlink - Filled with the Time, subseconds, as\n                                                                             obtained by #CFE_TIME_GetTime\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        -# The File has already been successfully opened using #OS_OpenCreate and\n           the caller has a legitimate File Descriptor.\n        -# The \\c SubType field has been filled appropriately by the Application.\n        -# The \\c Description field has been filled appropriately by the Application.\n        -# File offset behavior: Agnostic on entry since it will move the offset to the start of the file,\n           on success the offset will be at the end of the header, undefined offset behavior for error cases.\n\n \\param[in] FileDes File Descriptor obtained from a previous call to #OS_OpenCreate\n                    that is associated with the file whose header is to be read.\n\n \\param[out] Hdr    Pointer to a variable of type #CFE_FS_Header_t @nonnull that will be\n                    filled with the contents of the Standard cFE File Header. *Hdr is the contents of the\n                    Standard cFE File Header for the specified file.\n\n \\return Bytes read or error status from OSAL\n \\retval #CFE_FS_BAD_ARGUMENT  \\copybrief CFE_FS_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\note This function invokes OSAL API routines and the current implementation may return\n       OSAL error codes to the caller if failure occurs.  In a future version of CFE, the\n       status codes will be converted to a value in \\ref CFEReturnCodes.\n\n \\sa #CFE_FS_ReadHeader"]
3310    pub fn CFE_FS_WriteHeader(FileDes: osal_id_t, Hdr: *mut CFE_FS_Header_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3311}
3312unsafe extern "C" {
3313    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Modifies the Time Stamp field in the Standard cFE File Header for the specified file\n\n \\par Description\n        This API will modify the \\link #CFE_FS_Header_t::TimeSeconds timestamp \\endlink found\n        in the Standard cFE File Header of the specified file.  The timestamp will be replaced\n        with the time specified by the caller.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        -# The File has already been successfully opened using #OS_OpenCreate and\n           the caller has a legitimate File Descriptor.\n        -# The \\c NewTimestamp field has been filled appropriately by the Application.\n        -# File offset behavior: Agnostic on entry since it will move the offset,\n           on success the offset will be at the end of the time stamp, undefined offset behavior for error cases.\n\n \\param[in] FileDes File Descriptor obtained from a previous call to #OS_OpenCreate\n                    that is associated with the file whose header is to be read.\n\n \\param[in] NewTimestamp A #CFE_TIME_SysTime_t data structure containing the desired time\n                         to be put into the file's Standard cFE File Header.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes, or OSAL status\n \\retval #CFE_STATUS_EXTERNAL_RESOURCE_FAIL  \\covtest \\copybrief CFE_STATUS_EXTERNAL_RESOURCE_FAIL\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                        \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n\n \\note This function invokes OSAL API routines and the current implementation may return\n       OSAL error codes to the caller if failure occurs.  In a future version of CFE, the\n       status codes will be converted to a value in \\ref CFEReturnCodes."]
3314    pub fn CFE_FS_SetTimestamp(
3315        FileDes: osal_id_t,
3316        NewTimestamp: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t,
3317    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3318}
3319unsafe extern "C" {
3320    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get the default virtual mount point for a file category\n\n Certain classes of files generally reside in a common directory, mainly\n either the persistent storage (/cf typically) or ram disk (/ram typically).\n\n Ephemeral status files are generally in the ram disk while application\n modules and scripts are generally in the persistent storage.\n\n This returns the expected directory for a given class of files in the form\n of a virtual OSAL mount point string.\n\n \\returns String containing the mount point\n \\retval  NULL if no mount point is known for the given file category"]
3321    pub fn CFE_FS_GetDefaultMountPoint(FileCategory: CFE_FS_FileCategory_t) -> *const libc::c_char;
3322}
3323unsafe extern "C" {
3324    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get the default filename extension for a file category\n\n Certain file types may have an extension that varies from system to system.\n This is primarily an issue for application modules which are \".so\" on\n Linux systems, \".dll\" on Windows, \".o\" on VxWorks, \".obj\" on RTEMS, and so on.\n\n This uses a combination of compile-time configuration and hints from the\n build environment to get the default/expected extension for a given file\n category.\n\n \\returns String containing the extension\n \\retval  NULL if no default extension is known for the given file category"]
3325    pub fn CFE_FS_GetDefaultExtension(FileCategory: CFE_FS_FileCategory_t) -> *const libc::c_char;
3326}
3327unsafe extern "C" {
3328    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Parse a filename input from an input buffer into a local buffer\n\n \\par Description\n        This provides a more user friendly way to specify file names,\n        using default values for the path and extension, which can\n        vary from system to system.\n\n        If InputBuffer is null or its length is zero, then DefaultInput\n        is used as if it was the content of the input buffer.\n\n        If either the pathname or extension is missing from the input,\n        it will be added from defaults, with the complete fully-qualified\n        filename stored in the output buffer.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        -# The paths and filenames used here are the standard unix style\n            filenames separated by \"/\" (path) and \".\" (extension) characters.\n        -# Input Buffer has a fixed max length.  Parsing will not exceed InputBufSize,\n            and does not need to be null terminated.  However parsing will stop\n            at the first null char, when the input is shorter than the maximum.\n\n \\param[out] OutputBuffer     Buffer to store result @nonnull.\n \\param[in]  InputBuffer      A input buffer that may contain a file name (e.g. from command) @nonnull.\n \\param[in]  OutputBufSize    Maximum Size of output buffer @nonzero.\n \\param[in]  InputBufSize     Maximum Size of input buffer.\n \\param[in]  DefaultInput     Default value to use for input if InputBffer is empty\n \\param[in]  DefaultPath      Default value to use for pathname if omitted from input\n \\param[in]  DefaultExtension Default value to use for extension if omitted from input\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_FS_BAD_ARGUMENT    \\copybrief CFE_FS_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_FS_FNAME_TOO_LONG  \\copybrief CFE_FS_FNAME_TOO_LONG\n \\retval #CFE_FS_INVALID_PATH    \\copybrief CFE_FS_INVALID_PATH\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS            \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS"]
3329    pub fn CFE_FS_ParseInputFileNameEx(
3330        OutputBuffer: *mut libc::c_char,
3331        InputBuffer: *const libc::c_char,
3332        OutputBufSize: usize,
3333        InputBufSize: usize,
3334        DefaultInput: *const libc::c_char,
3335        DefaultPath: *const libc::c_char,
3336        DefaultExtension: *const libc::c_char,
3337    ) -> int32;
3338}
3339unsafe extern "C" {
3340    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Parse a filename string from the user into a local buffer\n\n \\par Description\n        Simplified API for CFE_FS_ParseInputFileNameEx() where input is\n        always known to be a non-empty, null terminated string and the fixed-length\n        input buffer not needed.  For instance this may be used where\n        the input is a fixed string from cfe_platform_cfg.h or similar.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        The parameters are organized such that this is basically like strncpy() with an\n        extra argument, and existing file name accesses which use a direct copy can\n        easily change to use this instead.\n\n \\sa CFE_FS_ParseInputFileNameEx()\n\n \\param[out] OutputBuffer     Buffer to store result @nonnull.\n \\param[in]  InputName        A null terminated input string @nonnull.\n \\param[in]  OutputBufSize    Maximum Size of output buffer @nonzero.\n \\param[in]  FileCategory     The generalized category of file (implies default path/extension)\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n\n---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------"]
3341    pub fn CFE_FS_ParseInputFileName(
3342        OutputBuffer: *mut libc::c_char,
3343        InputName: *const libc::c_char,
3344        OutputBufSize: usize,
3345        FileCategory: CFE_FS_FileCategory_t,
3346    ) -> int32;
3347}
3348unsafe extern "C" {
3349    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Extracts the filename from a unix style path and filename string.\n\n \\par Description\n        This API will take the original unix path/filename combination and\n        extract the base filename. Example: Given the path/filename : \"/cf/apps/myapp.o.gz\"\n        this function will return the filename: \"myapp.o.gz\".\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        -# The paths and filenames used here are the standard unix style\n            filenames separated by \"/\" characters.\n        -# The extracted filename (including terminator) is no longer than #OS_MAX_PATH_LEN\n\n \\param[in] OriginalPath The original path @nonnull\n \\param[out] FileNameOnly The filename that is extracted from the path @nonnull\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_FS_BAD_ARGUMENT    \\copybrief CFE_FS_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_FS_FNAME_TOO_LONG  \\copybrief CFE_FS_FNAME_TOO_LONG\n \\retval #CFE_FS_INVALID_PATH    \\copybrief CFE_FS_INVALID_PATH\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS            \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS"]
3350    pub fn CFE_FS_ExtractFilenameFromPath(
3351        OriginalPath: *const libc::c_char,
3352        FileNameOnly: *mut libc::c_char,
3353    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3354}
3355unsafe extern "C" {
3356    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Register a background file dump request\n\n \\par Description\n        Puts the previously-initialized metadata into the pending request queue\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        Metadata structure should be stored in a persistent memory area (not on stack) as\n        it must remain accessible by the file writer task throughout the asynchronous\n        job operation.\n\n \\param[inout] Meta        The background file write persistent state object @nonnull\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_FS_BAD_ARGUMENT                 \\copybrief CFE_FS_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_FS_INVALID_PATH                 \\copybrief CFE_FS_INVALID_PATH\n \\retval #CFE_STATUS_REQUEST_ALREADY_PENDING  \\copybrief CFE_STATUS_REQUEST_ALREADY_PENDING\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                         \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS"]
3357    pub fn CFE_FS_BackgroundFileDumpRequest(Meta: *mut CFE_FS_FileWriteMetaData_t) -> int32;
3358}
3359unsafe extern "C" {
3360    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Query if a background file write request is currently pending\n\n \\par Description\n        This returns \"true\" while the request is on the background work queue\n        This returns \"false\" once the request is complete and removed from the queue.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        None\n\n \\param[inout] Meta        The background file write persistent state object @nonnull\n\n \\return boolean value indicating if request is already pending\n \\retval true if request is pending\n \\retval false if request is not pending"]
3361    pub fn CFE_FS_BackgroundFileDumpIsPending(Meta: *const CFE_FS_FileWriteMetaData_t) -> bool;
3362}
3363#[doc = " @brief Normal priority level"]
3364pub const CFE_SB_QosPriority_CFE_SB_QosPriority_LOW: CFE_SB_QosPriority = 0;
3365#[doc = " @brief High priority"]
3366pub const CFE_SB_QosPriority_CFE_SB_QosPriority_HIGH: CFE_SB_QosPriority = 1;
3367#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_SB_QosPriority_Enum_t"]
3368pub type CFE_SB_QosPriority = libc::c_uint;
3369#[doc = " @brief Selects the priority level for message routing\n\n @sa enum CFE_SB_QosPriority"]
3370pub type CFE_SB_QosPriority_Enum_t = uint8;
3371#[doc = " @brief Normal (best-effort) reliability"]
3372pub const CFE_SB_QosReliability_CFE_SB_QosReliability_LOW: CFE_SB_QosReliability = 0;
3373#[doc = " @brief High reliability"]
3374pub const CFE_SB_QosReliability_CFE_SB_QosReliability_HIGH: CFE_SB_QosReliability = 1;
3375#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_SB_QosReliability_Enum_t"]
3376pub type CFE_SB_QosReliability = libc::c_uint;
3377#[doc = " @brief Selects the reliability level for message routing\n\n @sa enum CFE_SB_QosReliability"]
3378pub type CFE_SB_QosReliability_Enum_t = uint8;
3379#[doc = " @brief An integer type that should be used for indexing into the Routing Table"]
3380pub type CFE_SB_RouteId_Atom_t = uint16;
3381#[doc = " @brief  CFE_SB_MsgId_Atom_t primitive type definition\n\n This is an integer type capable of holding any Message ID value\n Note: This value is limited via #CFE_PLATFORM_SB_HIGHEST_VALID_MSGID"]
3382pub type CFE_SB_MsgId_Atom_t = uint32;
3383#[doc = " @brief  CFE_SB_MsgId_t type definition\n\n Software Bus message identifier used in many SB APIs\n\n Currently this is directly mapped to the underlying holding type (not wrapped) for\n compatibility with existing usage semantics in apps (mainly switch/case statements)\n\n @note In a future version it could become a type-safe wrapper similar to the route index,\n to avoid message IDs getting mixed between other integer values."]
3384#[repr(C)]
3385#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
3386pub struct CFE_SB_MsgId_t {
3387    pub Value: CFE_SB_MsgId_Atom_t,
3388}
3389#[doc = " \\brief  CFE_SB_PipeId_t to primitive type definition\n\n Software Bus pipe identifier used in many SB APIs, as well as SB Telemetry messages\n and data files."]
3390pub type CFE_SB_PipeId_t = CFE_ResourceId_t;
3391#[doc = " \\brief Quality Of Service Type Definition\n\n Currently an unused parameter in #CFE_SB_SubscribeEx\n Intended to be used for interprocessor communication only"]
3392#[repr(C)]
3393#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
3394pub struct CFE_SB_Qos_t {
3395    #[doc = "< \\brief  Specify high(1) or low(0) message priority for off-board routing, currently unused"]
3396    pub Priority: uint8,
3397    #[doc = "< \\brief  Specify high(1) or low(0) message transfer reliability for off-board routing,\ncurrently unused"]
3398    pub Reliability: uint8,
3399}
3400pub type CFE_MSG_Size_t = usize;
3401pub type CFE_MSG_Checksum_t = uint32;
3402pub type CFE_MSG_FcnCode_t = uint16;
3403pub type CFE_MSG_HeaderVersion_t = uint16;
3404pub type CFE_MSG_ApId_t = uint16;
3405pub type CFE_MSG_SequenceCount_t = uint16;
3406pub type CFE_MSG_EDSVersion_t = uint16;
3407pub type CFE_MSG_Subsystem_t = uint16;
3408pub type CFE_MSG_System_t = uint16;
3409#[doc = "< \\brief Message type invalid, undefined, not implemented"]
3410pub const CFE_MSG_Type_CFE_MSG_Type_Invalid: CFE_MSG_Type = 0;
3411#[doc = "< \\brief Command message type"]
3412pub const CFE_MSG_Type_CFE_MSG_Type_Cmd: CFE_MSG_Type = 1;
3413#[doc = "< \\brief Telemetry message type"]
3414pub const CFE_MSG_Type_CFE_MSG_Type_Tlm: CFE_MSG_Type = 2;

3416pub type CFE_MSG_Type = libc::c_uint;

3418pub use self::CFE_MSG_Type as CFE_MSG_Type_t;
3419#[doc = "< \\brief Invalid segmentation flag"]
3420pub const CFE_MSG_SegmentationFlag_CFE_MSG_SegFlag_Invalid: CFE_MSG_SegmentationFlag = 0;

3424pub const CFE_MSG_SegmentationFlag_CFE_MSG_SegFlag_First: CFE_MSG_SegmentationFlag = 2;
3425#[doc = "< \\brief Last segment of User Data"]
3426pub const CFE_MSG_SegmentationFlag_CFE_MSG_SegFlag_Last: CFE_MSG_SegmentationFlag = 3;
3427#[doc = "< \\brief Unsegmented data"]
3428pub const CFE_MSG_SegmentationFlag_CFE_MSG_SegFlag_Unsegmented: CFE_MSG_SegmentationFlag = 4;
3429#[doc = " \\brief Segmentation flags"]
3430pub type CFE_MSG_SegmentationFlag = libc::c_uint;
3431#[doc = " \\brief Segmentation flags"]
3432pub use self::CFE_MSG_SegmentationFlag as CFE_MSG_SegmentationFlag_t;
3433#[doc = "< \\brief Invalid endian setting"]
3434pub const CFE_MSG_Endian_CFE_MSG_Endian_Invalid: CFE_MSG_Endian = 0;
3435#[doc = "< \\brief Big endian"]
3436pub const CFE_MSG_Endian_CFE_MSG_Endian_Big: CFE_MSG_Endian = 1;
3437#[doc = "< \\brief Little endian"]
3438pub const CFE_MSG_Endian_CFE_MSG_Endian_Little: CFE_MSG_Endian = 2;
3439#[doc = " \\brief Endian flag"]
3440pub type CFE_MSG_Endian = libc::c_uint;
3441#[doc = " \\brief Endian flag"]
3442pub use self::CFE_MSG_Endian as CFE_MSG_Endian_t;
3443#[doc = "< \\brief Invalid playback setting"]
3444pub const CFE_MSG_PlaybackFlag_CFE_MSG_PlayFlag_Invalid: CFE_MSG_PlaybackFlag = 0;
3445#[doc = "< \\brief Original"]
3446pub const CFE_MSG_PlaybackFlag_CFE_MSG_PlayFlag_Original: CFE_MSG_PlaybackFlag = 1;
3447#[doc = "< \\brief Playback"]
3448pub const CFE_MSG_PlaybackFlag_CFE_MSG_PlayFlag_Playback: CFE_MSG_PlaybackFlag = 2;

3452pub use self::CFE_MSG_PlaybackFlag as CFE_MSG_PlaybackFlag_t;

3454pub type CFE_MSG_Message_t = CFE_MSG_Message;
3455#[doc = " \\brief cFS command header"]
3456pub type CFE_MSG_CommandHeader_t = CFE_MSG_CommandHeader;
3457#[doc = " \\brief cFS telemetry header"]
3458pub type CFE_MSG_TelemetryHeader_t = CFE_MSG_TelemetryHeader;
3459#[doc = " \\brief CCSDS packet primary header"]
3460#[repr(C)]
3461#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
3462pub struct CCSDS_PrimaryHeader {
3463    #[doc = "< \\brief packet identifier word (stream ID)"]
3464    pub StreamId: [uint8; 2usize],
3465    #[doc = "< \\brief packet sequence word"]
3466    pub Sequence: [uint8; 2usize],
3467    #[doc = "< \\brief packet length word"]
3468    pub Length: [uint8; 2usize],
3469}
3470#[doc = " \\brief CCSDS packet primary header"]
3471pub type CCSDS_PrimaryHeader_t = CCSDS_PrimaryHeader;
3472#[doc = " \\brief cFS command secondary header"]
3473#[repr(C)]
3474#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
3475pub struct CFE_MSG_CommandSecondaryHeader_t {
3476    #[doc = "< \\brief Command Function Code"]
3477    pub FunctionCode: uint8,
3478    #[doc = "< \\brief Command checksum  (all bits, 0xFF)"]
3479    pub Checksum: uint8,
3480}
3481#[doc = " \\brief cFS telemetry secondary header"]
3482#[repr(C)]
3483#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
3484pub struct CFE_MSG_TelemetrySecondaryHeader_t {
3485    #[doc = "< \\brief Time, big endian: 4 byte seconds, 2 byte subseconds"]
3486    pub Time: [uint8; 6usize],
3487}
3488#[doc = " \\brief Full CCSDS header"]
3489#[repr(C)]
3490#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
3491pub struct CCSDS_SpacePacket_t {
3492    #[doc = "< \\brief CCSDS Primary Header"]
3493    pub Pri: CCSDS_PrimaryHeader_t,
3494}
3495#[doc = " \\brief cFS generic base message\n\n This provides the definition of CFE_MSG_Message_t"]
3496#[repr(C)]
3497#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
3498pub struct CFE_MSG_Message {
3499    #[doc = "< \\brief CCSDS Header (Pri or Pri + Ext)"]
3500    pub CCSDS: CCSDS_SpacePacket_t,
3501}
3502#[doc = " \\brief cFS command header\n\n This provides the definition of CFE_MSG_CommandHeader_t"]
3503#[repr(C)]
3504#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
3505pub struct CFE_MSG_CommandHeader {
3506    #[doc = "< \\brief Base message"]
3507    pub Msg: CFE_MSG_Message_t,
3508    #[doc = "< \\brief Secondary header"]
3509    pub Sec: CFE_MSG_CommandSecondaryHeader_t,
3510}
3511#[doc = " \\brief cFS telemetry header\n\n This provides the definition of CFE_MSG_TelemetryHeader_t"]
3512#[repr(C)]
3513#[derive(Debug, Default, Copy, Clone)]
3514pub struct CFE_MSG_TelemetryHeader {
3515    #[doc = "< \\brief Base message"]
3516    pub Msg: CFE_MSG_Message_t,
3517    #[doc = "< \\brief Secondary header"]
3518    pub Sec: CFE_MSG_TelemetrySecondaryHeader_t,
3519    #[doc = "< \\brief Pad to avoid compiler padding if payload\nrequires 64 bit alignment"]
3520    pub Spare: [uint8; 4usize],
3521}
3522#[doc = " \\brief Software Bus generic message"]
3523#[repr(C)]
3524#[repr(align(16))]
3525#[derive(Copy, Clone)]
3526pub union CFE_SB_Msg {
3527    #[doc = "< \\brief Base message type without enforced alignment"]
3528    pub Msg: CFE_MSG_Message_t,
3529    #[doc = "< \\brief Align to support Long Integer"]
3530    pub LongInt: libc::c_longlong,
3531    #[doc = "< \\brief Align to support Long Double"]
3532    pub LongDouble: u128,
3533}
3534impl Default for CFE_SB_Msg {
3535    fn default() -> Self {
3536        let mut s = ::core::mem::MaybeUninit::<Self>::uninit();
3537        unsafe {
3538            ::core::ptr::write_bytes(s.as_mut_ptr(), 0, 1);
3539            s.assume_init()
3540        }
3541    }
3542}
3543#[doc = " \\brief Software Bus generic message"]
3544pub type CFE_SB_Buffer_t = CFE_SB_Msg;
3545unsafe extern "C" {
3546    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Creates a new software bus pipe.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine creates and initializes an input pipe that the calling\n          application can use to receive software bus messages.  By default, no\n          messages are routed to the new pipe.  So, the application must use\n          #CFE_SB_Subscribe to specify which messages it wants to receive on\n          this pipe.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[out]  PipeIdPtr   A pointer to a variable of type #CFE_SB_PipeId_t @nonnull,\n                          which will be filled in with the pipe ID information\n                          by the #CFE_SB_CreatePipe routine. *PipeIdPtr is the identifier for the created pipe.\n\n \\param[in]  Depth        The maximum number of messages that will be allowed on\n                          this pipe at one time.\n\n \\param[in]  PipeName     A string @nonnull to be used to identify this pipe in error messages\n                          and routing information telemetry.  The string must be no\n                          longer than #OS_MAX_API_NAME (including terminator).\n                          Longer strings will be truncated.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS          \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT  \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_SB_MAX_PIPES_MET \\copybrief CFE_SB_MAX_PIPES_MET\n \\retval #CFE_SB_PIPE_CR_ERR   \\copybrief CFE_SB_PIPE_CR_ERR\n\n \\sa #CFE_SB_DeletePipe #CFE_SB_GetPipeOpts #CFE_SB_SetPipeOpts #CFE_SB_GetPipeIdByName"]
3547    pub fn CFE_SB_CreatePipe(
3548        PipeIdPtr: *mut CFE_SB_PipeId_t,
3549        Depth: uint16,
3550        PipeName: *const libc::c_char,
3551    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3552}
3553unsafe extern "C" {
3554    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Delete a software bus pipe.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine deletes an input pipe and cleans up all data structures\n          associated with the pipe.  All subscriptions made for this pipe by\n          calls to #CFE_SB_Subscribe will be automatically removed from the\n          SB routing tables.  Any messages in the pipe will be discarded.\n\n          Applications should not call this routine for all of their\n          SB pipes as part of their orderly shutdown process, as the\n          pipe will be deleted by the support framework at the\n          appropriate time.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in]  PipeId       The pipe ID (obtained previously from #CFE_SB_CreatePipe)\n                          of the pipe to be deleted.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS         \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_SB_CreatePipe #CFE_SB_GetPipeOpts #CFE_SB_SetPipeOpts #CFE_SB_GetPipeIdByName"]
3555    pub fn CFE_SB_DeletePipe(PipeId: CFE_SB_PipeId_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3556}
3557unsafe extern "C" {
3558    #[doc = " @brief Obtain an index value correlating to an SB Pipe ID\n\n This calculates a zero based integer value that may be used for indexing\n into a local resource table/array.\n\n Index values are only guaranteed to be unique for resources of the same\n type.  For instance, the indices corresponding to two [valid] application\n IDs will never overlap, but the index of a pipe ID and an app ID\n may be the same.  Furthermore, indices may be reused if a resource is\n deleted and re-created.\n\n @note There is no inverse of this function - indices cannot be converted\n back to the original PipeID value.  The caller should retain the original ID\n for future use.\n\n @param[in]   PipeID  Pipe ID to convert\n @param[out]  Idx    Buffer where the calculated index will be stored @nonnull\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref CFEReturnCodes\n @retval #CFE_SUCCESS                      @copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n @retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID  @copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID"]
3559    pub fn CFE_SB_PipeId_ToIndex(PipeID: CFE_SB_PipeId_t, Idx: *mut uint32) -> CFE_Status_t;
3560}
3561unsafe extern "C" {
3562    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Set options on a pipe.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine sets (or clears) options to alter the pipe's behavior.\n          Options are (re)set every call to this routine.\n\n \\param[in]  PipeId       The pipe ID of the pipe to set options on.\n\n \\param[in]  Opts         A bit field of options: \\ref CFESBPipeOptions\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS         \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_SB_CreatePipe #CFE_SB_DeletePipe #CFE_SB_GetPipeOpts #CFE_SB_GetPipeIdByName #CFE_SB_PIPEOPTS_IGNOREMINE"]
3563    pub fn CFE_SB_SetPipeOpts(PipeId: CFE_SB_PipeId_t, Opts: uint8) -> CFE_Status_t;
3564}
3565unsafe extern "C" {
3566    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get options on a pipe.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine gets the current options on a pipe.\n\n \\param[in]  PipeId       The pipe ID of the pipe to get options from.\n\n \\param[out] OptsPtr     A bit field of options: \\ref CFESBPipeOptions  @nonnull\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS         \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_SB_CreatePipe #CFE_SB_DeletePipe #CFE_SB_SetPipeOpts #CFE_SB_GetPipeIdByName #CFE_SB_PIPEOPTS_IGNOREMINE"]
3567    pub fn CFE_SB_GetPipeOpts(PipeId: CFE_SB_PipeId_t, OptsPtr: *mut uint8) -> CFE_Status_t;
3568}
3569unsafe extern "C" {
3570    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get the pipe name for a given id.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine finds the pipe name for a pipe id.\n\n \\param[out] PipeNameBuf  The buffer to receive the pipe name @nonnull.\n\n \\param[in] PipeNameSize  The size (in chars) of the PipeName buffer @nonzero.\n\n \\param[in] PipeId        The PipeId for that name.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS         \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_SB_CreatePipe #CFE_SB_DeletePipe #CFE_SB_SetPipeOpts #CFE_SB_GetPipeIdByName"]
3571    pub fn CFE_SB_GetPipeName(
3572        PipeNameBuf: *mut libc::c_char,
3573        PipeNameSize: usize,
3574        PipeId: CFE_SB_PipeId_t,
3575    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3576}
3577unsafe extern "C" {
3578    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get pipe id by pipe name.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine finds the pipe id for a pipe name.\n\n \\param[in]  PipeName     The name of the pipe @nonnull.\n\n \\param[out] PipeIdPtr    The PipeId for that name @nonnull.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS         \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_SB_CreatePipe #CFE_SB_DeletePipe #CFE_SB_SetPipeOpts #CFE_SB_PIPEOPTS_IGNOREMINE"]
3579    pub fn CFE_SB_GetPipeIdByName(
3580        PipeIdPtr: *mut CFE_SB_PipeId_t,
3581        PipeName: *const libc::c_char,
3582    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3583}
3584unsafe extern "C" {
3585    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Subscribe to a message on the software bus\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine adds the specified pipe to the destination list associated\n          with the specified message ID.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          Note:   As subscriptions are received, the destinations are added to\n          the head of a linked list. During the sending of a message, the list\n          is traversed beginning at the head of the list. Therefore the\n          message will first be sent to the last subscriber. If an application\n          has timing constraints and needs to receive a message in the\n          shortest possible time, the developer may consider holding off its\n          subscription until other applications have subscribed to the message.\n\n \\param[in]  MsgId        The message ID of the message to be subscribed to.\n\n \\param[in]  PipeId       The pipe ID of the pipe the subscribed message\n                          should be sent to.\n\n \\param[in]  Quality      The requested Quality of Service (QoS) required of\n                          the messages. Most callers will use #CFE_SB_DEFAULT_QOS\n                          for this parameter.\n\n \\param[in]  MsgLim       The maximum number of messages with this Message ID to\n                          allow in this pipe at the same time.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS          \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_MAX_MSGS_MET  \\covtest \\copybrief CFE_SB_MAX_MSGS_MET\n \\retval #CFE_SB_MAX_DESTS_MET \\copybrief CFE_SB_MAX_DESTS_MET\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT  \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BUF_ALOC_ERR  \\covtest \\copybrief CFE_SB_BUF_ALOC_ERR\n\n \\sa #CFE_SB_Subscribe, #CFE_SB_SubscribeLocal, #CFE_SB_Unsubscribe, #CFE_SB_UnsubscribeLocal"]
3586    pub fn CFE_SB_SubscribeEx(
3587        MsgId: CFE_SB_MsgId_t,
3588        PipeId: CFE_SB_PipeId_t,
3589        Quality: CFE_SB_Qos_t,
3590        MsgLim: uint16,
3591    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3592}
3593unsafe extern "C" {
3594    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Subscribe to a message on the software bus with default parameters\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine adds the specified pipe to the destination list for\n          the specified message ID.  This is the same as #CFE_SB_SubscribeEx\n          with the Quality field set to #CFE_SB_DEFAULT_QOS and MsgLim set\n          to #CFE_PLATFORM_SB_DEFAULT_MSG_LIMIT (4).\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          Note:   As subscriptions are received, the destinations are added to\n          the head of a linked list. During the sending of a message, the list\n          is traversed beginning at the head of the list. Therefore the\n          message will first be sent to the last subscriber. If an application\n          has timing constraints and needs to receive a message in the\n          shortest possible time, the developer may consider holding off its\n          subscription until other applications have subscribed to the message.\n\n \\param[in]  MsgId        The message ID of the message to be subscribed to.\n\n \\param[in]  PipeId       The pipe ID of the pipe the subscribed message\n                          should be sent to.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS          \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_MAX_MSGS_MET  \\covtest \\copybrief CFE_SB_MAX_MSGS_MET\n \\retval #CFE_SB_MAX_DESTS_MET \\copybrief CFE_SB_MAX_DESTS_MET\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT  \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BUF_ALOC_ERR  \\covtest \\copybrief CFE_SB_BUF_ALOC_ERR\n\n \\sa #CFE_SB_SubscribeEx, #CFE_SB_SubscribeLocal, #CFE_SB_Unsubscribe, #CFE_SB_UnsubscribeLocal"]
3595    pub fn CFE_SB_Subscribe(MsgId: CFE_SB_MsgId_t, PipeId: CFE_SB_PipeId_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3596}
3597unsafe extern "C" {
3598    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Subscribe to a message while keeping the request local to a cpu\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine adds the specified pipe to the destination list for\n          the specified message ID.  This is similar to #CFE_SB_SubscribeEx\n          with the Quality field set to #CFE_SB_DEFAULT_QOS and MsgLim set\n          to #CFE_PLATFORM_SB_DEFAULT_MSG_LIMIT, but will not report the subscription.\n\n          Software Bus Network (SBN) application is an example use case,\n          where local subscriptions should not be reported to peers.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          - This API is typically only used by Software Bus Network (SBN) Application\n\n \\param[in]  MsgId        The message ID of the message to be subscribed to.\n\n \\param[in]  PipeId       The pipe ID of the pipe the subscribed message\n                          should be sent to.\n\n \\param[in]  MsgLim       The maximum number of messages with this Message ID to\n                          allow in this pipe at the same time.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS          \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_MAX_MSGS_MET  \\covtest \\copybrief CFE_SB_MAX_MSGS_MET\n \\retval #CFE_SB_MAX_DESTS_MET \\copybrief CFE_SB_MAX_DESTS_MET\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT  \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BUF_ALOC_ERR  \\covtest \\copybrief CFE_SB_BUF_ALOC_ERR\n\n \\sa #CFE_SB_Subscribe, #CFE_SB_SubscribeEx, #CFE_SB_Unsubscribe, #CFE_SB_UnsubscribeLocal"]
3599    pub fn CFE_SB_SubscribeLocal(
3600        MsgId: CFE_SB_MsgId_t,
3601        PipeId: CFE_SB_PipeId_t,
3602        MsgLim: uint16,
3603    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3604}
3605unsafe extern "C" {
