3606    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Remove a subscription to a message on the software bus\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine removes the specified pipe from the destination\n          list for the specified message ID.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          If the Pipe is not subscribed to MsgId, the CFE_SB_UNSUB_NO_SUBS_EID\n          event will be generated and #CFE_SUCCESS will be returned\n\n \\param[in]  MsgId        The message ID of the message to be unsubscribed.\n\n \\param[in]  PipeId       The pipe ID of the pipe the subscribed message\n                          should no longer be sent to.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS           \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT   \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_SB_Subscribe, #CFE_SB_SubscribeEx, #CFE_SB_SubscribeLocal, #CFE_SB_UnsubscribeLocal"]
3607    pub fn CFE_SB_Unsubscribe(MsgId: CFE_SB_MsgId_t, PipeId: CFE_SB_PipeId_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3608}
3609unsafe extern "C" {
3610    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Remove a subscription to a message on the software bus on the current CPU\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine removes the specified pipe from the destination\n          list for the specified message ID on the current CPU.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          This API is typically only used by Software Bus Network (SBN) Application.\n          If the Pipe is not subscribed to MsgId, the CFE_SB_UNSUB_NO_SUBS_EID\n          event will be generated and #CFE_SUCCESS will be returned\n\n \\param[in]  MsgId        The message ID of the message to be unsubscribed.\n\n \\param[in]  PipeId       The pipe ID of the pipe the subscribed message\n                          should no longer be sent to.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS           \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT   \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_SB_Subscribe, #CFE_SB_SubscribeEx, #CFE_SB_SubscribeLocal, #CFE_SB_Unsubscribe"]
3611    pub fn CFE_SB_UnsubscribeLocal(MsgId: CFE_SB_MsgId_t, PipeId: CFE_SB_PipeId_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3612}
3613unsafe extern "C" {
3614    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Transmit a message\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine copies the specified message into a software bus\n          buffer which is then transmitted to all subscribers.  The\n          software bus will read the message ID from the message header to\n          determine which pipes should receive the message.\n\n          The IsOrigination parameter should be passed as \"true\" if the message was\n          newly constructed by the sender and is being sent for the first time.  This\n          enables the message origination actions as determined by the CFE MSG module,\n          which may include (but not limited to):\n            - Updating sequence number\n            - Updating timestamp\n            - Calcualating a CRC, checksum, or other message error control field\n\n          Conversely, when forwarding a message that originated from\n          an external entity (e.g. messages passing through CI or SBN), the\n          parameter should be passed as \"false\" to not overwrite existing data.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          - This routine will not normally wait for the receiver tasks to\n            process the message before returning control to the caller's task.\n          - However, if a higher priority task is pending and subscribed to\n            this message, that task may get to run before returning\n            control to the caller.\n          - In previous versions of CFE, the boolean parameter referred to the\n            sequence number header of telemetry messages only.  This has been\n            extended to apply more generically to any headers, as determined by\n            the CFE MSG implementation.\n\n \\param[in]  MsgPtr       A pointer to the message to be sent @nonnull.  This must point\n                          to the first byte of the message header.\n \\param[in] IsOrigination  Update the headers of the message\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS         \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_SB_MSG_TOO_BIG  \\copybrief CFE_SB_MSG_TOO_BIG\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BUF_ALOC_ERR \\covtest \\copybrief CFE_SB_BUF_ALOC_ERR"]
3615    pub fn CFE_SB_TransmitMsg(
3616        MsgPtr: *const CFE_MSG_Message_t,
3617        IsOrigination: bool,
3618    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3619}
3620unsafe extern "C" {
3621    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Receive a message from a software bus pipe\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine retrieves the next message from the specified pipe.\n          If the pipe is empty, this routine will block until either a new\n          message comes in or the timeout value is reached.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          Note - If an error occurs in this API, the *BufPtr value may be NULL or\n          random. Therefore, it is recommended that the return code be tested\n          for CFE_SUCCESS before processing the message.\n\n \\param[in, out] BufPtr   A pointer to the software bus buffer to receive to @nonnull.\n                          Typically a caller declares a ptr of type CFE_SB_Buffer_t\n                          (i.e. CFE_SB_Buffer_t *Ptr) then gives the address of that\n                          pointer (&Ptr) as this parameter. After a successful\n                          receipt of a message, *BufPtr will point to the first\n                          byte of the software bus buffer. This should be\n                          used as a read-only pointer (in systems with an MMU,\n                          writes to this pointer may cause a memory protection fault).\n                          The *BufPtr is valid only until the next call to\n                          CFE_SB_ReceiveBuffer for the same pipe.\n\n \\param[in]  PipeId       The pipe ID of the pipe containing the message to be obtained.\n\n \\param[in]  TimeOut      The number of milliseconds to wait for a new message if the\n                          pipe is empty at the time of the call.  This can also be set\n                          to #CFE_SB_POLL for a non-blocking receive or\n                          #CFE_SB_PEND_FOREVER to wait forever for a message to arrive.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS         \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_SB_TIME_OUT     \\copybrief CFE_SB_TIME_OUT\n \\retval #CFE_SB_PIPE_RD_ERR  \\covtest \\copybrief CFE_SB_PIPE_RD_ERR\n \\retval #CFE_SB_NO_MESSAGE   \\copybrief CFE_SB_NO_MESSAGE"]
3622    pub fn CFE_SB_ReceiveBuffer(
3623        BufPtr: *mut *mut CFE_SB_Buffer_t,
3624        PipeId: CFE_SB_PipeId_t,
3625        TimeOut: int32,
3626    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3627}
3628unsafe extern "C" {
3629    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get a buffer pointer to use for \"zero copy\" SB sends.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine can be used to get a pointer to one of the software bus'\n          internal memory buffers that are used for sending messages.  The caller\n          can use this memory buffer to build an SB message, then send it using\n          the CFE_SB_TransmitBuffer() function.  This interface avoids an extra\n          copy of the message from the user's memory buffer to the software bus\n          internal buffer.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          -# The pointer returned by CFE_SB_AllocateMessageBuffer() is only good for one\n             call to CFE_SB_TransmitBuffer().\n          -# Once a buffer has been successfully transmitted (as indicated by a successful\n             return from CFE_SB_TransmitBuffer()) the buffer becomes owned by the SB application.\n             It will automatically be freed by SB once all recipients have finished reading it.\n          -# Applications must not de-reference the message pointer (for reading\n             or writing) after the call to CFE_SB_TransmitBuffer().\n          -# If #CFE_SB_ReleaseMessageBuffer should be used only if a message is not transmitted\n\n \\param[in]  MsgSize  The size of the SB message buffer the caller wants\n                      (including the SB message header).\n\n \\return A pointer to a memory buffer that message data can be written to\n         for use with CFE_SB_TransmitBuffer()."]
3630    pub fn CFE_SB_AllocateMessageBuffer(MsgSize: usize) -> *mut CFE_SB_Buffer_t;
3631}
3632unsafe extern "C" {
3633    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Release an unused \"zero copy\" buffer pointer.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine can be used to release a pointer to one of the software\n          bus' internal memory buffers.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          -# This function is not needed for normal \"zero copy\" transfers.  It\n             is needed only for cleanup when an application gets a pointer using\n             CFE_SB_AllocateMessageBuffer(), but (due to some error condition) never\n             uses that pointer in a call to CFE_SB_TransmitBuffer().\n\n \\param[in]  BufPtr  A pointer to the SB internal buffer @nonnull.  This must be a\n                     pointer returned by a call to CFE_SB_AllocateMessageBuffer(),\n                     but never used in a call to CFE_SB_TransmitBuffer().\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS           \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BUFFER_INVALID \\copybrief CFE_SB_BUFFER_INVALID"]
3634    pub fn CFE_SB_ReleaseMessageBuffer(BufPtr: *mut CFE_SB_Buffer_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3635}
3636unsafe extern "C" {
3637    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Transmit a buffer\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine sends a message that has been created directly in an\n          internal SB message buffer by an application (after a call to\n          #CFE_SB_AllocateMessageBuffer).  This interface is more complicated than\n          the normal #CFE_SB_TransmitMsg interface, but it avoids an extra copy of\n          the message from the user's memory buffer to the software bus\n          internal buffer.  The \"zero copy\" interface can be used to improve\n          performance in high-rate, high-volume software bus traffic.\n\n          The IsOrigination parameter should be passed as \"true\" if the message was\n          newly constructed by the sender and is being sent for the first time.  This\n          enables the message origination actions as determined by the CFE MSG module,\n          which may include (but not limited to):\n            - Updating sequence number\n            - Updating timestamp\n            - Calcualating a CRC, checksum, or other message error control field\n\n          Conversely, when forwarding a message that originated from\n          an external entity (e.g. messages passing through CI or SBN), the\n          parameter should be passed as \"false\" to not overwrite existing data.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          -# A handle returned by #CFE_SB_AllocateMessageBuffer is \"consumed\" by\n             a _successful_ call to #CFE_SB_TransmitBuffer.\n          -# If this function returns CFE_SUCCESS, this indicates the zero copy handle is\n             now owned by software bus, and is no longer owned by the calling application,\n             and should not be re-used.\n          -# However if this function fails (returns any error status) it does not change\n             the state of the buffer at all, meaning the calling application still owns it.\n             (a failure means the buffer is left in the same state it was before the call).\n          -# Applications should be written as if #CFE_SB_AllocateMessageBuffer is\n             equivalent to a \\c malloc() and a successful call to #CFE_SB_TransmitBuffer\n             is equivalent to a \\c free().\n          -# Applications must not de-reference the message pointer (for reading\n             or writing) after a successful call to #CFE_SB_TransmitBuffer.\n          -# This function will increment and apply the internally tracked\n             sequence counter if set to do so.\n\n \\param[in] BufPtr         A pointer to the buffer to be sent @nonnull.\n \\param[in] IsOrigination  Update applicable header field(s) of a newly constructed message\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS         \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_SB_MSG_TOO_BIG  \\copybrief CFE_SB_MSG_TOO_BIG"]
3638    pub fn CFE_SB_TransmitBuffer(BufPtr: *mut CFE_SB_Buffer_t, IsOrigination: bool)
3639    -> CFE_Status_t;
3640}
3641unsafe extern "C" {
3642    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Sets the length of user data in a software bus message.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine sets the field in the SB message header that determines\n          the size of the user data in a software bus message.  SB message header\n          formats can be different for each deployment of the cFE.  So, applications\n          should use this function rather than trying to poke a length value directly\n          into their SB message buffers.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          - You must set a valid message ID in the SB message header before\n            calling this function.\n\n \\param[in]  MsgPtr      A pointer to the buffer that contains the software bus message @nonnull.\n                         This must point to the first byte of the message header.\n\n \\param[in]  DataLength  The length to set (size of the user data, in bytes)."]
3643    pub fn CFE_SB_SetUserDataLength(MsgPtr: *mut CFE_MSG_Message_t, DataLength: usize);
3644}
3645unsafe extern "C" {
3646    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Sets the time field in a software bus message with the current spacecraft time.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine sets the time of a software bus message with the current\n          spacecraft time.  This will be the same time that is returned by the\n          function #CFE_TIME_GetTime.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          - If the underlying implementation of software bus messages does not\n            include a time field, then this routine will do nothing.\n\n \\param[in]  MsgPtr      A pointer to the buffer that contains the software bus message @nonnull.\n                         This must point to the first byte of the message header."]
3647    pub fn CFE_SB_TimeStampMsg(MsgPtr: *mut CFE_MSG_Message_t);
3648}
3649unsafe extern "C" {
3650    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Copies a string into a software bus message\n\n \\par Description\n    Strings within software bus messages have a defined/fixed maximum length, and\n    may not necessarily be null terminated within the message.  This presents a possible\n    issue when using the C library functions to copy strings out of a message.\n\n    This performs a very similar function to \"strncpy()\" except that the sizes\n    of _both_ buffers are passed in.  Neither buffer is required to be null-terminated,\n    but copying will stop after the first termination character is encountered.\n\n    If the destination buffer is not completely filled by the source data (such as if\n    the supplied string was shorter than the allotted length) the destination buffer\n    will be padded with NUL characters up to the size of the buffer, similar to what\n    strncpy() does.  This ensures that the entire destination buffer is set.\n\n \\note\n    If the source string buffer is already guaranteed to be null terminated,\n    then there is no difference between the C library \"strncpy()\" function and this\n    implementation.  It is only necessary to use this when termination of the source\n    buffer is not guaranteed.\n\n \\param[out] DestStringPtr    Pointer to destination buffer (component of SB message definition) @nonnull\n \\param[in]  SourceStringPtr  Pointer to source buffer @nonnull\n \\param[in]  DestMaxSize      Size of destination buffer as defined by the message definition\n \\param[in]  SourceMaxSize    Size of source buffer\n\n \\return Number of characters copied or error code, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT  \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT"]
3651    pub fn CFE_SB_MessageStringSet(
3652        DestStringPtr: *mut libc::c_char,
3653        SourceStringPtr: *const libc::c_char,
3654        DestMaxSize: usize,
3655        SourceMaxSize: usize,
3656    ) -> int32;
3657}
3658unsafe extern "C" {
3659    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get a pointer to the user data portion of a software bus message.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine returns a pointer to the user data portion of a software\n          bus message.  SB message header formats can be different for each\n          deployment of the cFE.  So, applications should use this function and\n          avoid hard coding offsets into their SB message buffers.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in]  MsgPtr  A pointer to the buffer that contains the software bus message @nonnull.\n\n \\return A pointer to the first byte of user data within the software bus message."]
3660    pub fn CFE_SB_GetUserData(MsgPtr: *mut CFE_MSG_Message_t) -> *mut libc::c_void;
3661}
3662unsafe extern "C" {
3663    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Gets the length of user data in a software bus message.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine returns the size of the user data in a software bus message.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in]  MsgPtr  A pointer to the buffer that contains the software bus message @nonnull.\n                     This must point to the first byte of the message header.\n\n \\return The size (in bytes) of the user data in the software bus message.\n \\retval 0 if an error occurs, such as if the MsgPtr argument is not valid."]
3664    pub fn CFE_SB_GetUserDataLength(MsgPtr: *const CFE_MSG_Message_t) -> usize;
3665}
3666unsafe extern "C" {
3667    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Copies a string out of a software bus message\n\n \\par Description\n    Strings within software bus messages have a defined/fixed maximum length, and\n    may not necessarily be null terminated within the message.  This presents a possible\n    issue when using the C library functions to copy strings out of a message.\n\n    This function should replace use of C library functions such as strcpy/strncpy\n    when copying strings out of software bus messages to local storage buffers.\n\n    Up to [SourceMaxSize] or [DestMaxSize-1] (whichever is smaller) characters will be\n    copied from the source buffer to the destination buffer, and a NUL termination\n    character will be written to the destination buffer as the last character.\n\n    If the DefaultString pointer is non-NULL, it will be used in place of the source\n    string if the source is an empty string.  This is typically a string constant that\n    comes from the platform configuration, allowing default values to be assumed for\n    fields that are unspecified.\n\n    IMPORTANT - the default string, if specified, must be null terminated.  This will\n    be the case if a string literal is passed in (the typical/expected use case).\n\n    If the default is NULL, then only the source string will be copied, and the result\n    will be an empty string if the source was empty.\n\n    If the destination buffer is too small to store the entire string, it will be\n    truncated, but it will still be null terminated.\n\n \\param[out] DestStringPtr    Pointer to destination buffer @nonnull\n \\param[in]  SourceStringPtr  Pointer to source buffer (component of SB message definition)\n \\param[in]  DefaultString    Default string to use if source is empty\n \\param[in]  DestMaxSize      Size of destination storage buffer @nonzero\n \\param[in]  SourceMaxSize    Size of source buffer as defined by the message definition\n\n \\return Number of characters copied or error code, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT  \\copybrief CFE_SB_BAD_ARGUMENT"]
3668    pub fn CFE_SB_MessageStringGet(
3669        DestStringPtr: *mut libc::c_char,
3670        SourceStringPtr: *const libc::c_char,
3671        DefaultString: *const libc::c_char,
3672        DestMaxSize: usize,
3673        SourceMaxSize: usize,
3674    ) -> int32;
3675}
3676unsafe extern "C" {
3677    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Identifies whether a given CFE_SB_MsgId_t is valid\n\n \\par Description\n    Implements a basic sanity check on the value provided\n\n \\return Boolean message ID validity indicator\n \\retval true  Message ID is within the valid range\n \\retval false Message ID is not within the valid range"]
3678    pub fn CFE_SB_IsValidMsgId(MsgId: CFE_SB_MsgId_t) -> bool;
3679}
3680unsafe extern "C" {
3681    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Converts a topic ID and instance number combination into a MsgID value integer\n\n \\par Description\n    This function accepts a data pair of topic ID + instance number and returns the\n    corresponding MsgID Value (integer) for commands.\n\n \\par Assumptions and Notes:\n    A topic ID identifies a certain data stream from an application, for example\n    the CFE Software bus ground commands (CFE_MISSION_SB_CMD_TOPICID).  In\n    contrast to MsgID, the topic ID is consistent across all CPUs in a system, whereas\n    each CPU instance will have a unique MsgID.\n\n    CPU instance numbers are 1-based.  The instance number of 0 is reserved\n    for \"global\" MsgID values that are the same on all CPUs.  The PSP function\n    may be used to obtain the current CPU number for the host processor.\n\n \\sa CFE_SB_TlmTopicIdToMsgId(), CFE_PSP_GetProcessorId()\n\n \\return Integer representation of the #CFE_SB_MsgId_t"]
3682    pub fn CFE_SB_CmdTopicIdToMsgId(TopicId: uint16, InstanceNum: uint16) -> CFE_SB_MsgId_Atom_t;
3683}
3684unsafe extern "C" {
3685    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Converts a topic ID and instance number combination into a MsgID value integer\n\n \\par Description\n    This function accepts a data pair of topic ID + instance number and returns the\n    corresponding MsgID Value (integer) for telemetry.\n\n \\par Assumptions and Notes:\n    A topic ID identifies a certain data stream from an application, for example\n    the CFE Software bus housekeeping telemetry (CFE_MISSION_SB_HK_TLM_TOPICID).  In\n    contrast to MsgID, the topic ID is consistent across all CPUs in a system, whereas\n    each CPU instance will have a unique MsgID.\n\n    CPU instance numbers are 1-based.  The instance number of 0 is reserved\n    for \"global\" MsgID values that are the same on all CPUs.  The PSP function\n    may be used to obtain the current CPU number for the host processor.\n\n \\sa CFE_SB_CmdTopicIdToMsgId(), CFE_PSP_GetProcessorId()\n\n \\return Integer representation of the #CFE_SB_MsgId_t"]
3686    pub fn CFE_SB_TlmTopicIdToMsgId(TopicId: uint16, InstanceNum: uint16) -> CFE_SB_MsgId_Atom_t;
3687}
3688unsafe extern "C" {
3689    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Converts a topic ID to a MsgID value integer for Global commands\n\n \\par Description\n    This is a wrapper around CFE_SB_CmdTopicIdToMsgId() for topic IDs which\n    are the same on all CPUs within a system (i.e. not specific to a certain\n    processor)\n\n \\par Assumptions and Notes:\n    Global MsgIDs may be used when only a single instance of a service exists\n    within the system.  The CFE framework does not use this feature for commands,\n    but is defined for future use.\n\n \\sa CFE_SB_CmdTopicIdToMsgId(), CFE_SB_LocalCmdTopicIdToMsgId()\n\n \\return Integer representation of the #CFE_SB_MsgId_t"]
3690    pub fn CFE_SB_GlobalCmdTopicIdToMsgId(TopicId: uint16) -> CFE_SB_MsgId_Atom_t;
3691}
3692unsafe extern "C" {
3693    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Converts a topic ID to a MsgID value integer for Global telemetry\n\n \\par Description\n    This is a wrapper around CFE_SB_TlmTopicIdToMsgId() for topic IDs which\n    are the same on all CPUs within a system (i.e. not specific to a certain\n    processor)\n\n \\par Assumptions and Notes:\n    Global MsgIDs may be used when only a single instance of a service exists\n    within the system.  An example for such telemetry is the time synchronization\n    service published by CFE_TIME.\n\n \\sa CFE_SB_TlmTopicIdToMsgId(), CFE_SB_LocalTlmTopicIdToMsgId()\n\n \\return Integer representation of the #CFE_SB_MsgId_t"]
3694    pub fn CFE_SB_GlobalTlmTopicIdToMsgId(TopicId: uint16) -> CFE_SB_MsgId_Atom_t;
3695}
3696unsafe extern "C" {
3697    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Converts a topic ID to a MsgID value integer for local commands\n\n \\par Description\n    This is a wrapper around CFE_SB_CmdTopicIdToMsgId() for topic IDs which\n    are unique on all CPUs within a system (i.e. specific to a certain\n    processor)\n\n \\par Assumptions and Notes:\n    This assumes the caller is referring to a service running on the same\n    processor instance as itself.\n\n \\sa CFE_SB_CmdTopicIdToMsgId(), CFE_SB_LocalTlmTopicIdToMsgId()\n\n \\return Integer representation of the #CFE_SB_MsgId_t"]
3698    pub fn CFE_SB_LocalCmdTopicIdToMsgId(TopicId: uint16) -> CFE_SB_MsgId_Atom_t;
3699}
3700unsafe extern "C" {
3701    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Converts a topic ID to a MsgID value integer for local telemetry\n\n \\par Description\n    This is a wrapper around CFE_SB_TlmTopicIdToMsgId() for topic IDs which\n    are unique on all CPUs within a system (i.e. specific to a certain\n    processor)\n\n \\par Assumptions and Notes:\n    This assumes the caller is referring to a service running on the same\n    processor instance as itself.\n\n \\sa CFE_SB_TlmTopicIdToMsgId(), CFE_SB_LocalCmdTopicIdToMsgId()\n\n \\return Integer representation of the #CFE_SB_MsgId_t"]
3702    pub fn CFE_SB_LocalTlmTopicIdToMsgId(TopicId: uint16) -> CFE_SB_MsgId_Atom_t;
3703}
3704unsafe extern "C" {
3705    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get the current spacecraft time\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine returns the current spacecraft time, which is the amount of\n        time elapsed since the epoch as set in mission configuration.  The time returned\n        is either TAI (no leap seconds) or UTC (including leap seconds).  This choice\n        is made in the mission configuration file by defining either #CFE_MISSION_TIME_CFG_DEFAULT_TAI\n        or #CFE_MISSION_TIME_CFG_DEFAULT_UTC as true at compile time.  To maintain re-usability\n        across missions, most applications should be using this function\n        rather than the specific routines for getting UTC/TAI directly.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\return The current spacecraft time in default format\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_GetTAI, #CFE_TIME_GetUTC, #CFE_TIME_GetMET,\n     #CFE_TIME_GetMETseconds, #CFE_TIME_GetMETsubsecs"]
3706    pub fn CFE_TIME_GetTime() -> CFE_TIME_SysTime_t;
3707}
3708unsafe extern "C" {
3709    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get the current TAI (MET + SCTF) time\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine returns the current TAI time to the caller.  TAI is an\n        international time standard that does not include leap seconds.\n        This routine should only be used in situations where TAI is absolutely\n        required.  Applications that call #CFE_TIME_GetTAI may not be portable\n        to all missions.  Maintenance of correct TAI in flight is not guaranteed\n        under all mission operations scenarios.  To maintain re-usability across\n        missions, most applications should be using #CFE_TIME_GetTime, rather\n        than the specific routines for getting UTC/TAI directly.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          -# The \"TAI\" time returned is referenced to the mission-defined time epoch,\n             which may or may not be the same as the standard TAI epoch.\n          -# Even though TAI does not include leap seconds, the time returned by this\n             function can still jump forward or backward without warning when the\n             spacecraft clock is set or adjusted by operators.  Applications using\n             this function must be able to handle these time discontinuities gracefully.\n\n \\return The current spacecraft time in TAI\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_GetTime, #CFE_TIME_GetUTC, #CFE_TIME_GetMET,\n     #CFE_TIME_GetMETseconds, #CFE_TIME_GetMETsubsecs"]
3710    pub fn CFE_TIME_GetTAI() -> CFE_TIME_SysTime_t;
3711}
3712unsafe extern "C" {
3713    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get the current UTC (MET + SCTF - Leap Seconds) time\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine returns the current UTC time to the caller.  This routine\n        should only be used in situations where UTC is absolutely required.\n        Applications that call #CFE_TIME_GetUTC may not be portable to all\n        missions.  Maintenance of correct UTC in flight is not guaranteed under\n        all mission operations scenarios.  If UTC is maintained in flight, it will\n        jump backwards occasionally due to leap second adjustments.  To maintain\n        re-usability across missions, most applications should be using\n        #CFE_TIME_GetTime, rather than the specific routines for getting\n        UTC/TAI directly.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          Note: The \"UTC\" time returned is referenced to the mission-defined time epoch,\n                which may or may not be the same as the standard UTC epoch.\n\n \\return The current spacecraft time in UTC\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_GetTime, #CFE_TIME_GetTAI, #CFE_TIME_GetMET,\n     #CFE_TIME_GetMETseconds, #CFE_TIME_GetMETsubsecs"]
3714    pub fn CFE_TIME_GetUTC() -> CFE_TIME_SysTime_t;
3715}
3716unsafe extern "C" {
3717    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get the current value of the Mission Elapsed Time (MET).\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine returns the current mission-elapsed time (MET).  MET is\n        usually derived from a hardware-based clock that is not adjusted\n        during normal operations.  Callers of this routine should not assume\n        that the MET return value has any specific relationship to any\n        ground-based time standard.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\return The current MET\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_GetTime, #CFE_TIME_GetTAI, #CFE_TIME_GetUTC,\n     #CFE_TIME_GetMETseconds, #CFE_TIME_GetMETsubsecs, #CFE_TIME_MET2SCTime"]
3718    pub fn CFE_TIME_GetMET() -> CFE_TIME_SysTime_t;
3719}
3720unsafe extern "C" {
3721    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get the current seconds count of the mission-elapsed time.\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine is the same as #CFE_TIME_GetMET, except that it\n        returns only the integer seconds portion of the MET time.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\return The current MET seconds\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_GetTime, #CFE_TIME_GetTAI, #CFE_TIME_GetUTC, #CFE_TIME_GetMET,\n     #CFE_TIME_GetMETsubsecs, #CFE_TIME_MET2SCTime"]
3722    pub fn CFE_TIME_GetMETseconds() -> uint32;
3723}
3724unsafe extern "C" {
3725    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get the current sub-seconds count of the mission-elapsed time.\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine is the same as #CFE_TIME_GetMET, except that it\n        returns only the integer sub-seconds portion of the MET time.\n        Each count is equal to 2^(-32) seconds.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\return The current MET sub-seconds\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_GetTime, #CFE_TIME_GetTAI, #CFE_TIME_GetUTC, #CFE_TIME_GetMET,\n     #CFE_TIME_GetMETseconds, #CFE_TIME_MET2SCTime"]
3726    pub fn CFE_TIME_GetMETsubsecs() -> uint32;
3727}
3728unsafe extern "C" {
3729    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get the current value of the spacecraft time correction factor (STCF).\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine returns the current value of the spacecraft time correction\n        factor.  This is the delta time between the MET and the TAI time.\n        There is no API provided to set or adjust leap seconds or SCTF, those\n        actions should be done by command only.  This API is provided for\n        applications to be able to include STCF in their data products to\n        aid in time correlation during downstream science data processing.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        Does not include leap seconds\n\n \\return The current SCTF\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_GetLeapSeconds, #CFE_TIME_GetClockState, #CFE_TIME_GetClockInfo"]
3730    pub fn CFE_TIME_GetSTCF() -> CFE_TIME_SysTime_t;
3731}
3732unsafe extern "C" {
3733    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get the current value of the leap seconds counter.\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine returns the current value of the leap seconds counter.\n        This is the delta seconds between international atomic time (TAI)\n        and universal coordinated time (UTC).\n        There is no API provided to set or adjust leap seconds or SCTF, those\n        actions should be done by command only.  This API is provided for\n        applications to be able to include leap seconds in their data products to\n        aid in time correlation during downstream science data processing.\n        Note that some mission operations\n        teams do not maintain the leap seconds count, preferring to adjust the\n        STCF instead.  Users of this function should check with their mission\n        ops team to see how they are planning to handle leap seconds.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\returns The current spacecraft leap seconds.\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_GetSTCF, #CFE_TIME_GetClockState, #CFE_TIME_GetClockInfo"]
3734    pub fn CFE_TIME_GetLeapSeconds() -> int16;
3735}
3736unsafe extern "C" {
3737    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Get the current state of the spacecraft clock.\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine returns the spacecraft clock state.  Applications that\n        are highly dependent on valid time may want to call this routine\n        before taking actions based on the times returned by the various\n        clock routines\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\return The current spacecraft clock state\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_GetSTCF, #CFE_TIME_GetLeapSeconds, #CFE_TIME_GetClockInfo"]
3738    pub fn CFE_TIME_GetClockState() -> CFE_TIME_ClockState_Enum_t;
3739}
3740unsafe extern "C" {
3741    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Provides information about the spacecraft clock.\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine returns information on the spacecraft clock in a bit mask.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\return Spacecraft clock information, \\ref CFETIMEClkStates.\n         To extract the information from the\n         returned value, the flags can be used as in the following: <br>\n         <tt> if ((ReturnValue & CFE_TIME_FLAG_xxxxxx) == CFE_TIME_FLAG_xxxxxx)</tt> then\n            the following definition of the \\c CFE_TIME_FLAG_xxxxxx is true. <br>\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_GetSTCF, #CFE_TIME_GetLeapSeconds, #CFE_TIME_GetClockState"]
3742    pub fn CFE_TIME_GetClockInfo() -> uint16;
3743}
3744unsafe extern "C" {
3745    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Adds two time values\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine adds the two specified times and returns the result.\n        Normally, at least one of the input times should be a value representing\n        a delta time.  Adding two absolute times together will not cause an error,\n        but the result will probably be meaningless.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in] Time1   The first time to be added.\n\n \\param[in] Time2   The second time to be added.\n\n \\return The sum of the two times.\n         If the sum is greater than the maximum value that can be stored in a\n         #CFE_TIME_SysTime_t, the result will roll over (this is not considered an error).\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_Subtract, #CFE_TIME_Compare"]
3746    pub fn CFE_TIME_Add(Time1: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t, Time2: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t)
3747    -> CFE_TIME_SysTime_t;
3748}
3749unsafe extern "C" {
3750    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Subtracts two time values\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine subtracts time2 from time1 and returns the result.  The\n        time values can represent either absolute or delta times, but not all\n        combinations make sense.\n           -  AbsTime - AbsTime = DeltaTime\n           -  AbsTime - DeltaTime = AbsTime\n           -  DeltaTime - DeltaTime = DeltaTime\n           -  DeltaTime - AbsTime = garbage\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in] Time1   The base time.\n\n \\param[in] Time2   The time to be subtracted from the base time.\n\n \\return The result of subtracting the two times.\n         If the subtraction results in an underflow, the result will\n         roll over (this is not considered an error).\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_Add, #CFE_TIME_Compare"]
3751    pub fn CFE_TIME_Subtract(
3752        Time1: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t,
3753        Time2: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t,
3754    ) -> CFE_TIME_SysTime_t;
3755}
3756unsafe extern "C" {
3757    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Compares two time values\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine compares two time values to see which is \"greater\".  It\n        is important that applications use this function rather than trying\n        to directly compare the component pieces of times.  This function will\n        handle roll-over cases seamlessly, which may not be intuitively obvious.\n        The cFE's internal representation of time \"rolls over\" when the 32 bit\n        seconds count reaches 0xFFFFFFFF.  Also, subtracting a delta time from\n        an absolute time close to the epoch could result in \"roll under\".  The\n        strange cases that result from these situations can be handled by defining\n        the comparison function for times as follows:\n        Plot the two times on the circumference of a circle where 0 is at the\n        top and 0x80000000 is at the bottom.  If the shortest arc from time A\n        to time B runs clockwise around the circle, then time A is less than\n        time B.  If the shortest arc from A to B runs counter-clockwise, then\n        time A is greater than time B.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in] TimeA   The first time to compare.\n\n \\param[in] TimeB   The second time to compare.\n\n \\return The result of comparing the two times.\n \\retval #CFE_TIME_EQUAL  \\copybrief CFE_TIME_EQUAL\n \\retval #CFE_TIME_A_GT_B \\copybrief CFE_TIME_A_GT_B\n \\retval #CFE_TIME_A_LT_B \\copybrief CFE_TIME_A_LT_B\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_Add, #CFE_TIME_Subtract"]
3758    pub fn CFE_TIME_Compare(
3759        TimeA: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t,
3760        TimeB: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t,
3761    ) -> CFE_TIME_Compare_t;
3762}
3763unsafe extern "C" {
3764    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Convert specified MET into Spacecraft Time\n\n \\par Description\n        This function returns Spacecraft Time given MET.  Note that Spacecraft\n        Time is returned as either UTC or TAI depending on whether the mission\n        configuration parameter #CFE_MISSION_TIME_CFG_DEFAULT_UTC or #CFE_MISSION_TIME_CFG_DEFAULT_TAI\n        was set to true at compile time.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in] METTime      The MET to be converted.\n\n \\return Spacecraft Time (UTC or TAI) corresponding to the specified MET\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_GetMET, #CFE_TIME_GetMETseconds, #CFE_TIME_GetMETsubsecs,\n     #CFE_TIME_Sub2MicroSecs, #CFE_TIME_Micro2SubSecs"]
3765    pub fn CFE_TIME_MET2SCTime(METTime: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t) -> CFE_TIME_SysTime_t;
3766}
3767unsafe extern "C" {
3768    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Converts a sub-seconds count to an equivalent number of microseconds\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine converts from a sub-seconds count\n        (each tick is 1 / 2^32 seconds) to microseconds (each tick is 1e-06 seconds).\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in] SubSeconds   The sub-seconds count to convert.\n\n \\return The equivalent number of microseconds.\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_MET2SCTime, #CFE_TIME_Micro2SubSecs,"]
3769    pub fn CFE_TIME_Sub2MicroSecs(SubSeconds: uint32) -> uint32;
3770}
3771unsafe extern "C" {
3772    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Converts a number of microseconds to an equivalent sub-seconds count.\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine converts from microseconds (each tick is 1e-06 seconds)\n        to a subseconds count (each tick is 1 / 2^32 seconds).\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in] MicroSeconds   The sub-seconds count to convert.\n\n \\return The equivalent number of subseconds.  If the number of microseconds\n         passed in is greater than one second, (i.e. > 999,999), the return\n         value is equal to \\c 0xffffffff.\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_MET2SCTime, #CFE_TIME_Sub2MicroSecs,"]
3773    pub fn CFE_TIME_Micro2SubSecs(MicroSeconds: uint32) -> uint32;
3774}
3775unsafe extern "C" {
3776    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Provides the 1 Hz signal from an external source\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine provides a method for cFE TIME software to be notified\n        of the occurrence of the 1Hz tone signal without knowledge of the\n        specific hardware design.  Regardless of the source of the tone,\n        this routine should be called as soon as possible after detection\n        to allow cFE TIME software the opportunity to latch the local clock\n        as close as possible to the instant of the tone.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          - This routine may be called directly from within the context of an\n            interrupt handler.\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_ExternalMET, #CFE_TIME_ExternalGPS, #CFE_TIME_ExternalTime"]
3777    pub fn CFE_TIME_ExternalTone();
3778}
3779unsafe extern "C" {
3780    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Provides the Mission Elapsed Time from an external source\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine provides a method to provide cFE TIME with MET acquired\n        from an external source.  There is a presumption that this function\n        will be called at the appropriate time (relative to the tone) such\n        that this call may be used by cFE TIME as the signal to generate the\n        \"time at the tone\" data command.  The \"time at the tone\" data command\n        must arrive within the configuration parameter specified window for\n        tone signal and data packet verification. <br> <br>\n        The MET value at the tone \"should\" have zero subseconds.  Although the\n        interface accepts non-zero values for sub-seconds, it may be harmful\n        to other applications that expect zero subseconds at the moment of the\n        tone.  Any decision to use non-zero subseconds should be carefully considered.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          - This routine is included in the API only when 3 specific configuration\n            parameters are set to true.  The first is #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SERVER which defines\n            this instantiation of cFE TIME as a time server (not a client).  The\n            second required configuration parameter is #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SOURCE which\n            enables time source selection commands to the cFE TIME task, and further\n            enables configuration definitions for the selected type of external time\n            data.  The third configuration parameter required for this routine is\n            #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SRC_MET, which indicates that the external time data consists\n            of MET.\n\n \\param[in]  NewMET   The MET value at the next (or previous) 1 Hz tone signal.\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_ExternalTone, #CFE_TIME_ExternalGPS, #CFE_TIME_ExternalTime"]
3781    pub fn CFE_TIME_ExternalMET(NewMET: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t);
3782}
3783unsafe extern "C" {
3784    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Provide the time from an external source that has data common to GPS receivers.\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine provides a method to provide cFE TIME with current time\n        data acquired from an external source.  There is a presumption that\n        this function will be called at the appropriate time (relative to the\n        tone) such that this call may be used by cFE TIME as the signal to\n        generate the \"time at the tone\" data command.  The \"time at the tone\"\n        data command must arrive within the configuration parameter specified\n        window for tone signal and data packet verification. <br> <br>\n        Internally, cFE TIME will calculate a new STCF as the difference between\n        this new time value and the spacecraft MET value at the tone.  This allows\n        cFE TIME to always calculate time as the sum of MET and STCF.  The value\n        of STCF will change only as much as the drift factor between spacecraft\n        MET and the external time source.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          - This routine is included in the API only when 3 specific configuration\n            parameters are set to true.  The first is #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SERVER which defines this\n            instantiation of cFE TIME as a time server (not a client).  The second\n            required configuration parameter is #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SOURCE which enables\n            time source selection commands to the cFE TIME task, and further enables\n            configuration definitions for the selected type of external time data.\n            The third configuration parameter required for this routine is\n            #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SRC_GPS, which indicates that the external time data consists\n            of a time value relative to a known epoch, plus a leap seconds value.\n\n \\param[in]  NewTime   The MET value at the next (or previous) 1 Hz tone signal.\n\n \\param[in]  NewLeaps The Leap Seconds value used to calculate time as UTC.\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_ExternalTone, #CFE_TIME_ExternalMET, #CFE_TIME_ExternalTime"]
3785    pub fn CFE_TIME_ExternalGPS(NewTime: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t, NewLeaps: int16);
3786}
3787unsafe extern "C" {
3788    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Provide the time from an external source that measures time relative to a known epoch.\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine provides a method to provide cFE TIME with current time\n        data acquired from an external source.  There is a presumption that\n        this function will be called at the appropriate time (relative to the\n        tone) such that this call may be used by cFE TIME as the signal to\n        generate the \"time at the tone\" data command.  The \"time at the tone\"\n        data command must arrive within the configuration specified window for\n        tone signal and data packet verification. <br> <br>\n        Internally, cFE TIME will calculate a new STCF as the difference between\n        this new time value and the spacecraft MET value at the tone.  This allows\n        cFE TIME to always calculate time as the sum of MET and STCF.  The value\n        of STCF will change only as much as the drift factor between spacecraft\n        MET and the external time source.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          - This routine is included in the API only when 3 specific configuration\n            parameters are set to true.  The first is #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SERVER which defines this\n            instantiation of cFE TIME as a time server (not a client).  The second\n            required configuration parameter is #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SOURCE which enables\n            time source selection commands to the cFE TIME task, and further enables\n            configuration definitions for the selected type of external time data.\n            The third configuration parameter required for this routine is\n            #CFE_PLATFORM_TIME_CFG_SRC_TIME, which indicates that the external time data consists\n            of a time value relative to a known epoch.\n\n \\param[in]  NewTime   The MET value at the next (or previous) 1 Hz tone signal.\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_ExternalTone, #CFE_TIME_ExternalMET, #CFE_TIME_ExternalGPS"]
3789    pub fn CFE_TIME_ExternalTime(NewTime: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t);
3790}
3791unsafe extern "C" {
3792    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Registers a callback function that is called whenever time synchronization occurs\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine passes a callback function pointer for an Application that wishes to\n        be notified whenever a legitimate time synchronization signal (typically a 1 Hz)\n        is received.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        Only a single callback per application is supported, and this function should only\n        be called from a single thread within each application (typically the apps main thread).\n        If an application requires triggering multiple child tasks at 1Hz, it should distribute\n        the timing signal internally, rather than registering for multiple callbacks.\n\n \\param[in]  CallbackFuncPtr   Function to call at synchronization interval @nonnull\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                       \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TIME_TOO_MANY_SYNCH_CALLBACKS \\copybrief CFE_TIME_TOO_MANY_SYNCH_CALLBACKS\n \\retval #CFE_TIME_BAD_ARGUMENT             \\copybrief CFE_TIME_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_UnregisterSynchCallback"]
3793    pub fn CFE_TIME_RegisterSynchCallback(
3794        CallbackFuncPtr: CFE_TIME_SynchCallbackPtr_t,
3795    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3796}
3797unsafe extern "C" {
3798    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Unregisters a callback function that is called whenever time synchronization occurs\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine removes the specified callback function pointer from the list\n        of Callback functions that are called whenever a time synchronization (typically\n        the 1Hz signal) is received.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        Only a single callback per application is supported, and this function should only\n        be called from a single thread within each application (typically the apps main thread).\n\n \\param[in]  CallbackFuncPtr   Function to remove from synchronization call list @nonnull\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                      \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TIME_CALLBACK_NOT_REGISTERED \\copybrief CFE_TIME_CALLBACK_NOT_REGISTERED\n \\retval #CFE_TIME_BAD_ARGUMENT            \\copybrief CFE_TIME_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_TIME_RegisterSynchCallback"]
3799    pub fn CFE_TIME_UnregisterSynchCallback(
3800        CallbackFuncPtr: CFE_TIME_SynchCallbackPtr_t,
3801    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3802}
3803unsafe extern "C" {
3804    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Print a time value as a string\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine prints the specified time to the specified string buffer\n        in the following format: <br> <br>\n           \\c yyyy-ddd-hh:mm:ss.xxxxx\\\\0 <br> <br>\n        where:\n           - \\c yyyy = year\n           - \\c ddd = Julian day of the year\n           - \\c hh = hour of the day (0 to 23)\n           - \\c mm = minute (0 to 59)\n           - \\c ss = second (0 to 59)\n           - \\c xxxxx = subsecond formatted as a decimal fraction (1/4 second = 0.25000)\n           - \\c \\\\0 = trailing null\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        - The value of the time argument is simply added to the configuration\n          definitions for the ground epoch and converted into a fixed length\n          string in the buffer provided by the caller.\n        - A loss of data during the string conversion will occur if the\n          computed year exceeds 9999.  However, a year that large would\n          require an unrealistic definition for the ground epoch since\n          the maximum amount of time represented by a CFE_TIME_SysTime\n          structure is approximately 136 years.\n\n \\param[out]  PrintBuffer   Pointer to a character array @nonnull of at least\n                           #CFE_TIME_PRINTED_STRING_SIZE characters in length. *PrintBuffer is the time as a\n                           character string as described above.\n\n \\param[in]  TimeToPrint   The time to print into the character array."]
3805    pub fn CFE_TIME_Print(PrintBuffer: *mut libc::c_char, TimeToPrint: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t);
3806}
3807unsafe extern "C" {
3808    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief This function is called via a timer callback set up at initialization\n        of the TIME service.\n\n \\par Description\n        Drives the time processing logic from the system PSP layer.  This must be called\n        once per second based on a hardware interrupt or OS kernel signal.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        This will update the global data structures accordingly, incrementing each\n        by the 1Hz amount."]
3809    pub fn CFE_TIME_Local1HzISR();
3810}
3811#[doc = " @brief Select the Inactive buffer for validate or dump"]
3812pub const CFE_TBL_BufferSelect_CFE_TBL_BufferSelect_INACTIVE: CFE_TBL_BufferSelect = 0;
3813#[doc = " @brief Select the Active buffer for validate or dump"]
3814pub const CFE_TBL_BufferSelect_CFE_TBL_BufferSelect_ACTIVE: CFE_TBL_BufferSelect = 1;
3815#[doc = " @brief Label definitions associated with CFE_TBL_BufferSelect_Enum_t"]
3816pub type CFE_TBL_BufferSelect = libc::c_uint;
3817#[doc = " @brief Selects the buffer to operate on for validate or dump commands\n\n @sa enum CFE_TBL_BufferSelect"]
3818pub type CFE_TBL_BufferSelect_Enum_t = uint16;
3819#[doc = " @brief The definition of the header fields that are included in CFE Table Data files.\n\n This header follows the CFE_FS header and precedes the actual table data.\n\n @note The Offset and NumBytes fields in the table header are to 32 bits for\n backward compatibility with existing CFE versions.  This means that even on\n 64-bit CPUs, individual table files will be limited to 4GiB in size."]
3820#[repr(C)]
3821#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone)]
3822pub struct CFE_TBL_File_Hdr {
3823    #[doc = "< Future Use: NumTblSegments in File?"]
3824    pub Reserved: uint32,
3825    #[doc = "< Byte Offset at which load should commence"]
3826    pub Offset: uint32,
3827    #[doc = "< Number of bytes to load into table"]
3828    pub NumBytes: uint32,
3829    #[doc = "< Fully qualified name of table to load"]
3830    pub TableName: [libc::c_char; 40usize],
3831}
3832impl Default for CFE_TBL_File_Hdr {
3833    fn default() -> Self {
3834        let mut s = ::core::mem::MaybeUninit::<Self>::uninit();
3835        unsafe {
3836            ::core::ptr::write_bytes(s.as_mut_ptr(), 0, 1);
3837            s.assume_init()
3838        }
3839    }
3840}
3841#[doc = " @brief The definition of the header fields that are included in CFE Table Data files.\n\n This header follows the CFE_FS header and precedes the actual table data.\n\n @note The Offset and NumBytes fields in the table header are to 32 bits for\n backward compatibility with existing CFE versions.  This means that even on\n 64-bit CPUs, individual table files will be limited to 4GiB in size."]
3842pub type CFE_TBL_File_Hdr_t = CFE_TBL_File_Hdr;
3843#[doc = " \\brief Table Callback Function"]
3844pub type CFE_TBL_CallbackFuncPtr_t =
3845    ::core::option::Option<unsafe extern "C" fn(TblPtr: *mut libc::c_void) -> int32>;
3846#[doc = " \\brief Table Handle primitive"]
3847pub type CFE_TBL_Handle_t = int16;
3848#[doc = "< \\brief File source\nWhen this option is selected, the \\c SrcDataPtr\nwill be interpreted as a pointer to a null\nterminated character string.  The string should\nspecify the full path and filename of the file\ncontaining the initial data contents of the table."]
3849pub const CFE_TBL_SrcEnum_CFE_TBL_SRC_FILE: CFE_TBL_SrcEnum = 0;
3850#[doc = "< \\brief Address source\nWhen this option is selected, the \\c SrcDataPtr will\nbe interpreted as a pointer to a memory location\nthat is the beginning of the initialization data\nfor loading the table OR, in the case of a \"user defined\"\ndump only table, the address of the active table itself.\nThe block of memory is assumed to be of the same size\nspecified in the #CFE_TBL_Register function Size parameter."]
3851pub const CFE_TBL_SrcEnum_CFE_TBL_SRC_ADDRESS: CFE_TBL_SrcEnum = 1;
3852#[doc = " \\brief Table Source"]
3853pub type CFE_TBL_SrcEnum = libc::c_uint;
3854#[doc = " \\brief Table Source"]
3855pub use self::CFE_TBL_SrcEnum as CFE_TBL_SrcEnum_t;
3856#[doc = " \\brief Table Info"]
3857#[repr(C)]
3858#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone)]
3859pub struct CFE_TBL_Info {
3860    #[doc = "< \\brief Size, in bytes, of Table"]
3861    pub Size: usize,
3862    #[doc = "< \\brief Number of Apps with access to the table"]
3863    pub NumUsers: uint32,
3864    #[doc = "< \\brief File creation time from last file loaded into table"]
3865    pub FileTime: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t,
3866    #[doc = "< \\brief Most recently calculated CRC by TBL services on table contents"]
3867    pub Crc: uint32,
3868    #[doc = "< \\brief Time when Table was last updated"]
3869    pub TimeOfLastUpdate: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t,
3870    #[doc = "< \\brief Flag indicating whether table has been loaded once or not"]
3871    pub TableLoadedOnce: bool,
3872    #[doc = "< \\brief Flag indicating Table is NOT to be loaded"]
3873    pub DumpOnly: bool,
3874    #[doc = "< \\brief Flag indicating Table has a dedicated inactive buffer"]
3875    pub DoubleBuffered: bool,
3876    #[doc = "< \\brief Flag indicating Table address was defined by Owner Application"]
3877    pub UserDefAddr: bool,
3878    #[doc = "< \\brief Flag indicating Table contents are maintained in a CDS"]
3879    pub Critical: bool,
3880    #[doc = "< \\brief Filename of last file loaded into table"]
3881    pub LastFileLoaded: [libc::c_char; 64usize],
3882}
3883impl Default for CFE_TBL_Info {
3884    fn default() -> Self {
3885        let mut s = ::core::mem::MaybeUninit::<Self>::uninit();
3886        unsafe {
3887            ::core::ptr::write_bytes(s.as_mut_ptr(), 0, 1);
3888            s.assume_init()
3889        }
3890    }
3891}
3892#[doc = " \\brief Table Info"]
3893pub type CFE_TBL_Info_t = CFE_TBL_Info;
3894unsafe extern "C" {
3895    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Register a table with cFE to obtain Table Management Services\n\n \\par Description\n          When an application is created and initialized, it is responsible for creating its table images via\n          the TBL API.  The application must inform the Table Service of the table name, table size and selection\n          of optional table features.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          Note: This function call can block.  Therefore, interrupt service routines should NOT create\n                their own tables.  An application should create any table(s) and provide the handle(s)\n                to the interrupt service routine.\n\n \\param[out] TblHandlePtr    a pointer to a #CFE_TBL_Handle_t type variable @nonnull that will be assigned the table's\n                                 handle.  The table handle is required for other API calls when accessing the data\n                                 contained in the table. *TblHandlePtr is the handle used to identify table to cFE\n                                 when performing Table operations. This value is returned at address specified by\n                                 TblHandlePtr.\n\n \\param[in] Name                 The raw table name.  This name will be combined with the name of the\n                                 application to produce a name of the form \"AppName.RawTableName\".\n                                 This application specific name will be used in commands\n                                 for modifying or viewing the contents of the table.\n\n \\param[in] Size                 The size, in bytes, of the table to be created @nonzero.  This is the size that will\nbe\n                                 allocated as a shared memory resource between the Table Management Service and\n                                 the calling application.\n\n \\param[in] TblOptionFlags       Flag bits indicating selected options for table.  A bitwise OR of the following\n                                 option flags:\n                                 \\arg #CFE_TBL_OPT_DEFAULT     - The default setting for table options is a\n                                                                 combination of #CFE_TBL_OPT_SNGL_BUFFER and\n                                                                 #CFE_TBL_OPT_LOAD_DUMP.  See below for a\n                                                                 description of these two options.  This option\n                                                                 is mutually exclusive with the\n                                                                 #CFE_TBL_OPT_DBL_BUFFER, #CFE_TBL_OPT_DUMP_ONLY\n                                                                 and #CFE_TBL_OPT_USR_DEF_ADDR options.\n                                 \\arg #CFE_TBL_OPT_SNGL_BUFFER - When this option is selected, the table will use\n                                                                 a shared session table for performing table\n                                                                 modifications and a memory copy from the session\n                                                                 table to the \"active\" table buffer will occur\n                                                                 when the table is updated.  This is the preferred\n                                                                 option since it will minimize memory usage.\n                                                                 This option is mutually exclusive with the\n                                                                 #CFE_TBL_OPT_DBL_BUFFER option\n                                 \\arg #CFE_TBL_OPT_DBL_BUFFER  - When this option is selected, two instances of\n                                                                 the table are created.   One is considered the\n                                                                 \"active\" table and the other the \"inactive\" table.\n                                                                 Whenever table modifications occur, they do not\n                                                                 require the use of a common session table.\n                                                                 Modifications occur in the \"inactive\" buffer.\n                                                                 Then, when it is time to update the table,\n                                                                 the pointer to the \"active\" table is changed to\n                                                                 point to the \"inactive\" buffer thus making it\n                                                                 the new \"active\" buffer.  This feature is most\n                                                                 useful for time critical applications (ie -\n                                                                 interrupt service routines, etc).  This option is\n                                                                 mutually exclusive with the\n                                                                 #CFE_TBL_OPT_SNGL_BUFFER and #CFE_TBL_OPT_DEFAULT\n                                                                 option.\n                                 \\arg #CFE_TBL_OPT_LOAD_DUMP   - When this option is selected, the Table Service\n                                                                 is allowed to perform all operations on the\n                                                                 specified table.  This option is mutually\n                                                                 exclusive with the #CFE_TBL_OPT_DUMP_ONLY option.\n                                 \\arg #CFE_TBL_OPT_DUMP_ONLY   - When this option is selected, the Table Service\n                                                                 will not perform table loads to this table.  This\n                                                                 does not prevent, however, a task from writing\n                                                                 to the table via an address obtained with the\n                                                                 #CFE_TBL_GetAddress API function.  This option is\n                                                                 mutually exclusive with the #CFE_TBL_OPT_LOAD_DUMP\n                                                                 and #CFE_TBL_OPT_DEFAULT options. If the Application\n                                                                 wishes to specify their own block of memory as the\n                                                                 Dump Only table, they need to also include the\n                                                                 #CFE_TBL_OPT_USR_DEF_ADDR option explained below.\n                                 \\arg #CFE_TBL_OPT_NOT_USR_DEF - When this option is selected, Table Services\n                                                                 allocates memory for the table and, in the case of a\n                                                                 double buffered table, it allocates the same amount\n                                                                 of memory again for the second buffer.  This option\n                                                                 is mutually exclusive with the\n                                                                 #CFE_TBL_OPT_USR_DEF_ADDR option.\n                                 \\arg #CFE_TBL_OPT_USR_DEF_ADDR- When this option is selected, the Table Service\n                                                                 will not allocate memory for the table.  Table\n                                                                 Services will require the Application to identify\n                                                                 the location of the active table buffer via the\n                                                                 #CFE_TBL_Load function. This option implies the\n                                                                 #CFE_TBL_OPT_DUMP_ONLY and the\n                                                                 #CFE_TBL_OPT_SNGL_BUFFER options and is mutually\n                                                                 exclusive of the #CFE_TBL_OPT_DBL_BUFFER option.\n                                 \\arg #CFE_TBL_OPT_CRITICAL-     When this option is selected, the Table Service\n                                                                 will automatically allocate space in the Critical\n                                                                 Data Store (CDS) for the table and ensure that the\n                                                                 contents in the CDS are the same\n                                                                 as the contents of the currently active buffer for\n                                                                 the table. This option is mutually exclusive of the\n                                                                 #CFE_TBL_OPT_USR_DEF_ADDR and #CFE_TBL_OPT_DUMP_ONLY\n                                                                 options.  It should also be noted that the use of\n                                                                 this option with double buffered tables will prevent\n                                                                 the update of the double buffered table from being\n                                                                 quick and it could be blocked.  Therefore, critical\n                                                                 tables should not be updated by Interrupt Service\n                                                                 Routines.\n\n \\param[in] TblValidationFuncPtr is a pointer to a function that will be executed in the context of the Table\n                                 Management Service when the contents of a table need to be validated.  If set\n                                 to NULL, then the Table Management Service will assume any data is valid.  If\n                                 the value is not NULL, it must be a pointer to a function with the following\n                                 prototype: <BR>\n                                 <B> int32 CallbackFunc(void *TblPtr);</B> <BR>\n                                 where  <BR>\n                                 <B>TblPtr </B> will be a pointer to the table data that is to be verified.  When the\n                                 function returns #CFE_SUCCESS, the data is considered valid and ready for a commit.\n                                 When the function returns a negative value, the data is considered invalid and an\n                                 Event Message will be issued containing the returned value.  If the function should\n                                 return a positive number, the table is considered invalid and the return code is\n                                 considered invalid.  Validation functions \\b must return either #CFE_SUCCESS or a\n                                 negative number (whose value is at the developer's discretion).  The validation\n                                 function will be executed in the Application's context so that Event Messages\n                                 describing the validation failure are possible from within the function.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                      \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_RECOVERED_TBL       \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_RECOVERED_TBL\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_DUPLICATE_DIFF_SIZE  \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_DUPLICATE_DIFF_SIZE\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_DUPLICATE_NOT_OWNED  \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_DUPLICATE_NOT_OWNED\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_REGISTRY_FULL        \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_REGISTRY_FULL\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_HANDLES_FULL         \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_HANDLES_FULL\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_SIZE         \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_SIZE\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_NAME         \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_NAME\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID  \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT             \\copybrief CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_OPTIONS      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_OPTIONS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_WARN_DUPLICATE           \\copybrief CFE_TBL_WARN_DUPLICATE\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_WARN_NOT_CRITICAL        \\copybrief CFE_TBL_WARN_NOT_CRITICAL\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_Unregister, #CFE_TBL_Share"]
3896    pub fn CFE_TBL_Register(
3897        TblHandlePtr: *mut CFE_TBL_Handle_t,
3898        Name: *const libc::c_char,
3899        Size: usize,
3900        TblOptionFlags: uint16,
3901        TblValidationFuncPtr: CFE_TBL_CallbackFuncPtr_t,
3902    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3903}
3904unsafe extern "C" {
3905    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Obtain handle of table registered by another application\n\n \\par Description\n        After a table has been created, other applications can gain access\n        to that table via the table handle.  In order for two or more\n        applications to share a table, the applications that do not create\n        the table must obtain the handle using this function.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[out]  TblHandlePtr  A pointer to a #CFE_TBL_Handle_t type variable @nonnull\n                           that will be assigned the table's handle.  The\n                           table handle is required for other API calls\n                           when accessing the data contained in the table. *TblHandlePtr is the handle used to\n                           identify table to cFE when performing Table operations.\n                           This value is returned at the address specified by TblHandlePtr.\n\n \\param[in]  TblName       The application specific name of the table of the form \"AppName.RawTableName\",\n                           where RawTableName is the name specified in the #CFE_TBL_Register API call.\n                           Example: \"ACS.TamParams\" for a table called \"TamParams\"\n                           that was registered by the application called \"ACS\".\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                     \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_HANDLES_FULL        \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_HANDLES_FULL\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_NAME        \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_NAME\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT            \\copybrief CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_Unregister, #CFE_TBL_Register"]
3906    pub fn CFE_TBL_Share(
3907        TblHandlePtr: *mut CFE_TBL_Handle_t,
3908        TblName: *const libc::c_char,
3909    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3910}
3911unsafe extern "C" {
3912    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Unregister a table\n\n \\par Description\n        When an application is being removed from the system, ES will\n        clean up/free all the application related resources including tables\n        so apps are not required to call this function.\n\n        A valid use-case for this API is to unregister a shared table if\n        access is no longer needed or the owning application was removed from\n        the system (CS app is an example).\n\n        Typically apps should only register tables during initialization and\n        registration/unregistration by the owning application during operation\n        should be avoided. If unavoidable, special care needs to be taken\n        (especially for shared tables) to avoid race conditions due to\n        competing requests from multiple tasks.\n\n        Note the table will not be removed from memory until all table access\n        links have been removed (registration and all shared access).\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in] TblHandle Handle, previously obtained from #CFE_TBL_Register or #CFE_TBL_Share,\n                      that identifies the Table to be unregistered.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                     \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS           \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_Share, #CFE_TBL_Register"]
3913    pub fn CFE_TBL_Unregister(TblHandle: CFE_TBL_Handle_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3914}
3915unsafe extern "C" {
3916    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Load a specified table with data from specified source\n\n \\par Description\n        Once an application has created a table (#CFE_TBL_Register), it must\n        provide the values that initialize the contents of that table.  The\n        application accomplishes this with one of two different TBL API calls.\n        This function call initializes the table with values that are held\n        in a data structure.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        This function call can block.  Therefore, interrupt service routines\n        should NOT initialize their own tables.  An application should initialize\n        any table(s) prior to providing the handle(s) to the interrupt service routine.\n\n \\param[in] TblHandle  Handle, previously obtained from #CFE_TBL_Register or #CFE_TBL_Share, that\n                       identifies the Table to be loaded.\n\n \\param[in] SrcType    Flag indicating the nature of the given \\c SrcDataPtr below.\n                       This value can be any one of the following:\n                          \\arg #CFE_TBL_SRC_FILE    - \\copybrief CFE_TBL_SRC_FILE\n                          \\arg #CFE_TBL_SRC_ADDRESS - \\copybrief CFE_TBL_SRC_ADDRESS\n\n \\param[in] SrcDataPtr Pointer @nonnull to either a character string specifying a filename or\n                       a memory address of a block of binary data to be loaded into a table or,\n                       if the table was registered with the #CFE_TBL_OPT_USR_DEF_ADDR option,\n                       the address of the active table buffer.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                     \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS           \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_DUMP_ONLY           \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_DUMP_ONLY\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_ILLEGAL_SRC_TYPE    \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_ILLEGAL_SRC_TYPE\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_LOAD_IN_PROGRESS    \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_LOAD_IN_PROGRESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_LOAD_INCOMPLETE     \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_LOAD_INCOMPLETE\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_BUFFER_AVAIL     \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_BUFFER_AVAIL\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_ACCESS              \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_ACCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_FILE_TOO_LARGE      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_FILE_TOO_LARGE\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_BAD_CONTENT_ID      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_BAD_CONTENT_ID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_BAD_SUBTYPE_ID      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_BAD_SUBTYPE_ID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_STD_HEADER       \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_STD_HEADER\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_TBL_HEADER       \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_TBL_HEADER\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_PARTIAL_LOAD        \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_PARTIAL_LOAD\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT            \\copybrief CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_Update, #CFE_TBL_Validate, #CFE_TBL_Manage"]
3917    pub fn CFE_TBL_Load(
3918        TblHandle: CFE_TBL_Handle_t,
3919        SrcType: CFE_TBL_SrcEnum_t,
3920        SrcDataPtr: *const libc::c_void,
3921    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3922}
3923unsafe extern "C" {
3924    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Update contents of a specified table, if an update is pending\n\n \\par Description\n        Typically, apps should just call #CFE_TBL_Manage as part\n        of routine processing which will perform validation, update, or dump\n        if pending. This API is provided for the case where just an\n        update should be performed.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in] TblHandle  Handle, previously obtained from #CFE_TBL_Register or #CFE_TBL_Share, that\n                       identifies the Table to be updated.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                     \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_NO_UPDATE_PENDING  \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_NO_UPDATE_PENDING\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS           \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_Load, #CFE_TBL_Validate, #CFE_TBL_Manage"]
