3925    pub fn CFE_TBL_Update(TblHandle: CFE_TBL_Handle_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3926}
3927unsafe extern "C" {
3928    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Perform steps to validate the contents of a table image.\n\n \\par Description\n        Typically, apps should just call #CFE_TBL_Manage as part\n        of routine processing which will perform validation, update, or dump\n        if pending. This API is provided for the case where just a\n        validation should be performed.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in] TblHandle  Handle, previously obtained from #CFE_TBL_Register or #CFE_TBL_Share, that\n                       identifies the Table to be managed.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                        \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_NO_VALIDATION_PENDING \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_NO_VALIDATION_PENDING\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID    \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS              \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE         \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_Update, #CFE_TBL_Manage, #CFE_TBL_Load"]
3929    pub fn CFE_TBL_Validate(TblHandle: CFE_TBL_Handle_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3930}
3931unsafe extern "C" {
3932    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Perform standard operations to maintain a table.\n\n \\par Description\n        Applications should call this API periodically to process pending\n        requests for update, validation, or dump to buffer.  Typically,\n        the application that created the table would call this function at the\n        start or conclusion of any routine processing cycle.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in] TblHandle  Handle, previously obtained from #CFE_TBL_Register or #CFE_TBL_Share, that\n                       identifies the Table to be managed.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                     \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATED            \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATED\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS           \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_DUMP_PENDING       \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_DUMP_PENDING\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATE_PENDING     \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATE_PENDING\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_VALIDATION_PENDING \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_VALIDATION_PENDING\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_Update, #CFE_TBL_Validate, #CFE_TBL_Load, #CFE_TBL_DumpToBuffer"]
3933    pub fn CFE_TBL_Manage(TblHandle: CFE_TBL_Handle_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3934}
3935unsafe extern "C" {
3936    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Copies the contents of a Dump Only Table to a shared buffer\n\n \\par Description\n        Typically, apps should just call #CFE_TBL_Manage as part\n        of routine processing which will perform validation, update, or dump\n        if pending. This API is provided for the case where just a\n        dump should be performed.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        If the table does not have a dump pending status, nothing will occur (no error, no dump)\n\n \\param[in]  TblHandle      Handle of Table to be dumped.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                     \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS           \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_DUMP_PENDING       \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_DUMP_PENDING\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_Manage"]
3937    pub fn CFE_TBL_DumpToBuffer(TblHandle: CFE_TBL_Handle_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3938}
3939unsafe extern "C" {
3940    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Notify cFE Table Services that table contents have been modified by the Application\n\n \\par Description\n        This API notifies Table Services that the contents of the specified table has been\n        modified by the Application.  This notification is important when a table has been\n        registered as \"Critical\" because Table Services can then update the contents of the\n        table kept in the Critical Data Store.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        None\n\n \\param[in]  TblHandle      Handle of Table that was modified.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                     \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS           \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_Manage"]
3941    pub fn CFE_TBL_Modified(TblHandle: CFE_TBL_Handle_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3942}
3943unsafe extern "C" {
3944    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Obtain the current address of the contents of the specified table\n\n \\par Description\n        When a table has been created and initialized, it is available to\n        any application that can identify it with its unique handle.  In\n        order to view the data contained in the table, an application must\n        call this function or #CFE_TBL_GetAddresses.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        -# This call can be a blocking call when the table is not double buffered\n           and is shared with another application of lower priority that just happens\n           to be in the middle of a table update of the specific table.  If this occurs,\n           the application performing the table update will automatically have its\n           priority elevated in order to release the resource as soon as possible.\n        -# An application must always release the returned table address using the\n           #CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddress or #CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddresses function prior to\n           either a #CFE_TBL_Update call or any blocking call (e.g. - pending on software\n           bus message, etc).  Table updates cannot occur while table addresses have not\n           been released.\n        -# #CFE_TBL_ERR_NEVER_LOADED will be returned if the table has never been\n           loaded (either from file or from a block of memory), but the function\n           will still return a valid table pointer to a table with all zero content.\n           This pointer must be released with the #CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddress API before\n           the table can be loaded with data.\n\n \\param[out]  TblPtr    The address of a pointer @nonnull that will be loaded with the address of\n                        the first byte of the table.  This pointer can then be typecast\n                        by the calling application to the appropriate table data structure. *TblPtr is the address of\n                        the first byte of data associated with the specified table.\n\n \\param[in]  TblHandle  Handle, previously obtained from #CFE_TBL_Register or #CFE_TBL_Share, that\n                        identifies the Table whose address is to be returned.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                     \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATED            \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATED\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS           \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_UNREGISTERED        \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_UNREGISTERED\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NEVER_LOADED        \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NEVER_LOADED\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT            \\copybrief CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddress, #CFE_TBL_GetAddresses, #CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddresses"]
3945    pub fn CFE_TBL_GetAddress(
3946        TblPtr: *mut *mut libc::c_void,
3947        TblHandle: CFE_TBL_Handle_t,
3948    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3949}
3950unsafe extern "C" {
3951    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Release previously obtained pointer to the contents of the specified table\n\n \\par Description\n        Each application is \\b required to release a table address obtained through\n        the #CFE_TBL_GetAddress function.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        An application must always release the returned table address using the\n        #CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddress function prior to either a #CFE_TBL_Update call\n        or any blocking call (e.g. - pending on software bus message, etc).\n        Table updates cannot occur while table addresses have not been released.\n\n \\param[in] TblHandle  Handle, previously obtained from #CFE_TBL_Register or #CFE_TBL_Share, that\n                       identifies the Table whose address is to be released.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                     \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATED            \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATED\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS           \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NEVER_LOADED        \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NEVER_LOADED\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_GetAddress, #CFE_TBL_GetAddresses, #CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddresses"]
3952    pub fn CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddress(TblHandle: CFE_TBL_Handle_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3953}
3954unsafe extern "C" {
3955    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Obtain the current addresses of an array of specified tables\n\n \\par Description\n        When a table has been created and initialized, it is available to\n        any application that can identify it with its unique handle.  In\n        order to view the data contained in the table, an application must\n        call this function or #CFE_TBL_GetAddress.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        -# This call can be a blocking call when the table is not double buffered\n           and is shared with another application of lower priority that just happens\n           to be in the middle of a table update of the specific table.  If this occurs,\n           the application performing the table update will automatically have its\n           priority elevated in order to release the resource as soon as possible.\n        -# An application must always release the returned table address using the\n           #CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddress or #CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddresses function prior to\n           either a #CFE_TBL_Update call or any blocking call (e.g. - pending on software\n           bus message, etc).  Table updates cannot occur while table addresses have not\n           been released.\n        -# #CFE_TBL_ERR_NEVER_LOADED will be returned if the table has never been\n           loaded (either from file or from a block of memory), but the function\n           will still return a valid table pointer to a table with all zero content.\n           This pointer must be released with the #CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddress API before\n           the table can be loaded with data.\n\n \\param[out] TblPtrs   Array of Pointers @nonnull to variables that calling Application\n                       wishes to hold the start addresses of the Tables. *TblPtrs is an array of addresses of the\n                       first byte of data associated with the specified tables.\n\n \\param[in] NumTables  Size of TblPtrs and TblHandles arrays.\n\n \\param[in] TblHandles Array of Table Handles, previously obtained from #CFE_TBL_Register or #CFE_TBL_Share,\n                       of those tables whose start addresses are to be obtained.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                     \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATED            \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATED\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS           \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_UNREGISTERED        \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_UNREGISTERED\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NEVER_LOADED        \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NEVER_LOADED\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT            \\copybrief CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_GetAddress, #CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddress, #CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddresses"]
3956    pub fn CFE_TBL_GetAddresses(
3957        TblPtrs: *mut *mut *mut libc::c_void,
3958        NumTables: uint16,
3959        TblHandles: *const CFE_TBL_Handle_t,
3960    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3961}
3962unsafe extern "C" {
3963    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Release the addresses of an array of specified tables\n\n \\par Description\n        Each application is \\b required to release a table address obtained through\n        the #CFE_TBL_GetAddress function.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        An application must always release the returned table address using the\n        #CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddress function prior to either a #CFE_TBL_Update call\n        or any blocking call (e.g. - pending on software bus message, etc).\n        Table updates cannot occur while table addresses have not been released.\n\n \\param[in] NumTables  Size of TblHandles array.\n\n \\param[in] TblHandles Array of Table Handles @nonnull, previously obtained from #CFE_TBL_Register or #CFE_TBL_Share,\n                       of those tables whose start addresses are to be released.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                     \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATED            \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATED\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS           \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NEVER_LOADED        \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NEVER_LOADED\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT            \\copybrief CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_GetAddress, #CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddress, #CFE_TBL_GetAddresses"]
3964    pub fn CFE_TBL_ReleaseAddresses(
3965        NumTables: uint16,
3966        TblHandles: *const CFE_TBL_Handle_t,
3967    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3968}
3969unsafe extern "C" {
3970    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Obtain current status of pending actions for a table.\n\n \\par Description\n        An application is \\b required to perform a periodic check for an update\n        or a validation request for all the tables that it creates.  Typically,\n        the application that created the table would call this function at the\n        start or conclusion of any routine processing cycle.  If a table update\n        or validation request is pending, the Application should follow up with\n        a call to #CFE_TBL_Update or #CFE_TBL_Validate respectively.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[in] TblHandle  Handle, previously obtained from #CFE_TBL_Register or #CFE_TBL_Share, that\n                       identifies the Table to be managed.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                     \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATE_PENDING     \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_UPDATE_PENDING\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_VALIDATION_PENDING \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_VALIDATION_PENDING\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_INFO_DUMP_PENDING       \\copybrief CFE_TBL_INFO_DUMP_PENDING\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS           \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE\n\n \\note Some status return codes are \"success\" while being non-zero. This\n       behavior will change in the future.\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_Manage, #CFE_TBL_Update, #CFE_TBL_Validate, #CFE_TBL_GetInfo"]
3971    pub fn CFE_TBL_GetStatus(TblHandle: CFE_TBL_Handle_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
3972}
3973unsafe extern "C" {
3974    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Obtain characteristics/information of/about a specified table.\n\n \\par Description\n        This API provides the registry information associated with the specified\n        table.  The function fills the given data structure with the data found\n        in the Table Registry.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          None\n\n \\param[out]  TblInfoPtr   A pointer to a CFE_TBL_Info_t data structure @nonnull that is to be populated\n                           with table characteristics and information. *TblInfoPtr is the description of the tables\n                           characteristics and registry information stored in the #CFE_TBL_Info_t data structure\n                           format.\n \\param[in]  TblName       The application specific name @nonnull of the table of the form \"AppName.RawTableName\",\n                           where RawTableName is the name specified in the #CFE_TBL_Register API call.\n                           Example: \"ACS.TamParams\" for a table called \"TamParams\"\n                           that was registered by the application called \"ACS\".\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS              \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_NAME \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_NAME\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT     \\copybrief CFE_TBL_BAD_ARGUMENT\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_GetStatus"]
3975    pub fn CFE_TBL_GetInfo(
3976        TblInfoPtr: *mut CFE_TBL_Info_t,
3977        TblName: *const libc::c_char,
3978    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3979}
3980unsafe extern "C" {
3981    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Instruct cFE Table Services to notify Application via message when table requires management\n\n \\par Description\n        This API instructs Table Services to send a message to the calling Application\n        whenever the specified table requires management by the application.  This feature\n        allows applications to avoid polling table services via the #CFE_TBL_Manage call\n        to determine whether a table requires updates, validation, etc.  This API should\n        be called following the #CFE_TBL_Register API whenever the owning application requires\n        this feature.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n        - Only the application that owns the table is allowed to register a notification message\n        - Recommend \\b NOT using the ground command MID which typically impacts command counters.\n          The typical approach is to use a unique MID for inter-task communications\n          similar to how schedulers typically trigger application housekeeping messages.\n\n \\param[in]  TblHandle      Handle of Table with which the message should be associated.\n\n \\param[in]  MsgId          Message ID to be used in notification message sent by Table Services.\n\n \\param[in]  CommandCode    Command Code value to be placed in secondary header of message\n                            sent by Table Services.\n\n \\param[in]  Parameter      Application defined value to be passed as a parameter in the\n                            message sent by Table Services.  Suggested use includes an application's\n                            table index that allows the same MsgId and Command Code to be used for\n                            all table management notifications.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS                     \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID \\copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS           \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_NO_ACCESS\n \\retval #CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE      \\copybrief CFE_TBL_ERR_INVALID_HANDLE\n\n \\sa #CFE_TBL_Register"]
3982    pub fn CFE_TBL_NotifyByMessage(
3983        TblHandle: CFE_TBL_Handle_t,
3984        MsgId: CFE_SB_MsgId_t,
3985        CommandCode: CFE_MSG_FcnCode_t,
3986        Parameter: uint32,
3987    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3988}
3989unsafe extern "C" {
3990    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Initialize a message\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine initialize a message.  The entire message is\n          set to zero (based on size), defaults are set, then the\n          size and bits from MsgId are set.\n\n \\param[out]      MsgPtr      A pointer to the buffer that contains the message @nonnull.\n \\param[in]       MsgId       MsgId that corresponds to message\n \\param[in]       Size        Total size of the message (used to set length field)\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS             \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT    \\copybrief CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT"]
3991    pub fn CFE_MSG_Init(
3992        MsgPtr: *mut CFE_MSG_Message_t,
3993        MsgId: CFE_SB_MsgId_t,
3994        Size: CFE_MSG_Size_t,
3995    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
3996}
3997unsafe extern "C" {
3998    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Gets the total size of a message.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine gets the total size of the message.\n\n \\param[in]  MsgPtr      A pointer to the buffer that contains the message @nonnull.\n \\param[out] Size        Total message size @nonnull\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS             \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT    \\copybrief CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT"]
3999    pub fn CFE_MSG_GetSize(
4000        MsgPtr: *const CFE_MSG_Message_t,
4001        Size: *mut CFE_MSG_Size_t,
4002    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
4003}
4004unsafe extern "C" {
4005    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Sets the total size of a message.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine sets the total size of the message.\n\n \\param[in, out]  MsgPtr      A pointer to the buffer that contains the message @nonnull.\n \\param[in]       Size        Total message size\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS             \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT    \\copybrief CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT"]
4006    pub fn CFE_MSG_SetSize(MsgPtr: *mut CFE_MSG_Message_t, Size: CFE_MSG_Size_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
4007}

4011        MsgPtr: *const CFE_MSG_Message_t,

4017    pub fn CFE_MSG_SetType(MsgPtr: *mut CFE_MSG_Message_t, Type: CFE_MSG_Type_t) -> CFE_Status_t;

4088    pub fn CFE_MSG_GetNextSequenceCount(SeqCnt: CFE_MSG_SequenceCount_t)
4089    -> CFE_MSG_SequenceCount_t;
4090}

4118}

4161unsafe extern "C" {
4162    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Calculates and sets the checksum of a message\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine calculates the checksum of a message according\n          to an implementation-defined algorithm.  Then, it sets the checksum field\n          in the message with the calculated value.  The contents and location of\n          this field will depend on the underlying implementation of\n          messages.  It may be a checksum, a CRC, or some other algorithm.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          - If the underlying implementation of messages does not\n            include a checksum field, then this routine will return\n            #CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE\n\n \\param[in, out]  MsgPtr      A pointer to the buffer that contains the message @nonnull.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS             \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT    \\copybrief CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE  \\copybrief CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE"]
4163    pub fn CFE_MSG_GenerateChecksum(MsgPtr: *mut CFE_MSG_Message_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
4164}
4165unsafe extern "C" {
4166    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Validates the checksum of a message.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine validates the checksum of a message\n          according to an implementation-defined algorithm.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          - If the underlying implementation of messages does not\n            include a checksum field, then this routine will return\n            #CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE and set the IsValid parameter false.\n\n \\param[in]  MsgPtr      A pointer to the buffer that contains the message @nonnull.\n                         This must point to the first byte of the message header.\n \\param[out] IsValid     Checksum validation result @nonnull\n                         \\arg true - valid\n                         \\arg false - invalid or not supported/implemented\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS             \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT    \\copybrief CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE  \\copybrief CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE"]
4167    pub fn CFE_MSG_ValidateChecksum(
4168        MsgPtr: *const CFE_MSG_Message_t,
4169        IsValid: *mut bool,
4170    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
4171}
4172unsafe extern "C" {
4173    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Sets the function code field in a message.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine sets the function code of a message.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          - If the underlying implementation of messages does not\n            include a function code field, then this routine will do nothing to\n            the message contents and will return #CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE.\n\n \\param[in, out]  MsgPtr      A pointer to the buffer that contains the message @nonnull.\n \\param[in]       FcnCode     The function code to include in the message.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS             \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT    \\copybrief CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE  \\copybrief CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE\n"]
4174    pub fn CFE_MSG_SetFcnCode(
4175        MsgPtr: *mut CFE_MSG_Message_t,
4176        FcnCode: CFE_MSG_FcnCode_t,
4177    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
4178}
4179unsafe extern "C" {
4180    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Gets the function code field from a message.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine gets the function code from a message.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          - If the underlying implementation of messages does not\n            include a function code field, then this routine will\n            set FcnCode to zero and return #CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE\n\n \\param[in]  MsgPtr      A pointer to the buffer that contains the message @nonnull.\n \\param[out] FcnCode     The function code from the message @nonnull\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS            \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT   \\copybrief CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE \\copybrief CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE"]
4181    pub fn CFE_MSG_GetFcnCode(
4182        MsgPtr: *const CFE_MSG_Message_t,
4183        FcnCode: *mut CFE_MSG_FcnCode_t,
4184    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
4185}
4186unsafe extern "C" {
4187    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Gets the time field from a message.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine gets the time from a message.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          - If the underlying implementation of messages does not\n            include a time field, then this routine will set Time\n            to zero and return #CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE\n          - Note default implementation of command messages do not have a time field.\n\n \\param[in]  MsgPtr      A pointer to the buffer that contains the message @nonnull.\n \\param[out] Time        Time from the message @nonnull\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS            \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT   \\copybrief CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE \\copybrief CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE"]
4188    pub fn CFE_MSG_GetMsgTime(
4189        MsgPtr: *const CFE_MSG_Message_t,
4190        Time: *mut CFE_TIME_SysTime_t,
4191    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
4192}
4193unsafe extern "C" {
4194    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Sets the time field in a message.\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine sets the time of a message.  Most applications\n          will want to use #CFE_SB_TimeStampMsg instead of this function.  But,\n          when needed, this API can be used to set multiple messages\n          with identical time stamps.\n\n \\par Assumptions, External Events, and Notes:\n          - If the underlying implementation of messages does not include\n            a time field, then this routine will do nothing to the message contents\n            and will return #CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE.\n          - Note default implementation of command messages do not have a time field.\n\n \\param[in, out]  MsgPtr      A pointer to the message @nonnull.\n \\param[in]       NewTime     The time to include in the message.  This will usually be a time\n                              from #CFE_TIME_GetTime.\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS            \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT   \\copybrief CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE \\copybrief CFE_MSG_WRONG_MSG_TYPE"]
4195    pub fn CFE_MSG_SetMsgTime(
4196        MsgPtr: *mut CFE_MSG_Message_t,
4197        NewTime: CFE_TIME_SysTime_t,
4198    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
4199}
4200unsafe extern "C" {
4201    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Gets the message id from a message.\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine gets the message id from a message.  The message id\n        is a hash of bits in the message header, used by the software bus for\n        routing.  Message id needs to be unique for each endpoint\n        in the system.\n\n \\param[in]  MsgPtr      A pointer to the buffer that contains the message @nonnull.\n \\param[out] MsgId       Message id @nonnull\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS            \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT   \\copybrief CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT"]
4202    pub fn CFE_MSG_GetMsgId(
4203        MsgPtr: *const CFE_MSG_Message_t,
4204        MsgId: *mut CFE_SB_MsgId_t,
4205    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
4206}
4207unsafe extern "C" {
4208    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Sets the message id bits in a message.\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine sets the message id bits in a message.  The message id\n        is a hash of bits in the message header, used by the software bus for\n        routing.  Message id needs to be unique for each endpoint\n        in the system.\n \\par Note\n        This API only sets the bits in the header that make up the message ID.\n        No other values in the header are modified.\n\n        The user should ensure that this function is only called with a valid\n        MsgId parameter value.  If called with an invalid value, the results\n        are implementation-defined.  The implementation may or may not return\n        the error code #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT in this case.\n\n \\param[in, out]  MsgPtr      A pointer to the buffer that contains the message @nonnull.\n \\param[in]       MsgId       Message id\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS            \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT   \\copybrief CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT"]
4209    pub fn CFE_MSG_SetMsgId(MsgPtr: *mut CFE_MSG_Message_t, MsgId: CFE_SB_MsgId_t) -> CFE_Status_t;
4210}
4211unsafe extern "C" {
4212    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Gets message type using message ID\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine gets the message type using the message ID\n\n \\param[in]  MsgId       Message id\n \\param[out] Type        Message type @nonnull\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS            \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT   \\copybrief CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT"]
4213    pub fn CFE_MSG_GetTypeFromMsgId(
4214        MsgId: CFE_SB_MsgId_t,
4215        Type: *mut CFE_MSG_Type_t,
4216    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
4217}
4218unsafe extern "C" {
4219    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Perform any necessary actions on a newly-created message, prior to sending\n\n \\par Description\n          This routine updates and/or appends any necessary fields on a message, is\n          invoked via SB just prior to broadcasting the message.  The actions include\n          updating any values that should be computed/updated per message, including:\n                  - setting the sequence number\n                  - updating the timestamp, if present\n                  - computing any error control or checksum fields, if present\n\n          The MSG module implementation determines which header fields meet this criteria\n          and how they should be computed.\n\n          The BufferSize parameter indicates the allocation size message of the buffer that\n          holds the message (i.e. the message envelope size).  In some implementations, the\n          allocated buffer may include extra space in order to append a CRC or digital signature.\n\n \\sa CFE_MSG_VerificationAction\n\n \\param[inout] MsgPtr       A pointer to the buffer that contains the message @nonnull.\n \\param[in]    BufferSize   The size of the buffer encapsulating the message\n \\param[out]   IsAcceptable Output variable to be set, indicates message acceptability @nonnull\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS             \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT    \\copybrief CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT"]
4220    pub fn CFE_MSG_OriginationAction(
4221        MsgPtr: *mut CFE_MSG_Message_t,
4222        BufferSize: usize,
4223        IsAcceptable: *mut bool,
4224    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
4225}
4226unsafe extern "C" {
4227    #[doc = "/\n/**\n \\brief Checks message integrity/acceptability\n\n \\par Description\n        This routine validates that any error-control field(s) in the message header\n        matches the expected value.\n\n        The specific function of this API is entirely dependent on the header fields\n        and may be a no-op if no error checking is implemented.  In that case, it\n        will always output \"true\".\n\n \\note  Due to the fact that software bus uses a multicast architecture, this function\n        must not modify the message, as the buffer may be shared among multiple receivers.\n        This should generally be the inverse of CFE_MSG_OriginationAction(), but on the\n        origination side it may update header fields and/or modify the message, on\n        the verification/receive side it must only check those fields, not modify them.\n\n \\sa CFE_MSG_OriginationAction\n\n \\param[in]  MsgPtr        Message Pointer @nonnull\n \\param[in]  BufferSize    The size of the buffer encapsulating the message\n \\param[out] IsAcceptable  Output variable to be set, indicates message acceptability @nonnull\n\n \\return Execution status, see \\ref CFEReturnCodes\n \\retval #CFE_SUCCESS            \\copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n \\retval #CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT   \\copybrief CFE_MSG_BAD_ARGUMENT"]
4228    pub fn CFE_MSG_VerificationAction(
4229        MsgPtr: *const CFE_MSG_Message_t,
4230        BufferSize: usize,
4231        IsAcceptable: *mut bool,
4232    ) -> CFE_Status_t;
4233}
4234unsafe extern "C" {
4235    #[doc = " @brief Get the Base value (type/category) from a resource ID value\n\n This masks out the ID serial number to obtain the base value, which is different\n for each resource type.\n\n @note The value is NOT shifted or otherwise adjusted.\n\n @param[in]   ResourceId   the resource ID to decode\n @returns     The base value associated with that ID"]
4236    pub fn CFE_ResourceId_GetBase(ResourceId: CFE_ResourceId_t) -> uint32;
4237}
4238unsafe extern "C" {
4239    #[doc = " @brief Get the Serial Number (sequential ID) from a resource ID value\n\n This masks out the ID base value to obtain the serial number, which is different\n for each entity created.\n\n @param[in]   ResourceId   the resource ID to decode\n @returns     The serial number associated with that ID"]
4240    pub fn CFE_ResourceId_GetSerial(ResourceId: CFE_ResourceId_t) -> uint32;
4241}
4242unsafe extern "C" {
4243    #[doc = " @brief Locate the next resource ID which does not map to an in-use table entry\n\n This begins searching from StartId which should be the most recently issued ID\n for the resource category.  This will then search for the next ID which does\n _not_ map to a table entry that is in use.  That is, it does not alias any\n valid ID when converted to an array index.\n\n returns an undefined ID value if no open slots are available\n\n @param[in]   StartId   the last issued ID for the resource category (app, lib, etc).\n @param[in]   TableSize the maximum size of the target table\n @param[in]   CheckFunc a function to check if the given ID is available\n @returns     Next ID value which does not map to a valid entry\n @retval      #CFE_RESOURCEID_UNDEFINED if no open slots or bad arguments.\n"]
4244    pub fn CFE_ResourceId_FindNext(
4245        StartId: CFE_ResourceId_t,
4246        TableSize: uint32,
4247        CheckFunc: ::core::option::Option<unsafe extern "C" fn(arg1: CFE_ResourceId_t) -> bool>,
4248    ) -> CFE_ResourceId_t;
4249}
4250unsafe extern "C" {
4251    #[doc = " @brief Internal routine to aid in converting an ES resource ID to an array index\n\n @param[in]  Id        The resource ID\n @param[in]  BaseValue The respective ID base value corresponding to the ID type\n @param[in]  TableSize The actual size of the internal table (MAX index value + 1)\n @param[out] Idx       The output index\n\n @return Execution status, see @ref CFEReturnCodes\n @retval #CFE_SUCCESS                     @copybrief CFE_SUCCESS\n @retval #CFE_ES_BAD_ARGUMENT             @copybrief CFE_ES_BAD_ARGUMENT\n @retval #CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID @copybrief CFE_ES_ERR_RESOURCEID_NOT_VALID"]
4252    pub fn CFE_ResourceId_ToIndex(
4253        Id: CFE_ResourceId_t,
4254        BaseValue: uint32,
4255        TableSize: uint32,
4256        Idx: *mut uint32,
4257    ) -> int32;
4258}
4259#[doc = " \\brief PSP Status type for readability and potentially type safety"]
4260pub type CFE_PSP_Status_t = int32;
4261#[doc = " @brief For the @ref CFE_PSP_StatusToString() function, to ensure\n everyone is making an array of the same length."]
4262pub type CFE_PSP_StatusString_t = [libc::c_char; 12usize];
4263unsafe extern "C" {
4264    #[doc = " @brief Convert status to a string\n\n @param[in] status Status value to convert\n @param[out] status_string Buffer to store status converted to string\n\n @return Passed in string pointer"]
4265    pub fn CFE_PSP_StatusToString(
4266        status: CFE_PSP_Status_t,
4267        status_string: *mut CFE_PSP_StatusString_t,
4268    ) -> *mut libc::c_char;
4269}
4270unsafe extern "C" {
4271    #[doc = " @brief This is a BSP-specific cache flush routine\n\n Provides a common interface to flush the processor caches. This routine is in\n the BSP because it is sometimes implemented in hardware and sometimes taken\n care of by the RTOS.\n\n @param[in] type\n @param[in] address\n @param[in] size"]
4272    pub fn CFE_PSP_FlushCaches(type_: uint32, address: *mut libc::c_void, size: uint32);
4273}
4274unsafe extern "C" {
4275    #[doc = " @brief Fetches the size of the OS Critical Data Store area.\n\n @param[out] SizeOfCDS Pointer to the variable that will store the size of the CDS\n\n @return 0 (OS_SUCCESS or CFE_PSP_SUCCESS) on success, -1 (OS_ERROR or CFE_PSP_ERROR) on error"]
4276    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetCDSSize(SizeOfCDS: *mut uint32) -> int32;
4277}
4278unsafe extern "C" {
4279    #[doc = " @brief Writes to the CDS Block.\n\n @param[in] PtrToDataToWrite Pointer to the data that will be written to the CDS\n @param[in] CDSOffset        CDS offset\n @param[in] NumBytes         Number of bytes to write\n\n @return 0 (OS_SUCCESS or CFE_PSP_SUCCESS) on success, -1 (OS_ERROR or CFE_PSP_ERROR) on error"]
4280    pub fn CFE_PSP_WriteToCDS(
4281        PtrToDataToWrite: *const libc::c_void,
4282        CDSOffset: uint32,
4283        NumBytes: uint32,
4284    ) -> int32;
4285}
4286unsafe extern "C" {
4287    #[doc = " @brief Reads from the CDS Block\n\n @param[out] PtrToDataFromRead Pointer to the location that will store the data to be read from the CDS\n @param[in]  CDSOffset         CDS offset\n @param[in]  NumBytes          Number of bytes to read\n\n @return 0 (OS_SUCCESS or CFE_PSP_SUCCESS) on success, -1 (OS_ERROR or CFE_PSP_ERROR) on error"]
4288    pub fn CFE_PSP_ReadFromCDS(
4289        PtrToDataFromRead: *mut libc::c_void,
4290        CDSOffset: uint32,
4291        NumBytes: uint32,
4292    ) -> int32;
4293}
4294unsafe extern "C" {
4295    #[doc = " @brief Write one byte (ByteValue) to EEPROM address MemoryAddress\n\n @param[out] MemoryAddress Memory address to write to\n @param[in]  ByteValue     Value to write to memory\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_TIMEOUT write operation did not go through after a specific timeout.\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_ADDRESS_MISALIGNED if the address is not aligned to a 16-bit addressing scheme.\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4296    pub fn CFE_PSP_EepromWrite8(MemoryAddress: cpuaddr, ByteValue: uint8) -> int32;
4297}
4298unsafe extern "C" {
4299    #[doc = " @brief Write two bytes (uint16Value) to EEPROM address MemoryAddress\n\n @param[out] MemoryAddress Memory address to write to\n @param[in]  uint16Value   Value to write to memory\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_TIMEOUT write operation did not go through after a specific timeout.\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_ADDRESS_MISALIGNED if the address is not aligned to a 16-bit addressing scheme.\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4300    pub fn CFE_PSP_EepromWrite16(MemoryAddress: cpuaddr, uint16Value: uint16) -> int32;
4301}
4302unsafe extern "C" {
4303    #[doc = " @brief Write four bytes (uint32Value) to EEPROM address MemoryAddress\n\n @param[out] MemoryAddress Memory address to write to\n @param[in]  uint32Value   Value to write to memory\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_TIMEOUT write operation did not go through after a specific timeout.\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_ADDRESS_MISALIGNED if the address is not aligned to a 16-bit addressing scheme.\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4304    pub fn CFE_PSP_EepromWrite32(MemoryAddress: cpuaddr, uint32Value: uint32) -> int32;
4305}
4306unsafe extern "C" {
4307    #[doc = " @brief Enable the EEPROM for write operation\n\n @param[in] Bank The bank of EEPROM to enable\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4308    pub fn CFE_PSP_EepromWriteEnable(Bank: uint32) -> int32;
4309}
4310unsafe extern "C" {
4311    #[doc = " @brief Disable the EEPROM from write operation\n\n @param[in] Bank The bank of EEPROM to disable\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4312    pub fn CFE_PSP_EepromWriteDisable(Bank: uint32) -> int32;
4313}
4314unsafe extern "C" {
4315    #[doc = " @brief Power up the EEPROM\n\n @param[in] Bank The bank of EEPROM to power up\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4316    pub fn CFE_PSP_EepromPowerUp(Bank: uint32) -> int32;
4317}
4318unsafe extern "C" {
4319    #[doc = " @brief Power down the EEPROM\n\n @param[in] Bank The bank of EEPROM to power down\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4320    pub fn CFE_PSP_EepromPowerDown(Bank: uint32) -> int32;
4321}
4322unsafe extern "C" {
4323    #[doc = " @brief Sets up the exception environment for the chosen platform\n\n On a board, this can be configured to look at a debug flag or switch in order\n to keep the standard OS exception handlers, rather than restarting the system."]
4324    pub fn CFE_PSP_AttachExceptions();
4325}
4326unsafe extern "C" {
4327    #[doc = " @brief Defines the CPU and FPU exceptions that are enabled for each cFE Task/App\n\n This function sets a default exception environment that can be used\n\n @note The exception environment is local to each task. Therefore, this must\n be Called for each task that wants to do floating point and catch exceptions."]
4328    pub fn CFE_PSP_SetDefaultExceptionEnvironment();
4329}
4330unsafe extern "C" {
4331    #[doc = " @brief Returns the unread exception count\n\n @return The unread exception count"]
4332    pub fn CFE_PSP_Exception_GetCount() -> uint32;
4333}
4334unsafe extern "C" {
4335    #[doc = " @brief Retrieves a summary of an exception log entry\n\n @note This function returns CFE_PSP_SUCCESS to indicate that an entry was\n popped from the queue. This doesn't necessarily mean that the output fields\n have valid data, but it does mean they are initialized to something.\n\n @param[in]      ContextLogId ID of the exception log entry to get a summary for\n @param[in, out] TaskId       Pointer to the TaskID buffer\n @param[out]     ReasonBuf    Pointer to the buffer that will store the exception summary string\n @param[in]      ReasonSize   Maximum size of the summary string to retrieve\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success (see note above)\n @retval CFE_PSP_NO_EXCEPTION_DATA if no context available for reading"]
4336    pub fn CFE_PSP_Exception_GetSummary(
4337        ContextLogId: *mut uint32,
4338        TaskId: *mut osal_id_t,
4339        ReasonBuf: *mut libc::c_char,
4340        ReasonSize: uint32,
4341    ) -> int32;
4342}
4343unsafe extern "C" {
4344    #[doc = " @brief Retrieves exception log entry context information\n\n @param[in]  ContextLogId ID of the exception log entry to copy\n @param[out] ContextBuf   Pointer to the buffer where the context information is to be copied to\n @param[in]  ContextSize  Maximum size of context information data to copy\n\n @return Size of the copied data\n @retval CFE_PSP_NO_EXCEPTION_DATA if data has expired from the memory log"]
4345    pub fn CFE_PSP_Exception_CopyContext(
4346        ContextLogId: uint32,
4347        ContextBuf: *mut libc::c_void,
4348        ContextSize: uint32,
4349    ) -> int32;
4350}
4351unsafe extern "C" {
4352    #[doc = " @brief Returns the CPU ID as defined by the specific board and BSP.\n\n @return The processor ID"]
4353    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetProcessorId() -> uint32;

4355unsafe extern "C" {
4356    #[doc = " @brief Returns the Spacecraft ID (if any)\n\n @return The Spacecraft ID"]
4357    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetSpacecraftId() -> uint32;
4358}
4359unsafe extern "C" {
4360    #[doc = " @brief Returns the processor name\n\n @return The processor name"]
4361    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetProcessorName() -> *const libc::c_char;
4362}
4363unsafe extern "C" {
4364    #[doc = " @brief Read one byte of memory.\n\n @param[in]  MemoryAddress Address to be read\n @param[out] ByteValue     The address content will be copied to the location pointed to by this argument\n\n @return Always returns CFE_PSP_SUCCESS (if implemented)\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4365    pub fn CFE_PSP_MemRead8(MemoryAddress: cpuaddr, ByteValue: *mut uint8) -> int32;
4366}
4367unsafe extern "C" {
4368    #[doc = " @brief Write one byte of memory.\n\n @param[out] MemoryAddress Address to be written to\n @param[in]  ByteValue     The content pointed to by this argument will be copied to the address\n\n @return Always returns CFE_PSP_SUCCESS (if implemented)\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4369    pub fn CFE_PSP_MemWrite8(MemoryAddress: cpuaddr, ByteValue: uint8) -> int32;
4370}
4371unsafe extern "C" {
4372    #[doc = " @brief Read 2 bytes of memory.\n\n @param[in]  MemoryAddress Address to be read\n @param[out] uint16Value   The address content will be copied to the location pointed to by this argument\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_ADDRESS_MISALIGNED if the address is not aligned to a 16-bit addressing scheme.\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4373    pub fn CFE_PSP_MemRead16(MemoryAddress: cpuaddr, uint16Value: *mut uint16) -> int32;
4374}
4375unsafe extern "C" {
4376    #[doc = " @brief Write 2 bytes of memory.\n\n @param[out] MemoryAddress Address to be written to\n @param[in]  uint16Value   The content pointed to by this argument will be copied to the address\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_ADDRESS_MISALIGNED if the address is not aligned to a 16-bit addressing scheme.\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4377    pub fn CFE_PSP_MemWrite16(MemoryAddress: cpuaddr, uint16Value: uint16) -> int32;
4378}
4379unsafe extern "C" {
4380    #[doc = " @brief Read 4 bytes of memory.\n\n @param[in]  MemoryAddress Address to be read\n @param[out] uint32Value   The address content will be copied to the location pointed to by this argument\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_ADDRESS_MISALIGNED if the address is not aligned to a 16-bit addressing scheme.\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4381    pub fn CFE_PSP_MemRead32(MemoryAddress: cpuaddr, uint32Value: *mut uint32) -> int32;
4382}
4383unsafe extern "C" {
4384    #[doc = " @brief Write 4 bytes of memory.\n\n @param[out] MemoryAddress Address to be written to\n @param[in]  uint32Value   The content pointed to by this argument will be copied to the address\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_ADDRESS_MISALIGNED if the address is not aligned to a 16-bit addressing scheme.\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4385    pub fn CFE_PSP_MemWrite32(MemoryAddress: cpuaddr, uint32Value: uint32) -> int32;
4386}
4387unsafe extern "C" {
4388    #[doc = " @brief Copy 'n' bytes from 'src' to 'dest'\n\n Copies 'n' bytes from memory address pointed to by 'src' to memory\n address pointed to by 'dest'.\n\n @note For now we are using the standard C library call 'memcpy' but if we\n find we need to make it more efficient then we'll implement it in assembly.\n\n @param[out] dest Pointer to the destination address to copy to\n @param[in]  src  Pointer to the address to copy from\n @param[in]  n    Number of bytes to copy\n\n @return Always returns CFE_PSP_SUCCESS"]
4389    pub fn CFE_PSP_MemCpy(dest: *mut libc::c_void, src: *const libc::c_void, n: uint32) -> int32;
4390}
4391unsafe extern "C" {
4392    #[doc = " @brief Copy 'n' bytes of value 'value' to 'dest'\n\n Copies 'n' number of bytes of value 'value' to memory address pointed\n to by 'dest'.\n\n @note For now we are using the standard C library call 'memset' but if we\n find we need to make it more efficient then we'll implement it in assembly.\n\n @param[out] dest  Pointer to the destination address to copy to\n @param[in]  value Value to set\n @param[in]  n     Number of bytes to copy\n\n @return Always returns CFE_PSP_SUCCESS"]
4393    pub fn CFE_PSP_MemSet(dest: *mut libc::c_void, value: uint8, n: uint32) -> int32;
4394}
4395unsafe extern "C" {
4396    #[doc = " @brief Returns the location and size of the ES Reset information area.\n\n This area is preserved during a processor reset and is used to store the ER\n Log, System Log and reset-related variables\n\n @param[out] PtrToResetArea  Pointer to the variable that will store the location of the reset area\n @param[out] SizeOfResetArea Pointer to the variable that will store the reset area size\n\n @return 0 (OS_SUCCESS or CFE_PSP_SUCCESS) on success, -1 (OS_ERROR or CFE_PSP_ERROR) on error"]
4397    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetResetArea(
4398        PtrToResetArea: *mut cpuaddr,
4399        SizeOfResetArea: *mut uint32,
4400    ) -> int32;
4401}
4402unsafe extern "C" {
4403    #[doc = " @brief Returns the location and size of the memory used for the cFE user-reserved area.\n\n @param[out] PtrToUserArea  Pointer to the variable that will store the location of the user-reserved area\n @param[out] SizeOfUserArea Pointer to the variable that will store the size of the user-reserved area\n\n @return 0 (OS_SUCCESS or CFE_PSP_SUCCESS) on success, -1 (OS_ERROR or CFE_PSP_ERROR) on error"]
4404    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetUserReservedArea(
4405        PtrToUserArea: *mut cpuaddr,
4406        SizeOfUserArea: *mut uint32,
4407    ) -> int32;
4408}
4409unsafe extern "C" {
4410    #[doc = " @brief Returns the location and size of the memory used for the cFE volatile disk.\n\n @param[out] PtrToVolDisk  Pointer to the variable that will store the location of the cFE volatile disk\n @param[out] SizeOfVolDisk Pointer to the variable that will store the size of the cFE volatile disk\n\n @return 0 (OS_SUCCESS or CFE_PSP_SUCCESS) on success, -1 (OS_ERROR or CFE_PSP_ERROR) on error"]
4411    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetVolatileDiskMem(
4412        PtrToVolDisk: *mut cpuaddr,
4413        SizeOfVolDisk: *mut uint32,
4414    ) -> int32;
4415}
4416unsafe extern "C" {
4417    #[doc = " @brief Returns the location and size of the kernel memory.\n\n This function returns the start and end address of the kernel text segment.\n It may not be implemented on all architectures.\n\n @param[out] PtrToKernelSegment  Pointer to the variable that will store the location of the kernel text segment\n @param[out] SizeOfKernelSegment Pointer to the variable that will store the size of the kernel text segment\n\n @return 0 (OS_SUCCESS or CFE_PSP_SUCCESS) on success, -1 (OS_ERROR or CFE_PSP_ERROR) on error\n or CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4418    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetKernelTextSegmentInfo(
4419        PtrToKernelSegment: *mut cpuaddr,
4420        SizeOfKernelSegment: *mut uint32,
4421    ) -> int32;
4422}
4423unsafe extern "C" {
4424    #[doc = " @brief Returns the location and size of the kernel memory.\n\n This function returns the start and end address of the CFE text segment. It\n may not be implemented on all architectures.\n\n @param[out] PtrToCFESegment  Pointer to the variable that will store the location of the cFE text segment\n @param[out] SizeOfCFESegment Pointer to the variable that will store the size of the cFE text segment\n\n @return 0 (OS_SUCCESS or CFE_PSP_SUCCESS) on success, -1 (OS_ERROR or CFE_PSP_ERROR) on error"]
4425    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetCFETextSegmentInfo(
4426        PtrToCFESegment: *mut cpuaddr,
4427        SizeOfCFESegment: *mut uint32,
4428    ) -> int32;
4429}
4430unsafe extern "C" {
4431    #[doc = " @brief Validates the memory range and type using the global CFE_PSP_MemoryTable\n\n @param[in] Address    A 32-bit starting address of the memory range\n @param[in] Size       A 32-bit size of the memory range (Address + Size = End Address)\n @param[in] MemoryType The memory type to validate, including but not limited to:\n                       CFE_PSP_MEM_RAM, CFE_PSP_MEM_EEPROM, or CFE_PSP_MEM_ANY\n                       Any defined CFE_PSP_MEM_* enumeration can be specified\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS           Memory range and type information is valid and can be used.\n @retval CFE_PSP_INVALID_MEM_ADDR  Starting address is not valid\n @retval CFE_PSP_INVALID_MEM_TYPE  Memory type associated with the range does not match the passed-in type.\n @retval CFE_PSP_INVALID_MEM_RANGE The Memory range associated with the address is not large enough to contain\n                                   Address + Size."]
4432    pub fn CFE_PSP_MemValidateRange(Address: cpuaddr, Size: usize, MemoryType: uint32) -> int32;
4433}
4434unsafe extern "C" {
4435    #[doc = " @brief Returns the number of memory ranges in the CFE_PSP_MemoryTable\n\n @return Positive integer number of entries in the memory range table"]
4436    pub fn CFE_PSP_MemRanges() -> uint32;
4437}
4438unsafe extern "C" {
4439    #[doc = " @brief Populates one of the records in the CFE_PSP_MemoryTable.\n\n @note Because the table is fixed size, the entries are set by using the\n integer index. No validation is done with the address or size.\n\n @param[in] RangeNum   A 32-bit integer (starting with 0) specifying the MemoryTable entry.\n @param[in] MemoryType The memory type to validate, including but not limited to:\n                       CFE_PSP_MEM_RAM, CFE_PSP_MEM_EEPROM, or CFE_PSP_MEM_ANY\n                       Any defined CFE_PSP_MEM_* enumeration can be specified\n @param[in] StartAddr  A 32-bit starting address of the memory range\n @param[in] Size       A 32-bit size of the memory range (Address + Size = End Address)\n @param[in] WordSize   The minimum addressable size of the range:\n                       (CFE_PSP_MEM_SIZE_BYTE, CFE_PSP_MEM_SIZE_WORD, CFE_PSP_MEM_SIZE_DWORD)\n @param[in] Attributes The attributes of the Memory Range:\n                       (CFE_PSP_MEM_ATTR_WRITE, CFE_PSP_MEM_ATTR_READ, CFE_PSP_MEM_ATTR_READWRITE)\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS              Memory range set successfully.\n @retval CFE_PSP_INVALID_MEM_RANGE    The index into the table is invalid\n @retval FE_PSP_INVALID_MEM_ADDR      Starting address is not valid\n @retval CFE_PSP_INVALID_MEM_TYPE     Memory type associated with the range does not match the passed-in type.\n @retval CFE_PSP_INVALID_MEM_WORDSIZE The WordSize parameter is not one of the predefined types.\n @retval CFE_PSP_INVALID_MEM_ATTR     The Attributes parameter is not one of the predefined types.\n @retval OP_INVALID_MEM_SIZE          The Memory range associated with the address is not large enough to contain\n                                      Address + Size."]
4440    pub fn CFE_PSP_MemRangeSet(
4441        RangeNum: uint32,
4442        MemoryType: uint32,
4443        StartAddr: cpuaddr,
4444        Size: usize,
4445        WordSize: usize,
4446        Attributes: uint32,
4447    ) -> int32;
4448}
4449unsafe extern "C" {
4450    #[doc = " @brief Retrieves one of the records in the CFE_PSP_MemoryTable.\n\n @note Because the table is fixed size, the entries are accessed by using the integer index.\n\n @param[in]  RangeNum   A 32-bit integer (starting with 0) specifying the MemoryTable entry.\n @param[out] MemoryType A pointer to the 32-bit integer where the Memory Type is stored.\n                        Any defined CFE_PSP_MEM_* enumeration can be specified\n @param[out] StartAddr  Pointer to the 32-bit integer where the 32-bit starting address of the memory range is stored.\n @param[out] Size       A pointer to the 32-bit integer where the 32-bit size of the memory range is stored.\n @param[out] WordSize   A pointer to the 32-bit integer where the minimum addressable size of the range:\n                        (CFE_PSP_MEM_SIZE_BYTE, CFE_PSP_MEM_SIZE_WORD, CFE_PSP_MEM_SIZE_DWORD)\n @param[out] Attributes The attributes of the Memory Range:\n                        (CFE_PSP_MEM_ATTR_WRITE, CFE_PSP_MEM_ATTR_READ, CFE_PSP_MEM_ATTR_READWRITE)\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS           Memory range returned successfully.\n @retval CFE_PSP_INVALID_POINTER   Parameter error\n @retval CFE_PSP_INVALID_MEM_RANGE The index into the table is invalid"]
4451    pub fn CFE_PSP_MemRangeGet(
4452        RangeNum: uint32,
4453        MemoryType: *mut uint32,
4454        StartAddr: *mut cpuaddr,
4455        Size: *mut usize,
4456        WordSize: *mut usize,
4457        Attributes: *mut uint32,
4458    ) -> int32;
4459}
4460unsafe extern "C" {
4461    #[doc = " @brief Read one byte of memory.\n\n @param[in]  PortAddress Address to be read\n @param[out] ByteValue   The address content will be copied to the location pointed to by this argument\n\n @return Always returns CFE_PSP_SUCCESS (if implemented)\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4462    pub fn CFE_PSP_PortRead8(PortAddress: cpuaddr, ByteValue: *mut uint8) -> int32;
4463}
4464unsafe extern "C" {
4465    #[doc = " @brief Write one byte of memory.\n\n @param[out] PortAddress Address to be written to\n @param[in]  ByteValue   The content pointed to by this argument will be copied to the address\n\n @return Always returns CFE_PSP_SUCCESS (if implemented)\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4466    pub fn CFE_PSP_PortWrite8(PortAddress: cpuaddr, ByteValue: uint8) -> int32;
4467}
4468unsafe extern "C" {
4469    #[doc = " @brief Read 2 bytes of memory.\n\n @param[in]  PortAddress Address to be read\n @param[out] uint16Value The address content will be copied to the location pointed to by this argument\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_ADDRESS_MISALIGNED if the address is not aligned to a 16-bit addressing scheme.\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4470    pub fn CFE_PSP_PortRead16(PortAddress: cpuaddr, uint16Value: *mut uint16) -> int32;
4471}
4472unsafe extern "C" {
4473    #[doc = " @brief Write 2 bytes of memory.\n\n @param[out] PortAddress Address to be written to\n @param[in]  uint16Value the content pointed to by this argument will be copied to the address\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_ADDRESS_MISALIGNED if the address is not aligned to a 16-bit addressing scheme.\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4474    pub fn CFE_PSP_PortWrite16(PortAddress: cpuaddr, uint16Value: uint16) -> int32;
4475}
4476unsafe extern "C" {
4477    #[doc = " @brief Read 4 bytes of memory.\n\n @param[in]  PortAddress Address to be read\n @param[out] uint32Value The address content will be copied to the location pointed to by this argument\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_ADDRESS_MISALIGNED if the address is not aligned to a 16-bit addressing scheme.\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4478    pub fn CFE_PSP_PortRead32(PortAddress: cpuaddr, uint32Value: *mut uint32) -> int32;
4479}
4480unsafe extern "C" {
4481    #[doc = " @brief Write 4 bytes of memory.\n\n @param[out] PortAddress Address to be written to\n @param[in]  uint32Value The content pointed to by this argument will be copied to the address\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_ADDRESS_MISALIGNED if the address is not aligned to a 16-bit addressing scheme.\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED if not implemented"]
4482    pub fn CFE_PSP_PortWrite32(PortAddress: cpuaddr, uint32Value: uint32) -> int32;
4483}
4484unsafe extern "C" {
4485    #[doc = " @brief Initializes the Solid State recorder memory for a particular platform\n\n @note For the MCP750, this simply initializes the Hard Disk device.\n\n @param[in] bus\n @param[in] device\n @param[in] DeviceName\n\n @retval CFE_PSP_SUCCESS on success\n @retval CFE_PSP_ERROR on error"]
4486    pub fn CFE_PSP_InitSSR(bus: uint32, device: uint32, DeviceName: *mut libc::c_char) -> int32;
4487}
4488unsafe extern "C" {
4489    #[doc = " @brief Sample/Read a monotonic platform clock with normalization\n\n Outputs an OS_time_t value indicating the time elapsed since an epoch. The\n epoch is not defined, but typically represents the system boot time. The\n value increases continuously over time and cannot be reset by software.\n\n This is similar to the CFE_PSP_Get_Timebase(), but additionally it normalizes\n the output value to an OS_time_t, thereby providing consistent units to the\n calling application. Any OSAL-provided routine that accepts OS_time_t inputs\n may be used to convert this value into other standardized time units.\n\n @note This should refer to the same time domain as CFE_PSP_Get_Timebase(),\n the primary difference being the format and units of the output value.\n\n @sa CFE_PSP_Get_Timebase()\n\n @param[out] LocalTime Value of PSP tick counter as OS_time_t"]
4490    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetTime(LocalTime: *mut OS_time_t);
4491}
4492unsafe extern "C" {
4493    #[doc = " Provides the resolution of the least significant 32 bits of the 64-bit time\n stamp returned by CFE_PSP_Get_Timebase in timer ticks per second. The timer\n resolution for accuracy should not be any slower than 1000000 ticks per\n second or 1 us (microsecond) per tick\n\n @return The number of timer ticks per second of the time stamp returned by\n CFE_PSP_Get_Timebase"]
4494    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetTimerTicksPerSecond() -> uint32;
4495}
4496unsafe extern "C" {
4497    #[doc = " Provides the number that the least significant 32 bits of the 64-bit time\n stamp returned by CFE_PSP_Get_Timebase rolls over. If the lower 32 bits rolls\n at 1 second, then the CFE_PSP_TIMER_LOW32_ROLLOVER will be 1000000. If the\n lower 32 bits rolls at its maximum value (2^32) then\n CFE_PSP_TIMER_LOW32_ROLLOVER will be 0.\n\n @return The number that the least significant 32 bits of the 64-bit time\n stamp returned by CFE_PSP_Get_Timebase rolls over."]
4498    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetTimerLow32Rollover() -> uint32;
4499}
4500unsafe extern "C" {
4501    #[doc = " @brief Sample/Read a monotonic platform clock without normalization\n\n Provides a common interface to system timebase. This routine is in the BSP\n because it is sometimes implemented in hardware and sometimes taken care of\n by the RTOS.\n\n This is defined as a free-running, monotonically-increasing tick counter. The\n epoch is not defined, but typically is the system boot time, and the value\n increases indefinitely as the system runs. The tick period/rate is also not\n defined.\n\n Rollover events - where the range of representable values is exceeded - are\n theoretically possible, but would take many years of continuous uptime to\n occur (typically hundreds of years, if not thousands). System designers\n should ensure that the actual tick rate and resulting timebase range is\n sufficiently large to ensure that rollover is not a concern.\n\n @note This is a \"raw\" value from the underlying platform with minimal/no\n conversions or normalization applied. Neither the epoch nor the resolution of\n this tick counter is specified, and it may vary from platform to platform.\n Use the CFE_PSP_GetTime() function to sample the timebase and also convert\n the units into a normalized/more consistent form.\n\n @sa CFE_PSP_GetTime()\n\n @param[out] Tbu Buffer to hold the upper 32 bits of a 64-bit tick counter\n @param[out] Tbl Buffer to hold the lower 32 bits of a 64-bit tick counter"]
4502    pub fn CFE_PSP_Get_Timebase(Tbu: *mut uint32, Tbl: *mut uint32);
4503}
4504unsafe extern "C" {
4505    #[doc = " @brief Obtain the PSP version/baseline identifier string\n\n This retrieves the PSP version identifier string without extra info\n\n @return Version string. This is a fixed string and cannot be NULL."]
4506    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetVersionString() -> *const libc::c_char;
4507}
4508unsafe extern "C" {
4509    #[doc = " @brief Obtain the version code name\n\n This retrieves the PSP code name. This is a compatibility indicator for the\n overall NASA cFS ecosystem. All modular components which are intended to\n interoperate should report the same code name.\n\n @return Code name. This is a fixed string and cannot be NULL."]
4510    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetVersionCodeName() -> *const libc::c_char;
4511}
4512unsafe extern "C" {
4513    #[doc = " @brief Retrieves the numeric PSP version identifier as an array of uint8 values\n\n The array of numeric values is in order of precedence:\n  [0] = Major Number\n  [1] = Minor Number\n  [2] = Revision Number\n  [3] = Mission Revision\n\n The \"Mission Revision\" (last output) also indicates whether this is an\n official release, a patched release, or a development version.\n  0 indicates an official release\n  1-254 local patch level (reserved for mission use)\n  255 indicates a development build\n\n @param[out] VersionNumbers A fixed-size array to be filled with the version numbers"]
4514    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetVersionNumber(VersionNumbers: *mut uint8);
4515}
4516unsafe extern "C" {
4517    #[doc = " @brief Obtain the PSP library numeric build number\n\n The build number is a monotonically increasing number that (coarsely)\n reflects the number of commits/changes that have been merged since the epoch\n release. During development cycles this number should increase after each\n subsequent merge/modification.\n\n Like other version information, this is a fixed number assigned at compile\n time.\n\n @return The OSAL library build number"]
4518    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetBuildNumber() -> uint32;
4519}
4520unsafe extern "C" {
4521    #[doc = " @brief Entry point back to the BSP to restart the processor.\n\n The flight software calls this routine to restart the processor.\n\n @param[in] resetType Type of reset"]
4522    pub fn CFE_PSP_Restart(resetType: uint32);
4523}
4524unsafe extern "C" {
4525    #[doc = " @brief Returns the last reset type\n\n @note If a pointer to a valid memory space is passed in, it returns the reset\n sub-type in that memory. Right now the reset types are application-specific.\n For the cFE they are defined in the cfe_es.h file.\n\n @param restartSubType"]
4526    pub fn CFE_PSP_GetRestartType(restartSubType: *mut uint32) -> uint32;
4527}
4528unsafe extern "C" {
4529    #[doc = " @brief Configures the watchdog timer.\n\n To set up the timer resolution and/or other settings custom to this platform."]
4530    pub fn CFE_PSP_WatchdogInit();
4531}
4532unsafe extern "C" {
4533    #[doc = " @brief Enables the watchdog timer."]
4534    pub fn CFE_PSP_WatchdogEnable();
4535}
4536unsafe extern "C" {
4537    #[doc = " @brief Disables the watchdog timer."]
4538    pub fn CFE_PSP_WatchdogDisable();
4539}
4540unsafe extern "C" {
4541    #[doc = " @brief Services the watchdog timer according to the value set in WatchDogSet.\n\n Load the watchdog timer with a count that corresponds to the millisecond\n time given in the parameter.\n\n @note Currently an ExpireTime value of zero will result in the minimum reset\n time of 4.5 seconds. All other ExpireTime values will result in a reset time\n of 5.5 seconds."]
4542    pub fn CFE_PSP_WatchdogService();
4543}
4544unsafe extern "C" {
4545    #[doc = " @brief Gets the watchdog time in milliseconds\n\n @return The current watchdog value"]
4546    pub fn CFE_PSP_WatchdogGet() -> uint32;
4547}
4548unsafe extern "C" {
4549    #[doc = " @brief Sets the watchdog time in milliseconds\n\n @param[in] WatchdogValue New watchdog value to set"]
4550    pub fn CFE_PSP_WatchdogSet(WatchdogValue: uint32);
4551}
4552unsafe extern "C" {
4553    #[doc = " @brief Aborts the cFE startup\n\n Provides a common interface to abort the cFE startup process and return back\n to the OS.\n\n @note This is called by the cFE Core startup code when it needs to abort the\n       cFE startup. This should not be called by applications.\n\n @param[in] ErrorCode Reason for exiting"]
4554    pub fn CFE_PSP_Panic(ErrorCode: int32);
4555}
4556unsafe extern "C" {
4557    #[doc = " @brief PSP Entry Point to initialize the OSAL and start up the cFE\n\n This is the entry point that the real-time OS calls to start our software.\n This routine will do any BSP/OS-specific setup, then call the entry point of\n the flight software (i.e. the cFE main entry point).\n\n @note The flight software (i.e. cFE) should not call this routine."]
4558    pub fn CFE_PSP_Main();
4559}
